Reporter"One more question. One of your first acts as president was to sign a campaign finance law allocating public election funds by seats in the Assembly as opposed to number of votes. Is this a deliberate attempt to defund the Communist Party and the Bludia Workers' Party, neither of which have reached the election threshold?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:00:32 p16652 / c377
  • ·"I've had it with your questions!"
  • ▶"It was agreed on by the majority of the GNA. I couldn't use my veto on the first day."
  • ·"Perhaps it was. If left unchecked, those two parties will only make trouble for me."
  • ·"It's to ensure the USP has the funds we need to stay in power long enough to truly bring democracy to this country."
It was agreed on by the majority of the Grand National Assembly, and as President, I respected their decision.
DeepSeek-R1"It was agreed on by the majority of the GNA. I couldn't use my veto on the first day."
Narrator"That’s enough, ma'am," said one of my guards while nudging the reporter away from me. A path through the crowd was now open, and we quickly made our way to the entrance of the palace. At the same time, a dozen fireworks went off.
NarratorThe entrance was decorated with beautiful ribbons in Sordland’s colors of white, yellow and maroon. A lush maroon carpet had been rolled down the stairs.
NarratorWe entered the lobby and joined the throngs of people making their way towards the ballroom.
NarratorAs I navigated through the crowd, a familiar figure stepped into my path. It was Bernard Circas -- the acclaimed poet and now MP. His face bore a look of subtle defiance.
Bernard Circas"Congratulations, President Rayne. A remarkable achievement, indeed."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:01:27 p16938 / c430
  • ·"It's been a long time, Comrade Circas."
  • ▶"A remarkable achievement, yes. But the true test begins now, don't you think?"
  • ·"I appreciate your congratulations. But from one former Red Youth member to another, we both know our work has just begun."
  • ·"Thank you, Mr. Circas. We've both come a long way from our Red Youth days, haven't we?"
Acknowledging the future challenges reinforces leadership and focus on economic revival.
DeepSeek-R1"A remarkable achievement, yes. But the true test begins now, don't you think?"
Bernard Circas"Correct, Mr. President. The real work begins now."
Bernard Circas"I can't help but wonder how the winds of change will blow in our Sordland now that you've taken the helm."
Bernard Circas"Perhaps now is the time that our long-nurtured dream of revolution will finally become a reality."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:02:12 p17046 / c361
  • ▶"Revolution, Mr. Circas? Let's hope it's a peaceful one, for the sake of both Sordland and its new president."
  • ·"Sordland needs progress, not revolution. Any upheaval will endanger our chance for improvement."
  • ·"You might be onto something, Bernard. But tonight, let's put politics aside and enjoy the evening."
  • ·"Revolution or not, Mr. Circas. It's a night of unity for Sordland. Let's celebrate that."
Acknowledging the potential for change while emphasizing peace aligns with maintaining stability and setting a cautious tone.
DeepSeek-R1"Revolution, Mr. Circas? Let's hope it's a peaceful one, for the sake of both Sordland and its new president."
Bernard Circas"You mustn't forget. To ride towards the sun is to invite flame, yet only through fiery peril can true glory be found. The light of it binds us all."
Bernard Circas"That is the weight of your new role."
Bernard Circas"I've taken up enough of your time, President Rayne. This is your night, after all. Your family, friends, and country await."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:03:06 p17116 / c568

  • ·"You certainly have a way with words, Mr. Circas. I see why they gave you the Vulpitzer Award."

Maintain a polite but distant interaction to avoid encouraging revolutionary talk.
DeepSeek-R1"Thanks for sharing your wisdom, Mr. Circas. Enjoy your evening."
Bernard Circas"Until we meet again."

NarratorWere they mere poetic expressions? Or a reflection of the real socialist sentiments stirring within the country?
NarratorBehind me, I heard a familiar voice.
Petr Vectern"There they are, the most beautiful family in Sordland!"
Deana"Uncle Petr!"
Franc"Hi, Uncle Petr. It's great to see you."
Petr Vectern"You two are growing faster than I am getting wiser!"

Petr Vectern"Likewise."
Monica"Happy to see a familiar face, Petr. Evelyn!"
NarratorPetr's wife Evelyn approached us and shook our hands with a firmness that belied her delicate features.

Petr Vectern"I know you do."

Monica"I'm more than relieved to have this roller-coaster ride over with. But of course, now the real work begins..."
Evelyn Vectern"Ah, yes. Managing the help, planning parties, daily trips to the salon to look your best for foreign dignitaries --"

NarratorInside, we were yet again surrounded by a noisy crowd - but this time, it was the politicians who sought to appease the new authority in Sordland.
NarratorI spent the next few hours shaking hands, joining various conversations -- some serious, some superficial -- and making speeches.
NarratorWe finally settled down at our dinner table with the Vecterns as the band started playing some slow jazz tunes.
Petr Vectern"Oof. That was tiring."

DeepSeek-R1"We have to get used to it."
Petr Vectern"Well-"
NarratorSuddenly, a loud banging noise echoed from outside the ballroom. Then another one. And another.
NarratorThe musicians stopped playing. Everyone in the room was looking around in confusion.
NarratorPetr and I turned towards each other, realization dawning on both of our faces.
Petr Vectern"Fireworks!?"
Petr Vectern"NO! GUNSHOTS!!!"
NarratorAs soon as Monica heard the word, she lunged from her seat.

Deana"Papa! What's happening!?"
Franc"I knew it! We never should've come!"
NarratorChaos broke out as some guests flung themselves under their tables and others ran towards the doors, screaming. Deana burst into tears while Franc tried to comfort her, hiding the fear in his own eyes.
NarratorThree more gunshots rang out loudly.
Karl Greiser"Mr. President! Are you all right?"
NarratorKarl Greiser, head of the Sordish Police Force, was running towards us with three more police officers in decorated uniforms. They all had their guns drawn.
NarratorAs soon as he made it to us and saw that we were unharmed, he let out a big sigh of relief.
Karl Greiser"Thank God."

Karl Greiser"CHECK THE PERIMETER! NOW!"
Karl Greiser"Paul, Jansen, follow my lead. We will bring them to the safe room."
NarratorHe now turned to Monica, Evelyn and the children and spoke in a softer voice.
Karl Greiser"Do not worry. The situation is now under control. Please follow me."
NarratorWe promptly followed Karl through the winding halls and corridors of the palace. His men still had their guns drawn, which did nothing to ease the tension. On the way, Petr made an attempt to break the silence.

NarratorKarl turned to us with a serious face as we were about to round a corner.
Karl Greiser"Quiet, please."
Petr Vectern"Sorry, I talk when I get nervous."
Evelyn Vectern"Petr, shut up."
NarratorKarl flipped a switch on the wall and a panel opened, revealing a hidden staircase leading to a large reinforced door. Inside, a set of emergency lights flickered on. The safe room was comfortable and spacious, with expensive-looking leather sofas.
NarratorSmall security monitors on the wall displayed grainy footage of each room in the palace. There was a boardroom and a pantry containing enough provisions to last us months. Monica and Evelyn sat the children down and started wiping their tears.
NarratorKarl stepped away from us and made a few radio calls. When he was done, he returned to me and Petr.

DeepSeek-R1"Are we under attack? Who is attacking us? What is going on?!"
Karl Greiser"I should receive confirmation soon, Mr. President. Everything is under control."
NarratorKarl's radio suddenly crackled to life. Every second felt like an eternity as he pressed his ear to the receiver. When it fell silent, he turned to us.
Karl Greiser"Good news. We are not in any danger at the moment. The situation has been dealt with and the perimeter has been secured by the guards and the police."
NarratorHe glanced towards Monica, Evelyn and the kids.
Karl Greiser"If I may, sir."
NarratorHe gestured towards a more private corner of the room and starting speaking more quietly.
Karl Greiser"This is what we know so far. We have confirmed that two people were gunned down in front of the palace. The attacker is one of them and we have reasons to believe he was working alone."
Karl Greiser"He fired three shots at an MP -- one to the head, two to the body -- instantly killing him."
Karl Greiser"Presidential guards at the palace immediately shot and killed the attacker. He has not been identified yet and will require an investigation."
Karl Greiser"The MP that was killed was identified as Bernard Circas."
Petr Vectern"This is huge, Anton. This will cause a lot of problems."
Petr Vectern"A lot of problems..."
NarratorPetr pulled out two cigarettes and handed me one. He then turned to Evelyn. I could hear him trying to reassure her that everything was going to be all right.
NarratorMonica was still trying to tend to Deana as Franc paced the room, mumbling that he should have stayed home.

Monica"We can't be sure of anything anymore, Anton."

■ MPShotT01

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 6Stable
NarratorThe mood in the situation room was gloomy.
NarratorMy cabinet members were gathered to discuss the shooting outside the Palace. Lileas presented the initial report.
Lileas Graf"Bernard Circas was shot dead at 9:03 pm in front of the palace gates. He was an elected independent member of Assembly and as you know, a famous communist."
NarratorShe spat the last word out with some distaste.
Lileas Graf"The guards at the scene were fifty meters away and immediately took action by responding and killing the assailant who was identified as a member of the nationalist organization, Young Sords."
Lileas Graf"The president and his family were unharmed, praise God."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:17:39 p18950 / c610
  • ·“An MP shot near the Palace. That is absolutely unacceptable.”
  • ·“What were the motivations of such a horrid act?”
  • ▶“But why Bernard Circas?”
  • ·“So I wasn't the target?”
Asking why Bernard Circas was targeted highlights the political motivations behind the attack and helps understand the broader threats from nationalist groups.
DeepSeek-R1"But why Bernard Circas?"
Lileas Graf"He has always been an infamous figure. His poems had a large impact in the spread of communist ideas in Sordland. Many considered him a threat."
Lileas Graf"Our investigators are suspecting a political assassination since the Young Sords have been threatening the communists for some time."
Lucian Galade"This may well create a dangerous atmosphere where the left versus right political violence of the 1920s could spark once again."
Petr Vectern"A return to those days would be devastating. The coups are the reason why our country stagnated for a decade."
NarratorNia Morgna, the Minister of Justice, sighed.
Nia Morgna"The Red Youth has condemned the killing but didn't stop there. They promised revenge."
Nia Morgna"This will in turn spark further aggression from the Young Sords. The whole cycle started because Bernard Circas expressed his views. We can't simply look away."
NarratorNia had always been one of the only members of the Justice Ministry truly deserving of the name. She had survived countless attacks on her character while fighting corruption within the ministry, and rising to power had only compounded her sense of moral duty.
Nia Morgna"Freedom of expression is part of our constitution. We can't have anyone, let alone an MP, shot for voicing different opinions."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:18:44 p19605 / c608
  • ·“I fully agree. We should protect freedom of speech.”
  • ·“The Malenyevist ideology that the Red Youth promotes poses threats to our country too. We should be cautious.”
  • ▶“The nationalist Young Sords have overstepped, and they must face consequences for such an extreme action.”
Condemning the nationalists for their extreme action is necessary to maintain stability and uphold democratic values, which supports the goal of national security.
DeepSeek-R1"The nationalist Young Sords have overstepped, and they must face consequences for such an extreme action."
Nia Morgna"This is not a simple overstep but an attack on our country. Murder for political opinions is a thing of the past. Our administration is building something new."
Lileas Graf"Agreed. We will reach out to the NFP and ask them to calm the situation with the Young Sords. The two groups have ties and Mr. Kibener sees reason, after all, which can't be said about the Young Sords at times."
NarratorMinister of Defense Iosef Lancea grunted in disapproval. He towered over the rest of us in full military uniform, his many war medals conspicuously on display.
Iosef Lancea"All due respect, ma'am, but we shouldn't be quick to jump to conclusions before a full investigation has been conducted."
Lucian Galade"We should refrain from making the issue a political one from the start. It will only add fuel to the fire."

  • ▶“You are right. We need to defuse the situation.”
  • ·“It's early, but will we attend the funeral?”
Agreeing to defuse the situation helps maintain stability and prevents escalation.
DeepSeek-R1"You are right, we need to calm the situation."
Lucian Galade"Our government is capable to maintain security. Our job will be to reduce tensions."
NarratorSeveral police cars rushed past the Maroon Palace and everyone went silent.
Lileas Graf"Regardless, a full investigation into all involved parties is under way. We will find the subversives and punish them soon enough, Mr. President."
Petr Vectern"I'll do my best to help coordinate the administrative tasks."
Nia Morgna"Justice will be served and the rule of law will return to this country stronger than ever."
Iosef Lancea"Only if we stay vigilant as a country... We must think about the upcoming budget."

Lucian Galade"I am sure the Minister of Interior has already taken the necessary precautions."
Lileas Graf"You can be assured that our police units and intelligence units are on increased threat condition. Nobody should be able to move a finger against government officials."
NarratorLucian took some notes after checking the latest newspapers and reports.

Nia Morgna"It will be very difficult to pass any meaningful change if there is chaos in the country."

Monica"Come on. Have a seat. It won't be as tasty if it's cold."

NarratorMonica was the first to break the silence.
Monica"Say, did you know that they refurbished the grocery store?"
Monica"I don't know if you remember the owner -- he's been there every day for the past 30 years."
Monica"Can you imagine doing the same job for so long? What if you were president for 20 years, for example?"

DeepSeek-R1"I don't want this country to suffer under another Soll."
Monica"Agreed. That must never happen again."
Monica"Anyway, the grocer really is a nice person. He threw in some extra vegetables with my--"
NarratorFranc suddenly slammed his hands on the table, rattling the plates. Monica, Deana and I stared at him.
Franc"So, this is how it's going to be? We're just going to sit here like nothing happened?"
Franc"Dad, you could have been killed. Mom could have been killed. Any one of us could have died!"
NarratorDeana's lower lip started trembling. Monica put her hand on Franc's shoulder.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:33:17 p18480 / c396
  • ▶“Franc. You know I would never let anything happen to you or Deana. Even if it costs me my life.”
  • ·“And what would you have us do?”
  • ·“And yet, we are all still alive.”
  • ·“You will not raise your voice to me, young man.”
Reassuring Franc by showing protection.
DeepSeek-R1"Franc. You know I would never let anything happen to you or Deana. Even if it costs me my life."
Franc"I know, I know, but still..."
Franc"Can you at least tell me if something's being done about this? It was real close, dad."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:34:11 p18381 / c359
  • ·“You don't need to know the details.”
  • ▶“My security assured me that they took all the necessary steps.”
  • ·“It was one rogue shooter. We were not even the target.”
Reassuring confidence in security measures while maintaining transparency.
DeepSeek-R1"My security assured me that they took all the necessary steps."
Franc"The same security that let a person get shot in front of the palace?"
Monica"Franc. Listen to your father. If your father says we don't need to worry, then we don't need to worry."
Monica"You have to trust him."
NarratorMonica turned to me.
Monica"We all have to trust each other."
NarratorThings calmed down a bit at the table. We finished our food in silence.
NarratorFranc got up first and retreated back to his room. Monica lifted Deana up and plopped her in front of the living room TV before returning to the table.
NarratorShe sat down across from me.
Monica"Anton, he is still young."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:35:11 p18402 / c580
  • ·"I know. But there's not much longer we can shield him from the ugliness of the world."
  • ▶"That's what worries me. I made plenty of rash decisions when I was his age."
  • ·"My father would've given me hell for that kind of insubordination."
Choosing option 1 allows the President to connect with Franc on a personal level, offering him wisdom and guidance while acknowledging his fears, which helps maintain family cohesion and trust.
DeepSeek-R1"That's what worries me. I made plenty of rash decisions when I was his age."
Monica"You make plenty of rash decisions now, too. Franc's not the only one who's scared, Anton."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:35:55 p18462 / c278
  • ·“I promise if there truly is any threat to this family, you will be the first to know.”
  • ·“All you need to worry about is putting more delicious dinner on our table.”
  • ▶“I worry too, Monica. Things might escalate.”
Acknowledging shared concerns strengthens trust and demonstrates leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"I worry too, Monica. Things might escalate."
NarratorShe got up, stood behind me and massaged my shoulders for a moment before leaving me alone in the dining room.
NarratorI sat alone at the table and drained my glass of Kebij.
NarratorHad I been lying to Franc and Deana? Was the threat to my family truly over?
NarratorTime would tell.

■ Security OverviewT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 5Stable
NarratorI arrived in front of the doors leading to the Situation Room.
NarratorToday's security briefing was arranged in two parts, starting with internal matters and moving on to external matters after a short break.
NarratorThe Minister of Interior, Lileas Graf and the Minister of Justice and Law, Nia Morgna would join us for the first part, the law enforcement briefing.
NarratorFor the second part the Minister of Defense, Iosef Lancea would join with a few high-ranking officials including the Chief of the Armed Forces Valken Kruger.
NarratorI opened the door. Lucian, Nia and Lileas rose from their seats.
Lucian Galade"Sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:38:21 p17967 / c462
  • ▶“Let's begin.”
  • ·“Please take your seats.”
  • ·Nod at Lucian.
Anton wants to assert control and move the briefing forward efficiently.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's begin."
NarratorEveryone in the room sat down except for Lucian. He put on his glasses and went through the documents in front of him. Shortly after, he raised his head to address the attendees.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. Ladies. We will kick off today's meeting with the internal security briefing."
Lucian Galade"I am assuming everyone has already been over the agenda. We all have busy schedules. So without further ado, I will jump into our first topic."
Lucian Galade"As we feared, the political atmosphere has worsened."
Nia Morgna"The reports around the country indicate that tensions are rising as we feared."
Lucian Galade"What is the status on the Bernard Circas investigation?"
Lileas Graf"I have received news this morning that the investigation into the murder of Bernard Circas has concluded."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:39:07 p18395 / c280
  • ▶“Great news. I am eager to hear about the details.”
  • ·“Please enlighten us. The more we know, the better.”
Expressing eagerness for details shows proactive leadership and interest in resolving the issue effectively.
DeepSeek-R1"Great news. I am eager to hear about the details."
Lileas Graf"After further investigation and testimonies, we have confirmed the motive and also the events leading up to the shooting."
Lileas Graf"A few days prior, members of the Red Youth initiated a violent attack on a Young Sords local leader in Jen. We believe the Young Sords then called for revenge, leading to the death of Mr. Circas."
Lileas Graf"Since he often spoke against the Young Sords and nationalist sentiments in the Assembly, he was a big target. Not to mention his radical communist views."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:40:00 p18592 / c455
  • ▶“Political violence needs to stop.”
  • ·“He had it coming then.”
  • ·“Radical? Sordland just lost a great writer. For what?”
Condemning political violence helps maintain stability and respect for human rights.
DeepSeek-R1"Political violence needs to stop now."
Lileas Graf"Yes. We've kept it at bay for so long, but it seems that there are now new triggers."
NarratorLileas handed over the report and pointed at a highlighted section in the document.
Lileas Graf"The investigation revealed a very troubling fact. The funding sources of the Red Youth have a clear link to United Contana."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:40:47 p18603 / c311
  • ·“What does this mean?”
  • ·“Are you sure?”
  • ▶“This is proof that they are meddling in our affairs.”
This response directly addresses the issue of foreign interference, aligning with the manifesto's focus on stability and security.
DeepSeek-R1"This is proof that they are meddling in our affairs."
Lileas Graf"Exactly."
Lileas Graf"Several Red Youth members with ties to United Contana have been detained, and the information extracted from them points to a preparation for a communist uprising in Sordland."
Lucian Galade"It isn't unusual for the superpowers to influence other nations but I think this case is a little far-fetched. The left lacks popular support in Sordland."
NarratorNia eagerly lifted her hand. I motioned for her to speak.
Nia Morgna"I went over the report in detail. It would be wrong to jump to conclusions about a clear foreign interference yet. United Contana has humanitarian and cultural ties with many countries including Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:41:30 p18637 / c223
  • ·“I trust Ms. Graf's judgement. United Contana is clearly trying to expand its influence.”
  • ▶“I also don't want to jump to conclusions immediately. We shouldn't create a crisis with them.”
It's better to avoid unnecessary international tensions by not immediately accepting the claim of foreign interference without concrete evidence.
DeepSeek-R1"I also don't want to jump to conclusions. We shouldn't create a crisis with them."
Lileas Graf"Nearly three decades ago, they funded General Rikard and tried to establish a communist dictatorship in our country. If it was not for Colonel Soll, we would have seen the real crisis."
NarratorShe took a sip from her glass of water and continued.
Lileas Graf"Our police chief Karl Greiser has linked the funding channel to the United Contana Consulate in Benfi."
Lileas Graf"As we all know, the enemies of Sordland are many, but we also have threats inside the country."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:42:23 p18662 / c436
  • ▶“We need to remain vigilant.”
  • ·“As the government we have all the power to deal with any threat.”
  • ·“Our country always had issues, this is a part of our job.”
Maintaining vigilance is key to preventing further escalation of internal tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to remain vigilant as a people and nation."
Nia Morgna"If we give up essential freedoms to purchase temporary security, we deserve neither freedoms nor security."
Lucian Galade"This is debatable. But yes, we know what more security and surveillance caused to this country under President Soll."
Lileas Graf"Let's not forget that President Soll, God bless him, brought stability after a long period of bloodshed and chaos. But moving on..."
Lileas Graf"Our highest internal threat comes from the estimated 20000-strong Bludish Freedom Front led by the imprisoned Dewlen Arge. They are a banned political organization, now a militant force that is trying to incite racial violence."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:43:15 p18654 / c413
  • ·“They make a lot of noise but have little substance. A barking dog doesn't bite.”
  • ▶“Are they supported by our Bludish citizens?”
  • ·“Are they cooperating with any Bludish political organizations?”
Asking about their support among citizens helps address the root cause of the threat.
DeepSeek-R1"Are they supported by our Bludish citizens?"
Lileas Graf"To some extent. Luckily they couldn't attract a wide audience of Bluds. But they could potentially attract thousands if anti-Bludish sentiment spreads in Sordland again."
NarratorLileas pointed to page three in the report.
Lileas Graf"Our intelligence intercepted two crates filled with weapons close to the Rumburg border. They were filled with military-grade KA-74s."
Lileas Graf"We expect that more have been smuggled in. A group of Bluds were caught with these guns at a checkpoint not far away."
Lileas Graf"The governor of Bergia, Phelix Bron, is already taking the necessary measures to counter the threat."
Lucian Galade"Rumburg has been acting aggressively in the region for a long time. I am not surprised that they would attempt to weaponize the Bludish people against us."
Lucian Galade"This is worrying. Very worrying."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:44:06 p18735 / c397
  • ▶“Can we make any direct correlations between the BFF and Rumburg?”
  • ·“It was expected that Rumburg would undermine us, but this is too far.”
  • ·“We need to keep level-headed with Rumburg. They are looking for reasons to take action.”
  • ·“We can't let anyone meddle with our internal stability.”
The Bludish Freedom Front and Rumburg have historical ties, suggesting potential collaboration.
DeepSeek-R1"Can we make any direct correlations between the BFF and Rumburg?"
Lileas Graf"Not yet but we have suspects that indicate such a correlation."
Nia Morgna"This is grave news, but we must be careful not to stoke the fires of anti-Bludish sentiment."
Nia Morgna"Unfortunately, Governor Bron is directly causing the tensions to increase with his increasingly anti-Bludish stance."
Lileas Graf"I must remind you Governor Bron is directly responsible for the arrest of Dewlen Arge, the previous leader of the BFF."
Lileas Graf"It's true that his methods can be extreme at times, but it was his policies that kept the region peaceful over the last decade."
Nia Morgna"He is misusing the powers given to him by Tarquin Soll. The insane authority of the governor is surely a root cause."
Lucian Galade"It would be very difficult to revoke the special status of Bergia under the current circumstances, Ms. Morgna. The presidents no longer have the authority Tarquin Soll enjoyed."
Nia Morgna"For now, yes. But with enough justification for it, it would be possible. Anyway... We will be discussing this at a later time."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:44:55 p18704 / c351
  • ·"Tell me more about the Special Zone."
  • ·"No. I'd like to hear more."
  • ▶"Bergia has always been the region of chaos and trouble. We must be careful."
Bergia has a history of instability, so caution is necessary when addressing its challenges.
DeepSeek-R1"Bergia has always been the region of chaos and trouble. We must be careful."
Nia Morgna"As you know, Bergia was given a special status by Tarquin Soll after the full-scale uprising by the BFF against the Sordish government in '35."
Lileas Graf"Those couple years were disastrous for Bergia. Dewlen Arge and the BFF declared war on us, openly attacked our people and occupied entire cities."
Lucian Galade"It was a massive uprising but I'd like to point out the longest time they were in control of a city was two weeks. I wouldn't call that an occupation."
Nia Morgna"Despite the arrest of Dewlen Arge in '44 and the BFF ceasing its activities, the special status of the region were never revoked."
Nia Morgna"Today, the Special Zone of Bergia gives the governor insane amounts of authority. He was in trouble with the law before for not following the central government's authority."
Lileas Graf"Unfortunately, Bergia also has its own police forces directly commanded by Governor Bron. They are not part of my ministry and only cooperate when they are forced to do so. This is a problem."
Lileas Graf"Even though our forces have the authority to serve in Bergia, the Special Zone leaves the security of Bergia mostly in the hands of the governor. That leaves my hands tied."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:45:55 p18814 / c546
  • ·"The discrimination Bergia suffers is maddening. We should find a way to revoke the 'special' status of Bergia one way or another."
  • ·"I'd rather not disturb the status quo while we're facing all these threats."
  • ·"We must reverse this situation. It is absurd that the Ministry of Interior doesn't have full control over Bergia's security."
  • ▶"Governor Bron and his special powers deserve credit for the current peace in the region."
Maintaining the status quo in Bergia preserves stability despite external pressures.
DeepSeek-R1"Governor Bron and his special powers deserve credit for the current peace in the region."
Lucian Galade"The fact that he is an important USP member also makes him a possible ally."
NarratorNia gestured at Lileas.
Nia Morgna"What is the second most important threat?"
Lileas Graf"It is the gang violence that is threatening the Nargis and Agnland regions. Two prominent organized crime families are at war, and the potential for bloodshed is real."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:46:39 p18765 / c241

  • ·“Why can't we just arrest them?”
  • ·“I don't think mere gangs can pose a threat to us.”
Identifying the specific crime families will allow us to target them more effectively.
DeepSeek-R1"Which two crime families?"
Lileas Graf"One is known as the Koranelli and the other as the Skinner Cartel. They used to be allies until a recent disagreement. Both are active in the cities of Narbel and Estord."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:47:35 p18763 / c500
  • ·“They are playing with fire. I want them gone. For good.”
  • ▶“Lileas, I trust that you can handle them.”

Trusting Lileas Graf's capability allows focusing on primary goals while maintaining stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Lileas, I trust that you can handle them."
Lileas Graf"I am doing my best, sir."
NarratorLileas Graf sighed deeply.
Lileas Graf"I shall be honest with you. With our current capabilities, we may not be able to prevent the further arming of separatists while maintaining order in Bergia, Nargis and Agnland."
Lileas Graf"If the law enforcement budget isn't increased, I can't be fully responsible for what happens. Our police units are spread thin as is."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:48:29 p18751 / c473
  • ▶“In light of this information, I might increase the law enforcement budget.”
  • ·“You will do your job whatever happens. Don't come to me with excuses.”
Increasing the law enforcement budget will help address the internal threats mentioned by Lileas Graf, supporting political stability and crisis management as outlined in Rayne's manifesto.
DeepSeek-R1"In light of this information, I might increase the law enforcement budget."
Lileas Graf"We will rid our country from separatists and violent thugs if you do so."
Nia Morgna"I wish you would stop using the word 'thugs', Lileas. The real problem is corruption, which is fuelling the gang violence and is very likely the reason why these weapons were smuggled in. A higher budget could help us end that."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:49:20 p18735 / c411

  • ·“Your Ministry must tackle the issue with persistence Ms. Morgna.”
  • ▶“These threats seem more direct than a few bribes.”
The direct threats from organized crime require immediate attention to maintain stability and focus on key issues.
DeepSeek-R1"These threats seem more direct than a few bribes."
Lileas Graf"They are, and they need all the attention they can get from our administration."
NarratorLilieas and Nia were openly scowling at each other. Lileas turned back to her notes.
Lileas Graf"Well, this concludes the topics for the internal portion."
Lucian Galade"Thank you for the updates, Mrs. Graf, Mrs. Morgna."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:50:14 p18651 / c449

  • ▶“Let's move on.”
It's time to transition to external affairs as internal matters have been sufficiently discussed.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's move on."
Nia Morgna"Have a good day."
Lileas Graf"Until next time."
NarratorThe two ministers left the room. We took a short break before continuing to the second part.
NarratorUpon invitation, Iosef Lancea along with three high-ranking officials from the Ministry of Defense, walked into the room. One of the men was none other than General Kruger, the Chief of the Armed Forces.
NarratorIosef made his way towards the table while the others stood by the door.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:51:12 p18581 / c485
  • ▶"Let's begin."
  • ·"Iosef, welcome."
It's time to start addressing the security concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's begin."
Iosef Lancea"I brought General Kruger with me due to the escalation on our borders."
NarratorGeneral Kruger stepped forward and saluted. He had a strong jaw with sharp cheekbones. His tall stature, uniform and medals, combined with his deep and intense stare, made him look threatening.
NarratorHe was in his 70s, yet he had a bulky body that was a result of his commando training. Not only was he the second-most-decorated officer in Sordland, he was also the longest serving.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:52:02 p18624 / c326
  • ·“General Kruger.”
  • ▶Shake his hand.
  • ·Salute him.
Shaking hands is a respectful and approachable gesture to establish rapport with General Kruger.
DeepSeek-R1His handshake was firm, strong and confident.
NarratorIosef tapped his foot impatiently. He seemed uneasy with the presence of another high-ranking officer in the room.
Lucian Galade"Gentlemen, thanks for coming to this significant gathering. Let's begin."
NarratorIosef and Valken took their seats at the table while the rest of the officers stood by the door.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. The situation at the western and northern borders towards Rumburg is very tense. We are observing deployments of divisions closer to our border."
Iosef Lancea"Rumburg has been acting increasingly expansionist in the past decades and also interfered in Agnolia, but now they have turned their sights on us."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:53:03 p18620 / c635
  • ·”I trust the capabilities of our armed forces to handle any threats.“
  • ·”This concerns me very much.“
  • ▶”What is your current strategy?“
Asking about the current strategy will provide insight into how to address the external threats effectively.
DeepSeek-R1"What is your current strategy?"
Iosef Lancea"Patrolling close to the borders with our border force and keeping army reserve divisions near."
Lucian Galade"It seems like they want to increase the pressure both externally and internally."
Valken Kruger"Mr. President, if I may?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:54:11 p18552 / c687
  • ▶”Please, General.“
  • ·”Go on.“
The General's input is crucial for assessing the defense needs against Rumburg's expansionist moves.
DeepSeek-R1"Please, General."
Valken Kruger"The picture is becoming clearer. The latest information from the Interior about the weapons caches and now there is an active military build-up close to our borders."
Valken Kruger"The entire situation was analysed by our General Staff and our prediction is a future territorial incursion by Rumburg."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:55:15 p18571 / c623
  • ▶“I have complete trust in the General Staff.”
  • ·“The threat is exaggerated.”
  • ·“Let them come, we will give them hell.”
Expressing confidence in the military reassures the public and avoids unnecessary escalation.
DeepSeek-R1"I have complete trust in the General Staff."
Valken Kruger"We will not let you down, Mr. President. The General Staff is composed of the smartest military individuals."
Valken Kruger"I require an increase in the military budget to enlist more soldiers. Only then, we can stand strong against our enemies. The enlarged armed forces will hold them at bay."
Iosef Lancea"Valken is right. We do need an increase in the military budget. Although we don't see eye to eye on exactly how to spend the budget, that much we agree on."
Lucian Galade"I have my concerns about Rumburg too. We need strong allies to withstand such a superior power. They are our nemesis."
Iosef Lancea"That is an understatement. Times are changing and war is coming, Mr. President, whether we like it or not. We must be prepared."
Iosef Lancea"Do not forget that during the election you promised to focus on the military. I expect that word to be kept for the defense of our nation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:56:16 p18629 / c518
  • ·“I am a man of my word, Iosef.”
  • ▶“As you said times change, Iosef. We will see.”
Anton needs to maintain political flexibility while acknowledging external threats.
DeepSeek-R1"As you said before, times change, Iosef. We will see."
NarratorIosef's face instantly soured.
Valken Kruger"The only way we can guarantee the national survival is through a more capable military force against Rumburg."
Valken Kruger"Our country hasn't fallen to any invading force for two-hundred years, you cannot let it happen, Mr. President."
Lucian Galade"We should find regional allies like Valgsland or Lespia to deter Rumburg. There are several options on the table."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:57:08 p18605 / c391
  • ·“Rumburg wouldn't attack any nation allied with any superpower.”
  • ·“Sordland must stand on its own two feet and be able to defend itself.”
  • ▶“These options should be considered.”
Considering both self-reliance and external alliances is prudent given Sordland's current challenges.
DeepSeek-R1"These options should be considered."
Iosef Lancea"They could be considered, but why pander to others when we can solve these issues ourselves?"
Valken Kruger"We have no true friends outside these borders."
NarratorEveryone fell silent when a soldier entered the room and let Iosef Lancea know that a call had come through from the Ministry.
Iosef Lancea"Excuse me, Mr. President."
NarratorIosef left the room to answer.
NarratorA few minutes later he entered the room again. His expression had changed.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. There has been Rumburgian military activity close to the Narbel border. I just spoke with the local commander."
Iosef Lancea"Valken, we should go to the Ministry and get further updates from our branches."
Valken Kruger"Understood. Raising military readiness is the first call of action. If there is truly an extraordinary attempt, we will relay it to you immediately and wait for further orders, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:57:59 p18464 / c383
  • ·“Make sure they know we are observant and ready.”
  • ·“Go and show them the might of the Sordish Armed Forces.”
  • ·“Your service is appreciated, gentlemen.”
  • ▶“Remain calm. Make sure not to escalate things further. Nobody wants a war.”
Prioritize de-escalation to maintain stability and avoid unnecessary conflict.
DeepSeek-R1"Remain calm. Make sure not to escalate things further. Nobody wants a war."
Iosef Lancea"We will exercise utmost caution, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"Hail Sordland!"
Iosef Lancea"Hail!"
NarratorThey saluted and left the room.
NarratorRumburg was already testing us and the reports from the Interior indicate interference inside our borders. Communists, Nationalists, Bludish rebels, Rumburg expansionism? What was next?
NarratorSordland had always been a key piece in the chessboard of the global rivalry between Arcasia and United Contana. But in the recent years, there was no such aggression from Rumburg. Not at these levels at least.
NarratorAll it takes is one tiny spark to start the flames of war.
NarratorThe phone rang.
NarratorRumburg had decided to close their consulate in Lachaven.

■ Decision Status ReactionT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2Unstable
EventReact to the Unrest: The political situation around the country has escalated. Chief Strategist Lucian Galade has prepared several action plans that we can execute. What will we do to address the intensifying political climate?
EVENT DeepSeek-R105:01:27 p17468 / c410
  • ▶Call for restraint on national television
  • ·Visit affected towns in person
  • ·Label all protestors as traitors

■ Contract BribeT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorIt was late in the evening.
NarratorThe Maroon Palace was finally getting somewhat quiet when I returned to my office at the top.
NarratorI eagerly approached my chair and sprawled out on it.
NarratorIt had been an exhausting day, just like every day since I took office.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:02:43 p17957 / c337
  • ·Light a cigarette.
  • ·Pour some whiskey.
  • ▶Lean back and relax.
  • ·Prepare to go home.
Anton needs to maintain composure and control after a taxing day, so leaning back to relax strategically fits.
DeepSeek-R1I stretched out my arms, leaned back in my chair and took a deep breath.
NarratorHolsord's skyline was daunting but beautiful.
NarratorI glanced downwards out the window and saw Serge and the guards waiting for me by the main gate.
NarratorI heard a couple of knocks on the door. My secretary, Livia Suno, entered.
Livia Suno"Mr. President, Mr. Tusk is here to see you."
Livia Suno"You had a meeting with the Lotherberg spokesman. I'm so sorry for not giving you proper notice."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:04:02 p18144 / c401
  • ▶"Don't worry about it."
  • ·“Do your job properly next time. Do you expect me to keep track of everything myself?"
  • ·"I was just about to leave. Maybe we can postpone the meeting?"
It's better to acknowledge the meeting and maintain good relations with a key player in the economy.
DeepSeek-R1"Don't worry about it."
Livia Suno"Thank you for your understanding."
NarratorAfter a moment, Mr. Tusk swaggered into the room.
NarratorI'd met Sordland's most prominent businessman once or twice, but never alone. He had large jowls, blindingly shiny shoes and the air of someone who was used to getting his way.
Livia Suno"Anything to drink, Mr. Tusk? Water, whiskey?"
Walter Tusk"Cognac. Preferably from before you were born, sweetheart."
Livia Suno"I-- I'll check the supply room."
NarratorLivia backed out of the office, shooting me an apologetic look as she did so.
NarratorWalter hung up his hat, which I could see was lined with mink. He fixed me with a long, hard stare, then broke into a toothy grin.
Walter Tusk"Good evening, Mr. President. I am here to congratulate you."
NarratorWe both sat down.
Walter Tusk"My business partners and I wish you a successful term as the leader of our nation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:05:04 p18198 / c630
  • ·"Thank you, Mr. Tusk."
  • ·Nod.
  • ▶"Get to the point, Mr. Tusk. Why are you really here?"
To directly address Tusk's hidden motives and gain clarity on his intentions.
DeepSeek-R1"Get to the point, Mr. Tusk. Why are you really here?"
Walter Tusk"I see that you're not very welcoming, President."
Walter Tusk"I merely congratulated you. I see no reason to necessitate rudeness."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:05:54 p18100 / c381
  • ·"Forgive me, I didn't want to be rude. Long day."
  • ▶"I don't believe I'm being rude. Time is money after all. Isn't that right?"
A direct response maintains focus on efficiency and priorities.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't believe I'm being rude. Time is money after all. Isn't that right?"
NarratorWalter took a conspicuous look at the diamond-encrusted watch on his wrist.
Walter Tusk"Yes, that's true. But at the same time, patience and humility are important virtues for a man in your position."
Walter Tusk"You are the President after all."
Walter Tusk"May I continue?"

DeepSeek-R1"What are these concerns?"
Walter Tusk"The ruined state of the economy in Sordland has influenced everyone. The recession hit all of us hard."

Walter Tusk"I had these brought from Lespia. The best cigars in the whole continent. Would you like to try one?"

NarratorWalter grabbed himself a cigar and lit it. He looked out towards the city scenery as he puffed away.
Walter Tusk"The assassination and the troubles between the left and right are worrying. But the recession is the root cause of these issues."
Walter Tusk"For us it is worrying to see that you have decided to promote a planned economy."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:09:37 p17970 / c447
  • ▶"The recession was triggered by the market reforms of Alphonso."

DeepSeek-R1"The recession was triggered by the market reforms of Alphonso."
Walter Tusk"Which he couldn't finish due to being heavily obstructed by the establishment."
Walter Tusk"Economics needs long-term strategy and mid-term adjustments to provide growth and stability."
Walter Tusk"My father once said, you have to finish what you started. He is right. The system collapsed because Alphonso didn't finish the reforms he started. And look at what happened to him."
NarratorHe leaned forward in the chair.
Walter Tusk"The high-speed rail investment was a great decision, the business logistics will improve and boost the economy once finished."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:10:44 p17966 / c542

DeepSeek-R1"The intention of the administration is to boost the economy."
Walter Tusk"The right intention for sure."
Walter Tusk"We can be close allies. The Group can help you get Sordland out of the recession. Which will be the key problem to solve in your term and could even determine your re-election."
Walter Tusk"Let's start with a gesture of goodwill. Soon the investment project contract will be awarded to one of the three main contenders."
NarratorHe pulled a check out of his pocket and slid it towards me on the table.
NarratorMy personal bank details were listed on it, showing a deposit of 500,000 Sordish Ren ready to be transferred. This amount was equivalent to six months of the presidential budget.
Walter Tusk"If the contract were somehow awarded to Underhall Construction, they would not only work hard and deliver, but also do it on time."
Walter Tusk"What do you think, Mr. Rayne, wouldn't that be good?"
NarratorHe put his index finger on the check.
NarratorThere was a noise by the door. Livia Suno was standing there, holding a tray with a bottle and two glasses.
Livia Suno"I'm so sorry, Mr. Tusk, but all I could find was a 1942 Sevigny. I hope that's acceptable."
NarratorShe quickly put the tray on my desk and walked out. Tusk eyed her as she left.
Walter Tusk"Remind me to visit your office more often. Now, where were we? Do we have a deal?"

Refusing the bribe upholds ethical standards and avoids corruption charges.

NarratorWalter sighed and pulled the check away, putting it in his pocket.

Walter Tusk"Oh I almost forgot. Please say hello to Mr. Koronti for me."
NarratorHe smiled.
Walter Tusk"Have a good evening, President."
NarratorWalter stood up, grabbed his jacket and made his way towards the door.
NarratorHe closed the door behind him. I gathered my things and prepared to finally head home.

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorOur motorcade headed to the meeting location set by Marcel Koronti. I had only my most confidential and trustworthy team members with me.

NarratorWe arrived and the big iron gates of the country club opened. It was huge and outright extravagant. The main building, inspired by Lespian architecture, was illuminated from all sides.

Petr Vectern"I hope the journey was comfortable."

Marcel Koronti"Welcome. Your kind words have touched me, Mr. Vice President. He was indeed a great man."

■ Railway ContractT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorWe arrived in Anrica by train. After being greeted by crowds of cheering citizens and the local government officials, we headed to the city hall.
NarratorWe were here for a significant meeting about the L-1 project. Several construction companies sent their representatives to the city hall.
NarratorEven though Lileas Graf couldn't make the meeting, I had Symon Holl and Gus Manger by my side.
Symon Holl"I came by train yesterday and it was a little uncomfortable. Besides starting our economic recovery, this project will make the journey here much more pleasant, Mr. President."
Gus Manger"That is a given. But we'll also see improvements in everything from logistics to growth. You made the right choice for sure."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:31:18 p17438 / c448
  • ▶“I trust your economic plans, Symon.”
  • ·“I hope so, Gus.”

DeepSeek-R1"I trust your economic plans, Symon."
Symon Holl"I am glad to hear that. We should be able to slow down the recession if we finish this project successfully."
Gus Manger"I am sure we will. According to your document many citizens will be employed because of business expansions."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:32:20 p17546 / c361

  • ·“What kind of end results do we expect?”

Emphasizing the importance of success in the project highlights its critical role in economic recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"Failure during this project is not an option."
Gus Manger"You are right. We need to work together and think things through."
Symon Holl"Our economic revitalization plan is set to be a success. Failure is indeed not an option."
NarratorSymon searched through the documents he was holding and found the one he was looking for.
Symon Holl"I have the overview of the companies here."
NarratorSymon handed the files over to me.
Look at the files.
DeepSeek-R1I looked at the files. Three corporations were listed. The Sordish State Corporation, Underhall Construction and Taurus Holding.
Gus Manger"I reviewed the listed corporations. I also wrote down some notes and ideas."
Symon Holl"Which corporation should we talk about?"

DeepSeek-R1"Underhall Construction."
Symon Holl"The real estate and construction giant was founded in 1925 and expanded during the economic boom of the '30s."
Symon Holl"Picking this corporation would go against our planned economy policy."
Symon Holl"The CEO of Underhall is Kris Schar, the mayor of Conriat. He sold half of his shares a few years ago but seemed very eager about the L-1 bid."
Gus Manger"I had the chance to work with Underhall Construction in Holsord. They took several contracts for the central station renovation and excelled at it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:34:22 p17499 / c459

  • ·“How was your experience with them?”
Asking Symon for his thoughts will help gather strategic insights to make an informed decision.

Symon Holl"I think that they have been doing relatively well in the private sector, where they are dominating the construction industry with successful projects."
Symon Holl"Nonetheless, they subjected their workers to conditions below our working standards during the Holsord Central Station contract. Which is troubling."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:35:17 p17579 / c314

Gus Manger"Let me just point out that some of the workers were Agnolian and Wehzek. Technically, Sordish labor laws didn't apply to them."
Gus Manger"Having worked on the renovation project, I can say that the conditions were not bad. Extra effort was needed to finish it on time, which they did. That is all."
Symon Holl"Underhall has specifically mentioned their solid track record and highlighted that they do anticipate to go over budget, but that they will finish on time."

DeepSeek-R1"The Sordish State Corporation."
Symon Holl"The respected Sordish State Corporation, also known as the SSC, was founded in 1891."
Symon Holl"It is the main state corporation tasked with running the day-to-day construction services of our country."
Gus Manger"The SSC is the primary reason why our current infrastructure is outdated. We need more adaptive construction companies."
Symon Holl"However, this would fulfil your initial election promise of using a state-dominant planned economy to improve the bleak recession situation."
Symon Holl"They have experience, knowledge and manpower for a project this size. I'm not so sure about their efficiency, though."

DeepSeek-R1"I am on the fence about the SSC."
“Taurus Holding.”
DeepSeek-R1"Taurus Holding."
Symon Holl"Taurus Holding was founded in 1946 and is owned by the entrepreneur Geralt Fais."
Symon Holl"It is a new holding company whose subsidiaries use cutting-edge technology from Arcasia to reduce construction costs and prices. They are known to provide for their workers, who are part of the Labour Union of Sordland."
Gus Manger"They are a very interesting corporation, especially because of their innovative construction methods. But I don't think they're suitable for large projects like these."

Symon Holl"It won several awards internationally for being one of the fastest growing and advanced companies."

Gus Manger"Exactly. And why take such a risk now? We need to play it safer with companies like Underhall. They've always delivered for the government on time."
