■ Decision Economic ReliefT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
EventEconomic Relief Plan: With the assistance of the National Business Council and the Central Bank, Symon Holl put forward a country-wide relief plan to mitigate the economic hardships that are being felt. We can either provide stimulus cheques for people in need or bailout businesses under serious financial strain or decide not to approve the plan. [-2 Government Budget]
EVENT DeepSeek-R105:45:23 p17392 / c520
  • ▶Provide stimulus cheques for people in need
  • ·Bailout businesses under financial strain
  • ·Don't enact plan
Providing stimulus cheques directly addresses immediate citizen needs, improving public sentiment and supporting economic stabilization.

■ Decision FuneralT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
EventFuneral of Bernard Circas: The death of Bernard Circas has shocked the country and his funeral will be held in Deyr among a large crowd of sympathizers. Circas was not only a communist poet but also an elected MP that had a voice. A state funeral has been organized to honor him. The question is whether the President and the administration will attend the funeral or not.
EVENT DeepSeek-R105:46:42 p17379 / c803
  • ▶Attend the funeral
  • ·Do not attend
Attending the funeral would demonstrate respect for Circas and show willingness to engage with diverse political views, which aligns with the goal of domestic stability.

■ Personal FuneralT02

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorDark clouds were looming over Deyr. They looked daunting as our convoy started approaching the century-old cemetery.
NarratorIt looked like it was going to rain. Certainly fitting weather for a funeral.
NarratorAs expected, Bernard Circas's passing maintained its media traction and all of Sordland was paying attention to the ceremony. Especially to what I would say in my speech today.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, we have arrived."
NarratorRain drops were running down the car window when Serge opened my door and held an umbrella over my head. As soon as I got out, flashes from the cameras blinded me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:47:44 p17646 / c410
  • ·“It's okay Serge. I will hold my own umbrella.”
  • ▶“Thank you.”
Attending the funeral shows respect and can help maintain public trust despite political differences.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you."
Serge Wolkner"It's my duty, sir."
NarratorHe silently started following me while holding the umbrella.
NarratorPetr and Karl exited from their own cars in the convoy. They made their way over to me.
Petr Vectern"Anton."
Karl Greiser"Mr. President."
Karl Greiser"I just wanted to inform you that the perimeter is clear and secured completely. There is no one getting in or out without our knowledge."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:48:35 p17485 / c330
  • ·“We don't want something similar happening again, do we?”
  • ·“You better be right for your sake.”
  • ▶“Good work. Thank you Karl.”
Acknowledging Karl's effective security measures reinforces trust and stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Good work. Thank you Karl."
NarratorKarl bowed his head with respect.
Petr Vectern"Good. If anything happens to Anton, you will have to answer to the First Lady."
Petr Vectern"And trust me Karl. You don't want that."
NarratorPetr let out a laugh, which drew some attention from bereaved attendees.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:49:22 p17429 / c396
  • ·“Petr, you are being inconsiderate.”
  • ▶“I don't think any of us want that.”
  • ·Gesture Petr to lower his voice.
  • ·“You are the only one who can make me laugh at a funeral.”
Anton needs to maintain decorum at a funeral, so acknowledging the humor without agreeing keeps things respectful.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't think any of us want that."
Petr Vectern"Indeed."
NarratorIt started to rain. Several unintelligible voices were heard from Karl's radio. He stepped out to reply and returned shortly.
Karl Greiser"We have a small problem, sir."
Karl Greiser"A few -- five to be exact -- Red Youth members are here to attend the funeral. They apparently said they wish to pay their respects to their fallen comrade."
Karl Greiser"Should we let them in?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:50:06 p17455 / c233
  • ·“Do you think they pose a security threat?”
  • ·“Let them in.”
  • ▶“Let them in but put a few guards on them.”
  • ·“Absolutely not.”
Allowing Red Youth members in with guards maintains security while showing restraint.
DeepSeek-R1"Let them in but put a few guards on them to be safe."
Karl Greiser"As you command."
Petr Vectern"Let's head over. They are about to start."
NarratorWe walked towards the crowd circled around the grave. Everyone in attendance was wearing black in mourning. The rain started pouring heavily.
NarratorBernard's widow and daughter were in the front row, staring at the coffin with a mixture of disbelief, confusion and above all, a cold, unforgiving sadness.
NarratorBoth of their eyes were very red and swollen from weeping.
NarratorBefore taking my place to observe the ceremony, I gave my condolences to Bernard's wife and his daughter who looked at me. She reminded me of Deana.
NarratorWhen the religious ceremony was done, I was invited by the priest to deliver my speech. I took the podium. I felt like I had a lump in my throat. I couldn't get the daughter's eyes out of my head.
“Today, we are mourning Bernard Circas, a father, a husband and a loyal servant of this country.”
DeepSeek-R1"Today, we are mourning Bernard Circas, a father, a husband and a loyal servant of this country."
NarratorTowards the back of the crowd, I spotted the members of the Red Youth, wearing red shirts and black bands of mourning on their arms. They seemed ruly and were respectfully bowing their heads.
NarratorThere was complete silence except for the sound of raindrops.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:50:55 p17736 / c384
  • ·“This is a great loss for all of us.”
  • ▶“I will confess that I disagreed with Bernard Circas almost entirely. But it doesn't mean that he deserved this. Our country can be a better place.”
  • ·“Regardless of his ideology, this was a heinous act made towards a good man. We must end this cycle of violence.”
  • ·“He was a true comrade and he fought for what he believed in, his ideal vision of Sordland, in every step of his life.”
It acknowledges the tragedy while maintaining political neutrality.
DeepSeek-R1"I will confess that I disagreed with Bernard Circas almost entirely. But it doesn't mean that he deserved this. Our country can be a better place."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:51:43 p17738 / c421
  • ·“We will make sure that unfortunate events such as this will never happen again.”
  • ▶“Circas had a dream of a unified Sordland. In these next months we need to fulfill his dream and band together.”
  • ·“I interpret this attack as an attack against Sordland and by extension, against me personally.”
  • ·“I promise you this. The blood of Bernard will not remain on the sacred ground of Sordland.”
Choosing option 1 emphasizes national unity and addresses the underlying tensions constructively.
DeepSeek-R1"Circas had a dream of a unified Sordland. In these next months we need to fulfill his dream and band together."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:52:32 p17791 / c424
  • ·“Let us all mourn the passing of Bernard Circas and remember him well.”
  • ▶“But, hatred begets hatred. There is only one way to overcome this vicious cycle. We must unite against the evil of this world.”
  • ·“The tears of the family he left behind have a price that needs to be paid. I will not stop until it is paid in full.”
Promoting unity and addressing the underlying hatred can help prevent further escalation of internal conflicts, aligning with Rayne's focus on stability.
DeepSeek-R1"But, hatred begets hatred. There is only one way to overcome this vicious cycle. We must unite against the evil of this world."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:53:27 p17739 / c520
  • ·“Thank you.”
  • ▶“I would now like to recite one of the poems he left us.”
Reciting a poem would show respect for Bernard Circas' legacy and promote unity during tense times.
DeepSeek-R1"I would now like to recite a part from one of the poems he left us."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:54:17 p17807 / c434
  • ·“Our only strength is the will to survive, knowing that we will perish, knowing nothing more absolute.”
  • ·“We rise, toward the sun! Those who died, died fighting. They are now buried in the sun. No need to mourn, there is a raid to the sun."
  • ▶"They did not let us sing our songs. They are afraid, afraid of the dawn. They are afraid of hope, but our songs will be sung."
This option promotes hope and unity, aligning with the goal of domestic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"They did not let us sing our songs. They are afraid, afraid of the dawn. They are afraid of hope, but our songs will be sung."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:55:00 p17771 / c292
  • ·“Thank you.”
  • ▶“A moment of silence please.”
A moment of silence will honor the deceased and foster national unity during tense times.
DeepSeek-R1"A moment of silence please."
NarratorDuring the silence, all that was heard were the ravens.
NarratorSome attendees started clapping, some crying.
NarratorThe coffin was lowered into the grave by the gravekeepers. After a couple of minutes, Bernard Circas was with the soil, no more.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:55:47 p17716 / c360
  • ·“As we bid farewell to Bernard, I want to close with the words every Sord lives by: A Morgna wes core!”
  • ▶Step down from the podium.
Calling for unity against hatred helps maintain internal stability and prevents further political violence, aligning with Rayne's goals of domestic stability and national security.
DeepSeek-R1I stepped down from the podium. It was time to leave.
NarratorThe widow thanked me with a soft and defeated voice before we dispersed.
NarratorOn my way back to the car, I still couldn't get the eyes of Bernard's daughter, nor the faltering voice of his wife, out of my head. Would Deana, Monica and Franc look the same way if something happened to me?
NarratorPetr put his hand on my shoulder.
Petr Vectern"That was a good speech."
NarratorI sensed only respect in his voice.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:56:39 p17895 / c522
  • ·“It's time to go.”
  • ▶“Thank you. I mean it.”
  • ·Nod.
Expressing gratitude acknowledges support and reinforces leadership, aligning with goals of unity and stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you. I mean it."
Petr Vectern"I know you do."
Petr Vectern"Come on, it's time to go."
Petr Vectern"We've got work to do."
NarratorHe was right. We had work to do. So much more to do.
NarratorI realized that the rain had stopped, and I looked up.
NarratorThe black clouds slowly dispersed and sunbeams enlightened our surroundings.
NarratorAs someone once said: 'Even the darkest night will end and the sun will rise.'
NarratorHope was still on the horizon.

■ Diplomacy OverviewT02

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorThe Blue Mansion in Lachaven hosted us for the important diplomacy gathering.
NarratorThe door opened, and Deivid Wisci -- once a lecturer to me, now the Minister of Foreign Affairs -- gently made his way towards my desk.
Deivid Wisci"May I sit down, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:00:40 p17443 / c261
  • ▶“Leave the formalities aside, Deivid. Please take a seat.”
  • ·“Of course. It's a pleasure to see you, Mr. Wisci.”
  • ·Gesture at the chair.
A polite and direct invitation for Deivid to sit down, maintaining professionalism while keeping the interaction simple.
DeepSeek-R1"Leave the formalities aside, Deivid. Please take a seat."
Deivid Wisci"Ah right. Anton, I am used to titles, diplomacy jobs train oneself."
NarratorHe slowly sat down, taking care not to aggravate the arthritis in his knees. He'd always had an air of gravitas about him, and old age had only served to deepen that.
NarratorAfter he settled himself, he regarded me with delight, his blue eyes sparkling.
Deivid Wisci"Look at you, Anton. The 4th President of Sordland. What a privilege for me."
Deivid Wisci"I still remember the first day you took my class at the Lachaven School of Economics."
Deivid Wisci"Somehow the inexplicable ebb and flow of life has led us here. To positions where we can change the course of history."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:01:20 p17561 / c294
  • ·“Everything happens for a reason, Deivid. It was meant to be.”
  • ·“We got a little lucky. That's all.”
  • ▶“This is our opportunity to shape the future and we will do precisely that.”
Emphasizing leadership and vision aligns with his manifesto goals.
DeepSeek-R1"This is our opportunity to shape the future and we will do precisely that."
Deivid Wisci"I am looking forward to our new foreign policy."
Deivid Wisci"I am excited to present you the latest bill I am working on. The TCPA has turned out to be an initiative that may significantly boost our tourism and culture industry."
Deivid Wisci"This untapped value would help us promote our position internationally. I am convinced it can also help improve our economic condition after discussions with Ministers Holl and Manger."
Deivid Wisci"The GNA factions are supportive too. I really shouldn't have worked overtime hours to get it done, but I really care for the position and value of our cult-"
NarratorHe started coughing again. This time for longer.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:02:10 p17729 / c363
  • ·“Would you like some tea or water?”
  • ▶“How is your health, Deivid?”
  • ·“Let's start talking about our foreign policy.”
Addressing Deivid Wisci's health shows concern and builds rapport before discussing business matters.
DeepSeek-R1"How is your health, Deivid?"
Deivid Wisci"Oh, I haven't had any big complications except the usual... After that heart attack earlier this year."
Deivid Wisci"The health complications aren't a hindrance to my work, of course. But thanks for asking."
Deivid Wisci"How is the Rayne family?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:02:59 p17630 / c459
  • ·“We are slowly starting to get used to the routine.”
  • ▶“Monica is really taking to the First Lady role.”
  • ·“Franc never liked the spotlight, so that hasn't changed.”
Monica has been portrayed as handling her role well, making this a positive and accurate statement.
DeepSeek-R1"Monica is really taking to the First Lady role."
Deivid Wisci"As time passes, I realize it more. Family is the most important thing in life. We must take care of them."
NarratorThe door opened. Iosef and Lucian joined us for the briefing as the tall case clock hit the hour mark.
NarratorPetr, however, was missing. I wondered if he was late from all the drinking he did at the party in Lachaven.
Deivid Wisci"We'll talk more later, President Rayne. Welcome everyone."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:03:44 p17691 / c380
  • ▶“How was your travel to Lachaven?”
  • ·“Let's begin.”
Asking about his travel shows interest and sets a polite tone for discussing the bill.
DeepSeek-R1"How was your travel to Lachaven?"
Iosef Lancea"A little bumpy due to some turbulence. I arrived via a military cargo plane that landed at Lachaven International."
Lucian Galade"I took the train. It was a little slow, but I can still recommend it for the beautiful landscape. I also had the possibility to work on a couple of things during the ride."
Deivid Wisci"From what I've heard, it will be much faster in the near future when the L-1 high-speed railway is finished."
Lucian Galade"Exactly. If all goes well, it will be one of our major achievements."
Lucian Galade"Well then. Since Mr. Vectern doesn't seem to be coming today... I think we are ready to start."
Deivid Wisci"I will start by providing a brief overview of our current status with our neighbours. What would you like to start with?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:04:27 p17777 / c303
  • ·“I'd like to hear about Agnolia.”
  • ·“I want to know more about Valgsland.”
  • ▶“What's the latest situation in Wehlen?”
Focusing on Wehlen aligns with the goal of addressing external threats and maintaining national security.
DeepSeek-R1"What's the latest situation in Wehlen?"
Deivid Wisci"It is tumultuous in the south, as always. Wiktor Smolak is expanding his powers among protests from Wehzek people. However, he is quite positive about a normalisation of relationship between our countries."
Deivid Wisci"Despite purging the Wehzek communists, reports indicate that they have been warming up to Contanan international aid. As you know, Wehlen’s stability was always a concern for the region."
Deivid Wisci"But if we want to help them, we must not treat refugees like we did in the '30s. I still can’t stomach the inhumanity those people suffered."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:05:23 p18094 / c577
  • ·“A trade agreement with them would improve stability and increase mutual trade.”
  • ·“Why help such a disorganized state? Not sure about what we can do to help them.”
  • ▶“Sordland paid enough by housing millions of refugees during the Wehlen Civil War. No more.”
Focusing on domestic priorities and budget constraints to avoid spreading resources thin.
DeepSeek-R1"Sordland paid enough by housing millions of refugees during the Wehlen Civil War. No more."
Deivid Wisci"And when we had our civil war, they were there for us. That is how refugee rights work internationally."
Deivid Wisci"Our main goal should be to avoid a refugee crisis like the late 1920’s. That civil war had major security and economic repercussions on Sordish soil."
Iosef Lancea"One of the demands of President Smolak was to tighten our immigration laws, so the political refugees don't escape to Sordland. There has been a recent purge that pushed many Bludish to flee to Bergia. It is definitely troubling."
Iosef Lancea"Gumren Outpost has regained its importance due to this. I'm sure you would remember that winter at the border outpost, Mr. President..."
NarratorHe was right, I did remember that winter. I remembered how cold it was when I let the refugees slip through. I was discharged of my duties as a result, by Major Iosef Lancea, no less, spending the rest of my service cleaning toilets and sleeping in a cell.
Iosef Lancea"In fact, there had been a similar refugee situation in the past. Even Mr. President himself chose to disobey direct orders from his commanding officer at the time."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:06:16 p18331 / c470
  • ·“I was young and I made a mistake.”
  • ▶“Past is past. Let's move on.”
  • ·“And you made me sleep in a prison cell for it. I will never forget that.”
  • ·“I did what was right.”
The response focuses on moving forward, aligning with Anton's pragmatic approach and maintaining professionalism.
DeepSeek-R1"Past is past. Let's move on."
NarratorHe opened his mouth to say something but Deivid cut in.
Deivid Wisci"Our focus should be on the current matters. The Bludish minority is very large inside Wehlen too, which is a factor to consider."
Deivid Wisci"It is in our national interest to prevent the formation of a failed state on our western border."
Lucian Galade"Wehlen has been increasingly aligning itself towards the communist alliance, another point to analyse."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:07:06 p18341 / c454
  • ·“I'd like to hear about Agnolia.”
  • ·“I want to know more about Valgsland.”
  • ▶“Let's move on.”
Focusing on immediate regional threats and internal stability is more critical than discussing distant regions.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's move on."
Deivid Wisci"Two important neighbouring countries remain. Lespia and Rumburg. Which would you like to hear about first?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:07:53 p18348 / c391
  • ·“What about Lespia?”
  • ▶“What is the situation with Rumburg?”
Rumburg represents an immediate external threat that requires attention to maintain national security and domestic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"What is the situation with Rumburg?"
Deivid Wisci"Now to the most troublesome nation; Rumburg. A constitutional monarchy led by Queen Beatrice Livingston."
Iosef Lancea"I highly advise caution and calculated action against Rumburg. Our military must be prepared, the threat is real."
Deivid Wisci"They are strong and have the motivation, as well as the means, to damage us. We shouldn't give them any reasons to pursue aggression."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:08:36 p18307 / c297
  • ·“I will take your advice in consideration.”
  • ·“Rumburg is and always will be a rogue like state. Nothing can be done about them.”
  • ▶“We must prevent them from interfering in our affairs.”
Preventing Rumburg from interfering aligns with military readiness and strategic priorities.
DeepSeek-R1"We must prevent them from interfering in our affairs."
Deivid Wisci"They have shown similar behaviour to their neighbouring countries before but we must be cautious."
Iosef Lancea"While Thornbourgh openly denies such claims, their past and current actions make it clear that they want to destabilize us."
Iosef Lancea"The weapons caches found by our intelligence agency clearly shows what their goals are. Our military must be steadfast and our diplomacy valiant."
NarratorIosef looked stern and full of resolve.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:09:23 p18226 / c352
  • ▶“The Rumburg threat is one of our top priorities. You can be assured of that.”
  • ·“I think the threat is far too overblown.”
Acknowledging the Rumburg threat aligns with Anton's manifesto focus on military readiness and national security.
DeepSeek-R1"The Rumburg threat is one of our top priorities. You can be assured of that."
Deivid Wisci"We are always in dialogue with our Rumburg ambassador to make sure a channel is open to solve disputes."
Lucian Galade"We could use their aggressive attitude against them by convincing the international community."
NarratorLucian grabbed the Lachaven Times newspaper and put his finger on the article about the latest diplomatic incident.
Lucian Galade"Their abrupt announcement about the closure of the Rumburg consulate in Lachaven is yet another step in their escalation."
“What about Lespia?”
DeepSeek-R1"What about Lespia?"
Deivid Wisci"Our southern neighbour is aligning itself more and more with Arcasia. Lespia is currently led by Prime Minister Patricio Alvarez."
Lucian Galade"They are the jewel of the continent and the second largest economy in Eastern Merkopa. Though wealthy, Lespia suffers from excessive economic inequality."
Deivid Wisci"Our past relations have been great but during the last decade, their investments to Sordland dwindled due to our instability."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:10:24 p18075 / c677
  • ▶“Allying with the richest regional nation could be beneficial.”
  • ·“Lespia is a sleeping giant. We should not awake them.”
  • ·“They keep turning a blind eye towards the region. Thinking that Lespia is above it all.”
Allying with Lespia could provide strategic and economic benefits against Rumburg's expansionism.
DeepSeek-R1"Allying with the richest regional nation could be beneficial."
Deivid Wisci"It could be but it would also mean that we would draw closer to the Arcasia block and near the Arcasian Treaty Organisation."
Deivid Wisci"I am certain that the recession has a lot to do with their retraction of investments. Once they see that money isn't in the picture they vanish rather quickly because they are capitalists."
Iosef Lancea"They have shown a selfish attitude during the Wehlen Civil War by closing their borders and funnelling refugees to cross into Sordland."
NarratorLucian cleared his throat.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:11:09 p18149 / c336
  • ·“Our values are too different with Lespia. It will be hard to see eye to eye.”
  • ▶“The Capitalism of Lespia is inspirational to me. Look at their economic power.”
Emphasizing Lespia's capitalism aligns with Rayne's economic focus and could foster beneficial alliances.
DeepSeek-R1"The Capitalism of Lespia is inspirational to me. Look at their economic power."
Deivid Wisci"Everything comes at a cost. In Lespia the cost is wealth inequality but business people are getting richer and richer."
Lucian Galade"There are hundreds of very privileged oligarchs who invest inside and outside of Lespia. Some made significant business investments in the cities of Benfi and Valgen."
Deivid Wisci"This covers the overview of the current situation. We will move on to our trade choice and the response to Rumburg's diplomatic escalation."
NarratorThe sun began to set over the hills surrounding Lachaven. The team was ready to give advice on the new trade initiative and the response to the latest diplomatic incident with Rumburg.
NarratorPetr arrived late. He looked a little rougher than usual. It must have been the drinking from last night.
Petr Vectern"Hey there, folks. Excuse my delay. I'm sure I didn't miss anything we didn't already know."
Iosef Lancea"You were still supposed to be a part of this meeting. An hour ago."
Lucian Galade"Punctuality is important. We are talking about serious subjects here."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:11:46 p18201 / c140
  • ·“Petr was assigned a task from me, that is why he is late so let's move on.”
  • ▶“They have a point, Petr. We should have discipline in the cabinet.”
  • ·Don't comment.
Emphasizing discipline in the cabinet is crucial for maintaining professionalism during critical discussions.
DeepSeek-R1"They have a point, Petr. We should have discipline in the cabinet."
Petr Vectern"Excuse me. It won't happen again."
NarratorLucian and Iosef looked at Petr before Deivid moved away from the subject.
Deivid Wisci"The Vice President will be part of the trade delegation, so it will be great to have his input on that subject as well as on the Rumburg incident."
Deivid Wisci"Rumburg Consul Sir Bran Harrington announced the closure of the 120-year-old consulate. Now their diplomatic staff is leaving this city to go back to Thornbourgh."
Lucian Galade"He blamed the lack of security in Sordland due to the assassination and the subsequent protests. But it is obvious that they are making excuses."
Iosef Lancea"Bastards! First they close their consulate and then they blame our security!?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:12:46 p18165 / c610
  • ·“The security situation is under control, they are playing with us.”
  • ▶“Their concerns could be genuine but this reaction is unnecessary.”
Their concerns could be genuine, but this reaction may not help resolve the situation.
DeepSeek-R1"Their concerns could be genuine, but this reaction is unnecessary."
Petr Vectern"Agreed. Other diplomatic missions have worked with our security units to increase the protection of staff and buildings."
Deivid Wisci"The neglect of President Alphonso, and the chaos of the elections, delayed our diplomatic efforts."
Deivid Wisci"Now there are only two Rumburg diplomatic missions left in Sordland. One is the consulate in Deyr and the other is the embassy in Holsord."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:13:41 p18322 / c482
  • ▶“We are accustomed to the usual rhetoric but this is far more serious.”
  • ·“I must display strength to my people. We can't be pushed around.”
  • ·“They are bluffing. We are used to it by now.”
Recognizing the gravity of Rumburg's actions while maintaining a strategic and measured response is crucial for national security and stability.
DeepSeek-R1"We are accustomed to the usual rhetoric but this is far more serious."
Petr Vectern"They are playing with fire. Look at what the Wehlen destabilization has done to the region. Sordland breaking apart would tear Merkopa apart."
Deivid Wisci"In my entire career I have never seen our Rumburg relations suffer this much. The previous low point was the War of Broken Wills."
NarratorIosef Lancea was furious. He barely kept himself from slamming the desk.
Iosef Lancea"There are worrying developments like the smuggled arms and the build-up near the border. Our military must be buffed up to stand against a great power like Rumburg."
Deivid Wisci"We either respond to this aggressive act with a diplomatic condemnation or we retaliate by closing our consulate in Dome."
Lucian Galade"I want to say that an escalation would be risky this early in our Presidency. We should focus on creating a negative image of Rumburg in the international scene."
Iosef Lancea"The people of Sordland must be protected, no matter the cost. I say we respond equally."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:14:37 p18562 / c503
  • ·“Retaliate by closing the Sordland Consulate in Dome! They think they can bully us. They were never so wrong.”
  • ▶“Write an official diplomatic response condemning this unreasonable act. Declare that Rumburg aims to destabilize Sordland and the region.”
A diplomatic condemnation is the prudent choice to avoid escalation while addressing Rumburg's provocative action.
DeepSeek-R1"Write an official diplomatic response condemning this unreasonable act. Declare that Rumburg aims to destabilize Sordland and the region."
Deivid Wisci"As you wish Mr. President. I shall begin work by immediately informing the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade."
Lucian Galade"Swaying the international stage is the key in long-term strategy."
Iosef Lancea"I wish we had reacted more strongly. If we don't confront them, they will keep escalating the situation."
Petr Vectern"I, on the other hand, am glad that we are taking the more cautious road."
Deivid Wisci"There are three trade-related topics to address today. Which topic should we tackle first?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:15:23 p18506 / c363
  • ▶“We should talk about the ongoing trade negotiations with our neighbors Wehlen and Agnolia.”
  • ·“I'm interested to hear the intentions of the Kingdom of Rizia.”
Focusing on trade negotiations can boost economic stability, supporting re-election goals.
DeepSeek-R1"We should talk about the ongoing trade negotiations with our neighbors Wehlen and Agnolia."
Deivid Wisci"Our primary goal will be to successfully sign at least one trade agreement with one of our smaller neighbors."
Deivid Wisci"We have two options with our trade initiative. Starting new negotiations with our old partner Agnolia to the north-east, and forming a new cooperation with Wehlen to the west."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:16:11 p18472 / c425
  • ▶“Tell me about the trade deal with Agnolia.”
  • ·“Tell me about the trade deal with Wehlen.”
Starting with Agnolia could provide immediate economic benefits due to its stability and historical trading relationship.
DeepSeek-R1"Tell me about the trade deal with Agnolia."
Deivid Wisci"First off, the reason behind this option is more diplomatic than economic, although of course we can't forgo the added economic value. Agnolia is an old ally and a valuable trade partner."
Deivid Wisci"The point we are at with the early negotiations is around the steel trade. They are demanding that we buy their steel for a higher price. "
Deivid Wisci"That is because they think that the deal is far too unbalanced as it is. "
Deivid Wisci"Their second main demand is a privileged access to our agricultural market."
Deivid Wisci"If we are to fulfil their demands, in return they are offering a full alliance with Sordland against Rumburg."
Iosef Lancea"Looking at the current advances of Rumburg near our borders, having a coalition against Rumburg would prove extremely useful."
Deivid Wisci"Obviously there will be consequences for entering a full alliance. Mainly the issue is around Heljiland. Valgsland has clear intentions on taking over the island one way or another and entering a military alliance with Agnolia will make them furious."
Iosef Lancea"I want to state my support for the decision to stick to our old ally, Agnolia. Even though I disagree with their demand to relax immigration, we have cooperated with them on many issues in the past."
Petr Vectern"There are obviously subjects that we would still need to tackle like our economies, the traded goods and what to do with immigration."
Deivid Wisci"My opinion is that this is a good deal and we should go for it. The new Agnolian Prime Minister is trying to improve the existing relationship."
Deivid Wisci"The downside is that we would need to make concessions. A reality of any trade deal."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:16:59 p18844 / c371
  • ▶“I would be open to creating a new agreement that would satisfy all sides.”
  • ·“I don't want to give any concessions to them.”
  • ·“We can't position ourselves against Valgsland.”
Creating a balanced trade agreement with Agnolia could strengthen diplomatic ties and provide economic benefits while addressing Valgsland's concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"I would be open to creating a new agreement that would satisfy all sides."
Deivid Wisci"That would be the best outcome."
Deivid Wisci"If you decide that Agnolia is a good option, another point is that they have a very democratic system. After the recent elections the PM has a mandate to arrange a fairer deal with us. Maartin Van Hoorten also is an avid supporter of relaxed immigration."
Deivid Wisci"Since we said we will keep immigration relaxed, it will help us."
Lucian Galade"Regardless, if we are going to increase our regional influence to previous levels, we might need to make amends with our neighbours."
Lucian Galade"Ultimately the decision will be up to you as always."
“Tell me about the trade deal with Wehlen.”
DeepSeek-R1"Tell me about the trade deal with Wehlen."
Deivid Wisci"Dealing with a fractured country like Wehlen has its positives and negatives. Although it will be lucrative in the short term, there are risks associated with dealing with such a country."
Deivid Wisci"They are proposing co-investment projects and a no-tariff agreement. They are also offering oil in return for attracting foreign capital interests from Sordland."
Deivid Wisci"However, it is possible that there will be international ramifications of getting closer with a country like Wehlen."
Deivid Wisci"All in all, this is a very lucrative deal for us. However, we need to think about the consequences."
Iosef Lancea"I certainly don't support the decision to become friendlier with Wehlen. We carried their burdens for decades. Their migrants flooded our country and now they are expecting a deal from us."
Petr Vectern"Maintaining influence with Wehlen could prove useful. Yes, it is risky, but think about our influence on a underdeveloped region like that. We could also sell more products and enter their market easily."
Deivid Wisci"I am of the opinion that it is simply too risky. Yes, we would gain resources and immediate capital, but I'm afraid of the consequences."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:17:54 p19034 / c515
  • ·“I think the return is too lucrative to pass on regardless of the risk.”
  • ▶“You may be right. It's risky.”
While the trade deal offers significant economic benefits, the potential risks and instability in Wehlen make it a cautious choice.
DeepSeek-R1"You may be right. It's risky."
Lucian Galade"I tend to agree."
Deivid Wisci"If we wish to go forward with the deal when the time comes, there is the topic of immigration to consider."
Deivid Wisci"Wiktor Smolak is a known isolationist. Our promise to keep our immigration policy relaxed will not help at all. He blames us for bringing western interests to the region. He also doesn't want his political opponents to flee here."
Lucian Galade"I personally don't like the idea of getting too close to Wehlen."
Deivid Wisci"Me too."
Iosef Lancea"Agreed."
Petr Vectern"I still think we should give it a try."
Deivid Wisci"Hmm."
Deivid Wisci"Our campaign promise to steer Sordland towards the west and Arcasia will have an effect on the negotiations."
Deivid Wisci"Especially with Wehlen. As you know the current affairs of Wehlen are partly a result of United Contana's support of Wiktor Smolak. This will be a problem but definitely worth trying."
Deivid Wisci"As for Agnolia, this promise has made them cautious, which might make it harder to get a good deal."
Deivid Wisci"We also need to consider the effects of our economic doctrine plan."
Deivid Wisci"The fact that we are headed towards a planned economy will have an influence."
Petr Vectern"Good negotiators optimize the interests which makes the differences become trivial, allowing both sides to reach a deal."
Deivid Wisci"Very true, Mr. Vectern. We have a lot of time to prepare before the scheduled visits to Wehlen and Agnolia."
Deivid Wisci"I believe this gives a brief overview of our current options."
Deivid Wisci"Our trade delegation will begin work towards the end of the year. We expect final negotiations and the signings of the trade agreements to happen next year."
Petr Vectern"I'm very much looking forward to visiting Staalport and Wraclawitz with party animals like Deivid and Symon! Haha!"
NarratorPetr laughed. Iosef narrowed his eyes and stared at him.

  • ·“I don't need any further information about Rizia.”

DeepSeek-R1"Let's discuss the deal potential and what's on the table."
Deivid Wisci"From what we have gathered, there is no clear offer yet. The interest clearly seems to be more control over the gas produced and sold in Sordland. I suspect this must be related to the recent tensions on the coast of Pales."
Deivid Wisci"Mr. Manger is working out the details to get more concrete headline terms soon. Of particular interest are the gas fields in the Narbel area of the Nargis region."
Petr Vectern"That must be related to Gasom operations. Gus has ties with the people around that corporation. Even though there is another CEO, Ewald Alphonso is still chairman."
Lucian Galade"Gasom is a private corporation, a fact that limits our control over any foreign investment. Tarquin Soll's Energy Protection Act of 1932 specifically limits the purchases of Sordish energy company shares by foreign nations to ten percent."
Lucian Galade"Ewald Alphonso adjusted this law in 1948 and increased it to up to 20 percent. Anything above that requires us to change the Energy Protection Act. Ewald holds most of Gasom's cards in his hands still."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:19:37 p19320 / c426

  • ·“Good thing, we handled the transfer of power within the party diplomatically. I'm sure he will be more open to talk.”
  • ·“I see a strong case for Tarquin Soll's regulation. Energy security is important for our nation.”

Highlighting Gasom's reliance on selling gas to Sordland underscores its national importance and potential impact on negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"Gasom is private, but it still sells most of the gas it produces to Sordland, right?"
Petr Vectern"I think so, yes. Lucian knows more than me."
Lucian Galade"Up to 70 percent goes to Sordland. This used to be higher, but international demand has seen Gasom diversify its deliveries outward."
Deivid Wisci"Rumburg and Lespia are increasing their demand each year. They have hungry industries waiting to be fulfilled."
Iosef Lancea"Fueling the Rumburg war machine is indisputably wrong."
NarratorLucian pulled out a pen and took a few notes.
NarratorDeivid coughed a bit and then continued talking.
Deivid Wisci"From our diplomatic channels, we can tell that Lespia is still very interested in Sordish petrol, whereas Rumburg wants more gas resources."
Iosef Lancea"As a sovereign nation, we must protect our resources. Our energy independence is part of our national security concern here; Gasom handles most of our gas production."

Deivid Wisci"I agree, but Gasom is a private corporation. Even though it mostly supplies the government, we have little control over it."

Lucian Galade"Precisely, but we don’t know if this will be the case. There is a scenario in which they might invest within the EPA limitations; we just have to hear more before weighing the options."

Deivid Wisci"Thank you. Our support staff and I will be working towards the next steps soon after receiving more context from Mr. Manger."

Lucian Galade"The kingdom is a developed nation that wielded considerable influence in Southern Merkopa before the reign of Valero. The country has lost some territory due to internal strife."
Deivid Wisci"The Alphonso government signed vital trade agreements with King Romus Toras, benefitting both nations at a time of need. Attempts at further cooperation stalled out due to the King's blood ties to Rumburg."

■ Reform BriefingT02

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorI came a little early to the meeting room before anybody showed up. We had scheduled a meeting to discuss the progress of the reform committee regarding the new Constitution.
NarratorI took a seat on the centre chair in the vacant meeting room as I waited for them to show up.
NarratorThe reforms were slowly becoming the most significant expectation of my government. We would be changing Soll's Constitution of '29.
NarratorMost people nowadays blamed the constitution for the outstanding problems of the country.
NarratorOn the other hand, the reformists in the Assembly had been getting louder about their demands. What I knew for sure was that I needed to do something about this encroaching chaos before it swallowed me.
NarratorMeanwhile, Lucian, Petr and Nia had been leading the committee to prepare an initial draft for the constitutional amendments. I was intrigued to hear their results.
NarratorThere was a knock on the door. It was Lucian.
Lucian Galade"Good afternoon, sir."
NarratorLucian arrived exactly on the hour mark again. His punctuality could almost be described as obsessive.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:28:56 p19004 / c480
  • ▶"Good afternoon, Lucian."
  • ·"Are you waiting behind the door to come at the exact right second?"
Maintaining professionalism by acknowledging Lucian's punctuality.
DeepSeek-R1"Good afternoon, Lucian."
NarratorHe took a seat on the table and took out a bunch of documents, spreading them on the long table.
Lucian Galade"We should begin the meeting as soon as Mrs. Morgna and Vice President Vectern arrive. We finally have some results from the reform committee."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:30:19 p19213 / c389
  • ·"How does it look?"
  • ▶"What results?"
  • ·"Good."
Asking for specific results shows engagement and interest in the reform committee's progress.
DeepSeek-R1"What results?"
Lucian Galade"The reform committee already drafted the amendments for the new Constitution thanks to the help of the members."
Lucian Galade"But the situation is still quite complicated politically. We'll go over the details in the meeting."
NarratorA few minutes later, Petr and Nia also arrived.
NarratorAfter a short chat, everybody sat down and took out their notes. I knew it was going to be a long meeting.
Petr Vectern"There is a lot to talk about the future of this process. Let us begin to tackle the points of contention."
Nia Morgna"Mr. President, I've heard from Mr. Galade that you're not intending to support the democratic reforms."
Nia Morgna"Our committee meetings have been really fruitful, and we already started on the constitutional draft together with the feedback from all the political parties."
Nia Morgna"We are making clear progress here. You wouldn't really block our hard work, would you?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:31:26 p19251 / c582

  • ·"What's that supposed to mean?"
  • ·"I would."

Maintaining flexibility allows assessing the impact of reforms on stability and re-election prospects without alienating key constituencies.
DeepSeek-R1"A final decision hasn't been made yet."
NarratorShe leaned slightly towards me.
Nia Morgna"This is our once in a lifetime chance to change the Constitution of Tarquin Soll, Mr. President."
Nia Morgna"You campaigned to bring real democracy to Sordland."
Nia Morgna"With all due respect, you should hold that promise you made to the Sordish people."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:32:26 p19190 / c441

DeepSeek-R1"Thanks for the advice, Mrs. Morgna."
NarratorPetr cut in before Nia could speak.
Petr Vectern"If I may continue, Mr. President. We've discussed the possibilities of your intentions with Mr. Galade."
Lucian Galade"We already know that the Old Guards will block any reforms to the Constitution. So, they would definitely want to work with you to preserve it, if that's still what you want to do."
Nia Morgna"I can't believe you are supporting this too."
Lucian Galade"I do not. I have already explained Mr. President my position on working with the Old Guards."

Lucian Galade"I just do not trust the Chief Justice in delivering any promises. I'm afraid that ignoring the demands for reform might create some future problems for our administration."

■ Bill Tourism CultureT02

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
Bill for decisionTourism and Cultural Preservation Act — The Sordland Tourism and Cultural Preservation Act aims to amplify the cultural allure and tourism viability of Borin, Ribery, Lachaven, Anrica, and Benfi. The Grand National Assembly is calling a collective push to establish Sordland as a global tourist destination and reduce the effects of the recession. Section I focuses on the cultural elevation of these cities, identifying local food, drink, music, dance, architecture, and historical sites as unique selling propositions. This cultivation of cultural tourism is positioned as a catalyst for economic invigoration. Section II imposes the restoration and preservation of key historical landmarks in Lachaven and Anrica. Concurrently, it proposes a comprehensive renovation of tourist infrastructure in Benfi to enrich the visitor experience and generate steady income for local communities. Section II Subsection I further commits to the safeguarding and development of the Caves of Antera in Ribery and the vineyards of Borin, recognizing their potential as tourism growth-drivers. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R106:43:25 p19050 / c342
  • ·Sign: Tourism and Cultural Preservation Act
  • ▶Veto: Tourism and Cultural Preservation Act
Given the current instability and budget constraints, focusing on immediate economic stabilization is crucial. Vetoing this bill helps preserve financial resources for critical areas like defense and social programs.

■ Autocrat BriefingT02

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorThe administration had been working day and night on the overwhelming amount of issues that were facing the country.
NarratorThe various government departments were sending me an avalanche of documents, all of which required my signature so they could start working on the new plans that had been decided on.
NarratorAfter I'd signed dozens of them, I closed my eyes to relax for a moment.
NarratorAnother long day...
NarratorOn top of all that, I'd finally managed to set up a meeting with the Old Guards to talk about a path of no reforms and cooperation. It was in thirty minutes.
NarratorI was either going to cooperate with them, or would have no choice but to work with the reformists on the new Constitution.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R106:45:01 p18953 / c644

  • ·Take a moment to relax.

NarratorIt was Lucian.
Lucian GaladeGood evening, sir.

Lucian Galade"We should be careful when dealing with the Old Guard, sir."
Lucian Galade"We should try not to antagonize them. In case we don't collaborate with them, we'll need their support in the Court's vote."
Lucian Galade"And... I know I told you this before, sir. But I wouldn't trust the Chief Justice."

Heron Garaci"How about the name, Mr. President? It is not the 'Workers Party of Sordland', is it? As far as I am aware, there is no nation called Bludia around here."
Lucian Galade"I see where this is going."
Orso Hawker"Today, the only reason they are not in the Assembly is because of the electoral threshold. They have gotten quite big too. They are clearly getting outside help and their connections to the paramilitary Bludish force cannot be denied."
Orso Hawker"And all of a sudden, the reformists are trying to decrease the electoral threshold, so that the Bludish separatists can be legitimized in the Assembly to achieve their aims of independence."

Orso Hawker"Now we have conflicts on the streets between the nationalists and the communists. And the communists are gathering really fast after the shooting of Circas."
Orso Hawker"This could potentially lead to civil war, Mr. President. We know that United Contana supports the communists in Sordland."

Orso Hawker"We should take the necessary measures against communist and Bludish plots as well as increasing our military budget against a possible conflict with Rumburg."
Heron Garaci"And of course not fall into the traps of the reformists by listening to their demands. They are clearly plotting to weaken the President and our whole administration in order to exploit the situation."
Orso Hawker"...Which brings us to the information that I have about this important figure."

DeepSeek-R1"Please explain, Mr. Hawker."
Orso Hawker"The person in question is Mr. Frens Ricter, himself."
Orso Hawker"We have enough information to infer that he has ties with Arcasia."
Orso Hawker"Arcasia is aggressively growing their influence around the world and now we have Mr. Ricter coming up with these ridiculous demands for a new Constitution amidst a chaotic period in Sordland."
Orso Hawker"All the pieces seem to fit perfectly. That is why the Supreme Court will be doing what it takes to stop these reforms and preserve the Constitution together with you."
Orso Hawker"...At least that's what we hope for."
Lucian Galade"How would you know that Mr. Ricter has ties to Arcasia?"
Heron Garaci"Mr. Galade, we all know he has been in Arcasia many times, because it's been documented. But what would you think if I told you he has been making hidden flights to the country?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:00:07 p19389 / c456
  • ▶"I need to know this. Please explain."
  • ·"He was hiding his flights to Arcasia?"

DeepSeek-R1"I need to know this. Please explain."
Orso Hawker"He was spotted in a hotel in Arcasia last year, where they held the 'Conference on Economic Development in Volatile Regions'. Right around that time, he started to influence the masses into believing that our Constitution is the reason for everything bad."
Orso Hawker"His trip was not documented, nor was he an official guest of the conference. We believe that he had a meeting with President Walker behind closed doors."
Orso Hawker"I also have more photos of him that were taken in Arcasia in different cities. One is from a couple months back. Again, none of it is documented."
Orso Hawker"He also attended another event in Lespia last week. The Arcasian Minister of Foreign Affairs was there too."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:01:03 p19403 / c531

  • ▶"If this is true, this is *big*."

DeepSeek-R1"If this is true, this is *big*."
Orso Hawker"I told you so, Mr. President."
Orso Hawker"Many Sollists were already following his leads for quite some time, Mr. President. I cannot give away their identities, but I can give you the evidence."
NarratorHe handed me a small file.
NarratorIt contained official documents from the Sordish Border Guard with highlighted dates that showed no trace of Frens Ricter's name.
NarratorThere were clear photos of him that were taken in the conference in Arcasia, as well as remarks by Lespian and Arcasian citizens who claim to have seen him with Arcasian businessmen around the same dates.
NarratorThere was also an official transcript of one of his speeches where he claimed to be in Benfi on the dates of the conference in Arcasia, where he was spotted.
NarratorLucian skimmed through the documents with skepticism which slowly turned into surprise.
Lucian Galade"These seem to be real."
Orso Hawker"Of course they are real, Mr. Galade."
Orso Hawker"These seem to suggest that Arcasia is behind the reformists. Their demands will only benefit Arcasia. They want us to have a weaker administration while we face all these threats."
Orso Hawker"This is clearly a plot against Sordland. I bet Rumburg is part of their plan too. It cannot be a coincidence that they are weaponizing our minorities against us around the same time."
Orso Hawker"Now you understand the reasons for our current stance. And also our suspicions about your advisers, who clearly have not given you this vital information."
NarratorLucian gave him a sharp look.
Lucian Galade"We appreciate the information, Mr. Hawker, but I advise against jumping to conclusions right away. We still need to check the information."
Orso Hawker"There is not much to think or check... This is clearly an emergency. In such cases, the Constitution gives the President the right to use his emergency powers. If we work together, you can be sure that the Supreme Court will not block your declaration of emergency."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:01:59 p19580 / c438

  • ·"You want me to declare an emergency?"

Declining to immediately accept their claims without concrete evidence maintains political integrity and avoids potential backlash.

Orso Hawker"It seems you are more influenced by Mr. Galade than you think."
Lucian Galade"What is that supposed to mean, Mr. Hawker?"
Orso Hawker"Why don't you explain your agenda yourself, Lucian? You are swaying the President to ignore the national security threats."

Heron Garaci"And if you believe you can enact these reforms, you are living in a land of dreams."
