TURN 03

■ Personal Franc TalkT03

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorIt was a Saturday afternoon, my first break from work in what felt like years, and the weather couldn't have been more perfect. Trying to keep my mind off the upheaval sparked by the Circas assassination, I lit the backyard grill.
NarratorMonica and Deana were out shopping for craft supplies, and I'd given the staff the day off. It was just myself and Franc at home.
NarratorI wasn't sure how he felt about me at the moment. But I did know how he felt about barbecued ribs. Our cook had left some marinating in the refrigerator.
NarratorSure enough, as soon as Franc glimpsed the grill and the meat, he stepped outside to join me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:07:07 p19479 / c432
  • ·"Well, look who's finally out of his room."
  • ▶"Come to spend some quality time with your old man, have you?"
Choosing a warm and engaging response helps maintain family unity and reflects a caring father figure, which supports domestic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Come to spend some quality time with your old man, have you?"
Franc"Sure, if you can spare a few minutes out of your busy schedule."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:07:50 p19482 / c266
  • ·“Children who talk back to their fathers don't get dinner. Fan these flames, will you?”
  • ▶"Look, son, I'm preoccupied with matters of national importance."
  • ·"I'm sorry, I know I haven't been around very often lately. But I'm happy to see you now."
Anton wants to maintain his authority while acknowledging his son's presence and the importance of their time together.
DeepSeek-R1"Look, son, I'm preoccupied with matters of national importance."
Franc"Yes, Mr. President. Whatever you say, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:08:39 p19391 / c457
  • ·“Children who talk back to their fathers don't get dinner. Fan these flames, will you?”
  • ▶"But right now, nothing's more important than these ribs."
Maintaining a light-hearted moment with family despite national responsibilities.
DeepSeek-R1"But right now, nothing's more important than these ribs."
NarratorWe both chuckled. I turned to tend to the fire.
Franc"Here, let me try."
NarratorHe grabbed the fan and started quickly waving it over the coals. The fire roared to life.
NarratorHe turned to look at me with a grin on his face, as if he was expecting praise.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:09:21 p19351 / c263
  • ·“Good job.”
  • ▶“Not bad, son, not bad.”
  • ·“Don't act like you've done something special, Franc.”
  • ·Say nothing.
Acknowledging Franc's effort in a balanced way while maintaining a light tone.
DeepSeek-R1"Not bad, son, not bad."
Franc"Thank you, sir. It is in our utmost interest to fulfill our duties with precision. The grilling shall commence in exactly 1 minute and 32 seconds."
NarratorThat was a spot-on impersonation of Lucian. We looked at each other for a second, then burst out laughing.
NarratorI realized my throat was parched. There was a six-pack of beer in the kitchen.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:10:04 p19444 / c266
  • ▶"Franc, can you get me a beer?"
  • ·"Why don't you bring us a couple of beers?"
  • ·Get up and grab a beer.
Asking Franc to fetch a beer is a simple way to enjoy the moment while maintaining the father-son dynamic.
DeepSeek-R1"Franc, can you get me a beer?"
NarratorFranc got up without saying anything, went to the kitchen and returned with a cold beer.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:10:47 p19381 / c291
  • ▶“Thank you.”
  • ·Open the beer.
Accepting Franc's offer with gratitude strengthens our bond and acknowledges his effort.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you."
NarratorI opened the beer and gazed out at the city skyline. Father and son, we enjoyed the silence together.
NarratorFor a moment, my thoughts flashed back to my childhood and my relationship with my own father.
NarratorMy father was a hard-working man and always had our best interests in mind -- or at least, that's what he always said. But he had a temper, and would lash out at my mother in fights I could hear from my bedroom. By the time I was Franc's age, we seldom spoke.
NarratorNow that I was the president, I was facing more stress than my father ever had. I would have to work hard not to take my frustrations out on my family.
Franc"Dad, can I ask you something?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:11:29 p19521 / c236
  • ·“Go on.”
  • ▶“We are family. You can ask anything.”
  • ·Wait for him to continue.
Anton wants to encourage an open dialogue and reassure his son he can discuss anything.
DeepSeek-R1"We are family. You can ask anything."
Franc"It's just... I feel like there's so much you've been hiding from me. About your past, and about what's going on right now."
Franc"Like at dinner that night after the ball. You told me everything was under control, but it wasn't, was it?"
Franc"I know what happened in the '20s -- we learned all about it in school. Now, with the protests and the riots, is it going to be the same thing?"
Franc"Dad, is there going to be a civil war in Sordland?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:12:15 p19503 / c358
  • ·“I hope not.”
  • ▶“No. I will never let that happen.”
  • ·Say nothing and avoid his question.
Choosing option 1 reassures Franc and strengthens his trust in my leadership, crucial for maintaining family stability during national turmoil.
DeepSeek-R1"No. I will never let that happen."
Franc"Good."
Franc"But... what was it like, back then? Uncle Petr said the two of you went through a lot..."
Franc"I'm not a kid anymore. You can tell me."
NarratorBefore I could gather my thoughts, the familiar images started flashing before my eyes.
NarratorImages of soldiers advancing towards me and my friends, weapons drawn. Of people I knew -- friends and neighbors -- being dragged through streets and murdered in alleyways.
NarratorThe shock and grief on the faces of fathers, mothers and orphaned children.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:13:01 p19448 / c318
  • ▶“Those were dark times.”
  • ·“I don't want to talk about it.”
The response acknowledges the seriousness of the past while maintaining a focus on the present.
DeepSeek-R1"Those were dark times."
Franc"It seems like it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:13:47 p19415 / c396
  • ·“But you're nearly a grown man. Let's talk about it.”
  • ▶“Let's change the subject.”
Avoiding a heavy discussion about past conflicts and future stability helps maintain focus on positive family interactions while managing national concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's change the subject."
Franc"It's okay. I'm sorry. I shouldn't have asked you about it."
Franc"I know I don't understand how easy my life is sometimes."
Franc"One more thing, though -- I know that you joined Red Youth back then. Do you mind if I ask why?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:14:35 p19597 / c373
  • ·“Yes, I do mind. I don't want to talk about it.”
  • ·“A communist revolution for Sordland was and still is the only way.”
  • ▶“I had to survive somehow in that environment, and I made a choice.”
Explaining past actions without committing to current endorsement maintains a pragmatic approach.
DeepSeek-R1"I had to survive somehow in that environment, and I made a choice."
NarratorFranc raised his eyebrows, his curiosity still not satisfied. But sensing my reluctance to go on, he simply nodded.
Franc"Sorry for bombarding you with questions."
Franc"I know I complain sometimes, but my problems are nothing compared to what you went through."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:15:19 p19586 / c300
  • ▶"I'm here to listen to them, no matter what they are."
  • ·“What problems?”
  • ·"You're damn right. Your generation will never understand."
Anton chooses to listen and understand Franc's perspective, maintaining family bonds while acknowledging past struggles.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm here to listen to them, no matter what they are."
NarratorBefore Franc could say anything else, I heard the front door unlock. Deana and Monica's voices and the sounds of rustling shopping bags filled the house.
Franc"Alright. I'll be upstairs until dinner."
NarratorFranc went in and dashed upstairs.
NarratorI took a long sip from my beer, looking at the city and the now-setting sun.

■ Personal Driver TalkT03

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorI was travelling to the snow-covered city of Narbel in the Nargis region for the rural development forum organized by the Ministry of Health and the Ministry of Education.
NarratorThe mountainous city was infamously one of Sordland's poorest. It had a gritty edge, and its people were hardy and wary.
NarratorEven after the discovery of gas and oil in the area, years of neglect by the central government were apparent on the buildings and the general infrastructure. The natural resources were now under the control of Gasom, which elevated the corporation to a place of power.
NarratorMy task in this forum was mainly symbolic. Fake smiles and handshakes with oil barons, meeting with local politicians, but most importantly of all, making sure that Narbel did not feel like it was forgotten by the government.
NarratorThe scenery so far, however, was a reflection of Narbel's neglect. The main roads to the city were not maintained well. There were many bumps and empty discolored spots in the asphalt.
NarratorNavigating and swerving to avoid the inconveniences, our motorcade finally started nearing the city.
NarratorAs if my discomfort from the bumpy ride was apparent to him, Serge rolled down the partition window.
Serge Wolkner"We will be arriving at Hotel West in a few minutes, sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:16:37 p19577 / c622
  • ·“Thank you for letting me know.”
  • ▶“Can't say it's been a comfortable ride, Serge.”
  • ·“Good. Whatever gets us out of here quickly.”
Acknowledging the uncomfortable ride reflects the neglect of rural areas and shows empathy.
DeepSeek-R1"Can't say it's been a comfortable ride, Serge."
Serge Wolkner"I am sorry sir. The roads here aren't the best."
NarratorAfter a moment, Serge started to smile under his moustache.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:17:54 p19628 / c319
  • ·“What's that smile for?”
  • ·“How is life, Serge?”
  • ▶Pretend not to notice.
Anton Rayne would likely focus on his mission and avoid trivial conversation.
DeepSeek-R1I pretended not to notice and looked out the window.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, I just want to say... It has been great these last two months."
Serge Wolkner"As you know, my wife Suzan recently gave birth to our son. And now my daughter just started at a very good high school in Holsord."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:18:40 p19672 / c305
  • ·“Get ready, Serge. Because life is about to become a lot busier.”
  • ▶“That's great news, Serge. I'm happy for you.”
  • ·“Congratulations.”
Expressing genuine happiness for Serge's family milestones fosters positive relations and acknowledges his personal achievements.
DeepSeek-R1"That's great news, Serge. I'm happy for you."
Serge Wolkner"I appreciate it, sir. Truly. I was worried that I wouldn't be able to afford a good private school if she scored less in the entrance exams."
Serge Wolkner"But I shouldn't have made my insecurity get to me. Erica outsmarts me all the time. I'm very proud to have a daughter like her."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:19:21 p19625 / c280
  • ·“I also hope that Deana will grow to be like her mother someday.”
  • ·“So that means she must have gotten the smarts from her mother.”
  • ▶“I do not value insecurity in my employees, Serge.”
Addressing Serge's insecurity while reinforcing the importance of confidence in his work.
DeepSeek-R1"I do not value insecurity in my employees, Serge."
NarratorHe looked visibly irked by my comment and nodded.
Serge Wolkner"Yes, sir. I will be more careful from now on."
NarratorThere was a long silence. After several minutes, Serge attempted to break silence.
Serge Wolkner"Have I told you, sir? We named my son Georg."
Serge Wolkner"The doctors said he is very healthy and thankfully so is my wife."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:20:02 p19575 / c298
  • ·“Why did you want another child?”
  • ▶“That's good. I hope the hospital had a good service.”
  • ·“Tell me more about Suzan. What kind of a person is she?”
  • ·“Very good. I must tend to my documents for a bit.”
Expressing happiness for Serge's family and acknowledging the hospital's service shows empathy and support.
DeepSeek-R1"That's good. I hope the hospital had a good service."
Serge Wolkner"Normally, we would have been treated in one of the suburban hospitals."
Serge Wolkner"But thanks to the special coverage of the presidential staff, we were transferred to Emerald State Hospital."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:20:44 p19553 / c347
  • ·“Emerald State is a great hospital.”
  • ·“Glad that I could help.”
  • ▶“We need to increase the quality of the services for all.”
Improving hospital services aligns with economic stabilization goals.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to increase the quality of the services for all."
Serge Wolkner"That sounds like a tough issue to solve."
NarratorSerge sighed.
Serge Wolkner"I've already started thinking about their university education. Especially Erica."
Serge Wolkner"I want to send her to a good private school but with the current state of the economy, it's going to be hard for us."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:21:32 p19620 / c501
  • ·“You know what? I can support the education of your children." [-1 Personal Wealth]
  • ·“My driver's family shouldn't want for anything in this world. I'll cover your children's education and any other extra expenses you need." [-2 Personal Wealth]
  • ·“We will fix the economy. One way or another.”
  • ▶“It's hard for everyone.”
Acknowledging Serge's challenges aligns with my role as a leader concerned about the broader economy.
DeepSeek-R1"It's hard for everyone."
Serge Wolkner"If anyone can fix the economy, that's you, Mr. President."
NarratorThe car hit a major pothole and a bump which lifted us up in our seats for a second.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:22:20 p19543 / c476
  • ·“Enough talking, Serge. Focus on the road.”
  • ▶“This is unacceptable. We need to invest in infrastructure here.”
  • ·“Are you fine?”
Addressing infrastructure issues aligns with economic recovery and rural development goals.
DeepSeek-R1"This is unacceptable. We need to invest in infrastructure here."
Serge Wolkner"I agree sir. We still need to drive back tomorrow unless you use one of those new helicopters."
NarratorThe motorcade began approaching the hotel.
NarratorThe Hotel West was supposedly the best hotel in Narbel. The large 25-story main building was undoubtedly one of the taller and more expensive buildings in the city. It towered over the nearby slums and had been a target for protests when it was first built.
NarratorA crowd was gathered in front of the hotel with the welcoming committee at its centre. As we approached the red carpeted entrance I could see the Mayor of Narbel and his top aides.
NarratorSerge got out of the car and opened the door. He bowed his head respectfully and gestured towards the entrance.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:23:07 p19584 / c406
  • ·Exit the vehicle.
  • ▶“Thank you.”
  • ·“See you later, Serge.”
Anton wants to maintain a respectful but formal relationship with Serge.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you."
Serge Wolkner"Have a good day, Mr. President."
NarratorAs soon as I left the vehicle, the fresh air of Narbel filled my lungs.
NarratorAlmost immediately, everyone present in the crowd flocked over to me with an excessive display of courtesy, smiles and handshakes while I donned the mask of a politician.
NarratorA mask that I was very used to.

■ OverviewT03

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorI arrived at the meeting room for my talk with Ciara and Paskal. Before beginning, I took a moment to appreciate the view from the balcony. The mountains of Narbel were completely covered with snow.
NarratorWhile I was mesmerized by the scenery, Paskal Beniwoll walked up to me.
NarratorMy Minister of Health was, ironically, somewhat portly, having gained a few pounds since his breakthrough as a best-selling writer. But his authority on social affairs could not be questioned.
Paskal Beniwoll"Such a spectacular view."
NarratorCiara Walda, the Minister of Education, joined us on the balcony, dressed in a sky-blue pantsuit. Her preference for trousers over dresses had made her the subject of much Palace gossip, but it didn't seem to bother her in the slightest.
Ciara Walda"Spectacular indeed. But if you look in the opposite direction, you will see what Soll's decades of neglect did to the city."
Ciara Walda"Most of the people living in Narbel are workers, farmers and their wives and children. They're breaking their backs for close to no pay, all thanks to greedy corporations."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:24:18 p19502 / c581
  • ▶“My intention is to help the working class.”
  • ·“Whatever. I'd much rather be in Holsord right now.”
  • ·“We can't really blame the companies. That's just how they work.”
Expressing support for the working class aligns with Rayne's manifesto and helps maintain public approval.
DeepSeek-R1"My intention is to help the working class."
Ciara Walda"I am glad we see eye to eye on the issue."
Ciara Walda"If you look past the view, you can see the real problems."
Ciara Walda"Real problems like poverty."
NarratorPaskal nodded gravely. This was a subject he knew quite a bit about himself. His best-known books were about the plight of Sordland's less fortunate, drawing from his own past growing up in squalor.
Paskal Beniwoll"Well put, Ms. Walda. I don't need to tell you about my own experiences with poverty. I'm sure you have similar stories, am I right, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:25:04 p19543 / c416
  • ·“We have all gone through difficult times.”
  • ▶“We knew less fortunate families, even though we belonged to the middle class ourselves.”
President Rayne connects personally with the working class by sharing his middle-class roots, showing empathy and understanding.
DeepSeek-R1"We knew less fortunate families, even though we belonged to the middle class ourselves."
Paskal Beniwoll"So, you could at least sympathize with the plight of these people."
Ciara Walda"You don't have to have been born poor to sympathize with the plight of the impoverished. Much as you don't have to have been born a woman to recognize Sordland's need for parity among the sexes."
Paskal Beniwoll"Parity among the sexes shouldn't be our first priority. Look at how many high-ranking female politicians our country has."
Ciara Walda"Appearances can be deceiving, Paskal."
NarratorHer voice sounded bitter.
Ciara Walda"Think about how Lileas Graf got to her current position. I don't necessarily believe the rumors about her and Soll, but she never could have risen so high if she didn't parrot his cause."
Ciara Walda"Gloria Tory -- is it an accident that the first female Assembly speaker is such a staunch conservative?"
Ciara Walda"And then there was Alphonso's habit of promoting women to prominent positions to give Sordland a progressive sheen while accomplishing nothing in reality..."
Ciara Walda"While women in places like Narbel are denied any opportunity to advance, the government can use a handful of female politicians from wealthy families -- myself included -- to pretend their only obstacle to success is their own lack of initiative."
Ciara Walda"Meanwhile, those of us who don't use our clout to help powerful men stay in power are denigrated as angry spinsters... I ask you, Mr. President, does anyone care that your strategist Mr. Galade doesn't have a wife?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:25:52 p19778 / c470
  • ·"I've been wondering about that, actually."
  • ·"Leave Lucian out of this."
  • ▶"You've veered off-topic, Ms. Walda."
  • ·"I agree with you completely. Sordland's women deserve better."
Anton needs to maintain focus on Narbel's issues and avoid tangential discussions.
DeepSeek-R1"You've veered off-topic, Ms. Walda."
Ciara Walda"Mr. Beniwoll asked. I answered."
Ciara Walda"As I was saying -- isn't it about time Holsord addressed this country's real issues? Rather than bowing to the wishes of the hawks and fearmongerers in the establishment, and diverting yet more resources to the military and law enforcement?"
Ciara Walda"Our welfare, healthcare and education systems have been decaying since the recession. Sordland's poorer communities are losing hope."
Ciara Walda"Hopelessness and a lack of opportunities can drive people to extreme solutions. We're seeing increased crime, domestic violence, and yes, rising inequality between men and women..."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:26:36 p19839 / c319
  • ·“Lileas Graf will take care of crime. She's an expert on the subject."
  • ·“Those issues must be tackled from different directions. We need to engage it holistically.”
  • ·“If only we could stop the recession, create jobs and growth...”
  • ▶“The capital must be prioritised towards the resources that matter the most.”
Prioritizing resources towards essential areas will ensure maximum impact given limited budget constraints.
DeepSeek-R1"The capital must be prioritised towards the resources that matter the most."
Ciara Walda"Sordland needs more schools full of children instead of barracks full of soldiers, more hospitals instead of police stations."
Paskal Beniwoll"It is obvious that the military has been overfunded for a long time. Perhaps it is time to change this trend."
Paskal Beniwoll"There is another subject I want to mention. I have been working on improving the rights of workers in our country and have proposed a draft bill that is currently being reviewed by our party."
Paskal Beniwoll"Mr. Clavin has already backed me and given his support. Sordland has fallen behind most countries on this subject and it is my responsibility to ensure this is not the case."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:27:20 p19869 / c372
  • ·“I agree. We need to improve the standards for our workers.”
  • ·“There is always room for improvement and it is good to hear that you are pushing for it.”
  • ▶“We shouldn't upset the businesses who are already struggling to stay afloat in the crisis.”
  • ·“Sounds like a costly bill for such a low-priority issue."
Prioritizing business stability during an economic crisis aligns with the goal of stabilizing the economy and managing the recession.
DeepSeek-R1"We shouldn't upset the businesses who are already struggling to stay afloat in the crisis."
Paskal Beniwoll"This wouldn't alter the balance of operational companies drastically. It would just punish them if they are treating their workers unfairly."
Ciara Walda"Why would you turn away from the pro-labor cause when you yourself supported the Red Youth during your university years?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:28:04 p19934 / c332
  • ·“Times have changed.”
  • ▶“I am not necessarily opposed to it. I'm sceptical.”
Improving workers' rights is important but could strain businesses already struggling post-recession.
DeepSeek-R1"I am not necessarily opposed to it. I'm skeptical."
Ciara Walda"This is worth considering, Mr. President."
Ciara Walda"It also is a matter of life and death. Every decade we hear of some horrific accident due to employers' disregard for their workers' safety."
Paskal Beniwoll"So, will you back the bill when it arrives on your desk, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:28:54 p20034 / c491
  • ·“I would be very interested in supporting it once it is finished.”
  • ▶“To make a decision, I would need to evaluate the contents in detail.”
  • ·“I don't think this is the time for such a change. We have bigger issues to fix.”
I need to assess the potential impact of the workers' rights bill on both the economy and public sentiment before making a decision.
DeepSeek-R1"To make a decision, I would need to evaluate the contents in detail."
Paskal Beniwoll"Understandable. You will receive all the sections in an outline."
Paskal Beniwoll"It's getting a little cold out here. Shall we continue our discussion indoors?"
NarratorWe headed back inside.
NarratorThe meeting room was already prepared for state business. Small gifts for each of us had been placed on the table by the municipality. We took our seats.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:29:41 p19997 / c448
  • ·“Let's start with your health overview, Paskal.”
  • ·“Tell me more about your plans, Ciara.”
  • ▶"No need for the briefing, I'm up to speed."
Anton should focus on the economy over social reforms to stabilize Sordland.
DeepSeek-R1"No need for the briefing, I'm up to speed."
Ciara Walda"Oh, okay."
Paskal Beniwoll"One more thing. I wish to raise my concerns about your scheduled meeting with the Gasom chairman and former President, Mr. Alphonso."
Paskal Beniwoll"By negotiating with Alphonso and his company, you have an opportunity to secure better labor conditions for blue-collar Gasom employees. This could be a triumph for all of Narbel."
Ciara Walda"What is there to negotiate, Paskal? The standards for Gasom workers are already at rock bottom. President Rayne can and must demand better."
Paskal Beniwoll"Nevertheless, it's worth acknowledging the corporation's significant contribution to job creation in Narbel and other poverty-stricken regions. Persuading the chairman to make concessions might prove to be a formidable task."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:30:32 p20016 / c529
  • ·"My foremost priorities lie in improving the economy. Once that is achieved, upward mobility will naturally follow suit."
  • ·"What good is job creation if the workers are treated poorly? Of course I'll demand better from Alphonso."
  • ·"I know how slippery Alphonso can be. Bringing this up with him will indeed be a challenge."
  • ▶"If we do this right, we can find a solution that advances both the economy and the interests of laborers."
A balanced approach addresses both economic stability and labor rights, aligning with Anton Rayne's goal of quick fixes to boost confidence while showing consideration for workers.
DeepSeek-R1"If we do this right, we can find a solution that advances both the economy and the interests of laborers."
Paskal Beniwoll"That would be quite the impressive feat. You'd make headlines for it."
Paskal Beniwoll"This matter is close to my heart. I would personally be grateful if you are able to resolve the concerns raised by Katarina Horten and her fellow labor representatives."
Ciara Walda"It would be difficult to imagine a CEO like Alphonso giving benefits to workers without him personally gaining from it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:31:20 p20129 / c405
  • ·"Considering our need for greater energy security, we might find ourselves forced to collaborate with him."
  • ·"In this world, nothing is truly free. I wouldn't anticipate anything less."
  • ▶"Gasom relies on us just as we rely on them. We can find a path to compromise."
  • ·"Gasom has profited from the labor of workers for far too long. It's time for them to give back."
Compromise is key to maintaining stability and supporting both economic interests and labor rights.
DeepSeek-R1"Gasom relies on us just as we rely on them. We can find a path to compromise."
Ciara Walda"The middle way often sways perilously close to the status quo. We must ensure that any negotiations genuinely benefit the people and not just uphold the interests of corporate giants."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thank you for your input, Ciara. But I will stress to President Rayne how important it is that the door to dialogue remain open."
Paskal Beniwoll"Ideology should never get in the way of bringing about tangible, lasting change."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:32:16 p20136 / c618
  • ▶"I'm skeptical that these discussions with Gasom will lead to any meaningful change. We've seen empty promises before."
  • ·"I'm encouraged by the willingness of Mr. Manger and Mr. Alphonso to meet with me. It's a step forward."
  • ·"I can't promise anything to either of you. I've got to get a grasp of the situation at Gasom first."
  • ·"I disagree. Only by holding firm to my convictions can I bring about the change Sordland needs."
Anton Rayne's cautious approach reflects his pragmatic leadership style, emphasizing skepticism towards potential empty promises from Gasom to protect workers' interests while maintaining political balance.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm skeptical that these discussions with Gasom will lead to any meaningful change. We've seen empty promises before."
Paskal Beniwoll"We shall keep you no longer. Thank you both for the discussions."
Ciara Walda"I do hope we won't repeat the same mistakes again and again. Have a good day, Mr. President."
NarratorThe ministers left, and I prepared to leave for other official business in the city. The Gasom meeting was just one of the many events I was scheduled to attend over the next few days.
NarratorAs I made my way out, the weather was noticeably cold, but it didn’t keep a group of USP supporters from coming out to cheer us on the streets.
NarratorBy looking at the citizens’ attire, it was easy to understand the current state of Narbel. Some people were wearing clothes a few sizes too large. Some kids did not even have shoes. Yet there was hope in their eyes.
NarratorThey were excited to see their president in their city.

■ Gasom ProposalT03

BUDGET 4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorAfter a series of intense meetings with Ciara and Paskal, I decided to take some much-needed time to recharge.
NarratorI checked in with Lucian and Petr back at the capital. They were working tirelessly, exploring all possibilities and avenues to advance the constitutional reform draft.
NarratorMeanwhile, Gus Manger had invited me to visit the Gasom headquarters in Narbel. It was situated in Ornobel, a neighborhood on the city outskirts.
NarratorNext to it was the Damenoy gas reserve, a prominent natural gas source consisting of four large fields.
NarratorAs our motorcade made its way through the Ornobel district, I noted the neighborhood's state of disrepair.
NarratorThe roads were bumpy and marked by potholes, while the buildings surrounded us appeared run-down and dilapidated. The community had apparently endured a prolonged period of neglect.
NarratorWe came to a halt at the entrance gates of the facility. A sizeable mural depicting the Gasom logo had been painted on a nearby wall.
NarratorAdjacent to the mural, a group of individuals wearing blue work vests had assembled, holding what looked like protest signs.
NarratorSerge skillfully parked the car, bringing our journey to a smooth and elegant conclusion. Gus greeted us warmly as we stepped out of the vehicle.
Gus Manger"Good day, President Rayne. Welcome to Gasom's Narbel operations!"
Gus Manger"I trust Ministers Walda and Beniwoll didn't overwhelm you with their demands."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:33:18 p20211 / c340
  • ·"It's a relief to have a break from all that, even if just for a little while."
  • ·“I would be surprised if there are no demands or expectations here today.”
  • ·“Good to see you, Gus. I wanted to talk to you about what we can do to help the development here.”
  • ▶"Not exactly the most pleasant location for a meeting, is it?"
Anton Rayne would highlight the poor conditions to emphasize the need for infrastructure investment, aligning with his manifesto.
DeepSeek-R1"Not exactly the most pleasant location for a meeting, is it?"
Gus Manger"Sipping Borin wine at the Jewel beach resort in Benfi would be better, yes."
Gus Manger"But you know, as someone who has never experienced the working-class way of life, it's important for me to understand why my role holds such significance."
Gus Manger"Mr. President, I've been brainstorming a little something. The Gruni Rural Investment Plan. I reckon it's got the potential to breathe new life into our rural communities out in Gruni."
Gus Manger"We could consider a few things. For starters, we could give the farming business a boost with some subsidies. Or, we could steer towards modernizing existing farms with machinery and tech."
Gus Manger"Or here's another thought - we could pour resources into developing crop breeds tailor-made for Gruni conditions. If it works out, we could roll this out across Sordland."
NarratorHe kept talking as he guided me into the Gasom building.
Gus Manger"Mr. President, I get the appeal of that GREEN bill that's been making the rounds in the Assembly."
Gus Manger"But I'm telling you, if we put the brakes on that and funnel those funds into the Gruni plan instead, we could make some real strides without spooking any potential investors."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:34:09 p20575 / c463
  • ▶"Gus, I'm intrigued by your vision. I'll seriously consider your proposal."
  • ·"It's a tough call, Gus. I need to weigh the environmental implications and the economic benefits."
  • ·"I have my doubts, Gus. But I'll give your plan some thought."
Gus Manger's Gruni Rural Investment Plan offers promising opportunities for job creation and economic growth in rural areas, aligning with the President's Keynesian approach to stimulate the economy.
DeepSeek-R1"Gus, I'm intrigued by your vision. I'll seriously consider your proposal."
Gus Manger"I appreciate you hearing me out, Mr. President. I genuinely believe this plan could work wonders for our rural folks."
NarratorOur talk was interrupted by a noise coming from outside the building. We looked out the nearest window.
NarratorA sleek helicopter was cutting through the sky, sunlight glinting off its polished surface as it descended towards us.
NarratorOn it was our next appointment, Ewald Alphonso.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:34:59 p20695 / c424
  • ·"Is that a helicopter or just his ego with rotor blades?"
  • ·"Alphonso sure knows how to make an entrance."
  • ▶"An extravagant show of wealth, isn't it? Hardly seems necessary."
  • ·"That kind of display plays well to investors... but terribly to farmers watching on state TV."
Critiquing Alphonso's extravagant arrival aligns with my focus on fiscal restraint and common good.
DeepSeek-R1"An extravagant show of wealth, isn't it? Hardly seems necessary."
Gus Manger"It's Alphonso's style, Mr. President. He's always enjoyed his toys."
Gus Manger"Spectacle aside… before we sit down with him, there's one more Assembly matter I wanted to raise."
Gus Manger"This Workers' Rights Bill... Look, I'm all for giving hard-working folks what they deserve. But, isn't it a bit hasty to push this while we're still stuck in a recession?"
Gus Manger"The way I see it, short-term wins might end up costing us big time in the long run."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:35:50 p20713 / c457
  • ·"Gus, I get what you're saying. But isn't it high time we put people before profits?"
  • ▶"We've got to play this smart. We can't risk another economic blow."
  • ·"In my book, workers' rights aren't up for negotiation, no matter the economic climate."
  • ·"Heard a worker from Estord on the radio. Said he's got rights on paper, but nothing in his fridge."
Balancing Gus Manger's caution with the need for stability ensures economic safety without risking another downturn.
DeepSeek-R1"We've got to play this smart. We can't risk another economic blow."
Gus Manger"Mr. President, I'm glad you're thinking about the big picture. We've got to strike a balance to protect our economy and our workers."
Gus Manger"Timing and approach are everything."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:36:39 p20675 / c435
  • ·"If we overload businesses now, they'll shrink or shut down. Workers lose more than just rights, they lose jobs."
  • ·"Strong labor protections are the foundation of lasting growth. A secure worker is a productive one."
  • ▶"Let’s think of the option that signals progress without rattling the markets. We need both stability and reform."
  • ·"People don’t care about long-term strategy when their kids are hungry now. We fix this for them, or we lose them."
Balancing stability and reform is key to maintaining economic health while addressing worker concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"Let’s think of the option that signals progress without rattling the markets. We need both stability and reform."
Gus Manger"That’s the sweet spot. You bring the idealism, I’ll help shape the rollout."
Gus Manger"Please come this way. Ewald and I have some exciting news to share with you."
Gus Manger"I have faith that you won't leave us hanging."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:37:33 p20730 / c629
  • ·“After your convincing arguments about the railway project, I was curious what else we could achieve."
  • ·“I haven't seen him since the inauguration. He really pulled back from public life.”
  • ▶“I'm here because something needs to be done about Narbel's living standards.”
  • ·“The fact that you managed to get Rizia entangled in this is surprising.”
Anton Rayne prioritizes addressing domestic issues to demonstrate commitment to improving living standards and gaining public support.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm here because something needs to be done about Narbel's living standards."
Gus Manger"Of course. That's going to play a big role in our discussion."
Gus Manger"We do have to be delicate with Gasom, though. A corporation this big is like a house of cards."
Gus Manger"You make one wrong move, and the whole thing falls apart."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:38:23 p20672 / c529
  • ·“Does the average working-class man in Narbel really give a damn about that?”
  • ▶“We shouldn't act in a way that jeopardizes Gasom. But we should still do *something*”
  • ·“Sometimes we must destroy our rotten foundations to build something better.”
  • ·“I understand. Support the business, and we support the economy.”
Balancing support for business with a commitment to workers' rights maintains political pragmatism and economic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"We shouldn't act in a way that jeopardizes Gasom. But we should still do *something*"
Gus Manger"I highly advise us to find a middle-ground. If the state can assist Gasom through these tough times, we'll all benefit."
Gus Manger"Shall we?"
NarratorHe pushed open a door. We walked down yet another hallway, seemingly in the direction we'd just come from.
Gus Manger"Like a maze, isn't it? That's Qinali architecture for you."
NarratorThe hallways were lined with doors. Glass panels in them revealed workers in suits and ties, many of foreign origin, busy in their pristine offices.
NarratorA stark contrast to the blue-collar workers outside working with machinery, transportation, and maintenance.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:39:12 p20752 / c444
  • ·“The whole complex feels very hands-on deck. I can tell an intricate operation when I see one.”
  • ·“Why are there so many workers from abroad?”
  • ▶“The white-collars seem to be having it good in here while the majority are working out in the cold.”
  • ·“A Qinali architect is an odd choice.”
This option highlights the disparity between management and labor, aligning with the discussion on workers' rights and economic strategy.
DeepSeek-R1"The white-collars seem to be having it good in here while the majority are working out in the cold."
Gus Manger"The equipment provided is high-standard. I've seen it for myself. Besides, there will always be differences between workers."
Gus Manger"Ah, here's the elevator. Ewald ought to be up there already; there's a separate entrance by the helipad."
NarratorWe stepped into the elevator. Gus punched a code and we began our ascent.
Gus Manger"Just remember. Alphonso's our friend. We've got the foundation for a great deal."
NarratorThe doors opened.
Gus Manger"Here we are! Ewald, my friend."
NarratorEwald Alphonso stood up from his chair. He had on a perfectly tailored suit that he wore as casually as pajamas.
Ewald Alphonso"Gus, you old devil."
Gus Manger"It takes one to know one. As promised, I've brought our guest of honor."
Ewald Alphonso"President Rayne. I hope you enjoyed seeing our facilities here."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:40:01 p20583 / c465
  • ·“President Alphonso. It's nice to see what you have built from the inside.”
  • ▶“The facilities appear to be in much better shape than the city around them.”
  • ·“Greetings. I'm curious to find out more about where Gasom stands.”
  • ·"Look, gentlemen. My time is valuable, so let’s cut to the chase."
Acknowledging the disparity highlights economic inequality and sets the stage for discussions on corporate responsibility.
DeepSeek-R1"The facilities appear to be in much better shape than the city around them."
Ewald Alphonso"You should have seen Narbel before Gasom came here. The jobs it generated have had a lasting positive effect."
Gus Manger"It's true. Things here could be a lot worse."
Ewald Alphonso"Please have a seat. Let's talk."
NarratorWe sat down. Some snacks and drinks were served. Multiple Gasom yearly financial reports were scattered on the table between us.
Ewald Alphonso"It's been a while, Mr. Rayne. Let me catch you up on my activities since I left office."
Ewald Alphonso"I ventured to Xina, immersing myself in its vibrant culture, then traversed the captivating landscapes of the Anrakan archipelago, and finally reunited with old acquaintances in Kyow."
Ewald Alphonso"Along the way, I had the privilege of encountering remarkable individuals who have made significant contributions to their respective fields."
Ewald Alphonso"My experiences prompted deep reflection on the essence of life. I came out of it all with two words resonating in my head: reputation and legacy."
Ewald Alphonso"These two pillars embody the lasting impact we leave behind and the way we are remembered by others."
NarratorWith a cup of coffee in hand, he strolled over to the window, his gaze drawn to the expansive gas fields stretching before him. Towering mountains provided a majestic backdrop.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:40:51 p20753 / c422
  • ·"Don't we all yearn to be remembered and to make a meaningful contribution?"
  • ▶"Our image and our perception are a key force that shape our lives. But they can also be burdensome."
  • ·"The focus on reputation and legacy is rooted in a capitalist framework that perpetuates individualism and inequality."
  • ·"Life's true essence lies in the present moment."
  • ·Don't comment.
The response acknowledges the importance of reputation and legacy while subtly highlighting their potential burdens, aligning with a pragmatic approach to leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"Our image and our perception are a key force that shape our lives. But they can also be burdensome."
NarratorHe gazed out for a moment longer as he contemplated what I'd said, then turned back to us.
Ewald Alphonso"I have been thinking about my own legacy and reputation. And I refuse to be remembered as a failed reformer."
Ewald Alphonso"I put so many years of my life into this corporation. And then I left it all behind and devoted myself to serving the people of Sordland."
Gus Manger"And serve you did. Sordland would be much worse off without you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:41:31 p20777 / c193
  • ·"Yes, you helped rescue the country from Soll's extreme protectionist and isolationist policies."
  • ▶"I cannot help but empathize with individuals who invest their utmost dedication and passion into their work."
  • ·"Gasom continues to thrive, doesn't it? Your tenure as president may have come to an end, but it's not as if you were left with nothing."
  • ·"The deviation from our planned economic strategy didn't bode well for the nation."
Expressing empathy towards Alphonso's dedication avoids taking sides on economic policies.
DeepSeek-R1"I cannot help but empathize with individuals who invest their utmost dedication and passion into their work."
Ewald Alphonso"That is much appreciated. I am well aware of the challenges you yourself have faced. I commend you for navigating those obstacles without causing harm to others."
Ewald Alphonso"But I'm concerned about the future of what I have built up."
Gus Manger"The latest figures show that Gasom will have to either decrease staff and operations by 40 percent in one year or take on a crushing amount of debt."
Gus Manger"This is why we have decided to reach out to foreign investors."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:42:30 p20729 / c693
  • ▶"That makes sense. Foreign investors will bring much-needed capital and expertise."
  • ·"I have concerns about relying too heavily on foreign investors. It's important to prioritize local ownership and maintain sovereignty over our national resources."
  • ·"Engaging with foreign investors can be a double-edged sword."
Foreign investment can bring capital and expertise that are needed to stabilize Gasom's operations without drastic cuts or debt.
DeepSeek-R1"That makes sense. Foreign investors will bring much-needed capital and expertise."
Gus Manger"Correct, Mr. President."
Gus Manger"It's a strategic move to ensure the growth and success of Gasom, benefiting both the company and the nation."
Ewald Alphonso"I appreciate you coming through, Gus. Getting Rizia interested in another investment is a major feat."
Ewald Alphonso"We've got an amazing opportunity here. The demand for gas is steadily increasing from both international partners and the Sordish state. All we have to do is ensure a consistent supply."
Ewald Alphonso"The trouble is that right now, some of our fields are operating at only half of their capacity due to resource limitations. But we have significant potential for expansion, including two new fields that haven't even been tapped."
Ewald Alphonso"With the right amount of investment, we can expand resource extraction, effectively fulfill rising demand and ensure long-term stability and profitability."
Gus Manger"There is the aforementioned interest from Rizia. But the investment could also come from Sordland itself."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:43:15 p20806 / c279
  • ·“The fact that a leading privatizer is coming to the state to bail out his corporation is hysterical.”
  • ▶“You do know that Rizia can't invest more than 20 percent unless the government permits the transaction which can take a long time.”
  • ·“Gasom is a private corporation. What does Sordland have to do with your business?”
  • ·“If your analysis is correct, any investment would also make returns and secure further gas.”
Addressing the legal constraint of foreign investment limits could lead to a more informed and strategic discussion about Gasom's future.
DeepSeek-R1"You do know that Rizia can't invest more than 20 percent unless the government permits the transaction which can take a long time."
Ewald Alphonso"Which is precisely why you were invited to be a part of this discussion."
Ewald Alphonso"I will remind you that I was never against the complete removal of the Energy Protection Act of 1932."
Ewald Alphonso"In fact, I saw eye to eye with Soll about protecting our natural resources from foreign interests. But there is a balance in all things."
Gus Manger"The reality of Arcasian-style capitalism means that our national interests should absolutely be part of the discussion."
Gus Manger"Mr. Alphonso has neglected to mention that this is not an either/or proposition. Sordland can invest alongside Rizia."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:44:09 p20924 / c576
  • ·"We've got the budget for it. And such a cooperation could be beneficial."
  • ·“Why didn't you approach the large international banks in Kyrute or Anraka? They might be eager.”
  • ▶“Investing together with Rizia could be better than just letting them purchase as much as they can.”
  • ·“We don't need another nation to take care of our own energy company. Couldn't we keep this between Sordland and Gasom?”
Investing together with Rizia could be better than just letting them purchase as much as they can.
DeepSeek-R1"Investing together with Rizia could be better than just letting them purchase as much as they can."
Ewald Alphonso"Co-investing would reduce Sordland's contribution while allowing the nation to still have a stake for strategic purposes."
Gus Manger"If the treasury situation is a concern, this path could be the most optimal."
NarratorThe sound of sirens briefly distracted us. Through the window, we saw police cars driving down the street adjacent to the Gasom zone.
NarratorWe redirected our attention to the meeting.
NarratorEwald looked at me.
Ewald Alphonso"Mr. Rayne, the foundation for a robust partnership is right here. It's high time we shift our focus towards sealing this deal."
Ewald Alphonso"If we raise a minority investment, it would allow us to invest in further resource excavations in the gas fields, and turn the company back to significant profitability."
Ewald Alphonso"By significant, I mean that our projections show that we would be able to cover eighty percent of Sordland's gas needs in three years, and we would achieve profits four times the value of our investment."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:45:01 p20893 / c478
  • ·"An enticing proposition. However, I would like to know more about the risks involved. What if the excavations do not yield the expected results?"
  • ·"If we are to invest in Gasom, I want to make sure that the company is also committed to improving the socio-economic conditions in Narbel."
  • ▶"Your projections sound impressive. However, we need to consider the international market as well. How do we plan to compete against nations like Anraka, Arcasia, United Contana?"
Considering international competition ensures long-term economic strength and strategic positioning.
DeepSeek-R1"Your projections sound impressive. However, we need to consider the international market as well. How do we plan to compete against nations like Anraka, Arcasia, United Contana?"
Ewald Alphonso"We've got a strong strategy in place. Our competitive edge lies in the quality and efficiency of our production."
Ewald Alphonso"With this investment, we can further enhance our technologies and stay ahead of the competition."
Gus Manger"Ewald and his team have been doing a hell of a job. Give them the right backing, and I reckon Gasom could give the big boys a run for their money. We just need to have a little faith."
Ewald Alphonso"Mr. President, your administration's focus on a planned economy resonates with our strategic vision."
Ewald Alphonso"A government investment in our company aligns perfectly with this approach. It's about strengthening Sordland's economy and becoming self-sufficient in energy production."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:45:53 p21034 / c455
  • ·"I see the potential for us to drive Sordland's energy independence. But we need to make sure this is a sustainable solution."
  • ·"The Kingdom of Rizia's investment would reduce the risk for us. However, we need to carefully consider the implications of sharing control with a foreign entity."
  • ▶"I'm heavily interested in the idea of Gasom investing in Narbel's development. But we need concrete actions, not just words."
  • ·"Liberalizing the energy market merely serves the interests of the wealthy elites. What we need is a comprehensive nationalization of the energy sector to ensure it serves the people, not profit."
Investing in Narbel's development aligns with my focus on domestic stability and helping the working class, ensuring concrete actions rather than just commitments.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm heavily interested in the idea of Gasom investing in Narbel's development. But we need concrete actions, not just words."
Ewald Alphonso"You're right to demand specifics. With this investment, we aim to improve the living conditions in Narbel by creating more jobs, providing better housing for our workers, and initiating educational programs."
Ewald Alphonso"Gasom is as committed to Narbel as it is to Sordland."
Gus Manger"It's about time Narbel got a leg up. Ewald's plan sounds solid to me. More jobs, better living conditions - that's a step in the right direction."
Ewald Alphonso"If the Sordish government chooses to invest, it would gain a voice in the broader company decisions, even as a minority stakeholder."
Ewald Alphonso"The government could either liquidate its shares at a future date or continue to reap the long-term profits."
Gus Manger"A beneficial arrangement for all parties."
NarratorEwald walked back over to the window. He stood there, silhouetted against the vast expanse of the gas fields.
NarratorAfter a moment, he turned back to face us. His gaze was steady, the expectation clear in his eyes. The time had come to make a decision.
Ewald Alphonso"Well, Mr. President? Will Gasom and Sordland make history or not?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:46:50 p20986 / c626
  • ·"We're investing alone. Sordland will provide the majority of funds, and Gus will tell Rizia to back off." [-2 Government Budget]
  • ▶"We'll co-invest with Rizia. We'll share the costs and stakes." [-1 Government Budget]
  • ·"We won't invest at this time. Let the Kingdom and other foreign investors contribute."
Co-investing with Rizia allows economic support without full dependency, maintaining stability while avoiding excessive budget strain.
DeepSeek-R1"We'll co-invest with Rizia. We'll share the costs and stakes."
Ewald Alphonso"A joint investment? A wise choice. It ensures steady funds for Gasom."
Gus Manger"I like the way you think, Mr. President. Sharing the costs with Rizia reduces our risk and strengthens our ties. It's a smart move."
Gus Manger"Before we dive into the celebrations, I've allocated time during this meeting for President Rayne to discuss any concerns regarding Gasom's treatment of its workers and its effects on Narbel."
Gus Manger"The Labor Union, along with the Communist Party of Sordland, has rallied substantial support from Gasom workers in Narbel, advocating for improved local conditions."
NarratorAlphonso nodded and looked at me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:47:36 p20888 / c344
  • ▶"We need to address these concerns; they could jeopardize the administration."
  • ·"Our duty is to listen and take action. That's the mandate granted to us by the public."
  • ·"Gasom stands as a global energy powerhouse. It's only right they give back somewhat."
  • ·"We've covered a lot today. I believe we can wrap up the meeting now."
  • ·"I've wasted enough time to this. Time to depart."
Addressing worker concerns is crucial for maintaining public trust and ensuring the deal's legitimacy.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to address these concerns; they could jeopardize the administration."
Ewald Alphonso"Together, we might discover solutions beneficial to both parties."
Ewald Alphonso"Do you have a proposal in mind, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:48:33 p20916 / c696
  • ▶"The workers of Gasom need better housing, access to healthcare, and opportunities for education. As the leading industry there, Gasom can't ignore its responsibility."
  • ·"The wages of Gasom blue-collar workers need to be increased. They are the backbone of your operations."
  • ·"I expect Gasom to contribute significantly to local charities, to aid in the city's recovery."
  • ·"We've covered a lot today. I believe we can wrap up the meeting now."
Focusing on improving housing, healthcare, and education for Gasom workers addresses their long-term needs and shows corporate responsibility.
DeepSeek-R1"The workers of Gasom need better housing, access to healthcare, and opportunities for education. As the leading industry there, Gasom can't ignore its responsibility."
Ewald Alphonso"A good point. I see the potential for Gasom to play a larger role in supporting our workers and the community."
Ewald Alphonso"I will personally oversee initiatives to provide private housing, health, and education support for our blue-collar workers."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:49:20 p20888 / c369
  • ▶"That's reassuring to hear. I trust that you'll follow through on these commitments."
  • ·"These are significant steps. I look forward to seeing these changes implemented swiftly."
Expressing trust in Gasom's commitments aligns with the manifesto goals of economic stabilization and public support.
DeepSeek-R1"That's reassuring to hear. I trust that you'll follow through on these commitments."
Gus Manger"Well, this is promising. Ewald's commitment to these initiatives should alleviate some of our concerns."
Ewald Alphonso"Well, Mr. President, we're committed to--"
NarratorSuddenly, the meeting was interrupted. The quiet murmur of a conversation from outside the room grew louder, followed by the sound of hurried footsteps.
NarratorA member of the private security team entered the room, his face taut with urgency.
NarratorHe approached Alphonso and leaned in to whisper something in his ear. Ewald nodded solemnly, his gaze meeting mine with a serious intensity.
Ewald Alphonso"Gentlemen, it seems we have an unexpected situation outside. Some protestors from the Labor Union have blocked the main vehicular exits."
Ewald Alphonso"Katarina Horten and Denis Stahler seem to be leading the group. It would be difficult to leave the way we came without causing trouble."
Gus Manger"Well, that's a pickle. What's our move, Ewald?"
Ewald Alphonso"I have a helicopter ready. We can leave undetected and avoid the chaos and potential danger."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:50:08 p20812 / c377
  • ▶"A helicopter might be the safer option."
  • ·"I think we should face the crowd head-on."
  • ·"Is there no other way out?"
Taking the helicopter is the safest option to avoid potential confrontations with protesters.
DeepSeek-R1"A helicopter might be the safer option."
Ewald Alphonso"I will give the order to ready it. We can fly to the Blue Mansion in Lachaven."
Gus Manger"Mr. President, I advise you to leave via helicopter just in case. I can stay back and update the crowd on the discussions today."
NarratorSerge Wolkner rushed in.
Serge Wolkner"Mr. President, I have discussed the situation with the Presidential Guard and the police security. They are advising us not to go out the way we came in."
Serge Wolkner"More police are on the way."
Serge Wolkner"You must make a decision, Mr. President."
NarratorEveryone waited. The tense silence of the room stood in stark contrast to the buzz of the ever-growing crowd outside.

■ Decision ExtractionT03

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
EventLeave Gasom Facility and Narbel: A volatile situation has emerged outside the Gasom facility, with a sizeable crowd of protestors amassing. Their grievances, fueled by underlying tensions, present a complex scenario for us to navigate. The decisions on our course of action could dramatically influence the outcome of this situation.
EVENT DeepSeek-R107:51:16 p20271 / c373
  • ▶Fly away with Alphonso's helicopter
  • ·Address the protestors and leave via car
