■ Personal Helicopter EscapeT03

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorThe low rumble of the helicopter's engines reverberated through the cabin as we soared over the vast expanse of Nargis.
NarratorA patchwork of verdant fields, sprawling forests, and untouched land stretched out beneath us.
NarratorThe further we moved from the mountains, the more rural the landscape became. Small clusters of wooden houses and farmers' huts began to appear, their rustic charm a stark contrast to the grey concrete of Narbel.
NarratorAlphonso had been quiet for most of the journey, but as we neared Lachaven, he finally broke his silence.
Ewald Alphonso"Now that we've escaped an angry mob together, may I call you Anton?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:52:04 p20210 / c335
  • ·"Of course, Ewald. You don't even need to ask."
  • ▶"I'd rather keep things on the professional level, if you don't mind."
Maintaining professionalism is crucial in a political context to preserve the proper distance and hierarchy.
DeepSeek-R1"I'd rather keep things on the professional level, if you don't mind."
Ewald Alphonso"Of course. Forgive my forwardness."
Ewald Alphonso"I must say, Mr. President, that was quite the adventure we just had. Never a dull moment with you around, eh?"
Ewald Alphonso"Your driver did a great job escorting us out of there. He's a keeper."
Ewald Alphonso"I heard from Gasom security and the local police about some armed gang activity in the area a week ago. Better safe than sorry, right?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:52:49 p20247 / c365
  • ▶"It was an adventure, wasn't it? And I agree, Serge's assistance was invaluable."
  • ·"Yes, our safety matters most. But it's unfortunate that we had to leave under those circumstances."
Maintaining a positive and professional demeanor helps preserve relationships with key figures like Alphonso and acknowledges security's crucial role.
DeepSeek-R1"It was an adventure, wasn't it? And I agree, Serge's assistance was invaluable."
Serge Wolkner"Just doing my job, Mr. President. I'm glad we're all safe."
Serge Wolkner"Mr. Alphonso, it's an honor to meet you in person. I was trained by your former driver Nouman Fevon. He always spoke highly of you."
Ewald Alphonso"Ah, Nouman! What a man he was. Always had a knack for lightening the mood, even in the direst of situations."
Ewald Alphonso"Once we were stuck in a traffic jam for hours, and he started singing old Sordish folk songs right there in the car. He had the whole motorcade laughing."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:53:33 p20314 / c364
  • ▶"Serge, can you sing any Sordish folk songs to lighten the mood?"
  • ·"Perhaps we should add singing ability to the job requirements for presidential drivers, eh Serge?"
  • ·"Serge, I didn't know your job had such... unusual requirements."
A light-hearted comment can ease tensions and build camaraderie after a stressful escape.
DeepSeek-R1"Serge, can you sing any Sordish folk songs to lighten the mood?"
Serge Wolkner"I'm afraid I'm not much of a singer, Mr. President. But I know how to avoid traffic jams."
Ewald Alphonso"Ha! A true professional, this one."
NarratorWe turned our attention to the stunning view below us.
NarratorSerge's gaze traced the path of the still-in-progress L-1 railway. The ribbon of steel gleamed bright in the sun.
Serge Wolkner"What a marvel, Mr. President. I can't wait for the new railway to transform transportation in Sordland."
Serge Wolkner"Seeing it from above is like witnessing history in the making."
Ewald Alphonso"The L-1 railway. Now that's a project I can stand behind."
Ewald Alphonso"It's not only efficient, it's a statement. It shows that Sordland is ready for the future."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:54:29 p20366 / c638
  • ·"Just wait until it's completed. Logistical operations in Sordland will never be the same again."
  • ▶"Yes, it's ambitious. But great change often requires bold decisions."
  • ·"Your man Gus Manger made an especially convincing case for the railway's construction. I hope he was right."
Anton Rayne wants to position himself as a decisive leader willing to take bold actions for economic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes, it's ambitious. But great change often requires bold decisions."
Serge Wolkner"A revolutionary vision from a visionary president."
Serge Wolkner"My apologies, gentlemen. I didn't mean to dominate the conversation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:55:09 p20312 / c237
  • ▶"You prefer to keep things professional, right? Let's do that going forward."
  • ·"Who needs Nouman's entertainment when we can listen to Serge Wolkner rhapsodizing about Sordland?"
  • ·"No need to apologize, Serge. It's always a pleasure to listen to you."
  • ·"That's quite all right. But I do have a few more things I'd like to bring up with Mr. Alphonso."
Maintaining professionalism is key in such situations.
DeepSeek-R1"You prefer to keep things professional, right? Let's do that going forward."
Serge Wolkner"Understood, Mr. President. I'll make sure to keep that in mind."
Ewald Alphonso"We're approximately 25 minutes away from the Blue Mansion State Building, Mr. President."
NarratorI peered out the window at the looming cityscape of Lachaven was quickly enlarging. The sea was visible in the distance, its blue surface dotted with cargo ships.
NarratorFrom here, it was even more evident what a pivotal role the city of Lachaven played in our international trade. Here, at the so-called "Gateway", was where Sordland opened itself to the global market.
Ewald Alphonso"I always used to go here to escape the political pressure in Holsord."
Ewald Alphonso"The fresh sea breeze, the energy of the city... it's always so invigorating. Makes you forget about the stuffy old bureaucrats trying to get in the way of your policies."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:55:54 p20294 / c305
  • ▶"I wish I could escape like that. But I never stop thinking about my duties in Holsord. Even now."
  • ·"I couldn't agree with you more. I really should get out of Holsord more often."
  • ·"You speak of escaping from old bureaucrats. But isn't the capitalist system you promoted just another form of bureaucracy? Possibly even the worst form of all, for those less fortunate."
Maintaining professionalism while acknowledging the sentiment.
DeepSeek-R1"I wish I could escape like that. But I never stop thinking about my duties in Holsord. Even now."
Ewald Alphonso"That's no good, Mr. President. You can't make the right political decisions if you're never exposed to life outside the bubble."
Ewald Alphonso"It reminded me of the potential of Sordland, of what our nation could be if we embraced change and diversity as Lachaven has."
Ewald Alphonso"You know, looking at the sea reminds me of an incident from my early days at Gasom."
Ewald Alphonso"We had a big deal with a client from Xina, but a gas tanker broke down mid-journey. The delivery was at risk. It was winter, the seas were rough."
Ewald Alphonso"We had two days to get the gas to its destination or we'd lose the client. So, I chartered a small cargo ship, braved the stormy seas, and supervised the transfer of the containers myself."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:56:40 p20324 / c377
  • ·"Wow. That story speaks volumes about your dedication to Gasom."
  • ▶"So Gasom's success hinged on your personal heroics? That's not a sustainable strategy for a corporation, is it?"
  • ·"Your story is impressive, but shouldn't Gasom have had contingency plans for such situations?"
  • ·"Fascinating. But I hope those incidents aren't too frequent at Gasom."
Anton prefers sustainable strategies over risky individual efforts.
DeepSeek-R1"So Gasom's success hinged on your personal heroics? That's not a sustainable strategy for a corporation, is it?"
Ewald Alphonso"No, of course not. We had a good look at our risk management and strategic planning after that debacle."
NarratorAlphonso seemed to retreat into his memories for a moment. A gust of wind rattled the helicopter as he continued.
Ewald Alphonso"Getting back to Lachaven... I was thinking about how the whole city displays the vision of King Egmund."
Ewald Alphonso"Without the investments that he and his chief architect made, Lachaven wouldn't stand shoulder-to-shoulder with Haelm, Porte Drazon, Konstantium and Falenco today."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:57:30 p20391 / c515
  • ·"I believe that Lachaven's success today is due to the collective effort of its people, not a deceased monarch."
  • ▶"Do you think the growth and prosperity of Lachaven can be replicated in other parts of Sordland?"
  • ·"It took time for their vision to be realized. They didn't even see it come to full fruition during their lifetimes."
  • ·"The Kingdom was a different era. Do you think its strategies can be applied in our modern-day context?"
  • ·"You may be right. But not all cities have Lachaven's potential or resources."
Anton would want to connect Lachaven's success to broader economic strategies, emphasizing replicability and practical application.
DeepSeek-R1"Do you think the growth and prosperity of Lachaven can be replicated in other parts of Sordland?"
Ewald Alphonso"Honestly, the key lies in strategic planning and targeted investments. We need to identify sectors with high growth potential and channel our resources there."
Ewald Alphonso"And of course, we need to create a business environment that encourages innovation and entrepreneurship."
Ewald Alphonso"It's akin to what we're trying to achieve with Gasom. Success isn't built overnight. It's the cumulative result of years of planning, investments, and execution."
Ewald Alphonso"Just as Lachaven's success is tied to its global connectivity, Sordland's wealth is tied to its connection to the world and its skilled, educated workforce."
Ewald Alphonso"Looking at the global landscape, I see great potential in Anraka. Despite their turbulent history, they've emerged as a beacon of resilience and innovation."
Ewald Alphonso"Their commitment to natural resource extraction and emphasis on naval engineering are commendable. And as a neutral power, the country offers a counterweight to the battle between United Contana and Arcasia."
Ewald Alphonso"Gasom has multiple engineers hired from Anraka too. I believe Sordland could find a valuable partner in Anraka in the coming decade."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:58:12 p20431 / c326
  • ·"I see potential for collaboration, particularly in offshore resource extraction and marine conservation. We've got much to learn from them."
  • ·"I'm impressed as well, but it's crucial we remain cautious. Anraka's history of piracy and stringent laws could pose challenges in a potential partnership."
  • ·"We should rather focus on Sordland's own strengths. Our future lies in building a self-reliant economy and bolstering our own industries."
  • ▶"Anraka has potential. But isn't Rumburg already a counterbalance to the United Contana-Arcasia power struggle?"
Rumburg is already positioned as a counterbalance, so emphasizing its role shifts focus away from Anraka and aligns with existing strategies.
DeepSeek-R1"Anraka has potential. But isn't Rumburg already a counterbalance to the United Contana-Arcasia power struggle?"
Ewald Alphonso"Rumburg does indeed possess significant power, Mr. President. But the Queen is far more opposed to United Contana and the CSP than Arcasia and the ATO."
Ewald Alphonso"The dynamics would need to shift significantly before they could serve the same function as Anraka."
Ewald Alphonso"These are the kind of factors you need to take into account if you're going to reform the Energy Protection Act, by the way. It might seem minor, but any alteration could shape Sordland's future."
Ewald Alphonso"I'm still mulling over the implications of your investment decision, by the way."
Ewald Alphonso"I'm sure that you agree it's important to maintain autonomy, even while co-investing with Rizia."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:58:58 p20645 / c376
  • ·"Agreed. We need to ensure our sovereignty isn't compromised."
  • ▶"I see this as an opportunity to learn and grow together with the Kingdom."
  • ·"Our collaboration is a mere stepping stone to bigger and better opportunities."
Emphasizing mutual growth strengthens diplomatic ties while maintaining sovereignty.
DeepSeek-R1"I see this as an opportunity to learn and grow together with the Kingdom."
Ewald Alphonso"Indeed, we can definitely learn a lot from each other in this partnership."
NarratorAlphonso scribbled something on his notepad and passed it to one of his assistants in the helicopter.
Ewald Alphonso"Following our earlier conversation, I've been contemplating how Gasom can play a larger role in supporting our workers and the community."
Ewald Alphonso"I'm considering initiatives to provide private housing, health, and education support for our blue-collar workers."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R107:59:49 p20621 / c535
  • ·"As long as the workers are doing their jobs and Gasom is profitable, I see no reason to change anything."
  • ·"That sounds promising. These initiatives could significantly improve workers' lives."
  • ·"That's an ambitious plan. I hope you can execute it."
  • ▶"I'm glad you're thinking along these lines. I expect more than mere consideration, though."
Encouraging concrete actions to improve workers' lives aligns with economic stabilization goals.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm glad you're thinking along these lines. I expect more than mere consideration, though."
Ewald Alphonso"You're absolutely right. It's about making these plans a reality."
NarratorWe were hovering over Lachaven now. The entire city was visible below us, its activity concentrated on its bustling port.
NarratorI cast my gaze over the harbor teeming with ships and the distinctive buildings of downtown. Their Lespian-style architecture lent the skyline a flavor unlike anywhere else in Sordland.
NarratorRepublic Avenue stretched directly beneath us, a grand boulevard lined with palm trees and decorated with sculptures. Even from this height, landmarks like the Evaria Gallery of Art, the Maroon Theatre, and the Lachaven Opera House were discernible.
NarratorAs we began our descent towards the Blue Mansion, Alphonso shifted the conversation.
Ewald Alphonso"One last thing before we land. I've been hearing noise about your administration making changes to the Energy Protection Act."
Ewald Alphonso"You might remember that I struggled to repeal it during my term, and that said struggle was in vain. The Energy Protection Act of 1932 remains in force, and the restrictions on foreign ownership of energy companies in Sordland persist."
Ewald Alphonso"The vote was 179 against the repeal to 71 in favor. The status quo prevailed, as it has to this day."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:00:37 p20848 / c360
  • ·"I understand why you sponsored the motion. But you of all people should understand the importance of protecting Sordland's crucial energy sector."
  • ·"The defeat of the repeal was a setback for our plans to liberalize the economy. We must strategize and push for it in the next Assembly session."
  • ▶"I'm glad the repeal was defeated. We should not open our energy sector to foreign control. We must prioritize Sordland's sovereignty."
  • ·"The defeat of the repeal wasn't surprising given the political climate. I'd rather focus my efforts elsewhere."
Emphasizing national sovereignty aligns with Anton Rayne's focus on internal stability and protecting key sectors.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm glad the repeal was defeated. We should not open our energy sector to foreign control. We must prioritize Sordland's sovereignty."
Ewald Alphonso"I still believe that the repeal would have attracted much-needed foreign investment."
Ewald Alphonso"Mr. President, this strategic partnership with Rizia in Gasom is a promising step forward. It allows us to spread the initial risks and invites additional interest."
Ewald Alphonso"Their 15 percent stake is a fair deal. But I believe we could attract even larger international investments if the EPA is removed. Imagine the potential if Rumburg joined in."
Ewald Alphonso"Of course they'd be a controversial partner, but as long as they hold a minority stake, we shouldn't face any significant issues. Qinal is also showing interest, by the way."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:01:30 p21004 / c527
  • ·"I understand your points, Mr. Alphonso, but we need to consider national security. Inviting multiple foreign powers into Sordland's energy sector could lead to problems down the line."
  • ·"Mr. Alphonso, don't you think we're gambling a bit too much with the future of our energy sector? What if Rumburg decides to use their stake for political leverage?"
  • ▶"This is a bold move. But it could indeed provide a much-needed boost to our economy."
Anton Rayne should acknowledge the bold move but focus on the potential economic benefits to stabilize Sordland's economy.
DeepSeek-R1"This is a bold move. But it could indeed provide a much-needed boost to our economy."
Ewald Alphonso"In my experience, fortune favors the bold. We're not only securing Gasom's future but also placing Sordland on the global energy map."
Ewald Alphonso"Don't forget that Rizia holds only a minority stake and we still have control over Gasom."
Ewald Alphonso"Were Rumburg to get involved, they would be limited to a minority stake as well. I don't think it would pose a problem."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:02:38 p21012 / c826
  • ·"Your confidence is reassuring. However, how can we ensure that Rumburg won't attempt to gain more influence over time?"
  • ·"I share your views. However, I do worry about a potential backlash. The prospect of Rumburg buying a stake in our energy sector might not sit well with the public."
  • ·"I am still concerned about the implications of letting foreign entities have a stake in our energy sector. Isn't there a way to boost our economy without compromising our sovereignty?"
  • ▶"The changes we make to the Energy Protection Act will have long-lasting implications for our nation's future. I can't decide this in a helicopter."
I need time to carefully consider the implications of repealing the Energy Protection Act, especially given its potential impact on national sovereignty and public sentiment.
DeepSeek-R1"The changes we make to the Energy Protection Act will have long-lasting implications for our nation's future. I can't decide this in a helicopter."
Ewald Alphonso"Take all the time you need, Mr. President. These are not decisions to be made lightly."
Ewald Alphonso"I trust your judgement in making the right call for our nation."
NarratorSuddenly, the former president's attention shifted as the pilot announced our landing over the intercom.
Ewald Alphonso"That'll be it then, Mr. President. I urge you to remain seated. These landings can be bumpy."
Serge Wolkner"Thank you again for your assistance with the helicopter, Mr. Alphonso."
Ewald Alphonso"Thank you for your fine company, Mr. Wolkner."
NarratorThe helicopter gradually reduced its speed, hovering for a moment before softly touching down on the helipad.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:03:24 p21059 / c328
  • ·"Good evening, Mr. Alphonso. It was a productive discussion."
  • ·"Good talking to you, Mr. Alphonso."
  • ·"Not bumpy at all. I appreciate the lift."
  • ▶"It was a pleasure, Mr. Alphonso. Goodbye."
A polite farewell maintains professionalism and good relations.
DeepSeek-R1"It was a pleasure, Mr. Alphonso. Goodbye."
Ewald Alphonso"Thank you for a stimulating discussion, Mr. President. I have high hopes for Sordland's future, and for the continuation of the work we began during my presidency."
Ewald Alphonso"Here's to a prosperous future for Sordland, Gasom, and the legacy we leave behind."
Ewald Alphonso"We will be in touch."
NarratorThe ground crew and Presidential Guard hurried to secure the landing site as Serge and I disembarked. Glancing back, I caught a final glimpse of Alphonso inside the helicopter.
NarratorHe winked.
NarratorIn a sudden rush of air and sound, the helicopter lifted off, headed towards the Gasom headquarters in the heart of Lachaven.
NarratorI watched as it disappeared into the cityscape, swallowed by the luminous glow of the Lachaven skyline.

■ Late BriefingT03

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorThe reform committee had intensified work after the negotiations with the Old Guard broke down. Options regarding the new Constitution were already laid out.
NarratorA few minutes later, Lucian and Petr arrived.
Petr Vectern"After what they pulled off I say let's screw them over. We can go all the way with the reforms and shatter the power of the Old Guard."
Petr Vectern"The people chose us to make real change and to do it, we need to gain as much constitutional power to remove the obstructionists from power."
Lucian Galade"Let's not act rash. They have already begun mobilising support against our reform attempts but if we go down the route of removing the Court vote on legislation, it must be thoroughly considered."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:04:29 p20685 / c384
  • ·"The Vice President has a point. It's us versus them now."
  • ▶"Let's consider all of our options carefully."
  • ·"The future lies in a true reform of the constitution. The people elected us for that, let us embrace our purpose."
Considering the risks of alienating key constituencies and the need for stability, it's prudent to evaluate all options before committing to major reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's consider all of our options carefully."
Petr Vectern"True, sorry I got a little hotheaded."
Lucian Galade"The reform committee has presented two options. It's either we take more power to ourselves to beat the Old Guard or we opt for a true reformist agenda to democratise Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:05:18 p20749 / c408
  • ·"Let's consolidate our constitutional power and remove the Old Guard equation."
  • ▶"We should not be blinded by hatred of foes. Our true purpose will be real reform for the future of this nation."
Focusing on true reforms maintains political balance and delivers on economic goals.
DeepSeek-R1"We should not be blinded by hatred of foes. Our true purpose will be real reform for the future of this nation."
Petr Vectern"Very well if that is your decision. We will need to engage further with the progressive wing."
Lucian Galade"If we can reach an agreement with the reformists, we will be very strong in the Assembly to propose such change."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:06:06 p20752 / c404
  • ·"Can we reach a two-thirds majority in the Assembly?"
  • ▶"Is it possible to pass the Supreme Court?"
Focusing on passing the Supreme Court ensures legal backing for reforms, bypassing potential Assembly gridlock.
DeepSeek-R1"Is it possible to pass this through the Supreme Court?"
Lucian Galade"They are the biggest obstacle. Even if we pass the Assembly, the Chief Justice will do his best to block our amendments."
Lucian Galade"We need at least 6 of the 11 judges to vote for the proposal. But Mr. Hawker has enormous influence over the judges."
Petr Vectern"We should reach out to the judges in secret. Some of them might be willing to break rank to support us."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:06:55 p20930 / c415
  • ·"All means are to be considered in order to succeed."
  • ▶"They will surely support a just cause for the country and its future."
Choosing to emphasize a just cause for the country's future maintains alignment with reformist goals and avoids alienating potential allies.
DeepSeek-R1"They will surely support a just cause for the country and its future."
NarratorLucian looked sceptical.
Lucian Galade"According to the Constitution, Nia is a member of the Supreme Court, which means we already have one vote. I am sure she will back us even after what happened, her moral compass is too strong."
Lucian Galade"Two of the judges are friends of hers, and of similar opinion about the reforms. Consider them onboard too."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:07:41 p20925 / c317
  • ·"So, we only have 3 votes out of 11?"
  • ▶"Who else would be willing to support reforms?"
  • ·"I'm sure the rest can be bribed."
Exploring additional judicial support is crucial for navigating the constitutional reform challenge.
DeepSeek-R1"Who else would be willing to support reforms?"
Lucian Galade"I think Judge Edmonds can be persuaded. And in extension, Mr. Dalton and Mr. Martin."
Lucian Galade"They weren't really blinded by the Sollist ideology."
Petr Vectern"Yes, those three don't seem to be working directly with the Chief Justice Hawker. If we can get to Isabel Edmonds, we might get the 6 votes needed. That's our best bet."
"Can we reach a two-thirds majority in the Assembly?"
DeepSeek-R1"Can we reach a two-thirds majority in the Assembly?"
Lucian Galade"If you can get Frens Ricter to work with you on this, our only problem would be uniting our own party. We'll never be able to get NFP to work with us."
Lucian Galade"We need the majority of our 130 seats in the Assembly to vote in favour. With PFJP's full support of 70 votes, we will easily surpass the needed 166."
Lucian Galade"But it will be tough to unite the USP with all the divisive wings that have formed. You must make them follow you loyally."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:08:35 p21341 / c531
  • ·"We did it before for the elections. We can do it again."
  • ▶"We will convince the leads."
Anton Rayne should choose option 1 to maintain flexibility and build consensus with reformist leaders, allowing him to navigate internal party divisions while keeping his options open for future negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"We will convince the leads."
Petr Vectern"Albin Clavin and his reformist wing would be supporting you. He already weighed in heavily in the committee meetings."
Petr Vectern"However, the conservatives would be tough to break. The influence of Soll is still living strong in the party."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:09:21 p21352 / c332
  • ·"We will eventually remove his influence."
  • ▶"How many votes do they have?"
  • ·"Do you think they can be persuaded?"
I need to know how many votes we're dealing with before making a strategic move.
DeepSeek-R1"How many votes do they have in total?"
Petr Vectern"The conservatives have around 40 seats. There are many unaligned people and some of your "loyalists" might decide to show their true faces too."
Petr Vectern"So we need as many of the conservatives to support us. And there is only one person who is key to make them back us."
Lucian Galade"Gloria Tory. She's well respected by the Sollists. She'll be our biggest obstacle in the Assembly."
Lucian Galade"I also have reasons to think she might be in Orso's pocket. She will be tough to break, but we'll need her help to get the conservative support."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:10:23 p21465 / c655
  • ·"What are the demands?"
  • ▶"Let's go through the draft."
To maintain political momentum and support for reforms, I'll engage with the draft to show leadership in addressing cultural preservation despite budget constraints.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's go through the draft details."
Lucian Galade"Of course."
Lucian Galade"We'll go through each of the nine points of the draft, and get your input. Mrs. Morgna will explain their demands as we go through them."
Lucian Galade"We'll start with Section 1. The new Constitution will fix the loopholes regarding the president's absolute veto powers."
Lucian Galade"Even though Mr. Ricter put on a whole show about why vetoes should completely be removed, the committee has almost unanimously agreed on fixing the loopholes, thus limiting presidential vetoes."
Lucian Galade"My suggestion is to add a clause detailing the methods the Assembly may override a veto with some sort of a super-majority. One person should never be able to block the rest."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:11:14 p21575 / c425
  • ·"I stand in agreement with Mr. Ricter. The vetoes should be removed."
  • ▶"The loopholes give the President too much power."
  • ·"They will not be allowed to dictate the negotiations."
Acknowledging the need to limit presidential power while retaining a check against Assembly decisions.
DeepSeek-R1"The loopholes give the President too much power which could be misused."
Lucian Galade"Whatever you decide it's important to get a balanced picture. We will be making history by changing the '29 Constitution of Soll."
Lucian Galade"On to Section 2. The new Constitution will remove the Supreme Court out of the constitutional amendment process."
Petr Vectern"This is yet another point that was unanimously agreed on by all parties. Tarquin Soll established this rule to protect his '29 Constitution. We all know most of the judges are his sheep."
Petr Vectern"Constitutional amendments will still be very difficult to accomplish, particularly because we will keep the two-thirds vote system to be able to propose a change. However, the judiciary will not have the final say in the amendments."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:12:05 p21564 / c480
  • ·"This is already what we wanted to do."
  • ·"I can't disagree with that."
  • ▶"We need the Court to even pass this change. Why would they support this?"
  • ·"And what good does that do to us?"
President Rayne questions whether the Supreme Court would support their reforms, highlighting the challenge of gaining judicial approval.
DeepSeek-R1"We need the Court to even pass this change. Why on earth would they support this?"
Petr Vectern"Most of them won't. But we already talked about my vote and a possible agreement with Isabel which may change things."
Lucian Galade"Moving on to Section 3. This is about the impeachment process of the President. Currently, in cases of high treason and breaking the Constitution, the Supreme Court may impeach and try the President."
Petr Vectern"Most reformists want the impeachment to be initiated by the Assembly. They think they do not have any power over potential rogue presidents that would abuse their power."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:12:51 p21595 / c327
  • ·"Not sure if it is correct to chastise the President this much though."
  • ▶"The Assembly should have oversight over the President."
The Assembly should have oversight over the President to ensure accountability and prevent abuse of power.
DeepSeek-R1"The Assembly should have oversight over the President, it is the most democratic institution."
Petr Vectern"This law is particularly dangerous for you in this case. We will be going against the Old Guard, and if we prove to be successful in the Assembly..."
Petr Vectern"...They will be looking for anything they can declare unconstitutional to tackle you. All they need is the Supreme Court to impeach you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:13:40 p21468 / c419
  • ·"What if we don't succeed? The Court can have me impeached for this."
  • ▶"We should define a better impeachment process."
  • ·"We could even remove impeachment altogether."
Improving the impeachment process ensures democratic oversight while maintaining accountability through structured procedures.
DeepSeek-R1"We should define a more refined impeachment process."
Petr Vectern"Yes, we will be collaboratively working on the specifics."
Lucian Galade"Section 4."
Lucian Galade"This is regarding the appointment of ministers. Currently, the President is free to appoint anyone from the members of the Assembly. Nobody else is involved with the process."
Petr Vectern"There is definitely a demand to involve the Assembly in the process."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:14:37 p21450 / c586
  • ·"That's going a bit too far."
  • ▶"We could introduce a confidence vote."
  • ·"I agree. We should involve the Assembly more in the process."
Introducing a confidence vote allows Anton to maintain control while addressing reformist demands, balancing stability and political strategy.
DeepSeek-R1"We could introduce a confidence vote."
Lucian Galade"That was my suggestion as well."
Petr Vectern"I'm sure the reformists would completely support that."
Lucian Galade"The fifth section is about our infamous electoral threshold. Most reformists want to lower the threshold as much as possible."
Petr Vectern"Mr. Ricter and his PFJP definitely wants to see change on this topic. They find our 10% threshold to be antidemocratic. Which is something I disagree with."
Petr Vectern"Yes, but this is a delicate matter. All the party, and I mean, not even Albin wants us to decrease the threshold. They think that... this would destroy our party in the long term."
Lucian Galade"Not wrong. The ten percent threshold is what that allowed us to control half of the Assembly with around one third of the majority in the elections."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:15:34 p21594 / c601
  • ▶"But the high threshold is the reason why Sordish politics has become so isolated."
  • ·"We should definitely work to decrease the threshold for better representation."
  • ·"Do you think it would really hurt the party?"
  • ·"This is out of the question."
Maintaining the high electoral threshold helps preserve political stability and prevents fragmentation, crucial for managing current tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"But the high threshold is the reason why Sordish politics has become isolated to this degree."
Petr Vectern"You're right. Even after Soll's resignation, we still have three parties in the Assembly. Even Mr. Kibener's NFP was barely elected."
Lucian Galade"Don't forget that the Socialist party and the Worker's Party of Bludia both had close to ten percent vote in the last election."
Lucian Galade"You would be opening their way into the parliament in the future if you were to decrease the threshold. It is important that you realize this."
Lucian Galade"Of course that would also mean that USP might lose its majority in the Assembly, sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:16:18 p21645 / c259
  • ·"If our own democracy is the reason of our downfall, we will have to accept it."
  • ·"Don't worry. With these reforms, we will be even more popular."
  • ▶"We can't just give away our majority like that."
  • ·"We can't allow communists to enter the Assembly."
Lowering the electoral threshold risks losing the USP's majority in the Assembly, which could destabilize governance and make it harder to implement crucial reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"We can't just give away our majority like that."
Lucian Galade"I know it is a delicate matter, sir. But you still have time to think about these."
NarratorLucian continued with the next section.
Lucian Galade"Section 6. This is about the presidential decrees. Reformists demand more limitations to the President's executive powers. Mr. Ricter has demanded them to be completely removed."
Lucian Galade"I have suggested to bring some limitations to decrees regarding their applicability just for arguments sake. While we keep them, we should try to make sure that they will only be used in emergency situations."
Petr Vectern"We could also make it dependent on an enabling act from the Assembly. Sure, it would make the whole process longer but as long as we have a majority, nothing really changes."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:17:11 p21710 / c457
  • ▶"I don't think presidential decrees need any reforms."
  • ·"We'll work with the reformists to limit the use of decrees."
  • ·"I may need to think a bit longer about this."
Presidential decrees are essential for swift economic stabilization, which is critical given the current instability.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't think presidential decrees need any reforms."
Lucian Galade"Me neither. I think it's important that the President can act quickly on matters of national importance."
Lucian Galade"Next one is Section 7. Term limits."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:18:06 p21746 / c555
  • ·"Uh-oh."
  • ·"I don't aim to leave any time soon. We keep the unlimited terms."
  • ▶"Term limits are necessary."
  • ·"Term Limits?"
Term limits are necessary to prevent excessive concentration of power and promote democratic renewal.
DeepSeek-R1"Term limits are necessary."
Petr Vectern"I agree. We can't allow somebody like Soll holding unto his seat for two decades again."
Lucian Galade"Hey, if people keep electing one person, that means they really are content. The masses are scared of change."
Petr Vectern"Even Soll knew he had to leave, but we will leave it to the President to decide."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:18:57 p21749 / c428
  • ·"Yes, he did. But it took a long time."
  • ▶"I agree. People should be able to elect the same person if they want to."
  • ·"I will take my time and seriously consider the options before settling on one."
Considering the need for stability and respecting the public's choice, it's better to avoid imposing term limits.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree. People should be able to elect the same person if they want to."
Lucian Galade"Now I'll move unto the 8th Section. We are reaching the end."
Lucian Galade"It's the question about the immunity of the Judges. While the Anti-Old Guard sentiment is increasing, there are talks that taking away their immunity would give us authority over judiciary."
Petr Vectern"I do not support taking away their power for yourself, Mr. President. The judiciary must be independent and long-lasting."
Petr Vectern"While I agree that the current court members are *problematic*, this is no way to resolve it. The law is supposed to ensure the judiciary's independence by giving legal protection so they can't be pressured."
Lucian Galade"Well, they need absolute immunity in theory. But look at what reality got us? We've got to deal with the most corrupt Chief Justice in the world, with no chance to deal with him!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:19:51 p21874 / c488
  • ·"We should respect their immunity."
  • ·"They should be able to be impeached, why do I get to be impeached but not them?"
  • ·"We should crush the Old Guards to clear ways for further reforms."
  • ▶"Maybe we shouldn't make the Supreme Court our enemies just yet."
Avoiding conflict with the Supreme Court allows focus on economic stabilization while maintaining political stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Maybe we shouldn't make the Supreme Court our enemies just yet. Let's keep the reforms light."
Lucian Galade"That is a better strategy. We'll need to sway some of their members to succeed after all."
Lucian Galade"And the last one is the question about Soll's legacy. Basically his *Member of Honor* law. It gave him absolute immunity even when he's retired. He is a permanent member of the Assembly as well, but luckily he doesn't really attend anymore."
Lucian Galade"According to the Constitution, the Member of Honor not only has legal immunity but the state also covers most of their expenses as well as providing security at all times. It can only be given to someone by an absolute majority vote in the Assembly and the approval of the President."
Petr Vectern"That's why only Tarquin Soll has that title. He gave it to himself right after his military coup. This law is basically made to save the Colonel and it has a symbolic importance for Sordland because of it."
Petr Vectern"We must get rid of this senseless law that protects him. He should be able to face justice for the atrocities that had taken place during his rule."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:20:39 p21985 / c321
  • ·"I agree, we should abolish this position."
  • ·"No, I think we should respect this law."
  • ▶"We could keep the title but take away the immunity clause."
  • ·"Every President should gain that title after their term."
Striking a balance between respecting historical figures and promoting justice while maintaining political stability.
DeepSeek-R1"We could keep the title but take away the immunity clause."
Petr Vectern"That would be enough to hold him accountable for his actions but why would you want to keep such a title, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:21:50 p21985 / c883
  • ·"Regardless of him, I see no problem with having a special title for great heroes."
  • ▶"It's just a title."
Removing the title is a strong statement against Soll's authoritarian past, aligning with democratic reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"It's just a title."
Petr Vectern"We will need to work together to push this agenda."
Lucian Galade"Now we have a better picture of your intentions, sir. I will start reaching out to my contacts to make a draft that works for you."
Petr Vectern"I'll present your input to the committee. In a few months, you would have the draft on your desk to make the final changes yourself."
Lucian Galade"I think this was yet another fruitful meeting, sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:22:47 p22016 / c489
  • ▶"Your service is appreciated."
  • ·"If we do this, we will be remembered as heroes."
  • ·"Together we will democratize Sordland."
  • ·"Thanks for the meeting, everybody."
Maintaining good relations with advisors is key for navigating complex political landscapes.
DeepSeek-R1"Your service is appreciated."
Lucian Galade"Thank you for the kind words, sir."
Petr Vectern"You can count on us, Anton."
Lucian Galade"Since we have arrived at the same page with Ms. Morgna again I will reach out to her to for guidance on the draft constitution work. She has principles and wouldn't go against her own code out of previous disagreements."
Lucian Galade"Anyway, I think this is where we'll call it, sir. Have a good day."

■ Decision Stability OrderT03

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
EventStability Order of 1954: An investigation on the influence of the Young Sords and Red Youth organizations on the post-assassination unrest has concluded. Neither youth group has officially organized or endorsed illegal actions, but several low- to mid-level leaders from both have links to inciting incidents that resulted in deaths, injuries and instability. This provides us with legal grounds to ban both organizations.
EVENT DeepSeek-R108:24:13 p21477 / c680
  • ·Ban the Young Sords
  • ·Ban the Red Youth
  • ▶Ban the Red Youth and the Young Sords
  • ·Don't ban either
Banning both groups addresses the immediate threat of instability and violence, supporting economic recovery while showing decisive leadership.

■ Reformist ConstitutionT03

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorI was walking to my office, since today was the day to edit and make the final changes for the proposal to change the Constitution of Sordland.
NarratorThe reform committee finally bore tangible results and the draft of the changes were about to be presented to me.
NarratorI walked in the marble corridors of the Palace, thinking about the huge decisions I was going to take.
NarratorAt the entrance to my office, my secretary Livia greeted me.
Livia Suno"Good morning, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:25:00 p21535 / c241

Livia Suno"Sorry to hear."
NarratorShe paused before taking my coat. It looked like she had something on her mind.
Livia Suno"Mr. Rayne -- can you give me a hint about your reform plans? If you ask me, taking away the judges' immunity is long overdue, but it might anger a lot of people in these halls..."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:26:44 p21495 / c336

Lucian Galade"Well, apart from the first two sections of decreasing the Supreme Court's authority and limiting the presidential vetoes... Nothing else has been agreed on unanimously."
Lucian Galade"We could start talking about Section 3. This is about the impeachment process of the President. Currently, in cases of high treason and breaking the Constitution, the Supreme Court may impeach and try the President."
Nia Morgna"Most reformists want the impeachment to be initiated by the Assembly. They think they do not have any power over potential rogue presidents that would abuse their power."

Nia Morgna"Yes, we will be collaboratively working on the specifics."
Lucian Galade"Section 4."
Lucian Galade"This is regarding the appointment of ministers. Currently, the President is free to appoint anyone from the members of the Assembly. Nobody else is involved with the process."
Nia Morgna"There is definitely a demand to involve the Assembly in the process."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:31:00 p21411 / c391
  • ·"That's going a bit too far."
  • ▶"We could introduce a confidence vote."
  • ·"I agree. We should involve the Assembly more in the process."
Introducing a confidence vote ensures democratic oversight without fully transferring appointment power.
DeepSeek-R1"We could introduce a confidence vote."
Nia Morgna"That was my suggestion as well."
Petr Vectern"I'm sure the reformists would completely support that."
Lucian Galade"The fifth section is about our infamous electoral threshold. Most reformists want to lower the threshold as much as possible."
Nia Morgna"Mr. Ricter and his PFJP definitely wants to see change on this topic. They find our 10% threshold to be antidemocratic. Which is something I also agree with."
Petr Vectern"Yes, but this is a delicate matter. The party, and I mean, not even Albin wants us to change the threshold. They think that... this would destroy our party in the long term."
Lucian Galade"Not wrong. The ten percent threshold is what that allowed us to control half of the Assembly with around one third of the majority in the elections."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:31:53 p21391 / c509
  • ·"But the high threshold is the reason why Sordish politics has become so isolated."
  • ·"We should definitely work to decrease the threshold for better representation."
  • ▶"Do you think it would really hurt the party?"
  • ·"This is out of the question."
Lowering the threshold could risk the USP's majority and political stability, which is crucial for governance and re-election.
DeepSeek-R1"Do you think it would really hurt the party?"
Nia Morgna"I think this is a national matter, Mr. Rayne. Not a partisan one."
Petr Vectern"It is a matter of both when we have the Communist Party and the Workers Party of Bludia getting more than five percent vote in the last elections."
Petr Vectern"Decreasing the threshold might mean opening their way into the Assembly."
Lucian Galade"Of course that would also mean that USP might lose its majority in the Assembly, sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:32:49 p21316 / c552
  • ·"If our own democracy is the reason of our downfall, we will have to accept it."
  • ·"Don't worry. With these reforms, we will be even more popular."
  • ▶"We can't just give away our majority like that."
  • ·"We can't allow communists to enter the Assembly."
Maintaining the current electoral threshold preserves USP's majority, crucial for implementing economic reforms without opposition interference.
DeepSeek-R1"We can't just give away our majority like that."
Lucian Galade"I know it is a delicate matter, sir. But you still have time to think about these."
NarratorLucian continued with the next section.
Lucian Galade"Section 6. This is about the presidential decrees. Reformists demand more limitations to the President's executive powers. Mr. Ricter has demanded them to be completely removed."
Nia Morgna"I have suggested to bring some limitations to decrees regarding their applicability. While we keep them, we should try to make sure that they will only be used in emergency situations."
Petr Vectern"We could also make it dependent on an enabling act from the Assembly. Sure, it would make the whole process longer but as long as we have a majority, nothing really changes."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:33:35 p21336 / c323
  • ·"I don't think presidential decrees need any reforms."
  • ▶"We'll work with the reformists to limit the use of decrees."
  • ·"I may need to think a bit longer about this."
Balancing executive power with checks is a pragmatic approach that maintains efficiency while addressing reformist concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"We'll work with the reformists to limit the use of decrees."
Petr Vectern"Frens will probably demand that we remove them completely. But he forgets that Sordland is a Presidential Republic."
Lucian Galade"Next one is Section 7. Term limits."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:34:39 p21282 / c756

  • ▶"I don't aim to leave any time soon. We keep the unlimited terms."
  • ·"Term limits are necessary."
  • ·"Term Limits?"
Disagreeing with term limits maintains governance stability.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't aim to leave any time soon. We keep the unlimited terms."
Lucian Galade"I agree, we could keep that in."
Petr Vectern"Hey, if people keep electing one person, that means they really are content. The masses are scared of change."
Lucian Galade"Even Soll knew he had to leave, but we will leave it to the President to decide."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:35:33 p21310 / c518
  • ·"Yes, he did. But it took a long time."
  • ▶"I agree. People should be able to elect the same person if they want to."
  • ·"I will take my time and seriously consider the options before settling on one."
Maintaining unlimited terms aligns with Anton's goal of winning re-election and keeping political stability.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree. People should be able to elect the same person if they want to."
Lucian Galade"Now I'll move unto the 8th Section. We are reaching the end."
Lucian Galade"It's the question about the immunity of the Judges. While the Anti-Old Guard sentiment is increasing, there are talks that taking away their immunity would give us authority over judiciary."
Nia Morgna"I do not support taking away their power for yourself, Mr. President. The judiciary must be independent and long-lasting."
Nia Morgna"While I agree that the current court members are *problematic*, this is no way to resolve it. The law is supposed to ensure the judiciary's independence by giving legal protection so they can't be pressured."
Petr Vectern"Well, they need absolute immunity in theory. But look at what reality got us? We've got to deal with the most corrupt Chief Justice in the world, with no chance to deal with him!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:36:31 p21422 / c648
  • ·"We should respect their immunity."
  • ·"They should be able to be impeached. Why do I get to be impeached but not them?"
  • ·"We should crush the Old Guards to clear ways for further reforms."
  • ▶"Maybe we shouldn't make the Supreme Court our enemies just yet."
Avoiding conflict with the Supreme Court preserves judicial cooperation and supports constitutional reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"Maybe we shouldn't make the Supreme Court our enemies just yet. Let's keep the reforms light."
Lucian Galade"That is a better strategy. We'll need to sway some of their members to succeed after all."
Lucian Galade"And the last one is the question about Soll's legacy. Basically his *Member of Honor* law. It gave him absolute immunity even when he's retired. He is a permanent member of the Assembly as well, but luckily he doesn't really attend anymore."
Lucian Galade"According to the Constitution, the Member of Honor not only has legal immunity but the state also covers most of their expenses as well as providing security at all times. It can only be given to someone by an absolute majority vote in the Assembly and the approval of the President."
Petr Vectern"That's why only Tarquin Soll has that title. He gave it to himself right after his military coup. This law is basically made to save the Colonel and it has a symbolic importance for Sordland because of it."
Nia Morgna"We must get rid of this senseless law that protects him. He should be able to face justice for the atrocities that had taken place during his rule."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:37:20 p21576 / c365
  • ·"I agree, we should abolish this position."
  • ·"No, I think we should respect this law."
  • ▶"We could keep the title but take away the immunity clause."
  • ·"Every President should gain that title after their term."
Keeping the title while removing immunity balances reformist demands with political pragmatism.
DeepSeek-R1"We could keep the title but take away the immunity clause."
Nia Morgna"That would be enough to hold him accountable for his actions but why would you want to keep such a title, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:38:02 p21577 / c247
  • ·"Regardless of him, I see no problem with having a special title for great heroes."
  • ▶"It's just a title, Mrs. Morgna."
Keeping the title while removing immunity balances historical recognition with accountability.
DeepSeek-R1"It's just a title, Mrs. Morgna."
Petr Vectern"Well, regardless of what Mr. Rayne decides, we will need to work together to push this agenda."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:38:45 p21472 / c232
  • ▶“Let's start working on the draft.”
  • ·“I thank you all for your input.”
Starting work on the draft will move the constitutional reform forward, aligning with the goals laid out in the manifesto.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's start working on the draft."
NarratorAfter placing the committee's report and my own notes in front of me, I buried myself in the papers.
Policy decisionSection I: Presidential Vetoes: According to Article 77 & 82, the President may veto a bill by returning it to the Assembly with a written statement. The articles do not contain any information on how to override a veto. As a consequence, the President has an "absolute" veto power that cannot even be overriden with a supermajority in the Grand Assembly of Sordland.
POLICY DeepSeek-R108:39:41 p21498 / c554
  • ·Remove Vetoes
  • ▶Limit Vetoes (3/5 Majority Can Override)
Limiting vetoes to require a 3/5 majority override strikes a balance between maintaining presidential authority and allowing democratic processes. This approach supports the economic stabilization goals by enabling quick legislative action while retaining some control to block harmful policies, aligning with the reformist agenda without fully ceding executive power.
Policy decisionSection III: Impeachment of the President: According to Article 17, the President of Sordland is not responsible for the actions performed in the exercise of presidential duties, except in the case of high treason or violation of the Constitution. In such cases, the President may be impeached by the Supreme Court in joint session, with an absolute majority of its members. Therefore, the impeachment process only includes the Supreme Court and it gives them great power over the executive.
POLICY DeepSeek-R108:40:27 p21581 / c282
  • ▶The Assembly and the Supreme Court may impeach with majority in both
  • ·The President may not be impeached
  • ·Do not change
To balance executive oversight with democratic checks, allowing both the Assembly and Supreme Court to participate in impeachment ensures accountability while preventing judicial overreach.
Policy decisionSection IV: Appointment of Ministers: According to the Constitution, the President appoints the ministers to the Council of Ministers from elected members of the Assembly. We may introduce a confidence vote by adding the line: "The President may nominate ministers which will require a confidence vote from the Grand National Assembly for their appointment," or include the Assembly more in the process with: "The Grand National Assembly may nominate a list of ministers, which the President may appoint."
POLICY DeepSeek-R108:41:25 p21683 / c625
  • ·Confidence Vote Is Required
  • ·The Assembly Nominates the Ministers
  • ▶Do not change
Maintaining the current system keeps decision-making swift and ensures alignment with the President's agenda, crucial for implementing economic stabilization policies.
Policy decisionSection V: Electoral Threshold: According to Article 50, a political party needs a minimum of 10% of the total national vote to win seats in the Grand National Assembly. The votes of the parties who pass the threshold are re-distributed proportionally, meaning USP, which currently holds the lead, would be the main beneficiary. Decreasing the threshold may allow the Workers Party of Bludia and the Communist Party of Sordland to be elected in the next elections.
POLICY DeepSeek-R108:42:20 p21747 / c523
  • ·Decrease threshold to 8%
  • ·Decrease threshold to 3%
  • ▶Do not change
Maintaining the current electoral threshold preserves political stability and the USP's majority, crucial for effective governance and re-election goals. Lowering it risks fragmenting the Assembly and complicating future policy-making.
Policy decisionSection VI: Presidential Decrees: According to Article 18 and 51, the President is able to issue decrees on political, social, and economic issues that would carry the force of law: they could not contradict the constitution and are subject to judicial review. Therefore, the President may issue decrees in many subjects without going through the Grand National Assembly. However, the Assembly may pass legislation on the same subjects to override presidential decrees. We may include the Assembly in the process by requiring an enabling act from the Assembly to be able to enact decrees or remove them completely, making the Assembly the sole lawmaking authority.
POLICY DeepSeek-R108:43:11 p21860 / c463
  • ▶Decrees require an Enabling Act
  • ·Remove presidential decrees
  • ·Do not change
Requiring an enabling act for presidential decrees balances the need for swift action with necessary legislative oversight, preventing potential executive overreach while maintaining some flexibility in decision-making.
Policy decisionSection VII: Term Limits: According to the Constitution, the President shall be elected to serve for four years. However, there is no mention of a term limit, allowing one individual to be able to run, be elected and serve as President without limits.
POLICY DeepSeek-R108:44:06 p21896 / c525
  • ·Introduce a limit of 2 terms
  • ▶Do not change
Introducing a term limit could divert attention from immediate economic concerns and weaken Anton's ability to push through necessary reforms. Keeping it allows him to maintain focus on his agenda and potentially secure re-election if the public supports his efforts.
Policy decisionSection VIII: Immunity of Justices: The Constitution gives the Justices of the Supreme Court absolute immunity before the law and describes no procedure of impeachment, making them untouchable by the other branches of government. We may describe an impeachment procedure by giving the Grand National Assembly the right to propose an impeachment of a Justice on the legal grounds of unconstitutional behaviour.
POLICY DeepSeek-R108:45:04 p21891 / c588
  • ·Justices may be impeached by the Assembly
  • ▶Do not change
Removing the immunity of Justices could lead to political instability and weaken the President's position, diverting focus from economic recovery. Maintaining their immunity ensures judicial independence while exploring alternative methods for accountability.
Policy decisionSection IX: "Member of Honor" Rights of Tarquin Soll: Article 99 of the Constitution defines the "Member of Honor" title, and the rights their members may exercise. A Member of Honor has absolute immunity and is considered to be a permanent member of the Grand National Assembly. They are given control of their own personal security team, provided by the state’s Presidential Guards. A member is also eligible to live in a special private residence provided by the State for free. The only person who holds the title is Tarquin Soll.
POLICY DeepSeek-R108:45:53 p22023 / c333
  • ·Abolish the "Member of Honor" title
  • ·Every president becomes a Member of Honor after their service
  • ▶Remove the immunity of the Member of Honor
  • ·Do not change
Removing the immunity clause would allow Soll to face justice while keeping the symbolic title, balancing reformist demands with practical considerations.
NarratorAfter I made the final changes for the proposal, I showed it to my team.
NarratorThey read the changes and discussed it amongst themselves.
NarratorLucian spoke afterwards.
Lucian Galade"Looks like the amendments are ready to be proposed."
Petr Vectern"But I have to say this too..."
Petr Vectern"Not reforming the minister laws will be frowned upon by the party. The Assembly members have insisted on getting involved in the process."
Lucian Galade"That's true. It will be tough to get all of their support. Some of our members may try to openly fight against this."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:46:40 p22111 / c309
  • ·"We'll figure it out."
  • ▶"I will make sure the party follows me."
  • ·"I'm prepared to reconsider if it comes to that."
Emphasizing party unity and strong leadership will help maintain control and support during critical economic times.

Lucian Galade"We will all do our best to make it work, sir."
Lucian Galade"Any other comments before we proceed?"
Petr Vectern"I don't think I have any problems with the changes."
Nia Morgna"Me neither."
Lucian Galade"Looks like we can proceed immediately."
Nia Morgna"I will personally work on the wording of this proposal with my hand-picked legal experts."
Lucian Galade"With Ms. Morgna's reputation as the most experienced attorney, this proposal will be bulletproof. Chief Justice Orso will have a hard time convincing others to oppose it."
Nia Morgna"In the meantime, you must introduce this proposal to our party. You will need to get them to back us if we want it to pass the assembly."
Lucian Galade"Sir, I advise you to first talk to the leaders of both conservative and reformist wings of the party."
Petr Vectern"As far as I'm aware, Mr. Clavin supports the reforms already. Mrs. Tory will be the real problem. She became the real heavy-weight after we left for the executive."

■ Bill Green Gruni DevT03

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
Bill for decisionGruni Regeneration and Ecological Economic Nurturing — The G.R.E.E.N. has been proposed by the Assembly, aiming to encourage a more sustainable approach to industry in Gruni. Section I of this Act would impose stricter regulations on industrial pollution and waste disposal, particularly in the Gruni region. Businesses not complying with these new standards may face hefty fines, the revenue from which would be invested back into environmental recovery projects. Section II proposes to offer tax breaks to businesses that can demonstrate a significant reduction in their environmental impact over a period of time. A small investment fund is created for eco-friendly business practices. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R108:50:14 p22161 / c566
  • ·Sign: Gruni Regeneration and Ecological Economic Nurturing
  • ▶Veto: Gruni Regeneration and Ecological Economic Nurturing
Given the current unstable economy and limited government budget, prioritizing immediate economic stabilization over additional environmental regulations is necessary to maintain public support and ensure fiscal responsibility.

■ Energy Protection ActT03

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorAs the morning sun streamed through the window of the Presidential Office, an imminent meeting loomed on my agenda. In a quarter of an hour, I was set to discuss the Energy Protection Act of 1932 with Ministers Graf, Holl, and Wisci.
NarratorThe recent abrogation in the Grand National Assembly, coupled with ongoing dialogues with Gasom, had only served to amplify the discourse surrounding the legislation.
NarratorI was sipping my morning coffee and enjoying a quiet moment of solitude when Deivid arrived, characteristically early.
Deivid Wisci"A very good morning to you, Mr. President. I trust I'm not imposing by arriving ahead of schedule?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:51:33 p22351 / c737
  • ▶"Not at all, Deivid. On the contrary, we've now got an extra few minutes to strategize before the meeting."
  • ·"Given the serious topic we're addressing, an early start might be just what we need."
  • ·Gesture towards the seat.
An early start allows for thorough strategizing before the critical meeting.
DeepSeek-R1"Not at all, Deivid. On the contrary, we've now got an extra few minutes to strategize before the meeting."
Deivid Wisci"I'm relieved to hear that, Anton."
Deivid Wisci"I depart earlier for my appointments nowadays, as the stairs of the capital have become more of a challenge than I would like to admit."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:52:19 p22536 / c275
  • ·“It's nothing to be ashamed of, Deivid. Would you like some coffee or tea?”
  • ·"Well, Deivid, it's not the stairs we're battling, but the relentless march of time."
  • ·“The Maroon District and the ministerial buildings are dispersed in a very artificial way. No wonder it takes longer.”
  • ▶"I'd like to clear my mind before the ministers arrive."
It sets a professional tone and focuses on preparation for the upcoming discussion.
DeepSeek-R1"I'd like to clear my mind before the ministers arrive."
Deivid Wisci"As you wish, Mr. President. I will be as silent as a mouse."
NarratorIt was nice to sit in silence and enjoy each other's company. Time passed.
NarratorThen the door swung open, ushering in the arrival of the other ministers. Symon Holl entered first with a confident stride.
Symon Holl"Good morning, Mr. President. Deivid. I trust the discussion is progressing well?"
NarratorLileas followed closely behind. She walked in with an air of calm authority, her gaze sweeping across the room.
Lileas Graf"Gentlemen. I hope I'm not interrupting anything crucial."
Deivid Wisci"Welcome, everyone. It's great to see you all here. We have some important discussions on the agenda today, particularly concerning the Energy Protection Act."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:53:37 p22522 / c911
  • ▶"Thank you, Deivid. I appreciate the urgency. Let's get right to the heart of the matter."
  • ·"Indeed, it's a critical issue that we need to address. I look forward to hearing everyone's perspectives."
  • ·"Proceed."
[ no reasoning recorded ]
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Deivid. I appreciate the urgency. Let's get right to the heart of the matter."
Symon Holl"Today, we have the opportunity to shape the economic strategy of Sordland for decades to come. The EPA has long stood as a key contention and debate point of the National Autarky Plan."
Symon Holl"This matter is a discussion of how Sordland sees itself and how we position ourselves in the world. Our nation has taken very different economic approaches in the last four decades."
Symon Holl"The President's decision to foster a planned economy provides us with significant insight into our policy direction."
Symon Holl"Even if we manage to stabilize our economy, loosening the EPA regulations could still be beneficial."
Symon Holl"By creating an attractive environment for foreign investors, we can ensure that Sordland is not merely surviving, but thriving in the long term."
Lileas Graf"A planned economy does not necessarily mean restricting foreign investment. It's about judicious management and control, ensuring that our nation's interests are always at the forefront."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:54:29 p22705 / c400
  • ▶"I've always admired your devotion to Sordland, Lileas. It's clear that you have our nation's interests at heart."
  • ·"While I respect your stance, Lileas, I must disagree. Despite my current support for it, I believe a planned economy could also hurt Sordland in the long run."
  • ·"You present a compelling argument, Lileas. It's certainly an aspect we must consider moving forward."
Complimenting Lileas Graf on her dedication to Sordland helps maintain a positive relationship and shows respect for her viewpoint.
DeepSeek-R1"I've always admired your devotion to Sordland, Lileas. It's clear that you have our nation's interests at heart."
Lileas Graf"I appreciate your kind words, Mr. President. It's our shared dedication to Sordland that drives us."
Symon Holl"Mr. President, Lileas, shall we return to the subject of the EPA?"
Symon Holl"Our current version of the Energy Protection Act dates back to 1949. President Alphonso's amendment raised the cap on foreign investment in Sordish energy companies up to 20 percent."
Symon Holl"In my view, it was a necessary step to attract foreign investment and stimulate our economy."
Symon Holl"We must remember that Sordland was facing an economic crisis during his tenure, and these changes provided a lifeline for our energy industries."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:55:25 p22878 / c522
  • ·"Wasn't this change a sign of desperation rather than a strategic move?"
  • ·"But did the increased foreign ownership actually lead to substantial economic growth?"
  • ▶"I must confess, when the changes to the Energy Protection Act were first implemented, I harbored serious reservations."
  • ·"I share your view. Soll's old policy was choking the growth of Sordish energy businesses."
Anton Rayne expresses personal reservations about the EPA changes, encouraging deeper analysis and maintaining a neutral stance to gather more information.
DeepSeek-R1"I must confess, when the changes to the Energy Protection Act were first implemented, I harbored serious reservations."
Deivid Wisci"Your reservations were not unfounded. It was indeed a significant change and, as with any policy shift, it brought about its share of controversies and criticisms."
Deivid Wisci"However, it's crucial to remember that policy-making often involves balancing competing interests and making tough decisions. The goal was, and remains, to advance Sordland's economy while protecting our strategic interests."
Lileas Graf"The Assembly has spoken on this matter, and as it stands, the Energy Protection Act of 1932 will not be repealed. It's clear that our colleagues have recognized the potential risks of exposing our energy sector to foreign ownership."
Lileas Graf"While this doesn't end the debate, it does underline the importance of respecting our Assembly's decision."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R108:56:16 p23006 / c329
  • ·"I don't only respect the Assembly's decision. I wholeheartedly support it."
  • ▶"A fair point, Lileas. We must respect the decision of the Assembly, even if it doesn't align with our own views."
  • ·"The Assembly's decision has been made, but that doesn't mean we will stop advocating for what we believe is right for Sordland."
  • ·"It's disappointing to see the Assembly resist change this much. Our debate is definitely not over."
Anton Rayne respects the Assembly's decision while maintaining his pragmatic stance.
DeepSeek-R1"A fair point, Lileas. We must respect the decision of the Assembly, even if it doesn't align with our own views."
Deivid Wisci"I respect the decision of the Assembly, Lileas. However, we mustn't allow this setback to deter us from exploring our options. The global landscape is ever-evolving and we must adapt accordingly."
Symon Holl"It's also important to remember that the Assembly's decision is not the final word. There is room for further legislative efforts and executive initiatives."
Symon Holl"When it comes to the Energy Protection Act, I believe we should increase our threshold for foreign investment. In fact, my preference would be to remove the threshold entirely."
Symon Holl"This would undoubtedly strengthen energy institutions such as Gasom and Sordish Petroleum. We've seen Rizia's interest in the former, and I know Lespia has had its eye on the latter for some time..."
Lileas Graf"That's precisely what I'm worried about. We have to remember that we're dealing with national security as well as economics. Reverting to the Soll-era 10 percent limit or keeping the law as it is would be a safer approach."
Lileas Graf"We cannot risk our energy infrastructure falling into foreign hands. Especially not to Rumburg!"
Deivid Wisci"I see merit in both your arguments. Yes, we need to protect our national security, but we also need to consider the economic implications. Perhaps a mixed approach would work best?"
Deivid Wisci"What if we increased the limit to 49 percent, thereby attracting foreign investment while maintaining Sordish control over critical energy sectors?"

Asking about the impact on energy prices directly relates to public welfare and economic stability, aligning with the President's focus on immediate economic concerns.

Deivid Wisci"With increased foreign investment, we could potentially see a decrease in energy prices."
Deivid Wisci"But we should also consider the potential impact on job security and national control of our energy sector."
Lileas Graf"Changing the Energy Protection Act could lead to increased energy prices, as foreign entities could manipulate the market."
Symon Holl"With increased competition and foreign investment, we could also see a decrease in energy prices. The more players in the market, the more competitive the prices will be."

Deivid Wisci"On the other hand, foreign investment would result in increased resources flowing to Gasom and its ilk."
Symon Holl"And those resources could be channeled into further development of our energy sector."
Symon Holl"Yes, Gasom could potentially sell to anyone, but wouldn't that be a show of the strength and attractiveness of our energy sector on the global stage?"

Considering potential foreign buyers such as Qinal, Rizia, Anraka, Rumburg, and Lespia, what are the worst-case scenarios if they gain control over our hydroelectric, gas, and oil sectors?
DeepSeek-R1"Considering potential foreign buyers such as Qinal, Rizia, Anraka, Rumburg, and Lespia, what are the worst-case scenarios if they gain control over our hydroelectric, gas, and oil sectors?"
Lileas Graf"Think about it, Mr. President. If any one of those countries -- but especially Rumburg -- gains control of our energy sector, they could cut off our power at any moment."
Lileas Graf"We would also be forced to comply with any decision they made regarding prices or environmental measures."
Deivid Wisci"There's no denying the potential risks of foreign buyers gaining control over critical sectors."
Deivid Wisci"If we increase the foreign ownership threshold to 49%, we can attract more investments while mitigating those risks."
Symon Holl"While we may see entities from Qinal, Rizia, Anraka, Rumburg, or Lespia as potential threats, it's also crucial to see them as potential partners."
Symon Holl"The scenarios Lileas mentioned are possible, of course, but think about the economic growth and international relations that could be fostered."
Symon Holl"If we aim to deal more closely with Lespia, for example, I very much doubt they'll settle for a mere 49% energy investment."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:00:31 p23397 / c623
  • ·"Lileas, you mentioned national security risks. Could you elaborate further on that?"
  • ▶"We can proceed. It's time to settle this subject."
The proposal to increase foreign ownership thresholds offers a balanced approach to attract investment while maintaining control over critical sectors.
DeepSeek-R1"We can proceed. It's time to settle this subject."
Deivid Wisci"Given the turbulent economic landscape, maintaining the status quo of the Energy Protection Act could offer us stability."
Deivid Wisci"We are already open to foreign investment at a 20 percent threshold. Changing this percentage may introduce unexpected challenges and could complicate our foreign relations."
Lileas Graf"I firmly believe in the sovereignty and strength of our nation."
Lileas Graf"Returning the threshold to 10 percent, as it was during the Soll era, could protect us from undue foreign influence and ensure that our energy sector remains in Sordish hands."
Symon Holl"While I maintain that increasing foreign investment could significantly stimulate our economy."
Symon Holl"An increase to 49 percent could be a balanced compromise, signaling to the world that Sordland is open for business while retaining majority control of our assets. But some potential partners -- particularly ATO countries -- might regard even that as insufficient."
Symon Holl"We must consider the far-reaching implications of our actions. Especially in light of our recent dealings with Gasom."
Symon Holl"Mr. President, your decision to pursue co-investment with Rizia allows us to distribute the financial burden and mitigate risks. It also promotes regional cooperation, aligning well with our fiscal policies."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:01:22 p23493 / c318
  • ·"A joint venture with Rizia is a strategic move, not a concession. It's a partnership that secures our place in the global energy landscape."
  • ▶"Sharing investment responsibilities with Rizia may ease our financial load, but it also means we've invited foreign influence into our energy sector. We must tread carefully."
  • ·"Our partnership with Rizia is our commitment to regional cooperation. It's a pragmatic move that will be beneficial for both nations."
  • ·"Rizia might not be the best partner given our history with them, but we have to take a balanced approach with our budget and economy."
The partnership with Rizia offers financial relief but risks foreign influence, necessitating careful consideration.
DeepSeek-R1"Sharing investment responsibilities with Rizia may ease our financial load, but it also means we've invited foreign influence into our energy sector. We must tread carefully."

Deivid Wisci"With Sordland and Rizia co-investing in Gasom, there's a chance to build a partnership that benefits both our economies. However, how we adjust the EPA could significantly impact the outcome."
Lileas Graf"Sharing the burden with Rizia does provide some reassurance."
Lileas Graf"However, if we raise the foreign ownership threshold, we must be wary of the influence of Rumburg."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:02:20 p23525 / c493

  • ▶"While your points are valid, Lileas, we can't ignore the potential economic benefits. Raising the threshold could attract more investors and increase the value of our shares."
  • ·"Even though we're sharing the investment with Rizia, we should still prioritize Sordish control. Let's consider reducing the foreign ownership limit."
Anton Rayne considers the potential economic benefits of raising the foreign ownership threshold while remaining cautious about national security concerns.

Lileas Graf"I understand the potential economic benefits, but we can't compromise our sovereignty for profits."

Deivid Wisci"Opting for a more balanced approach aligns well with our objective of maintaining positive trade relations without compromising our sovereignty."

  • ·"The EPA has been a safeguard for our national interests, but we must also be open to change. We need to carefully weigh the benefits and drawbacks."
  • ·"Our choices aren't just about the numbers. They hold significant implications for the people of Sordland. We must make our decision with them in mind."

NarratorThe door to the room opened. Livia Suno carried a tray laden with an assortment of beverages.
NarratorShe was a little clumsy as she maneuvered around the room, refilling cups and glasses with tea, coffee, and water.
Livia Suno"Excuse me, gentlemen and madam. I didn't mean to interrupt. What would you like to drink, Mr. President?"

NarratorLivia finished serving the beverages and retreated towards the door. She glanced around the room one last time before quietly backing out.
NarratorThe meeting resumed, our conversation growing slightly louder over the clink of cups and glasses.
Deivid Wisci"While we are here, I will inform you all that I've been lending my expertise to Minister Beniwoll in relation to the Workers Rights Bill."
Deivid Wisci"I believe it's crucial to ensure fair treatment for our workforce. And it is nearing presentation."

Deivid Wisci"Absolutely, Mr. President. We will ensure you receive the bill for review as soon as possible."
Symon Holl"It's important not to decide these things too hastily. Don't let emotions cloud your judgement."
Lileas Graf"That's never a concern with you, is it, Mr. Holl?"
Symon Holl"Anyway. I have been working on our upcoming term budget meeting and a proposal for business taxation changes."
Symon Holl"Specifically, we're considering a range of 10 percent tax changes for small-to-medium enterprises and large corporations. This could be a significant move for our economic landscape."

The proposed tax changes could impact businesses differently, so caution is needed to avoid unintended consequences.
