Symon Holl"I appreciate your insight, Mr. President. Ensuring the survival and growth of our SMEs is at the forefront of our considerations."
Deivid Wisci"Tax changes could indeed have a significant impact on our economy. We must consider their implications carefully."
Lileas Graf"Large private corporations have enjoyed significant benefits and loopholes for far too long. They should pay their fair share."
NarratorAs the ministers rose from their seats, the late-morning light spilled into the room through the ornate windows.
NarratorIt seemed to signal the conclusion of the meeting.

■ Business CouncilT03

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorWe were in Lachaven to work with the Business Council to assess weaknesses in the economy. Symon organised a dinner with the affluent economic figureheads of Sordland.
NarratorWhen we arrived, the streets were bustling with activity even though the atmosphere in the country was tense. Lachaveners didn't seem very worried.
NarratorTogether with Walter Tusk, Edith Agnoc, Mikail Aven and Symon Holl, we entered the renowned Esteria restaurant by the coast.
NarratorA short blonde man whose embroidered pocket read "Manager," greeted us and waved his arms in excitement as he told us how pleased he was to have us in his establishment.
NarratorBeaming with pride, he led us to a private room and got us seated at a large table, covered for the occasion with a maroon tablecloth.
NarratorWe were quickly served by a group of servers. Walter spoke as they started taking our orders.
Walter Tusk"I recommend the Lachaven Salmon, President. You have to taste this local delicacy here, the best in Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:09:01 p23170 / c304
  • ·“I appreciate the advice but I don't like salmon."
  • ▶Order Lachaven Salmon.
  • ·Order traditional Sordish bean stew.
  • ·Order Lespian Pasta.
Choosing the local delicacy shows support for local businesses and aligns with my image as a leader attuned to the needs of the people.
DeepSeek-R1I ordered Lachaven Salmon for the main course. The servers took their notes and asked about the second dish.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:09:53 p23162 / c377
  • ▶Order rice.
  • ·Order salad.
  • ·Order no more.
Choosing Lachaven Salmon aligns with supporting local cuisine and building a positive public image.
DeepSeek-R1I ordered rice.
NarratorThe servers placed glasses in front of each of us and served bread and water. After they were done taking orders, they quickly left us alone in the private room.
Edith Agnoc"Mr. President, I didn't have the possibility to thank you for coming today. Your presence means a great deal."
Mikail Aven"Indeed, sir. It's an honor to dine with you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:10:49 p23127 / c479

  • ·"It's great to be here with the members of the Business Council."
  • ·"Likewise."
Expressing gratitude is the most appropriate and respectful response in this social context.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you."
Mikail Aven"We should thank Symon for organizing this meeting."
Symon Holl"No need for that. I hope everybody enjoys the evening."
NarratorSymon gestured at the expensive looking red wine bottle that one of the servers had just brought to the table.
Symon Holl"Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:11:46 p23045 / c468
  • ·Nod.
  • ·"That looks amazing."
  • ·"Yes?"
  • ▶"No, thank you."
Declining politely maintains his image as a pragmatic leader focused on the bigger picture rather than indulging in luxuries.
DeepSeek-R1"No, thank you."
Walter Tusk"Are you serious, Mr. Rayne? Are you going to pass on a Chateau de Falenco 1934?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:12:33 p23038 / c229
  • ·"Let's try it."
  • ▶"Thank you, but I'm not going to drink this evening."
  • ·"I'd prefer something regional or at least Sordish."
Declining the wine shows self-control and prioritizes business matters over indulgence.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, but I'm not going to drink this evening."
Walter Tusk"I have to insist."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:13:30 p22995 / c478
  • ·"As I've said, I'm not going to drink."
  • ·"Fine... I can't turn down a man like you, Mr. Tusk."
  • ▶"I can't drink now when the country is suffering."
I cannot drink when the country is facing economic hardship and instability.
DeepSeek-R1"I can't drink now when the country is suffering."
Walter Tusk"Sad times, indeed."
NarratorThere was a brief silence at the table before Symon cut in.
Symon Holl"Let's not get ourselves down, gentlemen."
NarratorAfter everybody was served their preferred drinks, Symon stood up with his glass raised.
Symon Holl"I'd like to raise a toast to all of you here today."
Symon Holl"We're meeting here tonight in difficult times. But I believe that, with hard work, cooperation and trust..."
Symon Holl"We will get through this period as well."
Symon Holl"I'd like to thank Mr. Rayne again for his decisiveness when it comes to economic policies... I'm sure we will stop this recession."
Symon Holl"I also thank Mr. Tusk for being here with us today... His presence is an honor."
Symon Holl"And of course, my colleagues from the Business Council, Mrs. Agnoc and Mr. Aven. Without you, none of these would have been possible."
Symon Holl"Let's drink to future cooperation and growth."
Symon Holl"For a prosperous Sordland!"
NarratorEverybody raised their glasses and toasted each other.
NarratorAfter a couple of more minutes, the servers arrived with our meals.
NarratorThe food tasted amazing. Walter was right, this place must have been the best place in all of Lachaven.

Mikail Aven"Of course. I didn't mean to criticise your decision."
NarratorAs we conversed with Mikail, I heard Walter raise his voice in an apparent argument with Edith and Symon. We both stopped to listen.
Walter Tusk"...Arcasia knows the right way of doing business. Their approach to the world is real and raw. I'd prefer that cutthroat approach in comparison to the niceties."
Edith Agnoc"Well-"
Symon Holl"I mean... It's a nice country to visit too. I have travelled there often in business trips. Their attitude on-"
Edith Agnoc"All countries are nice to visit if you have the cash."

Mikail Aven"By the way..."

Mikail Aven"Of course."

Mikail Aven"Why? I thought you said you were great at economy."

Mikail Aven"I've heard that you've bought a new villa, Symon."
Symon Holl"I don't comment on rumours, but I'll make an exception since we are among friends. It's true."

Edith Agnoc"Come on, let's not talk about finance or politics for now. We came here to relax."
NarratorShe turned to Walter and Symon.
Edith Agnoc"Isn't that right, gentlemen?"
NarratorThey nodded at her and took a sip from their drinks. She slightly leaned towards me.
Edith Agnoc"So, Mr. Rayne, why don't you tell us what's behind that strong character of yours?"

NarratorShe looked at Mikail.
Mikail Aven"Well, the rascal didn't even ask for permission to use the yacht and just took it. He basically stole it..."
Mikail Aven"Good news is that I finally convinced him to go to university. He will study economics in Lachaven Business School."
Walter Tusk"That's a good choice. Having a degree in Sordland separates you from the rabble. And we especially need more people who understand economics."

NarratorAfter a long silence, followed by some chit-chat, Walter ordered a bottle of whiskey.

Symon Holl"I think it's time we call it a night."

DeepSeek-R1"What do you want?"
Walter Tusk"Usually the Lotherberg spokesman doesn't directly comment on the administration, but I'd like to say a few things since this was a special evening."

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorThe preparations for the government budget were finished. Most of the cabinet gathered in the White Room for the important occasion.

Symon Holl"I'm quivering in my loafers with excitement, Mr. President."
Lucian Galade"Sadly, Petr Vectern, Gus Manger and Deivid Wisci won't be attending this meeting."
Lucian Galade"They are working on the upcoming trade agreements with Wehlen and Agnolia."
Lileas Graf"May the Lord be with them."
NarratorCiara gave a barely disguised eye roll.
Nia Morgna"Before we begin, is nobody going to mention the ban on the Red Youth and Young Sords?"
Nia Morgna"However much their political aims might differ from our administration's, surely you must agree that freedom of expression must be protected at all costs."
Ciara Walda"Nia-- I mean, Ms. Morgna -- is right."
NarratorNia flashed her an appreciative smile.
Ciara Walda"I myself align far closer to the Red Youth's politics than the Young Sords' but I believe that both--"
NarratorShe broke off as she noticed that Lucian, Symon and Iosef were looking at her and Nia in stony silence.
Lucian Galade"Ladies. Let's leave the extraneous chit-chat for afternoon tea."

CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:31:04 p22040 / c297

DeepSeek-R1"The ban is final. Don't question it any further."
NarratorSymon started loudly shuffling some documents around. All eyes turned to the Minister of Economy.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:31:57 p22031 / c504
  • ▶“Let's begin, Mr. Holl.”
  • ·“Thanks to Symon's expertise, I am sure we will achieve lots of great things today.”
  • ·Nod at Symon.
Starting the discussion immediately allows the team to focus on addressing urgent economic issues efficiently.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's begin, Mr. Holl."
Symon Holl"Gladly."
Symon Holl"Today is a big day. We must prioritise our spending wisely in order to achieve the goals we set as a government."
Lucian Galade"While maintaining enough political support, If I may add. Promises were made during the election."
Symon Holl"There are three options regarding the budgeting of the four main branches of the government. It's either we increase, maintain or decrease the funding."
Symon Holl"Increasing will result in an improvement of the branch which will unlock new policy options. Maintaining will keep the funding, resulting in no visible change. Decreasing causes a loss of efficiency in that branch."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:32:50 p22066 / c490
  • ·“Is it possible to maintain as much state control as possible?”
  • ▶“The problem isn't the amount of money spent. It's how efficiently we spend it.”
Emphasizing efficiency aligns with Keynesian policies, focusing on effective spending to boost public confidence and economic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"The problem isn't the amount of money spent. It's how efficiently we spend it."
Ciara Walda"It's true that many of our policies could be far better implemented."
Paskal Beniwoll"Our welfare systems are full of issues that need to be addressed, for instance."
Symon Holl"We have to be careful as to what we fund so we have resources remaining for another investment project."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:33:53 p22064 / c684
  • ·“We should prioritise the most critical branches.”
  • ▶“We have to keep true to the election promises. Our people voted for us because of the promises we made.”
  • ·“Reduction of funding in certain branches can help fund investment projects.”
Keeping election promises will help maintain public trust and support, crucial for stabilizing the economy and ensuring political stability.
DeepSeek-R1"We have to keep true to the election promises. Our people voted for us because of the promises we made."
Symon Holl"As much as we realistically can. If we fail at improving our economy, the promises don't mean much in the big picture."
Symon Holl"Before we begin our discussion, there is one more thing that I want to voice my opinion on."
Symon Holl"As we manage the government budget, we can to go into debt to invest in different branches."
Symon Holl"However, under these circumstances, it would worsen the economic crisis if we push it too far. It’s difficult for us to raise debt with the current unhealthy economy, but we can increase the amount of debt we can take as the economy improves."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:34:48 p22077 / c511
  • ·“We should spend as much as possible to achieve growth.”
  • ▶“Our administration should be fiscally conservative to avoid a crash.”
Given the fragile economic situation, maintaining fiscal conservatism is crucial to prevent an economic crash.
DeepSeek-R1"Our administration should be fiscally conservative to avoid a crash."
Lucian Galade"Measures of austerity have shown difficulties in appeasing voter groups."
Lucian Galade"Given the current state of the country, improving economic situation is a political priority right now. More so than ever, if we want to win the 58 election."
Symon Holl"This budget will also be applicable to our entire term. It is very critical that we plan correctly."
Symon Holl"Let's begin, then."
Symon Holl"Which branch should we discuss first?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:35:41 p22059 / c444

  • ·“Let's discuss law enforcement.”
  • ·“It's time to hear the opinions on the military.”

DeepSeek-R1"Let's talk about healthcare."
Paskal Beniwoll"Our healthcare system is vast, and provides free services to most of our citizens. However, the quality of our health services is far worse in low-income and rural areas."
Paskal Beniwoll"Therefore, I think that we should receive more funding to address this disparity."
Ciara Walda"I agree. I have recently begun an investigation into healthcare in Narbel, and the numbers are not good."
Paskal Beniwoll"If we don't increase spending on health services, we at least need to maintain it. Otherwise, we are putting lives at risk."
Paskal Beniwoll"We have just started a new analysis on what would happen if a pandemic struck Sordland. According to the results, if our healthcare facilities remain in their current state, the death toll would be unimaginable."
Paskal Beniwoll"I'm not even talking about the damage to the economy."
Paskal Beniwoll"This is a matter of life and death, Mr. President and it should be treated as such."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:36:28 p22084 / c335

  • ·“That is only *if* a pandemic happens.”
  • ·“We need to improve our healthcare. What else are we here for if we can't keep our people alive?”
Balancing economic constraints with health concerns, choosing a measured response that acknowledges worry without committing additional funds.

CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:37:28 p22019 / c619

  • ·“It's time to hear the opinions on the military.”
  • ·“What about education?”
  • ▶“I have made up my mind about the budget. We can proceed.”
Anton Rayne decides to move forward with the budget discussion to allocate funds effectively, prioritizing immediate needs like healthcare for economic stability.

Symon Holl"Very good talking points from everyone."
Symon Holl"Well then..."
Symon Holl"...The time to make a decision on the budget has come."

Policy decision
POLICY DeepSeek-R109:38:26 p22037 / c584
  • ·Maintain — Sordland's health system and healthcare quality have stagnated due to administrative inefficiencies, increased service demand, and an increasing divide in service quality between urban and rural hospitals.   • ▶Increase — Sordland's health system and healthcare quality have stagnated due to administrative inefficiencies, increased service demand, and an increasing divide in service quality between urban and rural hospitals. The average life expectancy has dropped significantly, with issues such as maternity deaths and disease outbreaks.

The average life expectancy has dropped significantly, with issues such as maternity deaths and disease outbreaks.
Maintaining the current healthcare funding prevents worsening the situation and shows consideration for public health without risking economic instability.
Policy decision

  • ·Increase — The recently culminating political tension between the different youth groups and socioeconomic pressures between multiple classes of society is increasing the issues of the law and enforcement systems. Increasing healthcare funding is crucial due to the significant disparity in service quality between urban and rural areas, which poses serious health risks. Addressing these issues aligns with the manifesto's focus on immediate relief and economic stabilization.
NarratorI had finished allocating the government budget, to the satisfaction of some cabinet members and the dismay of others. They and I knew this would define a significant part of our term.
Symon Holl"We have a very healthy budget surplus left for investments and other expenditures. Splendid!"
Lucian Galade"Agreed."
Lucian Galade"Well then. Let's start with law enforcement."
NarratorNia and Lileas were frowning. When I looked at her, Lileas shook her head.
Lileas Graf"I heavily disapprove. We are barely making ends meet on the security side. I will be forced to reduce our force size and salary."
Nia Morgna"The reduction of the budget might also put as in a tight spot regarding our ability to handle the amount of court cases."
Lucian Galade"Any statements on the matter, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:41:54 p21863 / c433
  • ▶“I trust you to push the Ministry of Interior and Justice forward.”
  • ·“Our new priorities called for a reduction of law enforcement.”
  • ·“No, that should be all.”

DeepSeek-R1"I trust you to push the Ministry of Interior and Justice forward."
Lileas Graf"With the reduction, our work will be hampered and made more difficult."
Nia Morgna"Disappointing for the people who want a sense of justice returned. The backlogs won't get solved. The administration will hurt."
Lucian Galade"Let's hear the Minister of Education."
NarratorCiara was eager to comment.
Ciara Walda"Keeping the same budget will just result in the same problems. However, I prefer this over a decrease. I'll try to reform the system with my own resources."
Ciara Walda"Our future depends on our children. Education is the first step in the fight against ignorance."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:42:50 p21871 / c519

Lucian Galade"The military is next."
NarratorCiara clearly wasn't finished, but didn't interject. Iosef chimed in.
Iosef Lancea"It is a shame that the military won't be getting an increase. I will have to make sacrifices to maintain our defense."

Paskal Beniwoll"I am also planning to tackle issues like the lack of rural hospitals and the recently worrying numbers of maternal deaths."
Ciara Walda"Welfare must remain a priority for our term. There is no other way around it."

TURN 04

■ Decision Riot GearT04

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
EventBuy Riot Control Equipment: The recent unrest has shown weaknesses in our law enforcement's ability to maintain order and peace. The Ministry of Interior has run out of important vehicles, armor and other crowd control gear to be able to ensure stability in the country. Should we fund riot control equipment?
EVENT DeepSeek-R109:46:42 p21084 / c470
  • ▶Buy riot control equipment [-1 Government Budget]
  • ·Don't buy
Maintaining public order and safety is crucial for political stability, outweighing immediate budget constraints.

■ Bill Workers RightsT04

BUDGET 3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
Bill for decisionWorkers' Rights Act — Section I of this bill sets the groundwork for fair compensation by establishing a minimum wage of 2.5 Sordish Ren per hour. This serves as a benchmark for fair and equitable pay, ensuring that no worker in Sordland is compensated below this rate. Section II addresses the issue of excessive labour, setting a maximum limit of 60 hours of work per week. This provision aims to prevent worker exploitation and safeguard the wellbeing of Sordish labour force. Section III introduces the concept of double overtime pay. This stipulates that any work performed beyond a 40-hour workweek will be compensated at twice the normal hourly wage. This incentivises fair work practices and ensures employees are duly compensated for their extended efforts. Section IV focuses on worker safety and humane work conditions. It mandates that employers must take all necessary steps to protect their workers from occupational hazards and ensure a dignified and safe working environment. This promotes a culture of respect and care within workplaces across Sordland. Section V extends these protections to migrant or undocumented workers, granting them the same rights as Sordish citizens. Employers who fail to abide by these rules will face substantial fines from the government, and persistent non-compliance will invite legal action. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R109:47:57 p21451 / c328
  • ·Sign: Workers' Rights Act
  • ▶Veto: Workers' Rights Act
The bill would strain the government budget further, which is already constrained by the economic crisis. Prioritizing immediate economic stabilization is crucial for winning re-election.

■ Personal JoggingT04

BUDGET 2WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorIt was early evening, still light out with just a faint nip in the air. Franc was at a friend's and Deana was at an art class. Monica and I decided to go out for a walk before dinner.
NarratorOur favorite place for a stroll was the nearby Capitol Park, the largest green space in all of Holsord. Before the children were born, we had spent many an evening engaged in deep conversation along the riverside promenade.
NarratorAnother favored path wound through the woods behind the presidential housing complex, to a private pond where the children could swim in summer. I was told that Soll himself had it built. The benefits of being president.
NarratorMonica met me by the door, and we put on our coats and hats.
Monica"I was going to suggest we walk towards Soll Square, but the guards told me it's been closed for yet another protest."
Monica"I wonder who they are protesting this time."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:49:04 p21684 / c416
  • ▶“What does it matter? They are two sides of the same coin.”
  • ·“Common thugs. I will make sure they are punished.”
  • ·“Must be those damn communists again.”
  • ·“The Young Sords are taking it too far.”
Maintaining neutrality while acknowledging both groups' roles in instability.
DeepSeek-R1"What does it matter? They are two sides of the same coin."
Monica"The Anton I knew in university never would have said that."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:49:48 p21637 / c218
  • ▶"Funny how becoming president changes one's perspective."
  • ·"I was young and stupid. I didn't know any better."
Recognizing the change in perspective due to the responsibilities of office.
DeepSeek-R1"Funny how becoming president changes one's perspective."
Monica"In any case, where shall we go? I'm not sure Capitol Park is a wise idea in this climate, either...
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:50:37 p21601 / c411
  • ▶“You're right -- it's not worth the security risk. Let's stay inside the green perimeter.”
  • ·“Nonsense. We have our guards -- there's nothing to be afraid of. Let's go to Capitol Park.”
It's prudent to prioritize security given the current unstable political climate.
DeepSeek-R1"You're right -- it's not worth the security risk. Let's stay inside the green perimeter."
Monica"I agree. Let's go, then, before the children get home."
NarratorI informed the guards about our route and we left the house.
NarratorThe guards followed at a discreet distance as we headed towards the walking trail.
NarratorAs we entered the woods, Monica broke the silence.
Monica"So, how was your day?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:51:28 p21523 / c443
  • ·“Paperwork, endless meetings... the usual nightmare.”
  • ·“I'd rather not say.”
  • ▶“How was yours?”
Shifting focus to Monica shows interest in her day, strengthening our personal connection.
DeepSeek-R1"How was yours?"
Monica"Avoiding my question, are we? Very well."
Monica"Oh, it was fine. I caught up on some reading while the children were at school."
Monica"Serge said he found something in the car after bringing it up from the garage."
Monica"A letter. Hand-folded, no envelope. Just the initials E.M. on the corner."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:52:13 p21468 / c339
  • ·"How’d it get there?"
  • ▶"Someone got close enough to leave a note in my car?"
  • ·"That's unusual."
The letter's presence suggests a security concern, indicating someone accessed the car closely.
DeepSeek-R1"Someone got close enough to leave a note in my car?"
Monica"That’s the thing. He swore no one’s been near the car since last night."
Monica"But he passed it on after I read it, and the Presidential Guard took it -- standard protocol -- apparently. They check for poison, residue, that sort of thing."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:53:07 p21516 / c497
  • ▶"You read it first? What did it say?"
  • ·"Should’ve let them take it before you opened it."
  • ·"Probably just someone trying to mess with us."
I need to know if Monica read the letter to assess potential exposure and ensure security protocols were followed.
DeepSeek-R1"You read it first? What did it say?"
Monica"I didn’t get far before passing it on, but enough."
Monica"I only remember parts... but they’re hard to forget."
Monica"The Palace was built on lies... from the beginning. Not by the divine, nor the people, but by intent."
Monica"I have borne the same burden. I only understood when it came for E.A."
Monica"And then something cryptic: ‘Burn the old. Through fire, the new can be born. Until then, an interregnum will bring pain... and the prairie will strangle itself.’"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:54:02 p21514 / c506
  • ·"Maybe it’s just some bored revolutionary kid, trying to sound important."
  • ·"Let the Guard finish their sweep. I will check on it sometime."
  • ▶"Maybe they’re warning me. Or warning me off."
  • ·"E.M. knew too much. If they got that close, they could be watching everything."
The note suggests a serious warning or threat, indicating that E.M. could be monitoring the President closely.
DeepSeek-R1"Maybe they’re warning me. Or warning me off."
Monica"That’s what I thought. It didn’t sound like a threat. It sounded like regret."
Monica"Anyway, we should enjoy the run. It's one of the few things we have just for *us*"
NarratorWe jogged on for a few quiet minutes.
NarratorA sleek cat leapt across the path ahead of us. Silver fur, one blue eye, one green, an Agn cat, no doubt. After a brief look it then disappeared into a hedge with a flick of its marbled tail.
Monica"I had two like that when I was young. My mother raised them. They used to nap in the windows all day and vanish at night like little ghosts."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:54:50 p21523 / c368
  • ·"I don’t think I ever had a place like that. Maybe that’s why I never learned how to miss it."
  • ·"I always thought you looked most at peace when you talked about that house."
  • ▶"I remember you told me one used to sit on your piano stool while you practiced... like it was judging your technique."
  • ·"The past is the past. We have a great future to look forward to. One that we can build."
Engaging with Monica's memory by referencing the piano stool shows attentiveness to her past, strengthening our connection.
DeepSeek-R1"I remember you told me one used to sit on your piano stool while you practiced... like it was judging your technique."
Monica"He was merciless. Always left the room when I hit the wrong notes."
NarratorJust outside the gate, I glimpsed Ionas Crona, the former Minister of Justice, walking towards Capitol Park. I remembered that Nia Morgna used to be his Vice Minister. He didn't seem to notice us as he walked by.
NarratorIonas had once fought hard and strong against Soll’s judiciary reforms. But after years of the government blocking his every move, he gave up on his ideals and became a part of the administration.
NarratorWhat did our ideals really mean if they could be changed so easily? What about the people we idolize?
Monica"Evelyn also came round for tea. She says hi. She was feeling rather low, though. Apparently she and Petr had a bit of a disagreement."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:55:28 p21524 / c98

  • ·“Petr's in the doghouse again? How'd it happen?”

It's better to keep the peace and focus on the present.

Monica"Apparently Petr had too much whiskey -- as usual -- and didn't behave when Evelyn's father was over for dinner yesterday."
Monica"You know Petr. What do you think of this?"

  • ·“It's just a rough patch, love. They'll be fine."
  • ·“To be honest, I don't care."
Addressing Petr's behavior directly shows leadership and concern for maintaining a professional image.
DeepSeek-R1"That's Petr -- perpetually pickled. I'll have a talk with him."
Monica"Good. Evelyn seemed close to tears."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:56:52 p21458 / c226

DeepSeek-R1"Aren't you glad you and I don't have these kinds of quarrels?"
NarratorMonica didn't say anything. We kept walking.
Monica"You know Evelyn hasn't been the same since what happened to her at Holsord Post last year."
Monica"You remember -- how she got fired for supporting Alphonso? Petr tried everything to get her job back, but it didn't work."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:57:38 p21450 / c334
  • ·“Maybe he didn't try hard enough.”
  • ▶“She had it coming.”
  • ·“A sad day for journalism and free speech in Sordland.”
Anton Rayne would prioritize maintaining order and supporting those in power, suggesting Evelyn's dismissal was justified.
DeepSeek-R1"She had it coming."
Monica"How can you say that? It was a sad day for freedom of the press in Sordland."
Monica"But enough about the Vecterns. I also spoke to Janice on the phone."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:58:20 p21379 / c274
  • ·“Who?”
  • ▶“Oh, Janice... Right.”
Recognizing that Janice is someone both are familiar with allows the conversation to continue smoothly.
DeepSeek-R1"Oh, Janice... Right."
Monica"You remember, my friend from university? We were in the Sordish Literature class together?"
Monica"A few years younger than me, red hair, scar on her forehead? We had dinner with her and her husband once or twice."
NarratorNeither the name nor the description rang a bell.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:59:04 p21335 / c302
  • ·“Right, right, that Janice."
  • ▶“I still don't remember her, but go on."
Anton chooses to admit he doesn't remember Janice, prompting Monica to continue the conversation.
DeepSeek-R1"I still don't remember her, but go on."
Monica"Anyway, she's been working as an executive assistant at the Violet Pictures film studio."
Monica"She recently became pregnant, and asked her boss for paid maternity leave. And guess what?"
Monica"She was fired for it!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R109:59:47 p21334 / c245
  • ·“Can't her husband provide for the both of them while she raises the child?"
  • ▶“Look at it from a cost standpoint. Obviously, they don't want to lose money.”
  • ·“After asking for maternity leave? That's terrible.”
  • ·“So what?”
Considering the cost perspective highlights the company's financial considerations, which is relevant given the current economic instability.
DeepSeek-R1"Look at it from a cost standpoint. Obviously, they don't want to lose money."
Monica"But she's been working there for years without so much as a raise. Something is wrong with this, Anton. And that's not all."
Monica"They immediately replaced her with a younger, less experienced man -- the son of an executive, no less."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:00:27 p21274 / c212
  • ▶“That is utterly ridiculous.”
  • ·“So she called the First Lady to ask for help?"
  • ·"Nepotism, eh? They must have wanted him in there all along."
Calling it utterly ridiculous emphasizes the absurdity of the situation where a pregnant employee is fired and replaced by an executive's son.
DeepSeek-R1"That is utterly ridiculous."
Monica"I'm happy you agree with me."
Monica"As it happens, another friend works for a television station that's hiring secretaries, so I connected the two of them."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:01:13 p21241 / c373
  • ▶“That doesn't solve the deeper problem, though."
  • ·"Terrific! Problem solved, then."
The solution proposed does not address the underlying issues, which could lead to further complications.
DeepSeek-R1"That doesn't solve the deeper problem, though."
Monica"Exactly! I knew you'd be on the same page."
Monica"Women in this country are still seen as wives and mothers first, human beings second."
Monica"They're harassed at work, underpaid, blocked from positions of power... and those are the lucky ones!"
Monica"The fact that there are still places in this country where little girls like Deana are denied the right to an education--"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:02:03 p21165 / c431
  • ▶“Yes, and I will do what I can to change that.“
  • ·“Go on.”
  • ·“I'm sure the parents have their reasons.”
Recognizing Monica's concerns and showing willingness to address the potential threat.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes, and I will do what I can to change that."
Monica"It can't just be you. These little girls and people like Janice need someone to speak on their behalf. A role model. A successful woman."
NarratorWe had reached the pond at the end of the trail. A park bench overlooked the water. Monica gestured to it, and we sat down.
Monica"Which brings me to my point."
Monica"I know that the mayor of Anrica, Curtan Leste, will be delivering a speech at the annual opening of the Benfi festival."
Monica"My wish is to take his place and deliver the speech instead."
NarratorLeste was one of Sordland's most influential politicians. He had started his career in Benfi before rising to power in Anrica. Benfi's current mayor had personally invited him to make the opening speech.
NarratorReplacing him with Monica would likely anger him -- probably not enough to cause a huge incident, but enough that he might use his influence over the conservatives to block me in the Assembly.
Monica"I want to base the speech on women's rights in Sordland. On the oppression and discrimination we face everyday, on the basis of our sex alone."
Monica"I want our voice heard, and this festival is the time to do it. I just need your blessing as my husband and the President of Sordland."
Monica"Remember my time with the Sordish League of Women in university? You supported me then. I'm asking you to support me now."
NarratorMonica's eyes were blazing with a fire I hadn't seen since we were students. She wanted this badly.
NarratorBut I had to be careful with my promises.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:02:58 p21627 / c512
  • ·“You have my full support. Always.”
  • ·“Sure, it's just a speech. What's the worst that can happen?”
  • ·“Does it have to be at the festival? Why can't you make your speech another time?"
  • ▶“Absolutely not. Out of the question.”
Allowing Monica to replace Curtan Leste at the festival could provoke political backlash and strain relations with conservative figures, risking legislative support.
DeepSeek-R1"Absolutely not. Out of the question."
Monica"I don't understand. Isn't this something you care about as well?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:03:44 p21528 / c338
  • ·“I don't want you to step into politics.”
  • ·“Sorry, but I can't make Curtan Leste angry."
  • ▶“It's not the right time. Sordland is facing too much political strife -- we'll have to deal with women's rights later.”
Focusing on women's rights now risks diverting attention from economic recovery and could provoke political backlash, given the unstable climate.
DeepSeek-R1"It's not the right time. Sordland is facing too much political strife -- we'll have to deal with women's rights later."
Monica"What little trouble this causes in the short term is nothing compared to the lasting, positive change it will bring -- not just for women, but for all of Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:04:36 p21492 / c418
  • ·"All right. If it means so much to you.,I'll make the arrangements for you to speak."
  • ▶“I don't think so.”
The current political instability and economic challenges make supporting Monica's speech less urgent than addressing immediate national concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't think so."
NarratorMonica was silent for a while. When she finally spoke, there was a chill in her voice.
Monica"If that's truly how you feel, so be it."
NarratorWe walked back home, neither of us looking at the other.

■ Law Enforcement BudgetT04

BUDGET 2WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorI was in my office reading reports from the ministries about the current situation of the protests.
NarratorMy next scheduled meeting was with Nia and Lileas, to discuss the law enforcement budget. I heard some knocks on the door.
NarratorNia entered and took a seat in front of my desk. Lileas was absent.
Nia Morgna"Good afternoon, Mr. President."
Nia Morgna"Lileas will be late. Apparently there was an urgent matter at the Ministry of the Interior she had to attend to. Shouldn't take long."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:05:38 p20873 / c324
  • ·“What urgent matter?”
  • ▶“Very well. Let's wait.”
  • ·“That's a couple of minutes less of Lileas. Great.”
Maintaining professionalism and patience in response to an urgent matter.
DeepSeek-R1"Very well. Let's wait."
NarratorI returned to my reports and Nia smoothed back an errant strand of her hair. There was an awkward silence.
Nia Morgna"While we wait, why don't we have a little chat?"
Nia Morgna"This might come off as a little strange, but I am proud of you."
Nia Morgna"Despite everything, you are actually trying to reform the country."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:06:28 p20943 / c453
  • ·“This is just the beginning. There is so much more to do.”
  • ▶“It's *we* Nia, not *me*. I couldn't do any of this by myself. So no, I am proud of you.”
  • ·“Flattery won't work on me. What do you want?”
Acknowledging Nia's contribution strengthens team unity and deflects individual praise.
DeepSeek-R1"It's *we* Nia, not *me*. I couldn't do any of this by myself. So no, I am proud of you."
Nia Morgna"Well, thank you."
Nia Morgna"You certainly have a way with words, don't you?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:07:20 p20950 / c530
  • ·“Words are just words. Actions are what's important.”
  • ▶“I wouldn't be the President otherwise.”
Acknowledging Nia's support while emphasizing the necessity of being a proactive leader reinforces unity and shared goals.
DeepSeek-R1"I wouldn't be the President otherwise."
NarratorShe laughed.
Nia Morgna"Fair enough."
NarratorShe leaned forward.
Nia Morgna"How are you really holding up, Anton? Being the President, I mean."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:08:08 p20886 / c404
  • ▶“It's the hardest thing I have ever done in my life but it is also the best thing I have ever done in my life.”
  • ·“Nothing I can't handle.”
  • ·“It feels like the whole weight of the country crushes me down every single day.”
Anton reflects on the dual challenges and satisfactions of his role, acknowledging the difficulty while emphasizing the significance.
DeepSeek-R1"It's the hardest thing I have ever done in my life but it is also the best thing I have ever done in my life."
Nia Morgna"I can imagine. So much responsibility and so much power at the same time."
Nia Morgna"I want to assist you and the country as best as I can. Unfortunately, my hands are tied with the current budget allocation we have for the law enforceme--"
NarratorShe barely finished the sentence before we heard a knock on the door. Nia almost jumped in her chair. Lileas Graf entered the room.
Lileas Graf"Mr. President. Nia."
Lileas Graf"I hope that I'm not interrupting your conversation."
Nia Morgna"No, not at all. We were just discussing...a few things."
NarratorNia smiled at Lileas.
Lileas Graf"I am sorry for being late. There was an important matter at the ministry. The security forces of Bergia are arbitrarily not cooperating with my department and created some minor annoyances."
Lileas Graf"Unfortunately, due to my limited authority in the Special Zone of Bergia, I cannot control their operations."
Lileas Graf"Luckily they didn't completely botch their tasks, and actually uncovered a large network of priests that are recruiting Bergians for the Bludish Freedom Front."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:08:59 p21177 / c461
  • ·"Good. Do your best to keep the miscommunication with the Governor's forces to a minimum. We need to take the war against terror seriously."
  • ▶"You were late, Ms. Graf. But now that you are here, let's begin with the meeting."
  • ·"This sounds very significant. Do you want to explain more?"
Proceed with the meeting as scheduled to address the budget despite the security development.
DeepSeek-R1"You were late, Ms. Graf. But now that you are here, let's begin with the meeting."
NarratorLileas took the seat next to Nia as she continued talking.
Lileas Graf"I must thank you, Mr. President, for the support with the riot gear. The Police Forces definitely needed it."
Lileas Graf"Well then... Let's go straight in."
Lileas Graf"We will provide a brief update on the situation of law enforcement."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:09:56 p21110 / c642
  • ▶“Go ahead.”
  • ·Nod.
Maintaining the current budget for law enforcement ensures stability while allowing focus on other critical areas.
DeepSeek-R1"Go ahead."
Lileas Graf"Nia?"
Nia Morgna"The Ministry of Justice and the Supreme Court is working in close collaboration with the Ministry of Interior to address the protests in the country."
Nia Morgna"I must add that thanks to the hard work of our judiciary, the law enforcement forces assigned to my ministry are doing their jobs efficiently."
Lileas Graf"I would call it the success of our forces on the ground, not due to the judicial branch of our government."
NarratorLileas shot Nia a brief withering glance.
Lileas Graf"As expected, after the budget reduction, the morale of the employees are at an all time low. Many of them face the risk of losing their jobs now."
Lileas Graf"Yet here we are, with mass protests happening in the country, talking about how we have to fire our security force personnel."
Lileas Graf"At least you have fulfilled our request to fund additional riot control equipment that will allow us to maintain order to some degree."
Lileas Graf"The situation is still not good."
Nia Morgna"Agreed. The Ministry of Justice was hit the worst. The backlog of the court cases are so high that the judges do not even have time to look at smaller cases, postponing these to months or years later."
Nia Morgna"This causes a disastrous bottleneck. How can we deal justice if we can't even find time for these cases?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:10:39 p21197 / c223
  • ·“Don't worry. I am sure you will manage.”
  • ▶“I had to make sacrifices for the good of Sordland.”
  • ·“You will do as you are told with what you have.”
The current economic and political instability makes it crucial to maintain focus on immediate priorities rather than addressing additional issues like women's rights.
DeepSeek-R1"I had to make sacrifices for the good of Sordland."
Lileas Graf"These sacrifices might've very well damaged the structural integrity of Sordland."
Nia Morgna"That remains to be seen but the ripples are already being felt across the country. I hope this decision is not the beginning of something bad."
Lileas Graf"Well then. Let's move on to what we are actually here for."
Lileas Graf"Due to the budget decrease, we have no choice but to cut funding from a branch."
Lileas Graf"However..."
Nia Morgna"You need to make a decision to determine which branch gets the cut."
Lileas Graf"Exactly. When deciding you must look at the bigger picture and I believe you will make the right choice."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:11:36 p21236 / c600
  • ·“Why should I cut funding from Ministry of Interior?”
  • ·“What if I decide to cut funding equally from Ministry of Interior and Ministry of Justice?”
  • ▶“Why should I cut funding from Ministry of Justice?”
  • ·“I have already made my decision.”
Cutting the Ministry of Justice's budget is preferable to maintaining public safety by keeping law enforcement funded.
DeepSeek-R1"Why should I cut funding from Ministry of Justice?"
Lileas Graf"Simple. It's because Ministry of Interior can't afford a reduction unless you want the whole country to be overrun with protestors."
Nia Morgna"Hah. And tell me Lileas, how are you going to bring the criminals to justice if our judges have to postpone court dates to 6 months, 1 year later."
Nia Morgna"It doesn't even matter who or how many criminals you catch with your forces if justice can't be dealt."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:12:21 p21268 / c318
  • ·“Why should I cut funding from Ministry of Interior?”
  • ▶“What if I decide to cut funding equally from Ministry of Interior and Ministry of Justice?”
  • ·“I am ready to make a decision.”
Cutting funding equally from both ministries would mitigate the impact on each, allowing them to adjust while maintaining some functionality.
DeepSeek-R1"What if I decide to cut funding equally from Ministry of Interior and Ministry of Justice?"
Nia Morgna"We need decisive action. Cutting funding from both branches won't help anyone."
Nia Morgna"Look at it this way. Ministry of Justice has never been well-funded in Sordland while the Ministry of Interior was always more lucky, already causing this uneven balance in between the two."
Lileas Graf"That doesn't mean anything. The Ministry of Interior is far more critical in function. Especially now."
Lileas Graf"Tell me, Nia. Who brings the criminals before the justices? Let me answer. The police forces connected to Ministry of Interior."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:13:08 p21265 / c364
  • ▶“Why should I cut funding from Ministry of Interior?”
  • ·“I am ready to make a decision.”
Cutting funding from the Ministry of Justice would worsen an already strained system, leading to slower justice and increased public frustration.
DeepSeek-R1"Why should I cut funding from Ministry of Interior?"
Nia Morgna"The Ministry of Interior is one of the well funded government agencies in Sordland. The same can't be said about Ministry of Justice."
Nia Morgna"Our court cases have been piling up with no end in sight. We already have a bottleneck. If we worsen the situation, our whole justice system will fall."
Lileas Graf"Ministry of Interior and our police forces on the ground play a much larger and a more important role during these times where there is a threat to our internal safety."
“I am ready to make a decision.”
DeepSeek-R1"I am ready to make a decision."
Nia Morgna"What will it be?"
NarratorLileas and Nia leaned forward.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:13:56 p21299 / c386
  • ·“The funds will be decreased from the Ministry of the Interior. We need to solve the court backlog issue to deal justice.”
  • ·“Budget will be cut equally from the Ministry of Interior and Ministry of Justice.”
  • ▶“The funds will be decreased from the Ministry of Justice. It is clearly the less important agency with the current situation.”
Cutting funds from the Ministry of Justice is preferable as it prioritizes maintaining internal security over judicial efficiency, which is critical for current stability.
DeepSeek-R1"The funds will be decreased from the Ministry of Justice. It is clearly the less important agency with the current situation."
Nia Morgna"This is ridiculous."
Lileas Graf"I knew that we would be on the same side, Mr. President."
NarratorA brief moment of deliberation passed. Lileas and Nia got up from their seats.
Nia Morgna"I must return to the ministry to implement necessary changes."
Lileas Graf"Me too."
NarratorThey left the room.
NarratorAs soon as the door closed, I could their muffled voices as they argued.

■ Decision Regional InvestmentT04

BUDGET 2WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
EventRegional Investment Decision: Gus Manger and Symon Holl have prepared an extensive regional investment package to revitalize one of top three economically fragile regions by boosting the municipality budgets, providing large sums of business grants and giving cheap loans to companies with liquidity problems. The proposal can be implemented in one of the following regions: Bergia, Lorren and Agnland or not. [-2 Government Budget]
EVENT DeepSeek-R110:15:02 p20856 / c278
  • ·Bergia
  • ·Lorren
  • ·Agnland

■ Taurus Railway IssueT04

BUDGET 2WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorThe L-1 Railway construction created the perfect opportunity to visit the green technological nerve centre of Sordland. Dozens of electrical engineers from the city were part of the electrification upgrade of the rail track.
NarratorThe main purpose of our visit was to solve construction issues that began to endanger the deadline.
NarratorThe Gelsord Research Council let us use their facilities, but the rectors weren't interested in our affairs.
NarratorSymon looked a little more stressed than usual. Gus was also here to find solutions. He eyed me for a while.
Symon Holl"The strike that popped up at the construction site is causing issues."
Symon Holl"After the terrible Morbel mine disaster, workers have been on edge. Vetoing the Workers' Rights bill certainly did not help with the situation either."
Symon Holl"Strikers primary demand is for the bill to be reinstated. We have to solve the issue or the construction will stay halted longer."
Symon Holl"We scheduled the construction to finish sooner but Taurus Holding might miss that target with the current trajectory."
Gus Manger"I don't support that bill just like you Mr. President. The reason is simple, I know it will cause issues with corporations and result in increased employee costs."
Gus Manger"There is little point in passing that bill late. Maybe the best way out of this is replacing the workers on strike."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:16:11 p21273 / c719
  • ·“We could just wait it out until the strikers get tired.”
  • ·“Why don't we just do what they want and pass the Workers' Rights bill?”
  • ▶“Indeed we could just replace the striking workers.”
Replacing the striking workers is a pragmatic choice to maintain project momentum despite potential long-term labor relations issues.
DeepSeek-R1"Indeed we could just replace the striking workers."
Gus Manger"I think this is our best bet. We can't stand idle and we can't allow a few strikers to change the laws of the state."
Symon Holl"This will only serve to rile up the strikers even more. We might end up with a bigger mess."
Symon Holl"We need to show people that we can finish this project, our first mega project, on time."
Symon Holl"As much as I hate to say it, I think passing the Workers' Rights bill might be our best bet."
Symon Holl"However, following the veto, we have ceased the implementation of the policy and disbanded the expert team, resulting in a potential increase in the cost of the bill if new teams are to be established."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:16:54 p21171 / c292
  • ·“Absolutely not. I will not let a few strikers decide the future of our country.”
  • ·“You have a point.”
  • ▶“We need to cut the head of the snake to truly stop this issue by replacing the strikers.”
Replacing the strikers will ensure the project continues despite their demands, preventing further economic instability.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to cut the head of the snake to truly stop this issue by replacing the strikers."
Gus Manger"Exactly. This is the only way to stop this from happening again. Make an example of the strikers so that no one else attempts it again."
Gus Manger"One thing is for certain, our administration must avoid working with Taurus in the future and opt for safe choices like Underhall."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:17:42 p21253 / c384
  • ·“Mistakes happen in any project.”
  • ▶“Underhall is a better option indeed.”
  • ·“The SSC could be a partner.”
Replacing the strikers with a more reliable company like Underhall Holding is the pragmatic choice to ensure project continuity.
DeepSeek-R1"Underhall is a better option indeed."
Gus Manger"There are people I know in the management of Underhall. They will be available for a second project in the future."
Symon Holl"How should we handle the strikes?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:18:32 p21291 / c445
  • ·“We will begin the procedure to pass the vetoed Workers' Rights bill. [-2 Government Budget]”
  • ·“I'm not worried. Let's just wait it out. They will stop at one point and everything will be alright.”
  • ▶“Order Taurus Holding to replace the strikers with new workers. This should fix our issue.”
Replacing the strikers will immediately resolve the issue and keep the project on track, showing decisive leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"Order Taurus Holding to replace the strikers with new workers. This should fix our issue."
Symon Holl"Very well. Hopefully the backlash doesn't affect us too much."
Symon Holl"Regardless, the decision is made."
Symon Holl"Well then. Thank you for coming. That is all on this matter."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:19:19 p21111 / c349
  • ·“Let's enjoy the hospitality of the Mayor.”
  • ▶“Thank you everyone.”
  • ·“Good night.”
Express gratitude to colleagues after a productive meeting.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you everyone."
NarratorSymon nodded and we left the meeting room.

■ Tax ReformT04

BUDGET 2WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorWe were at the top floor of the business council building which had a great view of the beautiful Lachaven. I looked outside from the tall glass windows.
NarratorThe Markian Sea stretched as far as the eye could see and it was dotted with many ships towards the horizon. They were mostly cargo ships, carrying precious goods in and out of Sordland.
NarratorTracing back the paths of the ships, I looked at the harbour. Lachaven was home to one of the largest harbours in the entire continent. Many dockyard workers were loading or unloading the containers. Work never stopped here.
NarratorThe door opened. Symon, Mikail and Edith entered the meeting room.
Symon Holl"Mr. President."
Edith Agnoc"Mr. President. So nice to see you again."
Mikail Aven"Good afternoon, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:20:24 p20833 / c372
  • ·“It's great to see you all again.”
  • ·“Good afternoon.”
  • ▶“Take a seat and let's begin.”
Anton Rayne prefers a direct and efficient approach in meetings with business leaders to focus on economic matters.
DeepSeek-R1"Take a seat and let's begin."
NarratorSymon put down his bag on the table and started collecting his documents while the others slowly took their seats.
Mikail Aven"Mr. President. Before we start, I would like to mention some rumours that I heard."
Mikail Aven"Vetoing the recent Workers' Rights Act was welcomed by a lot of business owners."
Mikail Aven"The first section about the minimum wages would've been very damaging to many corporations."
Edith Agnoc"I heard the same."
Edith Agnoc"A good decision I'd say. Especially during a recession."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:21:15 p20864 / c455
  • ·“I wanted to sign it but I had to make the right call.”
  • ▶“I didn't want to hamper our businesses and their employment drive.”
  • ·“You are in no position to evaluate my decision.”
  • ·“I don't care about rumours. If someone wants to say something, they can say it to me directly.”
Acknowledging the business community's perspective helps maintain their support without dismissing labor concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"I didn't want to hamper our businesses and their employment drive."
Edith Agnoc"You have done the right thing."
NarratorSymon waited patiently. Once everyone was ready, he adjusted his glasses.
Symon Holl"First of all, I would like to thank everyone here for their work on our tax system reform."
Symon Holl"We all know how complicated our current system is and this work wouldn't be possible without you."
NarratorHe turned to me.
Symon Holl"The ultimate goal here is to end the recession."
Symon Holl"Before we move ahead and start formulating a plan, we need you to determine our direction, Mr. President."
Symon Holl"We have a few possibilities as a result of the work we have done so far."
Symon Holl"We can increase, maintain or decrease the taxes for large corporations. I believe some of us in the room have a few strong opinions about this."
Symon Holl"In parallel, we can increase, maintain or decrease the taxes for small and medium businesses."
Symon Holl"Let's start with the large corporations. And we'll move on to small businesses."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:22:11 p20364 / c631
  • ▶“What is the outcome if we increase the taxes for large corporations?”
  • ·“What if we decrease the taxes for large corporations?”
  • ·“Is there any downside to keeping the things as they are?”
  • ·“What does everyone think we should do?”
Understanding the impact of increasing taxes on large corporations is crucial for balancing fiscal responsibility and economic growth.
DeepSeek-R1"What is the outcome if we increase the taxes for large corporations?"
Symon Holl"It's not an option at this point. Our economy is in shambles."
Symon Holl"Taking what's remaining from the businesses will destroy the economy further."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:22:58 p20366 / c390
  • ▶“We could balance it by decreasing taxes of small businesses to put some relief on them.”
  • ·“I see.”
  • ·“So, we can't do it?”
Replacing the striking workers would allow the project to continue without further delays, demonstrating strong leadership and commitment to infrastructure development.
DeepSeek-R1"We could balance it by decreasing taxes of small businesses to put some relief on them."
Symon Holl"I see. I'm not sure if that's a good idea with the current state of our economy..."
Edith Agnoc"I do believe there is one way to get out of recession. It is through the trade and the economic contribution of large corporations. Increasing their taxes will turn them on you."
Mikail Aven"I will go a step further. Increasing their taxes will have disastrous results and it will make you an enemy of them. Trust me, Mr. President. Nobody wants to make an enemy of them."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:23:42 p20395 / c359
  • ·“Maybe you are right.”
  • ▶“It goes both ways, Mr. Aven.”
Decreasing taxes on small businesses could provide needed relief and stimulate growth, while avoiding the risks of increasing taxes on large corporations during a recession.
DeepSeek-R1"It goes both ways, Mr. Aven."
Mikail Aven"Of course."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:24:26 p20496 / c386
  • ·“What if we decrease the taxes for large corporations?”
  • ·“Is there any downside to keeping the things as they are?”
  • ▶“What does everyone think we should do?”
  • ·“I am ready to make a decision.”
It's wise to seek everyone's opinion before making a decision, as it allows for a well-informed choice.
DeepSeek-R1"What does everyone think we should do?"
Symon Holl"My personal opinion is that we should decrease the taxes for large corporations. Although in short term it would cost us, in the long run it would be the most helpful option in ending the recession."
Edith Agnoc"Our international trade is driven mostly by the products of these corporations. They are the backbone of our economy."
Mikail Aven"Exactly. If we let them perform better by decreasing their taxes, this should in turn lead to Sordland receiving more investment. This means more capital which means a quicker way out of the recession."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:25:15 p20561 / c482
  • ·“What if we decrease the taxes for large corporations?”
  • ·“Is there any downside to keeping the things as they are?”
  • ▶“I am ready to make a decision.”
Anton Rayne is ready to make a decision on tax policies to address the recession.
DeepSeek-R1"I am ready to make a decision."
Symon Holl"Very well, Mr. President. We want to ensure the economy is on a balanced track as further destabilization might have drastic consequences."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:26:24 p20524 / c955
  • ·“Increase the taxes for large corporations.” [+3 Government Budget]
  • ·“We'll increase the taxes for large corporations and balance it with a decrease for small businesses.”
  • ·“Maintain the taxes for large corporations.”
  • ▶“Decrease the taxes for large corporations.” [-4 Government Budget]
  • ·“We'll decrease the taxes for large corporations and balance the budget with an increase for smaller businesses.” [-2 Government Budget]
Decreasing taxes for large corporations could stimulate investment and economic growth despite the impact on the government budget.
DeepSeek-R1"Decrease the taxes for large corporations."
Mikail Aven"An excellent decision."
Edith Agnoc"I agree. Thanks to this, the recession will be solved in no time."
Symon Holl"I also think this was a good choice."
Symon Holl"Well then. I have taken a note of our action and I will put it to implementation as soon as possible."
NarratorSymon opened his briefcase and swapped out the stack of papers he had in front with another stack.
Symon Holl"Let's move on to the small and medium businesses."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:27:11 p20501 / c409
  • ·“What is the outcome if we increase the taxes for small and medium businesses?”
  • ▶“What if we decrease the taxes for small and medium businesses?”
  • ·“Is there any downside to keeping the things as they are?”
Decreasing taxes for small and medium businesses would provide much-needed relief and stimulate economic activity during a recession.
DeepSeek-R1"What if we decrease the taxes for small and medium businesses?"
Symon Holl"It should be helpful in curbing the recession for sure. Of course that will come at the cost of losing some of our government budget."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:28:00 p20459 / c455
  • ·“What is the outcome if we increase the taxes for small and medium businesses?”
  • ▶“Is there any downside to keeping the things as they are?”
  • ·“I am ready to make a decision.”
Keeping taxes as they are avoids further burdening small businesses while allowing time to assess economic impacts.
DeepSeek-R1"Is there any downside to keeping the things as they are?"
Symon Holl"Sordland needs change to get out of recession. But the current state of the economy is in shambles. There is not much change we can do about taxes."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:28:47 p20381 / c419
  • ·“What is the outcome if we increase the taxes for small and medium businesses?”
  • ▶“I am ready to make a decision.”
Decreasing taxes for small businesses would stimulate economic growth and job creation, supporting the manifesto goal of economic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"I am ready to make a decision."
Symon Holl"Very well."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:29:42 p20344 / c647

  • ·“Maintain the taxes for small and medium businesses.”
  • ▶“Decrease the taxes for small and medium businesses.” [-3 Government Budget]
Decreasing taxes for small and medium businesses will stimulate economic growth by encouraging investment and job creation.
DeepSeek-R1"Decrease the taxes for small and medium businesses."
Symon Holl"Very well. I have taken another note. We will look into how to go about implementing these changes."
Symon Holl"Well then... Thank you everyone. That will be all for today."

NarratorThey left the meeting room. I decided to stay a bit longer.

■ School VisitT04

BUDGET -5WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorAs part of the planned program, we arrived in Leiren to visit a middle school.
NarratorThe prestigious Toren Hill School was situated close to the suburbs just outside of the city centre. The building was flanked by two hills, each beautifully dotted with shrubs and flowers.
NarratorLucian, Ciara and I got out of our cars and met at the entrance. Our presence had not gone unnoticed. Apart from our guards, there were at least ten reporters following us around.
Lucian Galade"That must be the principal. Right this way, sir."
NarratorThe principal was a man of short stature with a bushy moustache and a pair of glasses. He approached us quickly, but was stopped by the security guards. They started patting him down and searching him.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:31:41 p20289 / c341
  • ▶“That won't be necessary. Let him through.”
  • ·Wait for them to finish.
Allowing the principal to pass without further delay shows respect and confidence in our security protocols.
DeepSeek-R1"That won't be necessary. Let him through."
Principal"Mr. President. Such an honor to meet with you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:32:29 p20253 / c421
  • ▶Shake his hand and smile for the cameras.
  • ·Shake his hand.
Shaking hands and smiling for the cameras would project a positive image and enhance public relations.
DeepSeek-R1We shook hands and I turned around to smile for the cameras. Reporters instantly snapped a few photos.
Principal"Welcome to our humble school."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:33:17 p20225 / c481
  • ·“Sorry about the security guards. It's a necessity. Standard procedure.”
  • ▶“It's good to be here.”
  • ·“Let's begin.”
To maintain a positive public image and show support for the principal's concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"It's good to be here."
Principal"Excellent. You must be Mr. Galade."
Lucian Galade"It's a pleasure to meet you."
Principal"And Ms. Walda. So good of you to come all the way out here."
Ciara Walda"Thank you."
NarratorAt the entrance, the school's motto was inscribed on the wall in giant letters. "Instreten sis er Anginicar av Gereigna, int olla Almergwolltene, int olla Mexis."
Principal"Education is the premise of progress, in all societies, in all families."
NarratorWe walked through the gates. There were many students lined up in front of the stairs of the main building.
NarratorAs we entered the students started singing the national anthem.
NarratorThe principal proudly looked at them.
Principal"Marvelous, my dear students."
NarratorAfter the anthem finished. All of the students immediately snapped to attention and turned to me.
Ciara Walda"Looks more like a military institution than a school."
Principal"Exactly. That's what we pride ourselves on. Discipline has the utmost importance in our school."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:34:03 p20174 / c364
  • ·“Some discipline never hurt anyone.”
  • ·“Very good.”
  • ▶“We need more educated people, not soldiers.”
Emphasizing education over military discipline aligns with long-term development goals.
DeepSeek-R1"We need more educated people, not soldiers."
Ciara Walda"On that we are aligned."
Principal"I can assure you that our students are very well educated. In fact our school is one of the top ten middle schools in all of Sordland."
Principal"Now, we will watch the students take their daily Student Oath."
NarratorA boy and a girl went up to the stage and grabbed their microphones. The students turned to them.
Boy"I am a Sord! My strength lies in my blood!"
Girl"I will respect our honorable elders! I will protect the young!"
Boy"My life belongs to Sordland now and it will belong to it forever!"
Girl"I will always follow the guiding light!"
Boy"Of Colonel Soll and the Great Sords!"
Girl"A Morgna wes core! Vectern sis da!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:34:48 p20073 / c375
  • ·Clap loudly.
  • ▶Nod at the kids.
  • ·Do nothing.
Nodding at the kids is a subtle show of approval that maintains decorum and acknowledges their effort without reinforcing an overly nationalistic environment.
DeepSeek-R1I nodded at the kids.
Lucian Galade"Words to remember."
Ciara Walda"What? No one even remembers it after they graduate."
Principal"If you wish to follow me, we will take a look at one of our classrooms."
NarratorThrough the hallways, we made our way to classroom 7-A. As soon as I entered the whole classroom rose up and snapped to attention.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:35:39 p19979 / c544
  • ▶“Sit down, please.”
  • ·“At ease.”
Anton wants to maintain discipline and order, emphasizing his role as a strong leader.
DeepSeek-R1"Sit down please."
NarratorThe students sat down. The same boy and girl walked up to us.
Principal"These are our top students and our top class. This here is my son."
NarratorThe boy came before me and saluted me like a military officer.
Principal"Tell Mr. President what you want to be."
Boy"Mr. President! I want to be a general, sir!"
Boy"I am ready to sacrifice myself for my country and you, sir!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:36:35 p19860 / c660
  • ·“This is nonsense. What are you teaching to these students?”
  • ▶“Good. You will make a fine soldier in the future. At ease, general!”
  • ·“I will do whatever I can to not let that happen.”
Supporting the student's military aspirations aligns with maintaining stability and order.
DeepSeek-R1"Good. You will make a fine soldier in the future. At ease, general!"
Boy"Thank you, sir!"
NarratorThe principal squeezed his son's shoulders and the boy looked up and smiled to his father. He patted him on the back and sent him off to his seat.
NarratorThe cameras flashed.
Ciara Walda"This is simply ridiculous. You are raising soldiers here, not students!"
Principal"I am so sorry. I will immediately perform disciplinary actions."
Ciara Walda"How about you, young lady? What do you think about this?"
Principal"She is our top student in this school. Her excellent grades are to--."
Ciara Walda"I didn't ask you."
NarratorShe bent down and gently addressed the young student.
Ciara Walda"What do you think about your school? Just between us girls."
Girl"I-"
Girl"I'm sick of having to knit and sew while the boys learn science and chemistry. I'm smarter than all of them. Why do I have to take these stupid handcraft classes?"
Principal"Ssssh! Do you know who you are talki-"
Ciara Walda"Shut up. Let her speak."
NarratorCiara's presence had emboldened the girl. She went on.
Girl"My math and reading scores are the best in the school. But they won't let me take the classes I want, just because I'm a girl."
Girl"I want to be a scientist! Not some soldier's wife!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:37:25 p19671 / c405
  • ·“You heard her, Mr. Principal. You will make the necessary changes. At once.”
  • ·“But knitting and sewing are important skills to have for a woman.”
  • ▶“We need more people like you in this country. I applaud your determination.”
Praising her determination supports gender equality without overstepping into policy.
DeepSeek-R1"We need more people like you in this country. I applaud your determination."
Ciara Walda"We need more than words. We need action."
Ciara Walda"Concrete action. We need to protect girls like this."
NarratorThe cameras flashed once again.
NarratorI looked around the classroom and saw a large portrait of Colonel Soll.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:38:14 p19516 / c505
  • ·“Take that down.”
  • ▶Don't say anything.
  • ·“An excellent portrait of Colonel Soll. Very lifelike.”
Maintaining neutrality allows me to focus on addressing the girl's concerns without alienating traditional supporters.
DeepSeek-R1I didn't say anything.
Lucian Galade"Well then. Unfortunately we are almost out of time. We have other meetings to attend to today."
Lucian Galade"Thank you for the warm welcome."
Ciara Walda"Mr. President. Lucian. Please go ahead. I would like to speak with the girl alone."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:39:06 p19439 / c587
  • ▶“Very well.”
  • ·“Lucian. Let's leave.”
  • ·“Thank you for the tour.”
Decreasing taxes for large corporations is chosen to stimulate economic growth through business investment, despite potential budget strain.
DeepSeek-R1"Very well."
Principal"Thank you for visiting our school, sir."
Principal"Come on class. One. Two."
NarratorAll students in the classroom yelled but the boy whom I met was the loudest.
Boy"A Morgna wes core!"
