■ Education ReformT04

BUDGET -5WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorWe were still in Leiren, staying at a modest guesthouse for state employees. Ciara set up a meeting with me and Lucian to go over her plans for education reform after our school visit.
NarratorLucian was seated across from me at the table, going through documents. Ciara entered the room.
Ciara Walda"After the visit yesterday, I couldn't even sleep thinking about that little boy and girl."
Ciara Walda"I have never been more sure of my intentions to reform our education system."
NarratorLucian took off his reading glasses and put it on the table.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:41:52 p19037 / c519
  • ▶“I agree. An educational reform is necessary.”
  • ·“I don't see what's wrong with our education system.”
Anton Rayne agrees with the need for educational reform after witnessing the issues at the middle school, recognizing it as a necessary step despite current economic challenges.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree. An educational reform is necessary."
Ciara Walda"Exactly."
Ciara Walda"Those students have to repeat that same Sollist propaganda every single day. They are being brainwashed at an age when their minds need to be free."
Ciara Walda"On top of that, young girls are denied the same opportunity as boys. They're forced into the role of housewife before they even reach puberty! Nothing's changed since I went to school."
Ciara Walda"Women like me, Lileas, Nia, even your wife -- we had to study twice as hard to receive the same university education that you and Lucian took for granted. Is that the kind of country you want your daughter to grow up in?"
Ciara Walda"Mr. President. We need to bring change. We need to free the minds of these young people. They are our future."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:42:33 p19181 / c334
  • ·“Very well. What do we need to do for an education reform?”
  • ▶“Everything is fine as it is. Why change it?”
Anton decides to maintain the status quo due to economic constraints and the need for stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Everything is fine as it is. Why change it?"
Ciara Walda"I don't think my words are getting through to you."
Ciara Walda"Are you really fine with filling the heads of our children with Sollist propaganda? With teaching girls like Deana that their only value to this country is as vessels for childbearing?"
Ciara Walda"You know the words of Artor S. Wisci. You know them better than I do."
Ciara Walda"Education is the premise of progress, in all societies, in all families."
Ciara Walda"We need to reform our education system. Otherwise, Sordland's future is bleak."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:43:17 p19290 / c357
  • ·“You have a point. Alright. So what's your plan?”
  • ▶“We have more important matters to think about than education for a bunch of kids.”
  • ·“We will not stray away from Soll's path.”
Prioritizing economic stability over education reform reflects a pragmatic focus on immediate challenges.
DeepSeek-R1"We have more important matters to think about than education for a bunch of kids."
Ciara Walda"I can't believe it. This will be seen as a huge mistake when people look back."
NarratorCiara slumped in defeat.
NarratorCiara looked furious. She shook her head slowly.
Ciara Walda"I am utterly disappointed. Especially after yesterday's school visit. I thought it would awaken something in you."
NarratorShe let out a deep sigh.
Lucian Galade"Now that the decision was made, let's move on."
Ciara Walda"Very well."
NarratorCiara skimmed over her documents before continuing.
Ciara Walda"With no change in budget, the ministry was left to its own devices. We made some tough choices but still lack additional funds."
Ciara Walda"Therefore we are investigating if privatizing education would draw enough funds to conduct investments."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:43:57 p19180 / c286
  • ·“What are the advantages of privatization?"
  • ▶“There is a possibility of privatization having negative side effects.”
Privatizing education could worsen existing economic inequalities and divert resources from critical areas, risking social unrest.
DeepSeek-R1"There is a possibility of privatization having negative side effects."
Ciara Walda"Trust me, I know that. It could increase the cost of a quality education, but that is hardly avoidable now anyway."
Lucian Galade"Private education is inconsistent with our overall economic strategy of a planned economy."
NarratorA few minutes of deliberation passed.
Ciara Walda"We can manage without additional funds. The only reason I wanted to consider it is to have more money to improve education."
Ciara Walda"So, what is your decision?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:44:40 p19220 / c385
  • ·“Initiate privatization to draw more funds. This should allow for more competition and quality.” [+1 Government Budget]
  • ▶“Keep education solely a matter of the state. We need to prevent interference from private interests."
Maintaining education as a state matter preserves equality and prevents potential social division from privatization.
DeepSeek-R1"Keep education solely a matter of the state. We need to prevent interference from private interests."
Ciara Walda"Will do. That should be it for now. I will get to work and report back later."
Lucian Galade"This has been a very productive day. I will be returning to Holsord."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:45:17 p19235 / c270
  • ▶“Thank you for your time.”
  • ·“See you later.”
  • ·“Have a good day.”
A polite and appropriate response to conclude the conversation after discussing the donation request.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you for your time everybody."
NarratorWe dispersed after spending nearly the entire day together. I took a moment simply to breathe the fresh evening air of Leiren.
NarratorI felt calmer here, somehow. But I would have to return to the chaos of Holsord soon enough.

■ Decision Gruni Rural DevelopmentT04

BUDGET -5WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
EventGruni Rural Development Investment: The Gruni Rural Development Investment is a proposal by Rural Development Minister Gus Manger to bolster Sordland's rural economies and secure the nation's food sustainability. The plan envisages a judicious allocation of resources to vitalize agricultural communities, fortify food security, and elevate the regional economy of Gruni. One potential strategy is the provision of subsidies for new farms, encouraging more players into the agricultural field. Alternatively, Manger proposes an investment in the mechanization of existing farms to improve productivity or the initiation of crop research to foster innovation.
EVENT DeepSeek-R110:46:12 p19066 / c305
  • ·Subsidies for new farms [-2 Government Budget]
  • ▶Mechanize existing farms [-1 Government Budget]
  • ·Initiate new crop research [-1 Government Budget]
  • ·Don't fund
Mechanizing existing farms will improve productivity efficiently within the limited budget.

■ Convince GloriaT04

BUDGET -6WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorI arrived at the Grand National Assembly, which was only a five-minute drive away from the Palace.
NarratorToday, I had a private meeting with Gloria Tory, one of the leading figures of the USP, to get her approval for the proposal. She was also key in reaching 150 signatures to propose the new Constitution, as she had extensive control over our party's conservative and moderate wings.
NarratorAs I walked through the corridors of the Grand National Assembly, I was confronted by my memories of the historic building. Despite being a member of Assembly not long ago, it now felt like the distant past.
NarratorI walked into the left wing of the Assembly which currently acted as the quarters of the USP. Gloria's office was the first one inside.
NarratorI knocked on the door and entered.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:46:53 p19396 / c215
  • ·"Good morning, Mrs. Tory."
  • ▶"Hello Gloria."
  • ·"Here I am."
Maintaining a balance between formality and approachability.
DeepSeek-R1"Hello Gloria."
Gloria Tory"Good to see you here, Mr. Rayne."
NarratorIt was a small, neatly organised office. There were small figurines, photos and plants around the place. It had a homey feel, unlike my own Assembly office when I had worked there.
NarratorOn her gesture, I took a seat. The chair was very uncomfortable.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:47:34 p19449 / c350
  • ▶"How are you doing, Mrs. Tory?"
  • ·"I love what you did with the office."
A polite greeting is appropriate in a formal setting to establish rapport.
DeepSeek-R1"How are you doing, Mrs. Tory?"
Gloria Tory"I'm fine, Mr. Rayne. How are you?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:48:15 p19433 / c346
  • ·"I'm very happy to see you, Mrs. Tory. It's been a while."
  • ▶"I'm fine too, thank you."
  • ·"As good as I can be."
A polite and neutral response is appropriate for a formal meeting with an influential party figure.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm fine too, thank you."
Gloria Tory"Anyway, let's keep this simple, Mr. Rayne."
Gloria Tory"We both know why we are here. You want me to give you my support."
Gloria Tory"I don't see why the backbone of the USP would support your rushed reforms. Don't you realize they go directly against the wishes of the founder of this party and the state?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:49:02 p19502 / c534
  • ·"The reforms are not against anyone. It's all in the name of democracy."
  • ▶"Mrs. Tory, I need you to help me here."
  • ·"My reforms are very critical for the future of Sordland, Mrs. Tory."
Gloria's support is crucial for party unity, and appealing to her sense of duty as a key figure in the USP can leverage her influence.
DeepSeek-R1"Mrs. Tory, I need you to help me here."
Gloria Tory"So you want me to help you destroy what our party has worked so hard to build?"
Gloria Tory"Even though you owe everything to Colonel Soll, I think you never liked him, nor the party he built."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:49:47 p19438 / c430
  • ·"There is nothing personal about this, Mrs. Tory."
  • ▶"I would never betray our party."
  • ·"How dare you suggest I never liked Soll? I'm a proud Sollist."
Affirming loyalty to the party helps maintain support from key figures like Gloria Tory.
DeepSeek-R1"I would never betray our party."
Gloria Tory"Whatever you intend to do, you will be facing quite the opposition."
Gloria Tory"That's all I can say."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:50:39 p19416 / c628
  • ·"I'm sure we can make a deal."
  • ·"Perhaps I could change your thoughts on the matter...with some well placed investments?"
  • ▶"You must understand that these reforms are a necessity. Let me explain."
Explaining the necessity of reforms can address Gloria's concerns about betraying party principles.
DeepSeek-R1"You must understand that these reforms are a necessity. Let me explain."
Gloria Tory"How?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:51:18 p19426 / c292
  • ·"We need these reforms to fix our problems with the unrestricted power of the Supreme Court."
  • ▶"The people of Sordland demanded change. The USP must give them what they want. We need the people's support."
  • ·"The USP is a party that knows how to react in changing times. We must lead the nation into the future."
This response directly addresses Gloria's concern by emphasizing the people's demands and the need for the USP to maintain their support, thereby justifying the reforms as necessary for the party's survival and relevance.
DeepSeek-R1"The people of Sordland demanded change. The USP must give them what they want. We need the people's support."
Gloria Tory"I am aware that our popularity has been on the decline."
Gloria Tory"But I blame it on this administration, Mr. Rayne. Plus, these reforms are not about the people, are they?"
Gloria Tory"I seriously do not know if the conservatives would be willing to support you at all."
Gloria Tory"Especially considering the fact that you're trying to change the Member of Honor laws. This is clearly a move against President Soll."
Gloria Tory"You want to punish him for the aggressive policies he pursued in the past? Is that the intention here? You can't win this fight, Mr. President. Soll built this republic in the midst of blood and chaos. He demands respect."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:52:04 p19459 / c490
  • ·"This is not about Soll."
  • ·"This is a shame. I was hoping we could work together."
  • ▶"I have no intention of putting Colonel Soll on trial."

This response directly addresses Gloria's concerns about betraying Colonel Soll, showing respect for his legacy while clarifying that the reforms are not targeting him personally.
DeepSeek-R1"I have no intention of putting Colonel Soll on trial."
Gloria Tory"Then why are you taking his immunity away? He isn't even a public figure since he retired from politics. What prompted you to include this in your so called 'democracy' package?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:52:54 p19423 / c578

DeepSeek-R1"How about you answer me this: Why did Soll grant himself absolute immunity and protection? What is he scared of?"
Gloria Tory"Maybe it was a security mechanism against traitors like..."
NarratorShe cleared her throat.
Gloria Tory"Anyway... I am not going to drag this out for any longer. Will you listen to the party or keep pushing your own agenda?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:53:33 p19473 / c295

DeepSeek-R1"No. We could talk about the other clauses instead."
Gloria Tory"It seems you don't understand."
Gloria Tory"All right, Mr. President. I'm sorry I won't be able to help you. I wish you luck gathering the signatures you need."
NarratorShe suddenly stood up.
Gloria Tory"I must get back to work. Thanks for stopping by, Mr. President."
NarratorShe extended her hand to me for a handshake.

NarratorI left her room.
NarratorAlbin and the reformist wing was my last hope to get the needed 150 signatures for the proposal.
NarratorEven then, it looked quite uncertain if we could get 166 votes in the Assembly.
NarratorI went outside. Serge drove me back to the palace, where I met Lucian and told him the bad news.

■ Decision Play Chess With LucianT04

BUDGET -6WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
EventLucian Offers to Play Chess: Late in the evening, Lucian extends an invitation for a strategic game of chess within the solemn quiet of the Presidential Office.
EVENT DeepSeek-R110:56:09 p19130 / c313
  • ·Play chess
  • ▶Don't play and relax at home with family
Prioritizing rest and family over further work to maintain focus and energy for future challenges.

■ Convince AlbinT04

BUDGET -6WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorAfter our meeting with Gloria Tory, and further discussions with the party and my cabinet, it was time to talk to Albin Clavin to get the reformist wing to support the proposal.
NarratorI also planned to finally present a more fleshed out proposal and declare our agenda to the party after our meeting.
NarratorI walked into the left wing of the Assembly and entered the quarters of the USP. When I reached the meeting hall, I saw many of our members conversing inside.
NarratorAfter taking a glimpse inside, I went back into the corridor to look for the office of Albin Clavin, which was around the back of the place.
NarratorWhen I reached his room, I knocked on his door and entered.
Albin Clavin"Mr. President! Welcome!"
NarratorThe whole room was surrounded by bookshelves packed with files and papers. It was a small office, with books and dossiers lying on every corner. It felt claustrophobic.
NarratorOn his gesture, I took a seat.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:56:54 p19368 / c329
  • ▶"How is everything going, Mr. Clavin?"
  • ·"Did a bomb drop in this office?"
Choosing a polite greeting opens the door for constructive dialogue with Albin Clavin.
DeepSeek-R1"How is everything going, Mr. Clavin?"
Albin Clavin"It's been busy. The party has been working very hard, Mr. President. Thank you for asking."
Albin Clavin"How is it going for you in the Palace?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:57:39 p19380 / c450
  • ·"It's pretty tough."
  • ·"I'm feeling pretty good."
  • ▶"It depends on our meeting today."
Anton should acknowledge the critical nature of their meeting to secure support from Albin Clavin.
DeepSeek-R1"It depends on our meeting today."
Albin Clavin"Oh, I see. Then I'll try my best not to make it any worse for you, Mr. President."
Albin Clavin"Our team was disappointed by the veto of the Workers' Rights Act. We worked day and night for weeks. It would have boosted your public opinion too, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:58:20 p19439 / c334
  • ·"It wasn't a good time for a drastic changes which would also costs the government."
  • ▶"The businesses would be burdened even further. In this climate it would damage the economy."

The businesses would be burdened even further. In this climate it would damage the economy.
DeepSeek-R1"The businesses would be burdened even further. In this climate it would damage the economy."
Albin Clavin"I understand your perspective but haven't the companies benefitted from this situation and made profit for a long time?"
Albin Clavin"It's just that we thought the Palace wouldn't let us down."
NarratorAlbin sighed.
Albin Clavin"Anyway, sir, let's talk about the proposal. Thanks for coming here for my input because there are some subjects where Gloria and I don't see eye to eye."
Albin Clavin"I have to say that most of the progressives were already starting to warm up to you. Your attempt at changing the constitution created quite a bit of excitement. One could dare to say an excitement that hasn't been felt since the 30's..."
Albin Clavin"Yet, there are some issues with the current status of the draft proposal. So we may need to contain our excitement a little longer."
Albin Clavin"I cannot support this in its current state."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R110:59:00 p19409 / c298

  • ·"What's the problem?"
  • ·"I thought you were a democrat."
  • ·"The party should respect my decisions."
I need your support, Mr. Clavin.

Albin Clavin"A fact that I'm aware of."
Albin Clavin"But at this point, even my support would not be enough."

Albin Clavin"Where to even begin?"

Albin Clavin"Yet I see that our suggestions were completely ignored."

Albin Clavin"Oh?"

TURN 05

■ Bill Religious HarmonyT05

BUDGET -6WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
Bill for decisionReligious Harmony Bill — In an endeavor to promote religious harmony and unity, the Grand National Assembly introduces the Religious Harmony Bill (R.H.B.), establishing regulations for religious affairs across Sordland. Section I of the R.H.B. mandates that all sermons conducted during the Day of Descension ceremony at the Arch-sanctuary of Deyr will henceforth be held in the Sordish language. This move is aimed at promoting inclusivity and common understanding among worshippers. Section II of the R.H.B. regulates the language of sermons in other sanctuaries across Sordland. It prohibits the use of Bludish for sermons unless explicit approval has been obtained from the Archpriest of Sordland. This measure ensures consistency and unity in religious practices across the country. Section III of the R.H.B. sets forth new regulations for priests seeking state-sanctioned positions. It requires that all applicants must have Sordish lineage to be eligible for their salaries and pensions. This criterion ensures a deeper connection with Sordland's cultural heritage among the religious leaders of the country.
BILL DeepSeek-R111:10:05 p19471 / c568
  • ·Sign: Religious Harmony Bill
  • ▶Veto: Religious Harmony Bill
The Religious Harmony Bill risks alienating minority groups by restricting language use and imposing lineage requirements, potentially increasing social tensions. As a reformist aiming to maintain stability and unity, vetoing the bill preserves diversity and prevents potential unrest.

■ Decision Financial Aid ArcasiaT05

BUDGET -6WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
EventArcasia Offers Aid: Arcasian Foreign Minister Laurent Martell has proposed an extensive aid package for Sordland. This package includes financial assistance, logistical support, and equipment aid. In return, Arcasia seeks refueling and repair access at Sordland's Erlory Air Force Base, in addition to occasional flyover rights in Sordish airspace. Arcasian President Dwight Walker has personally assured this agreement aims for peaceful objectives.
EVENT DeepSeek-R111:11:14 p19543 / c555
  • ▶Accept Arcasian aid and give access to base [+2 Government Budget]
  • ·Reject
Accepting the aid provides immediate budget relief and resources needed for stability, outweighing concerns about foreign influence.

■ Immigration PolicyT05

BUDGET -4WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorWe were in the middle of a meeting in the Palace about our immigration policy.
NarratorThe ensuing discussion quickly became heated.
Iosef Lancea"I don't think so, Mr. Wisci!"
Iosef Lancea"It just doesn't make any sense to me that a foreign citizen should come before a Sordish citizen. In Sordland!"
Lileas Graf"Well said. My analysis is also in line with this. Especially during the economic recession... We are giving Sordish jobs to migrants from other countries. What about our own people?"
Deivid Wisci"Immigrants are the reason our economy is not completely unsalvageable."
Deivid Wisci"Look at Agnolian immigrants in Agnland. They have revitalized the whole region and in turn, boosted the economy of the entire country. Symon can attest to that."
Symon Holl"Statistically speaking, allowing immigrants to become part of the workforce contributes to our economy. But I agree that the current situation is very nuanced."
Symon Holl"With that said, we can't just tighten our immigration policy and expect the economy to stay the same."
Symon Holl"We need to put our economy and the recession at the forefront."
Symon Holl"Thankfully, we have just started receiving financial aid from Arcasia."
Iosef Lancea"And then what? We'll become their servants?"
Iosef Lancea"First they will place their war planes in Erlory. Then their armies will come. You will see."
Iosef Lancea"I do not approve of this decision at all."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:12:35 p20007 / c561
  • ·“I will not let you question my decisions, Iosef.”
  • ·“Don't be dramatic. We need to be friendly with our potential allies.”
  • ▶“Desperate times call for desperate measures.”
  • ·“I have done this with Sordish citizens in mind. Nothing else.”
Desperate times require taking desperate measures to secure much-needed aid for the struggling economy.
DeepSeek-R1"Desperate times call for desperate measures."
Iosef Lancea"That's how they take over countries, you know."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Lancea has a point. We have seen it happen many times."
Deivid Wisci"Gentlemen."
Deivid Wisci"Can we please get back on topic?"
Symon Holl"Right. Sorry, Mr. Wisci."
Deivid Wisci"As I was just saying before I was interrupted..."
Deivid Wisci"Look at the superpowers. Do you see them closing off their borders? Why do you think their economies are in such good shape? They are in their current state because they welcome immigration, and use it to their advantage."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:13:20 p19930 / c359
  • ·“We need to modernize and reach the level of the superpowers.”
  • ▶“I agree that we need to consider the economic impact of immigration.”
  • ·“Sordish citizens must come first.”
Considering the current economic instability, evaluating the impact of immigration on the economy is crucial for ensuring stability.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to consider the impact on economy."
Symon Holl"Our money flow should be our utmost concern, if we want to have the capability to accomplish actual change."
Deivid Wisci"We need a decision. But before we move on..."
Deivid Wisci"Mr. President. Do you have any questions about the current state of immigration?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:14:04 p19983 / c357
  • ·“What is the current legal status of immigrants?”
  • ·“How many immigrants live in Sordland?”
  • ▶“How do immigrants usually arrive here?”
  • ·“I don't have any questions. Let's move on to solutions.”
Understanding how immigrants arrive will provide insight into current immigration processes and challenges.
DeepSeek-R1"How do immigrants usually arrive here?"
Deivid Wisci"There are three major paths they choose. The first one is the Agnland to Agnolia border. The second is the Bergia to Wehlen border, and the third is the Lorren Coast."
Iosef Lancea"Agnolians are the largest group. It's a light border over there. Although recently, political fugitives from Valgsland have been crossing the Grey Sea by boat to land on our coast."
Lileas Graf"Politically persecuted Wehzek refugees tend to use the dangerous mountain passes at the Wehlen border. Many of them also have Bludish blood. This is significantly increasing the Blud populace inside Bergia."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:14:50 p20208 / c439
  • ·“What is the current legal status of immigrants?”
  • ·“How many immigrants live in Sordland?”
  • ▶“Let's move on and see what can be done about the immigration situation.”
Moving on to solutions demonstrates proactive leadership and aligns with the manifesto's focus on economic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's move on and see what can be done about the immigration situation."
Deivid Wisci"Very well."
NarratorDeivid Wisci opened his dossier stamped with the seal of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade.
Deivid Wisci"Before we make any decisions, I have to remind you."
Deivid Wisci"During the elections we promised to keep our immigration laws relaxed. Which I think is the right course of action."
Iosef Lancea"I disagree. We are supposed to control and reduce the negative effects of immigration, like crime and subversion."
Symon Holl"I see your point, Iosef but the economy certainly benefits from more immigration."
Lileas Graf"Internal order on the other hand, suffers immensely."
Lileas Graf"We should tighten the laws to prevent further chaos."
NarratorDeivid sighed wearily.
Deivid Wisci"This is going nowhere."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:15:35 p20021 / c392
  • ▶“If we decide to tighten immigration, what does that entail?”
  • ·“What are the consequences of keeping our immigration policy relaxed?”
  • ·“I am ready to make a decision.”

DeepSeek-R1"If we decide to tighten immigration, what does that entail?"
Deivid Wisci"We would have to impose quotas and, connected to that, stricter border control."
Symon Holl"On top of allowing our economy to take a hit."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Holl. Surely the well-being of our citizens and our internal security will more than compensate for any downturn in the markets."
Iosef Lancea"Agreed. Sordish citizens should take priority."

Deivid Wisci"Aside from keeping us on the path to prosperity, modernity and continuous improvement...Not much. Our current system is good for our era. We also have the capacity."
Symon Holl"This will allow us to continue protecting investments from foreign countries, which in turn will keep our trade steady. We would be more trustworthy partners. Stability draws money."
Lileas Graf"Stability? What stability? We're already seeing clashes between our people and foreign interlopers. On the contrary, the only way we can achieve stability is through tightening our immigration policy."
Iosef Lancea"You heard the lady. Sordland first..."
“I am ready to make a decision.”

Deivid Wisci"Yes?"
NarratorEveryone leaned forward, eager to hear my response.

Deivid Wisci"I am glad you made the right choice."
Iosef Lancea"We will be forced to handle new waves of migrants from Agnolia. Our border posts aren't ready."
Lileas Graf"Not only that, we've just abandoned the native Sords who need our support the most."

■ Personal Petr PartyT05

BUDGET -4WEALTH 2STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorFar from becoming accustomed to my workload as president, I only felt more and more snowed under as the weeks passed. It had been ages since I had any time to myself.
NarratorI was starting to lose my focus, and my temper was growing increasingly thin. Petr was the first to realize I needed a break.
NarratorAfter much cajoling, he finally persuaded me to come to a meeting of his new venture: Gentlemen's Club.
NarratorFor the past few months, he'd been hosting a salon of sorts, not just for politicians and businessmen but also for artists, entertainers -- people of taste, he told me with a smile on his face.
NarratorThere was only one rule: no wives or girlfriends allowed. Hence the name.
NarratorAnd so I found myself in front of his new luxury villa in Erlory. Very loud jazz music emanated from inside. I waited and waited. Finally Petr opened the door. I could smell the whiskey on his breath.
Petr Vectern"Anton! Finally! Now the real party can start. Come on in!"
NarratorAs soon as I entered, Petr closed the door and turned to the small crowd.
Petr Vectern"Gentlemen! A minute of your time please."
Petr Vectern"I now have the privilege to present to you... The man himself... The fourth President of Sordland, Mr. Anton Rayne!"
NarratorI'd known him for long enough to understand that he was more than a little drunk. He made an elaborate mock-curtsy as I passed him and walked into the room. The music stopped, and I felt everyone's eyes on me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:21:07 p19329 / c392
  • ·“Thank you, Petr. What's next? A choir? A marching band?”
  • ·“And I present to you, Petr Vectern. The first alcoholic Vice President of Sordland.”
  • ▶“Good evening.”
Maintaining decorum and focus on current challenges is key.
DeepSeek-R1"Good evening."
NarratorPetr nodded at me and turned to the attendees.
Petr Vectern"Dear members, please give a warm welcome to our very special guest! If I may, Anton. Here, take this."
NarratorHe handed me a glass of whiskey and put his hand on my shoulder.
Petr Vectern"We all know why we're gathered here tonight. To celebrate life. To have a brief escape from our tumultuous professions."
Petr Vectern"I personally don't know any better cure for stress than a little drink."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:21:55 p19323 / c469
  • ·“Only a little one?”
  • ▶“I'll drink to that!”
  • ·Raise your glass.
Accepting the drink would strengthen camaraderie with Petr and maintain political alliances.
DeepSeek-R1"I'll drink to that!"
NarratorPeople in the crowd laughed and raised their glasses.
Petr Vectern"Now. The tradition dictates that as the club president, I have to remind you of our house rules. There are three. No politics. No wives. No one sober! Cheers!"
NarratorPetr drank the whiskey in his glass in one go.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:23:01 p19371 / c876
  • ▶Sip your whiskey.
  • ·Drain your glass.
Maintaining composure while engaging with political allies.
DeepSeek-R1I took a sip from my whiskey.
Petr Vectern"Anton Rayne might have the power to make presidential decrees, but, I, Petr Vectern, the president of this club, now declare this party started! Music! Go!"
NarratorThe band started up again.
NarratorAfter greeting the crowd around me, I felt a hand on my shoulder.
Petr Vectern"Let me show you around."
NarratorWe walked through the corridors of his mansion. Statues and paintings lined the stark white walls. An arched window provided a view of the neatly manicured grounds, complete with a swimming pool.
NarratorA grand Lespian marble staircase led up to the second story. Petr gestured at the massive crystal chandelier hanging overhead.
Petr Vectern"That chandelier was made in the 18th century. Can you believe it? There's even a hedge maze in the garden!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:23:46 p19646 / c388
  • ·“What's the story behind the chandelier?”
  • ▶“How are you paying for all of this?”
  • ·“An 18th-century chandelier? A hedge maze? A little much, isn't it?”
I want to understand if Petr's extravagance aligns with fiscal responsibility, given the budget constraints.
DeepSeek-R1"How are you paying for all of this?"
Petr Vectern"I'll let you in on a secret. Gus Manger has a lot of contacts in real estate. He brokered an amazing price for me."
Petr Vectern"He'll also be here tonight, by the way."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:24:23 p19645 / c199

DeepSeek-R1"Why am I not surprised? Gus the wheeler and dealer would be right at home at a party like this."
Petr Vectern"Hell, half of the guests here owe their considerable riches to him."
NarratorWe continued walking until we circled back to where we had started.
Petr Vectern"So, what do you think of my little hideaway?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:25:05 p19652 / c354
  • ·“Not bad. Not bad at all.”
  • ▶“It's amazing, but I'd hardly call it a hideaway. More like a palace.”
  • ·“Frankly, I find it disgusting."
A witty compliment fits the social context and maintains good rapport.
DeepSeek-R1"It's amazing, but I'd hardly call it a hideaway. More like a palace."
Petr Vectern"It definitely doesn't rival yours."
Petr Vectern"Anyway, let's get back to the party."
Petr Vectern"I arranged for some caviar to be brought in from Lespia. You're going to love it."
NarratorHe opened the door to the main hall, and we rejoined the other guests. The music was louder now, and the mood decidedly more inebriated.
NarratorCocktail waitresses were carrying around plates of canapés, wearing dresses that left little to the imagination.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:25:58 p19499 / c622
  • ▶“I thought this was a *Gentlemen*'s Club.”
  • ·Gesture at one of the waitresses to come over.
  • ·“So this is what you've been hiding from Evelyn.”

DeepSeek-R1"I thought this was a *Gentlemen*'s Club."
Petr Vectern"Would you rather get served by wrinkly old men? Sweetheart! Over here!"
NarratorHe waved at one of the waitresses, and she came over to us.
NarratorShe was in her mid-twenties, wearing red lipstick, her blonde hair neatly tied into a ponytail. On her plate were toast points slathered in the Lespian caviar Petr had mentioned.
NarratorI took a bite. It was rich, salty, tasting of pure seaside. I could almost hear the sound of waves and seagulls.
Petr Vectern"Lespian caviar, best in the world! I'm telling you, Anton, there are few pleasures in life that are this..."
NarratorAs the waitress left us to serve another attendee, she flashed a quick smile at Petr over her shoulder. He smiled back, a little too broadly.
Petr Vectern"...Sensual."
NarratorHis eyes were still fixed on her, with an expression I remembered from our many nights out together as students.
Petr Vectern"Oh look, there's Gus. Why don't you go talk to him? I'll be back in a minute."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:26:50 p19583 / c569

DeepSeek-R1"Where are you going?"
Petr Vectern"Oh, just to the washroom. See you shortly."
NarratorHe left the room and I headed over to Gus.
NarratorGus Manger was standing next to a couple of men that I recognized as banking industry magnates. As I approached, all of them turned to me and bowed their heads.
Gus Manger"Mr. President. A toast to our new member, everyone!"
NarratorGus and I clinked our glasses as the people around us raised theirs.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:27:29 p19652 / c275
  • ▶“You don't seem at all surprised to see me here.”
  • ·“How long have you been a member?”

DeepSeek-R1"You don't seem at all surprised to see me here."
Gus Manger"That's because Petr told me you were going to come."
Gus Manger"He also told me that you might be interested in potential opportunities such as, well... this."
NarratorHe opened his arms, gesturing at our opulent surroundings.
Gus Manger"Let's take a walk. The balcony has an amazing view of Erlory."
NarratorWe left the room and made our way to the balcony. On the way, he took out two cigars and held one out for me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:28:09 p19569 / c297
  • ·Take the cigar.
  • ▶Refuse the cigar.
Refusing the cigar maintains professionalism and avoids potential distractions.
DeepSeek-R1I turned the cigar down. Gus lit up his own and started smoking.
NarratorWe went out to the balcony. All of Erlory was at our feet. From this vantage point, I was able to see how expansive the building plot was. The swimming pool and the hedge maze were visible from here.
NarratorLeaning over the railing, I admired the view for a moment before Gus spoke up.
Gus Manger"I know you're wondering about the deal Petr made. In a nutshell, thanks to my network he was able to procure this house for half the asking price, which would've been impossible under any other circumstances."
Gus Manger"I would be delighted to discuss a similar arrangement with you. In fact, I have prepared a document with my own personal favorite opportunities."
Gus Manger"Your willingness to invest in Gasom showed you were a man after my own heart. I'd love to talk about how my network can benefit you personally."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:28:53 p19807 / c360
  • ▶“I am not looking to make any investments at the moment.”
  • ·“What's in it for you?”
  • ·“I'm glad my investment decisions are paying off.”
  • ·“Thank you.”
Declining to invest at this time helps preserve the government's limited budget and avoids potential conflicts of interest.
DeepSeek-R1"I am not looking to make any investments at the moment."
Gus Manger"Real estate is the best investment anyone can make. With the current economic situation, it only makes sense to put your money into such a tangible asset."
Gus Manger"But I also have a few other opportunities for you to make a side profit, if real estate doesn't interest you."
Gus Manger"Is there any chance I can convince you?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:29:38 p19782 / c400

DeepSeek-R1"No. That's my final answer."
Gus Manger"Very well. I won't push further."
Gus Manger"If you'll excuse me, I'm going to refresh my drink. See you around."
NarratorHe left to rejoin the party. Alone on the balcony, I looked out over Erlory.
NarratorSuddenly, I heard a rustling from the hedge maze below.
NarratorI looked down and saw a silhouette. Two silhouettes, actually, so close together they seemed to be entangled. I squinted and tried to get a better look. It was definitely two people kissing, but I couldn't see either of their faces.
NarratorI returned to the party and spent some time mingling with the other guests, engaging them in small talk about their businesses, their children, their relationships.
NarratorI realized it had been a while since Petr left. I looked around, but couldn't find him. Instead, my eyes landed on Ewald Alphonso.
NarratorWith a smile, the former president walked over to meet me.

Ewald Alphonso"I saw you speaking to Mr. Manger earlier."

NarratorHe poured us two large glasses of whiskey, and we drank them in one go. Throughout the party, we drank and drank. Just like old times.
NarratorI woke up the next day with a pounding headache and more than a few regrets.
NarratorMy recollections of the evening were hazy, but there was one important detail that kept coming back to me.
NarratorThe red lipstick on Petr's collar.
NarratorBut maybe my memory was playing tricks on me.

■ Personal Festival OpeningT05

BUDGET -4WEALTH 2STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorOur motorcade slowly made its way towards the magnificent Benfi High Arts Center. Night was falling, and the festival was about to begin. I flipped through the brochure that had been given to us at the hotel.
NarratorOn the front page, there was the face of Curtan Leste, mayor of Anrica. He was a Benfi native who had a longstanding relationship with the city's current mayor, and had been personally invited to make tonight's opening speech.
NarratorThe Benfi Festival was one of the biggest events of the year. It originally marked the start of the annual religious pilgrimage, a weeks-long journey still undertaken by devout Nurists. But today it was better known as a cultural celebration that drew thousands of revelers to the city.
NarratorThere were film screenings, concerts, theater and dance performances, art exhibitions, and celebrity-studded nightly galas. The opening ceremonies were broadcast on TV and watched by millions.
NarratorAs the car slowed, a coterie of reporters and security guards rushed to meet us. I turned my attention to Monica.
NarratorSince I'd refused to let her make the opening speech, her attitude toward me had changed. She now only spoke to me if I asked her a question, or if it was something important concerning the children.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:36:03 p19985 / c421
  • ·Say nothing.
  • ▶“Are you still angry about my decision?”
  • ·"Will you ever forgive me? I told you my reasons."
Anton wants to acknowledge Monica's feelings without committing to a deeper conversation.
DeepSeek-R1"Are you still angry about my decision?"
NarratorShe didn't reply at all. She clearly did not want to pursue this topic any further.
NarratorAs soon as Serge opened the door, we were enveloped by the roar of the crowd.
NarratorThe next few minutes were a blur of handshakes, photos and reporters' questions. The onlookers cheered as we walked the red carpet to the newly built arts center.
NarratorWe followed the guards through the cavernous white halls until we came to a set of arched doors leading to the auditorium. We waited outside as security did a quick sweep of the area, then headed in.
NarratorI had been asked to make a quick set of remarks introducing Curtan Leste. I beckoned Monica to join me on the podium.
Monica"I'm going to find my seat."
NarratorMonica brushed past me, took her seat in the front row and crossed her arms. I walked alone to the podium.
NarratorI spoke for just a minute, reading a paragraph my speechwriter had churned out about the importance of the festival and Benfi to Sordland.
NarratorI concluded by inviting Curtan Leste, the Mayor of Anrica, to the podium. I stepped down and joined Monica in the front row. Her arms were still folded.
Curtan Leste"Welcome to the first day of the Benfi Festival."
NarratorHe paused for a moment to let the crowd cheer, and his eyes briefly met mine.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:36:55 p20089 / c493
  • ▶Nod.
  • ·Look away.
Nodding shows respect and acknowledges Curtan Leste's presence, which is appropriate in a public event.
DeepSeek-R1I nodded.
Curtan Leste"This is a night for the people of Benfi -- indeed, for the people of Sordland -- to put aside their differences and come together in celebration."
Curtan Leste"Yet as we unite for the festivities, we must remember the real reason we are gathered here."
NarratorThe crowd fell silent. The people were giving their full attention to what he had to say.
Curtan Leste"This festival was founded around the principles of our cherished religion, Nurity. Yet over the years, it has been severed from its sacred roots and become something else. Something more perverse."
Curtan Leste"An excuse for public drunkenness and grotesque displays of the flesh. An occasion for young women to parade their bodies and engage in lascivious behavior."
NarratorAs he went on, I could see Monica become increasingly agitated.
Monica"What in the hell is he talking about? Lascivious behavior? What century does he think we live in?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:37:39 p20169 / c357
  • ·“Ssshh. Quiet.”
  • ·“I don't know. Just let it be.”
  • ▶Pretend not to hear her.
Anton would choose to pretend not to hear Monica to avoid drawing attention and maintain public composure.
DeepSeek-R1I pretended not to hear her.
NarratorMonica turned back to watching the speech, a frown on her face.
Curtan Leste"Perversion hidden under the guise of art is not befitting the beautiful city of --"
NarratorThe crowd was becoming restless. Many of the younger attendees were murmuring in disagreement, while a few of the older ones were nodding vigorously.
Curtan Leste"As I said, perversion hidden under the guise of art is not be--"
Monica"Enough!"
NarratorThe hall fell silent. Monica rose and continued.
Monica"Mr. Leste, it is not young women who are a disgrace to this fine festival, but you. You and your backwards, puritanical attitudes, which have been holding this country back for far too long--"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:38:28 p20035 / c488
  • ▶“Monica, sit down. You're making a fool of yourself.”
  • ·“Ssshh. Monica. Please sit down.”
  • ·Watch the events unfold.
To maintain public decorum and avoid potential fallout.
DeepSeek-R1"Monica, sit down. You're making a fool of yourself."
Monica"No, I will not sit down this time."
NarratorMonica turned to face the audience, speaking loud enough to be heard without a microphone.
Monica"Women of Sordland, we must not let people like Mr. Leste use religion as a justification to demean and oppress us. Our time has come!"
NarratorShocked gasps ran through the crowd. A few people rose and applauded. Curtan Leste waited to speak.
Curtan Leste"I think Mrs. Rayne is forgetting her place."
Monica"What do you think my place is, Mr. Leste? The kitchen? This festival is not about your retrograde traditions. We are living in modern times now. It's 1954."
Monica"And it isn't *your* place to decide how the people of Benfi -- especially the women -- should be celebrating their own festival."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:39:15 p20055 / c428
  • ▶“Monica, that's enough.”
  • ·Do nothing.
Maintaining decorum and public image is crucial for stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Monica, that's enough."
Monica"What do you mean, enough? I've barely even started."
NarratorI looked at the podium to see Curtan Leste's piercing gaze on me. There would surely be a price to pay for this.
Curtan Leste"Mr. President, could you please control your wife before she embarrasses herself any further?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:40:01 p20125 / c407
  • ·“Monica, get back in your goddamn seat!"
  • ▶“Monica, we've been over this. Now is not the time. Sit down, please.”
  • ·“I'm so sorry about this, Mr. Leste. Please finish your speech.”
  • ·“You know what, Curtan? My wife is absolutely right.”
Maintaining calm and order while addressing Monica's outburst appropriately.
DeepSeek-R1"Monica, we've been over this. Now is not the time. Sit down, please."
Monica"I--"
NarratorThe crowd was in uproar. I had to lean over to catch Monica's next words.
Monica"I'm *so* sorry, Anton. Next time, I'll stay in the kitchen."
NarratorShe sat back down, seething. Curtan's voice filled the hall.
Curtan Leste"Benfi has always been a beacon of culture, tradition and religion. Many battles have been fought over this precious city."
Curtan Leste"Army after army has tried to claim it for their own, but we fought back and won each time."
Curtan Leste"We stand here thanks to the strong men who were able to protect their families, their lands, their way of living."
Curtan Leste"Every day, I praise God that we have succeeded so far."
NarratorScattered applause broke out from some of the older members of the audience.
NarratorI leaned over to Monica.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:40:46 p20058 / c377
  • ·“He's a bit extreme, but he is right. We owe everything to God.”
  • ▶“He sounds more like a priest than a politician.”
  • ·“I praise God he's only allowed to speak for another two minutes.”
Curtan Leste's speech emphasizes tradition over modern values, risking alienation of progressive voters. Highlighting his role as a priest rather than politician underscores the need for balanced governance.
DeepSeek-R1"He sounds more like a priest than a politician."
NarratorMonica said nothing, her gaze fixed forward.
Curtan Leste"Yet the traditions that form the very fabric of our society are being tested. Even as I speak!"
NarratorHe looked pointedly at Monica.
Curtan Leste"We must stay true to our values so that Sordland can prevail. So that Benfi can prevail!"
Curtan Leste"With these at the forefront, I am convinced that Sordland will continue to be the greatest nation in the world!"
Curtan Leste"I invite every single one of you to celebrate our roots and our traditions at this holy Benfi Festival!"
Curtan Leste"I hereby declare the festival open! Praise God! Praise Sordland! Praise Benfi!"
Curtan Leste"A Morgna wes core!"
Narrator"Vectern sis da!". Most of the people in the hall finished the decades-old saying, including me. Monica's lips moved, but she didn't say the words out loud.
NarratorHe exited the stage as the band struck up the official festival anthem.
NarratorI spent the rest of the evening alternately watching the festivities and conversing with local politicians. By the time we got back to the hotel, I was exhausted.
NarratorBut thanks to the festivities still raging outside -- and the sound of Monica tossing and turning beside me -- I couldn't sleep at all.

■ Personal Monica TalkT05

BUDGET -4WEALTH 2STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorIt felt like eternity since the Benfi Festival. Had it really only been less than 24 hours?
NarratorI was at home now, drinking an afternoon coffee on my balcony. A stack of newspapers lay by my side. I picked them up one by one, reading and re-reading the articles about the festival.
NarratorSome of the newspapers were merciless against Monica for interrupting Curtan during his speech. A few even called for her to be barred from political matters. Others expressed their support.
NarratorIn any event, I was relieved that the incident hadn't reflected too badly on me.
NarratorI was putting the last newspaper down as Monica joined me on the balcony.
Monica"So, you've seen the news?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:41:45 p19538 / c284
  • ▶"Monica, I know. We can talk about this--”
  • ·“What news?”
  • ·Do not reply.
Acknowledge Monica's concern and express willingness to discuss later.
DeepSeek-R1"Monica, I know. We can talk about this--"
Monica"What is there to talk about? I'm a disgrace with no support from my husband. It says so right here."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:42:27 p19586 / c338
  • ·"You shouldn't have spoken out.”
  • ·Do not reply.
  • ▶“Darling, believe me. My hands were tied.”
Apologizing shows understanding and supports the relationship despite the public incident.
DeepSeek-R1"Darling, believe me. My hands were tied."
Monica"Anton, how could you? First you deny me the opportunity to speak at the festival, then you let that dinosaur Leste make a mockery of me and my cause!"
NarratorShe grabbed one of the newspapers from the top of the stack and started reading out what a columnist wrote.
Monica"With her shameful outburst at Anrica mayor Curtan Leste, First Lady Monica Rayne once again proved that politics is an area best left to gentlemen."
Monica"With the President providing no support, her challenge to Leste was quickly defeated."
Monica"The President provided no support."
Monica"You can't even stand Leste, and you took his side! While making me look like a fool!"
NarratorShe started ripping the newspaper to shreds.
Monica"After all these years by your side, playing the doting wife..."
Monica"You know... my mother was right about you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:43:08 p19587 / c323

  • ·“What's that supposed to mean?”
  • ▶“I'm so sorry. I understand if you never forgive me.”
Expressing sincere regret and acknowledging potential non-forgiveness shows empathy and understanding, which can help mend the relationship despite the conflict.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm so sorry. I understand if you never forgive me."
Monica"Just...tell me why. Couldn't you have said something?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:43:48 p19572 / c319

  • ·“Sometimes you have to make sacrifices for the good of the country. You've got to learn that if you want to enter politics.”

Maintaining political alliances is crucial for stability and support.
DeepSeek-R1"I didn't want to lose Curtan's support."
Monica"I see. That's where your priorities lie."
NarratorThere was a brief silence.
Monica"There is another chance, you know. For you to show your *real* support."
Monica"Perhaps the festival wasn't the proper forum to air my views on women's rights. But I still need to do this, Anton."
Monica"I want to officially adopt this cause as First Lady. To actually get involved in politics, not just give impromptu speeches. I could even help your education minister with those reforms she's working on..."
Monica"I know this is something you're passionate about as well. Can I count on you?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:44:43 p19617 / c616

DeepSeek-R1"I'm sorry, but no. Leave politics to the politicians. I promise I'll pass the reforms you want."
NarratorMonica turned pale with anger. I had never seen her like this.
Monica"You know what? Fine."
Monica"But know this. I will never forget how you let me down. Never!"
NarratorA noise came from the balcony door. We both turned our heads to see Deana standing there, a confused look in her eyes.
Deana"Papa. Mama. Why are you fighting?"

NarratorHer words repeated in my head again and again.
Narrator"I will never forget how you let me down. Never!"

NarratorShe was never going to.

■ Military BudgetT05

BUDGET -4WEALTH 2STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorTo determine the current situation of the military and its spending policy, we held a meeting in the White Room.
NarratorPetr, Valken and Iosef were seated.
Petr Vectern"General Valken. Mr. Lancea. Let's start with the briefing."
Valken Kruger"Very well, Mr. Vice President."
Valken Kruger"We have prepared a briefing to convey further details about the current situation of our military."
Iosef Lancea"First off, we have received an updated communication from the Arcasian military command."
Iosef Lancea"Their planes are being moved into Erlory airbase as we speak."
Iosef Lancea"Under our supervision of course."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:46:29 p19686 / c351
  • ·“Do your best to make them feel at home.”
  • ·“Good. Make sure you keep an eye on them.”
  • ▶“Ensure that they do not forget which country's soil they are on.”
Emphasizing sovereignty ensures we maintain control and respect from foreign forces.
DeepSeek-R1"Ensure that they do not forget which country's soil they are on."
Valken Kruger"Of course, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"Now let's move on to our military."
Iosef Lancea"Which branch would you like to know about?"

  • ·“Let's talk about our navy.”

DeepSeek-R1"What is the situation of our army?"
Iosef Lancea"According to the latest reports from Camp Strongarm, currently, we field six armies totalling up to approximately 400.000 active troops."
Iosef Lancea"Which is a good amount. The real issue we are having is the quality, not quantity."
