Knowing the current deployment of our army will help assess immediate threats and resource allocation needs.
DeepSeek-R1"Where exactly is our army deployed at right now?"
Valken Kruger"The 1st Army is stationed at the Lespia and Wehlen borders to protect the way into the central regions of Sordland."
Valken Kruger"The 2nd Army, our largest force, has been situated by the Rumburg border."
Valken Kruger"The 2nd Army has a large mountain commando force and it is the most adequately equipped army to face Rumburg."
Valken Kruger"The 3rd Army is near the Agnolian border and the coastal areas facing Valgsland."
Valken Kruger"Due to our bitter past our coastal defense force is always on alert. The decimation of our fleet still is not forgotten."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:48:42 p20027 / c340
  • ▶“What is the current equipment status?”
  • ·“How do we compare to other armies?”
  • ·“I am curious about another branch.”
Understanding the current equipment status is crucial to assess military capability and address potential vulnerabilities.
DeepSeek-R1"What is the current equipment status?"
Valken Kruger"Our army is quite outdated by today's military standards. Most of our equipment is two decades old."
Iosef Lancea"To make up for our lack of modern military equipment, we resort to a large number of soldiers which is not really a long term solution."
Valken Kruger"I have been trying hard to reduce the negative effects of this problem. We don't need a modernisation program. A standardized purchase of lacking equipment would do."
Valken Kruger"We need more funding towards expanding the military size. Otherwise, our army will be seen as weak."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:49:25 p20056 / c360
  • ▶“How do we compare to other armies?”
  • ·“I am curious about another branch.”
Comparing Sordland's army to others will highlight potential weaknesses and inform strategic decisions.
DeepSeek-R1"How do we compare to other armies?"
Valken Kruger"Rumburg fields six active field armies and two reserve armies, together surpassing one million soldiers in the field. Lespia maintains five armies. With ATO deployments included, their numbers rise to nearly 800,000."
Valken Kruger"Valgsland fields one active army of around 140,000 troops, a significant portion of them marines. They also maintain three reserve armies that can be mobilized quickly due to their rapid mobilization military structure."
Valken Kruger"Wehlen commands two armies totaling roughly 300,000 soldiers, though only their professional core is considered highly capable. Agnolia fields three armies amounting to 250,000 troops. They are well equipped, but have not seen combat in over three decades."
Iosef Lancea"Rumburg’s sheer size stems from its forced conscription laws. In addition, several ducal regions maintain their own forces at their own expense, further bolstering the crown’s strength. They can sustain this scale thanks to their vast gold reserves, much of which flowed in from Rizia over the past decades."
Iosef Lancea"The Lespian army is the most advanced. Arcasian investment and the Alvarez administration’s expansion of the military-industrial complex have accelerated their modernization."
Iosef Lancea"Valgsland follows closely. Although they prioritize economic development and social welfare in their budget, CSP funding has allowed them to remain competitive, keeping the gap with Lespia narrow."
“I am curious about another branch.”
DeepSeek-R1"I am curious about another branch."
Iosef Lancea"Very well."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:50:07 p20291 / c277
  • ·“Let's talk about our navy.”
  • ·“Can you provide the details on our air force?”
  • ▶“Actually, let's move on.”
Focusing on pressing matters related to the army's modernization is more urgent given the economic constraints.
DeepSeek-R1"Actually, let's move on."
Iosef Lancea"Let's."
NarratorHe cleared his throat.
Iosef Lancea"The Ministry of Defense is disappointed about the lack of additional funds."
Petr Vectern"We knew that keeping the same budget for the military would cause for some concern from you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:50:56 p20225 / c445
  • ▶“The military is important but it is not a priority right now.”
  • ·“Resources were more needed elsewhere. The military has always been well funded.”
  • ·“I'd be willing to support the military in other ways. We could come to an agreement.”
Prioritizing economic recovery over military spending to address the current financial strain and focus on long-term stability.
DeepSeek-R1"The military is important but it is not a priority right now."
Iosef Lancea"How come a matter of defense has not been prioritised as more important?"
Valken Kruger"Especially with the current political climate."
Petr Vectern"We know that there have been high hopes but the President has made his decision to prioritise other branches."
Valken Kruger"We are stuck between a rock and a hard place without money. The military suffers from budget overextension in order to keep up with other countries."
NarratorIosef let out a long sigh.
Iosef Lancea"No use complaining now, Valken. Let's look at what we can do with what we have."
Iosef Lancea"I have been planning to improve the quality of the armed forces by buying better military equipment."
Petr Vectern"How would that be possible without funds?"
Valken Kruger"It won't be."
Iosef Lancea"There is one way."
Iosef Lancea"If we relieve half the forces that could create enough of a surplus then we could invest in new equipment."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:51:44 p20264 / c457
  • ·“Go on.”
  • ▶“Relieving half the armed forces sounds risky at best and destructive at worst.”
  • ·“Quality over quantity. I like it.”
Maintaining adequate military strength is crucial for national security, and reducing forces risks instability.
DeepSeek-R1"Relieving half the armed forces sounds risky at best and destructive at worst."
NarratorValken's eyes widened.
Valken Kruger"We are barely matching the size of half our neighbours while the other half have armies two times bigger than ours."
Valken Kruger"How on earth would discharging half the armed forces and buying new gear solve our defense issues?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:52:32 p20225 / c460
  • ▶“Valken raises valid questions.”
  • ·“A better equipped smaller force is much more preferable.”
Valken raises valid questions about the risks of reducing military forces while facing significant threats from neighboring countries.
DeepSeek-R1"Valken raises valid questions."
Iosef Lancea"Quality over quantity is the right strategy for the future of warfare."
Iosef Lancea"Having a large force doesn't necessitate superiority."
Petr Vectern"The reduction of the army would cause more unemployment which could hurt the administration."
Petr Vectern"What do you think, Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:53:20 p20274 / c491
  • ·“Leave the military untouched. There is no reason to break a working structure and risk defense weakness.”
  • ▶“Reduce size and buy better equipment with surplus. Let's reform the army and create a smaller better equipped force.”
Investing in better equipment despite the risks could lead to a more efficient military long-term.
DeepSeek-R1"Reduce size and buy better equipment with surplus. Let's reform the army and create a smaller better equipped force."
Iosef Lancea"Excellent. I shall begin the plans immediately."
Valken Kruger"I can hardly say that I welcome this change."
NarratorIosef and Valken looked at each other.
Petr Vectern"That will be all for today then."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:54:09 p20258 / c494
  • ·“Good work everyone.”
  • ·“Have a good day.”
  • ▶“Dismissed.”
Anton needs to focus on economic priorities and cannot commit additional military funds.
DeepSeek-R1"Dismissed."
Petr Vectern"I'd like to extend my thanks for your attendance."
NarratorThe meeting concluded as the sun set over the concrete jungle that was Holsord.

■ Wehlen Trade VisitT05

BUDGET -4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorSordland One's flight from Holsord to Wraclawitz took approximately five hours. Despite heavy turbulence, I managed to sleep a little.
NarratorSymon, Deivid and I landed at the international airport of the capital city of Wehlen. Our aim here was to finalise the trade deal drafted between Sordland and Wehlen.
NarratorOur plane landed, and the door opened. The sun blinded me for a moment. I stepped outside.
NarratorI saw the soldiers lined up on either side of a red carpet. There must've been at least a hundred of them. A marching band started playing and the soldiers immediately saluted me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:55:12 p19896 / c413
  • ▶Salute the soldiers.
  • ·Proceed to the red carpet without any gestures.
Saluting the soldiers reinforces respect for the military and strengthens national unity.
DeepSeek-R1I saluted the soldiers.
NarratorAt the bottom of the stairs, Wiktor Smolak was waiting for me next to a military jeep with a few soldiers by his side. There were a few reporters at the edge of the crowd, waiting with their cameras.
NarratorHe was a tall man, almost as tall as me. He was wearing large sunglasses and a regular suit, contrasting with the military attire of everyone else in the field.
Wiktor Smolak"Mr. President. Welcome to Wraclawitz."
NarratorHe immediately motioned for an embrace. We hugged and many cameras went off at the same time. He turned around for a couple of additional poses.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:55:57 p19990 / c381
  • ·“Nice to see you, Mr. President.”
  • ▶Pull him aside for a couple of more poses yourself.
  • ·“Thank you for inviting us.”
Pulling him aside for additional poses will enhance the visual alliance between Sordland and Wehlen, bolstering international perception and aiding negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1I pulled him aside for a couple of more poses.
Wiktor Smolak"Now that the pleasantries are done, let's go! We will take the jeep for a tour."
NarratorWith another tune from the marching band, we boarded the military jeep. He sat in the driver's seat and I sat next to him.
NarratorAs we started speeding up, the soldiers saluted again. Wiktor saluted back.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:56:36 p20006 / c238
  • ▶Salute the soldiers.
  • ·Do nothing.
Saluting the soldiers is a respectful gesture that maintains good diplomatic relations.
DeepSeek-R1I saluted the soldiers.
NarratorBoth of our security details started following us immediately, along with a team of reporters.
Wiktor Smolak"We will first go to the city center to see the unveiling of a statue."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:57:21 p20082 / c424
  • ·“Whose statue?”
  • ·“Sounds ...exciting.”
  • ▶“I'd rather not waste time. Let's get straight to business.”
Anton should prioritize economic discussions over ceremonial events to focus on achieving tangible benefits for Sordland.
DeepSeek-R1"I'd rather not waste time. Let's get straight to business."
Wiktor Smolak"Ah, Mr. President. I must insist. Also, I am in the driver's seat."
NarratorWe kept making small talk about the flight and the weather as we made our way towards the city center.
NarratorOn almost every corner, there were reporters taking photos of us. Aside from them, though, the streets seemed completely empty.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:58:06 p20080 / c429
  • ·“Is Wraclawitz always this empty?”
  • ·“Is this all a big photo op for you?”
  • ▶“How long is this going to take?”
Focusing on efficiency aligns with economic priorities.
DeepSeek-R1"How long is this going to take?"
Wiktor Smolak"Patience is a virtue. We will be done soon, and then we can discuss our trade deal."
Wiktor Smolak"There we are."
NarratorWe arrived at the center. In the middle of a large plaza, there was a covered statue. Large crowds of people were gathered behind fences. waving both Sordland and Wehlen flags. Wiktor waved back at them.
Wiktor Smolak"My people have been very supportive of this visit, Mr. President. I am sure you are delighted to see them."
NarratorA man came closer to the fences and started yelling at Wiktor. With my limited knowledge of the Wehzek language, I understood the words 'Wiktor' and 'murderer'.
NarratorAlmost instantaneously, two soldiers came up from behind the man and he was taken away.
Wiktor Smolak"Thankfully the sun is out. Today will be a good day for the reveal."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:58:52 p20151 / c457
  • ▶“I see that you know how to handle dissidents. Very good.”
  • ·“What will happen to the man who was protesting you?”
  • ·“Very good.”
Complimenting Wiktor on handling dissent maintains positive relations and focuses on professional diplomacy.
DeepSeek-R1"I see that you know how to handle dissidents. Very good."
Wiktor Smolak"I am glad you approve. We are of the same mind."
Wiktor Smolak"See, I knew we would get along fine."
NarratorWe took our places in front of the statue. With a drumroll, the statue of Wiktor Smolak was uncovered.
NarratorIt was a fairly good likeness, albeit more muscular than the real Wiktor. He was wearing a cloak and holding a sword in his hand.
Wiktor Smolak"Excellent statue. Very lifelike. What do you think?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R111:59:39 p20247 / c411
  • ▶“Mr. Smolak, I don't have time for these games. Let's get straight to the business.”
  • ·“Yes, it's very good. Very lifelike.”
  • ·“Our artists can do better in Sordland.”
Focus on business to avoid wasting time and resources.
DeepSeek-R1"Mr. Smolak, I don't have time for these games. Let's get straight to the business."
Wiktor Smolak"Don't worry. We will."
Wiktor Smolak"Join me for a walk."
NarratorI followed him through the streets. At every corner, more and more photos were taken.
NarratorWe stopped in front of a pastry vendor on the street.
Wiktor Smolak"Give me two strudels."
NarratorThe man behind the stand smiled and gave us two strudels.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:00:38 p20196 / c734
  • ·“Are you going to pay for it?”
  • ·Pay the street vendor.
  • ▶Wait for him to pay the street vendor.
Maintaining diplomatic protocol by allowing Wiktor Smolak to pay respects the host's role and avoids potential awkwardness.
DeepSeek-R1I waited for him to pay the street vendor. He simply took the two strudels and didn't pay anything.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:01:17 p20167 / c295
  • ·“Are you going to pay for it?”
  • ▶Pay the street vendor.
  • ·Keep walking.
Paying for the strudels shows respect and integrity.
DeepSeek-R1I put my hand on my wallet and brought up some money to give the vendor.
Wiktor Smolak"Hold up. Your money doesn't count here. I got this."
NarratorHe handed the money to the street vendor with a huge smile on his face. The cameras flashed again.
NarratorAfterwards, we walked a short distance to the palace.
NarratorPassing the gates, we made our way into the main building made from ivory-colored stones. The old guard towers flanked the magnificent structure from both sides.
NarratorThe inside of the palace was very spacious, with a very large chandelier hanging from the ceiling. Grand staircases made from the same ivory stone had red carpets on them. On the upper levels, guards stood wearing the traditional outfits of the old Wehzek kingdom.
NarratorWe finally reached the office of the President of Wehlen. The door closed behind us. I looked around at the golden ornaments on the walls and the various jewel-encrusted artifacts.
NarratorAs it was customary in Wehlen, we sat down on the carpet.
Wiktor Smolak"Now we can talk about our trade deal."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:02:06 p20290 / c441
  • ▶“Finally.”
  • ·Nod.
Anton Rayne acknowledges readiness to discuss the trade deal succinctly.
DeepSeek-R1"Finally."
Wiktor Smolak"But before that."
NarratorHe clapped his hands twice.
Wiktor Smolak"Bring the Khisha."
NarratorA few assistants immediately entered the room, carrying a large ornamented Khisha, the well-known smoking instrument originating from Wehlen. They immediately lit it up and the room was filled with the smell of tobacco.
NarratorWiktor inhaled the smoke and handed me the Khisha.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:02:52 p20312 / c371
  • ▶“I don't smoke.”
  • ·Partake.
Declining the Khisha maintains personal principles and focus for negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't smoke."
Wiktor Smolak"Your loss. It's the finest tobacco in the land."
NarratorHe took another puff, filling the room with more smoke.
Wiktor Smolak"So... Mr. President. Bah, that's too long. Can I call you Anton?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:03:33 p20227 / c315
  • ▶“I prefer my official title.”
  • ·“Sure.”
Maintaining formal protocol with a foreign dignitary enhances international relations and presidential authority.
DeepSeek-R1"I prefer my official title."
Wiktor Smolak"Very well, Mr. President."
Wiktor Smolak"Tell me. What do you really think about Rumburg?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:04:18 p20312 / c431
  • ·“A barking dog doesn't bite.”
  • ·“They are a threat to the region.”
  • ·“They can go to hell!”
  • ▶“Not much in particular. Like any other country, they have their needs and their own agenda.”
Rayne maintains neutrality to keep strategic options open.
DeepSeek-R1"Not much in particular. Like any other country, they have their needs and their own agenda."
Wiktor Smolak"Yes, they do. But their ambitions are over Wehlen and Sordland."
Wiktor Smolak"We must be careful, Mr. President."
Wiktor Smolak"I assure you their time will come."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:04:59 p20212 / c320
  • ·“In due time, yes.”
  • ·“Are you planning something?”
  • ▶“Regardless, I would rather prevent further conflict in the region.”
Emphasizing prevention of conflict aligns with maintaining stability and supporting economic goals.
DeepSeek-R1"Regardless, I would rather prevent further conflict in the region."
Wiktor Smolak"You know as well as I do, Mr. President. They are coming whether we like it or not. They are the aggressor, not us."
Wiktor Smolak"And now, I need to solve some problems they are creating for me."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:05:39 p20194 / c281
  • ▶“What is it?”
  • ·“I'm listening.”
I need to prompt Wiktor Smolak to elaborate on his statement about Rumburg to better understand the situation.
DeepSeek-R1"What is it?"
NarratorHe clapped his hands again. Two assistants came into the room with a map of the border regions between Wehlen and Sordland. He pointed at the border.
Wiktor Smolak"Look."
Wiktor Smolak"Bluds and their so called *BFF* are causing problems for me in this region. They are getting increasingly dangerous with Rumburg's active support. I know that they are a problem for you as well."
Wiktor Smolak"I will not allow these traitors to exist within my borders. So I will hit them with all the might of Wehlen. These terrorists need to be destroyed."
Wiktor Smolak"I will cut all their supplies from Rumburg and trap them on the mountains."
Wiktor Smolak"I call it Operation Bear Trap."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:06:25 p20221 / c442
  • ·“I don't support any military action this close to our borders.”
  • ▶“What do you want from me?”
  • ·“I like the sound of it.”
Asking for clarification on Smolak's intentions allows Anton to assess the situation and make an informed decision.
DeepSeek-R1"What do you want from me?"
NarratorHe grinned and lay back on the large pillow behind him.
Wiktor Smolak"Here is the deal. I will give you objectively the *best* trade deal in the region."
Wiktor Smolak"You will give me your assistance crushing the terrorists on both of our borders."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:07:11 p20222 / c434
  • ▶“I need specifics.”
  • ·“Absolutely not. I won't meddle in Wehlen's military interventions.”
  • ·“Two birds, one stone.”
Requesting specifics allows me to evaluate the proposal thoroughly without immediate commitment.
DeepSeek-R1"I need specifics."
Wiktor Smolak"Sure."
Wiktor Smolak"My spies have infiltrated BFF cells and they found caches of KA-74s. We traced them back to Rumburg. This alone justifies my retaliation to protect my country!"
Wiktor Smolak"Worst of all, we also found that similar shipments are going to Sordland. Rumburg is trying to unite the BFF forces in both of our countries."
Wiktor Smolak"However, the main problem is the border security between our nations. BFF militants have been using the mountains between Sordland and Wehlen to escape justice in both of our countries."
Wiktor Smolak"If we don't act and trap them in those mountains, they will keep replenishing their forces in both of our countries and keep waging war against our citizens."
Wiktor Smolak"Yes, Mr. President. You see. This is not just Wehlen's problem."
Wiktor Smolak"Let's take a step back and talk about what you will get in return."
Wiktor Smolak"I propose you a no-tariff agreement, co-investment projects and oil."
Wiktor Smolak"In return, you will assist me in destroying the armed militants in my country."
Wiktor Smolak"You know that as long as they use the mountains in Wehlen as a base, Sordland and Lespia will equally suffer. This is in the interest of the whole region."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:07:53 p20390 / c286
  • ·"Why would you offer me so much?"
  • ·“What kind of support do you need from me? Can't Wehlen handle it by itself?”
  • ▶“There might be international ramifications.”
Considering potential diplomatic fallout and focusing on broader implications.
DeepSeek-R1"There might be international ramifications."
Wiktor Smolak"To hell with international ramifications! I will protect my country! I don't care what they say!"
Wiktor Smolak"What I want from you in return for all the money and the resources, is only a joint operation between our armies against the BFF."
NarratorI took a moment to think about it.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:08:39 p20254 / c428
  • ·“Deal.”
  • ·“No deal. It's too risky. I'm afraid I can't accept.”
  • ▶“Let's negotiate Sordland's contribution in this operation.”
Negotiating Sordland's contribution allows for strategic involvement without assuming full risk, aligning with economic priorities and cautious foreign policy.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's negotiate Sordland's contribution in this operation."
Wiktor Smolak"You are a tough one, Mr. President."
Wiktor Smolak"Normally, I wouldn't offer anything else but, I like you."
Wiktor Smolak"Here is my final offer."
Wiktor Smolak"When the operation starts, with you or without you, there will be stragglers trying to cross into Sordland."
Wiktor Smolak"We just want you to support the operation even if you do not join. Stop the terrorists from fleeing into your country and you will have your deal. Simple, isn't it?"
Wiktor Smolak"So what do you think?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:09:30 p20251 / c515
  • ▶“Deal.”
  • ·“No deal. I'm afraid I can't accept.”
Accepting the deal offers economic benefits and regional security, aligning with Sordland's goals despite potential risks.
DeepSeek-R1"Deal."
Wiktor Smolak"I knew I could trust you, Mr. President."
Wiktor Smolak"Tonight we will drink!"
NarratorHe clapped his hands again.
Wiktor Smolak"Drinks! Dancers! Music!"
NarratorA group of dancers and performers entered and smoke filled the room.
NarratorAs they performed their songs, we clinked our glasses to our new partnership while sipping the sweet Wehzek liquor.
NarratorThe next day, I returned to Sordland with a hangover.

■ Agnolia Trade VisitT05

BUDGET -4WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorSordland One's flight from Holsord to Staalport took three hours but went very smoothly. We were about to touch down.
NarratorDeivid left his seat and came over while Symon was enjoying his newspaper.
Deivid Wisci"Looks like we are about to land, Mr. President."
NarratorI looked outside. I could see Heljiland, the island in between Valgsland and Agnolia.
Deivid Wisci"So many conflicts over such a small piece of land. The island can barely sustain itself without outside help."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:10:31 p20036 / c373
  • ·“I know. I was just thinking the same thing.”
  • ·“If I was in the shoes of the Agnolian Prime Minister, I would do anything to protect the motherland.”
  • ▶“I don't blame them. It is in a very strategic position.”
Anton Rayne would respond pragmatically, acknowledging the strategic importance without committing to action.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't blame them. It is in a very strategic position."
Deivid Wisci"Certainly. If you have Heljiland, you are in control of the whole Valgos Channel."
Deivid Wisci"I suspect it will be one of the topics that Mr. Van Hoorten will bring up as a bargaining chip. We must be careful."
Deivid Wisci"Heljiland might be under the control of Agnolia, but the international community has yet to recognise it. For a good reason, too."
Deivid Wisci"Nobody wants to make an enemy of Valgsland. Especially because of the support of United Contana and the CSP."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:11:20 p20319 / c495
  • ·“I don't care who we're up against. We will cross that bridge when we come to it.”
  • ▶“Me included. I wouldn't want do anything to injure our relationship with Valgsland.”
Maintaining good relations with Valgsland is crucial given the strategic importance of Heljiland and to avoid potential conflicts with United Contana and the CSP.
DeepSeek-R1"Me included. I wouldn't want do anything to injure our relationship with Valgsland."
Deivid Wisci"I agree. We should try our best to stick to the middle ground."
NarratorSymon folded his newspaper, got up from his seat and sat right next to us.
Symon Holl"Aside from the point of Heljiland, we need to be careful with the additional requests."
Symon Holl"According to the deal that was negotiated so far, they want to sell us their steel for a higher price and they are requesting easier access to our agricultural market."
Symon Holl"In return, they are promising cash flow and investments, especially in the Agnland region. It's not hard to guess why."
Deivid Wisci"Precisely. Agnolian immigrants in our Agnland region make up a good chunk of the area's economy now."
Deivid Wisci"During the initial negotiations, they brought up the topic of immigration many times."
Deivid Wisci"The fact that we have relaxed our immigration laws will help tremendously."
Deivid Wisci"Let's see if we can shake hands on it."
NarratorThe pilot made an announcement that the descent had started. Deivid and Symon went back to their seats.
NarratorI took another look outside the window. I could see Staalport, the capital city of Agnolia.
NarratorThe large dockyards of the city and the canals -- "Staal" in Agnolian -- appeared closer as our descent continued. Staalport was certainly a fitting name.
NarratorA couple of minutes later, we finally touched down. The plane parked. I took a final look out the window. The welcoming ceremony from Agnolia was waiting along the red carpet outside.
NarratorAfter the guards made their way out, I exited the plane.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:12:03 p20390 / c301
  • ▶Wave at the crowd.
  • ·Proceed without a gesture.
Waving at the crowd will demonstrate engagement and friendliness, crucial for maintaining public support during an unstable economic period.
DeepSeek-R1I waved at the crowd that gathered. There were many camera flashes. I started to make my way downstairs.
NarratorPrime Minister Van Hoorten was nowhere in sight. Instead, I was greeted by the Foreign Minister.
NarratorAfter handshakes and a couple of photos, I moved to the car where Serge was waiting to drive me to the office of Prime Minister.
Serge Wolkner"Good day, sir. I hope the flight was comfortable."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:12:45 p20358 / c329
  • ▶“Yes, it was. Thank you, Serge.”
  • ·“Let's go.”
  • ·Nod.
Maintaining professionalism by acknowledging the staff's effort.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes, it was. Thank you, Serge."
NarratorI got in the car and we made our way through the foreign streets. Our convoy was protected by Sordish and Agnolian guards in their vehicles.
NarratorWe got on the highway beside the port and saw dozens of dockyards and ships anchored. Some of the boats and machinery looked old.
NarratorThe city itself looked very grey and grim. The buildings had a touch of Rumburg's famous Monarkian architecture with a mixture of Sordish signature domes on the older ones.
NarratorWe were now on the main road to the office of the Prime Minister.
Serge Wolkner"Looks like they are not too happy to see you, sir."
NarratorSerge pointed at the crowd that gathered on both sides of the street. It was a protest. They were shouting and yelling at our convoy. Some of them held signs written in Sordish: 'Sordland Must Pay Its Debts To Agnolia'
Serge Wolkner"You know... I never knew Agnolians were so ungrateful. Not everyone sees a great leader like you everyday."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:13:31 p20610 / c360
  • ·“You are absolutely right, Serge. So ungrateful.”
  • ·“They can yell all they want.”
  • ·“There have been so many issues in Sordland. I don't know if I can call myself a great leader.”
  • ▶“I wonder what propaganda their state is feeding them..."
Suspecting propaganda aligns with a cautious foreign policy and acknowledges potential manipulation by Agnolia.
DeepSeek-R1"I wonder what propaganda their state is feeding them..."
Serge Wolkner"They are afraid of you, sir. They are scared that Sordland will reach its potential. They don't want you to be seen as the great hero you are."
NarratorThe convoy started slowing down.
Serge Wolkner"We have arrived, sir."
NarratorThe car continued through the gates while the security vehicles went the other way. We finally came to a stop in front of the door.
NarratorSerge left the car and opened the car door. I exited and began walking to the door where dozens of press and the Prime Minister were waiting for me.
NarratorI saw the PM much more clearly as I neared the door. He was shorter than I thought.
Maartin Van Hoorten"Mr. President. Welcome to Agnolia and to the beautiful city of Staalport."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:14:16 p20497 / c392
  • ·“Prime Minister.”
  • ▶“Thank you so much for the warm welcome. It is great to be here.”
  • ·“Good to see you, Maartin.”
Expressing gratitude and enthusiasm sets a positive tone for negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you so much for the warm welcome. It is great to be here."
NarratorHe reached out for a handshake. The press surrounding us had been waiting for this moment.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:15:00 p20499 / c375
  • ·Overpower his handshake.
  • ▶Shake hands.
  • ·Shake hands and hug after.
Shaking hands respectfully maintains professionalism and composure in a tense situation.
DeepSeek-R1We shook hands and smiled at the cameras as the press took pictures.
NarratorHe gestured to the entrance of the two-hundred-year old Parliament building.
Maartin Van Hoorten"This way, please."
NarratorWe entered and the press followed us inside.
Maartin Van Hoorten"I would like to present you with a gift as a sign of friendship between our countries."
NarratorAn assistant showed up immediately with a large glass case in her hands. It contained a single sheet of parchment inside.
Maartin Van Hoorten"This here is one of the first treaties written between Sordland and Agnolia. May it mark the renewal of our friendship today as well!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:15:42 p20545 / c273
  • ·“I hope so, Mr. Van Hoorten.”
  • ▶“This is an amazing gift. Thank you.”
  • ·“Very good.”
Accepting the gift strengthens diplomatic ties and symbolizes mutual friendship.
DeepSeek-R1"This is an amazing gift. Thank you."
NarratorOur photos were taken again. I gestured at one of my retainers who was holding our gift to the PM.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:16:25 p20542 / c396
  • ·Gift one of the original copies of the first constitution of Sordland.
  • ·Gift a Sordish-bred puppy despite Maartin's known fear of dogs.

The ceremonial sword symbolizes strength and mutual respect, making it a fitting diplomatic gift.
DeepSeek-R1Our gift was an ornamented ceremonial sword made with Sordish steel.
Maartin Van Hoorten"Ah! An excellent specimen. This must be hundreds of years old. It looks like this is Sordish steel as well. Very fitting. Thank you."
NarratorThe cameras flashed once again.
Maartin Van Hoorten"Let's go to my office. Follow me."
NarratorI followed him and we entered the office of the Prime Minister. With its minimalist wooden decor, the room was far more modest than I expected.
NarratorWe sat down. He leaned back and made himself comfortable.
Maartin Van Hoorten"Sheesh. I'm thankful to be done with the ceremonies."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:17:09 p20428 / c359
  • ·“Are you not fond of these ceremonies?”
  • ▶“I was just about to say the same. I'm not fond of these either.”
  • ·Nod.
Agreeing with Van Hoorten's sentiment fosters camaraderie and moves the discussion towards more substantive topics.
DeepSeek-R1"I was just about to say the same. I'm not fond of these either."
Maartin Van Hoorten"It's one of the tougher parts of our job."
NarratorHe got up from his chair and moved over to the bar. There were many varieties of liquor.
Maartin Van Hoorten"Would you like a drink, Mr. Rayne?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:17:50 p20435 / c282
  • ·“I wouldn't mind some Agnolian vodka.”
  • ·“I will have an Erlory Maroon 30-year-old.”

Choosing water maintains professionalism and avoids potential risks associated with drinking.
DeepSeek-R1"Just water is fine."
Maartin Van Hoorten"As you wish."
NarratorHe poured the drinks and gave mine to me before sitting down again.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:18:44 p20463 / c584
  • ▶“Let's go straight to business.”
  • ·“A true gentleman. I can respect that about you, Mr. Hoorten.”
  • ·“What was with that protest outside? Can't you control them?”
Prioritize economic discussions to focus on mutual benefits and budget constraints.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's go straight to business."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Let's."
Maartin Van Hoorten"First of all, I would like to say that I have been watching your recent immigration policy changes. I am glad that you have decided to keep it relaxed."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Immigrants are very important for beneficial economical development. We believe that very much in Agnolia."
Maartin Van Hoorten"It is reassuring to see Sordland move in a more modern direction."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:19:34 p20485 / c449

  • ▶“Thank you.”
Expressing gratitude strengthens diplomatic ties and supports economic objectives.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you."
NarratorHe took a sip from his vodka.
Maartin Van Hoorten"So tell me, Mr. Rayne. Is Sordland a reliable trading partner to Agnolia?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:20:16 p20468 / c300

Affirming reliability builds trust and strengthens potential trade relations.
DeepSeek-R1"Of course, Mr. Van Hoorten. You can trust Sordland."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Very good."
NarratorVan Hoorten leaned back further in contemplation.

  • ·“Let's change the course of our nations.”

DeepSeek-R1"Are you going to talk all day about past deeds or are we going to sign this deal?"
Maartin Van Hoorten"First of all, if we shake hands on it, this agreement is going to be extremely beneficial for both of us."
Maartin Van Hoorten"You should be able to solve your recession in no time with the investment and money flow coming from us. This is, as you know, on top of the reduced tariffs for trade between our countries."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Of course in return, as discussed between my people and your people before, you will allow us privileged access to your agricultural market."
Maartin Van Hoorten"You will be buying steel from us for a higher price, which is a small price to pay really. That is the deal."
Maartin Van Hoorten"What do you say, Mr. Rayne?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:21:44 p20597 / c515
  • ·“I accept.” [-1 Government Budget]
  • ▶“I'm sorry, but I can't agree to this."
  • ·“This is not a beneficial deal for Sordland.”
Refusing the deal maintains Sordland's financial stability and prioritizes domestic economic recovery over foreign trade concessions.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm sorry, but I can't agree to this."
Maartin Van Hoorten"You went through all this effort to come and say no to me?"
Maartin Van Hoorten"You are throwing many months of work down the drain. This is a mistake."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:22:28 p20613 / c381
  • ▶“I can't agree to the higher prices. We prefer to continue with the current steel deal.”
  • ·“Agnolian steel isn't worth that much, Mr. Van Hoorten.”
  • ·“I'm sorry but my answer is no.”
Anton Rayne should focus on economic priorities and avoid committing to higher costs.
DeepSeek-R1"I can't agree to the higher prices. We prefer to continue with the current steel deal."
Maartin Van Hoorten"We believe that Sordland has been given an unfair advantage for too long because of the previous agreements. We can no longer accept this, Mr. Rayne."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Our people believe that we have long been overshadowed by Sordland. I am here to fix that. With a beneficial trade for both us, nonetheless."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:23:14 p20631 / c419
  • ·“Is there no room for discussion?”

Accepting the trade deal maintains Sordland's important partnership with Agnolia, potentially stabilizing the economy despite a temporary budget strain.
DeepSeek-R1"Then I will accept, Mr. Van Hoorten. We don't want to lose such an important partner as Agnolia."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Excellent."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Before we dive into the paperwork for the trade deal, I want to offer you another possibility."
Maartin Van Hoorten"I would like to expand on our new partnership. We have regional forces like Rumburg that have been imposing danger on both of our countries. I am of the mind that we must unite. So..."
Maartin Van Hoorten"By the powers bestowed upon me as the Prime Minister of Agnolia, I am offering a military alliance between Sordland and Agnolia."
Maartin Van Hoorten"What do you think?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:24:10 p20724 / c598
  • ·“Very well. I accept.”

Maintaining a cautious foreign policy aligns with economic priorities and sovereignty concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"Sordland is not ready to go that far with Agnolia."
Maartin Van Hoorten"That is unfortunate. It would've been beneficial against the Rumburg threat. However, Agnolia can stand by itself against any threat just fine."
Maartin Van Hoorten"But there is another topic I would like to bring up."
Maartin Van Hoorten"As you know, Heljiland has been long contested between Agnolia and Valgsland. Those warmongers want to take back the island."
Maartin Van Hoorten"You know how the international community does not recognise our rightful claim on the island. *Including Sordland*."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Wouldn't it be fitting for Sordland to finally announce the recognition of Heljiland as Agnolian territory?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:25:01 p20859 / c488

Maartin Van Hoorten"Most excellent!"
Maartin Van Hoorten"Today is a historic day!"
Maartin Van Hoorten"Congratulations, Mr. Rayne, for making all the right decisions today."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Well then."
NarratorHe stood up from his chair.
Maartin Van Hoorten"We must continue the official program. As you know, we will pay a visit to our founder's grave for another ceremony. Afterwards, we will move on to the state dinner."

Maartin Van Hoorten"But this is customary. Every Sordish president before you has done this. This won't sit well with us. I must insist we follow the protocol."

NarratorAs soon as we left the office, I briefed Symon and Deivid on the outcome of the meeting.
NarratorThe rest of our state trip was full of fake smiles, handshakes, incredibly long ceremonies and even longer meetings between our ministers and their counterparts.
NarratorWhen we came to the end of the trip, the way back was mostly silent.
NarratorI couldn't help but let out a sigh of relief when we touched down in Holsord.

■ Reformist SpeechT05

BUDGET -5WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorI came to the Grand National Assembly of Sordland to deliver a speech regarding the new Constitution and ask for the support of the Assembly before the upcoming vote.
NarratorI walked quickly through the corridors, giving my greetings to people on my way towards the parliamentary hall.
NarratorWhen I entered the hall, the Assembly was in complete chaos, with shouting and cursing between the parties. There was a heavy argument about a proposal made by an MP from NFP.
NarratorI took my seat inside the special area reserved for the ministers. Gloria was shouting from her seat at the centre of the hall as she hit the gavel three times, with each strike heavier than the one before.
Gloria Tory"ORDER! ORDER!"
NarratorI saw Kesaro Kibener standing up from his seat and shouting towards the members of the PFJP.
Gloria Tory"Mr. Kibener! Calm yourself! Show respect to the procedures of this Assembly!"
NarratorDespite her efforts, the argument continued for a couple of minutes before everybody took back to their seats and finally went quiet.
Gloria Tory"Mr. Kibener, next time when I tell you to do so, either take your seat or leave the hall; so we can proceed with the procedures of this Assembly and continue our duties."
NarratorSome MP's from the NFP side shouted back at her in protest.
Gloria Tory"Honorable ladies and gentlemen, if you allow me to continue..."
NarratorShe pointed at me.
Gloria Tory"We have the President here with us today. He has asked to give an important announcement."
NarratorThe Assembly fell silent.
Gloria Tory"You have the floor, Mr. President."
NarratorI walked to the stand as loud clapping started in the USP's side.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:27:38 p20623 / c305
  • ▶"Ladies and Gentlemen..."
  • ·"Honorable Members of Assembly..."
  • ·"Brothers and sisters..."
Starting with 'Ladies and Gentlemen' provides a formal and respectful tone, suitable for addressing the chaotic assembly and projecting leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"Ladies and Gentlemen of the Grand National Assembly of Sordland! I am here today to raise important questions regarding our outdated Constitution."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:28:27 p20714 / c413
  • ·"The Supreme Court holds too much power..."
  • ·"Perhaps it's not expected of me to say this, but the President holds too much authority..."
  • ▶"Our elected Assembly has always been overshadowed..."
Emphasizing the Assembly's role aligns with my goal to win re-election by showing respect for democratic processes.
DeepSeek-R1"Our elected Assembly has always been overshadowed by the executive and the designated judiciary branches in Sordland. The Grand National Assembly must be able to exercise its job properly and without obstruction. However, our current Constitution fails to guarantee this."
NarratorI continued.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:29:06 p20771 / c217
  • ·"...Therefore, we propose changes to the Constitution to prepare Sordland for a more democratic era!"
  • ·"We must finally rewrite the Constitution of Soll and break our chains of the past!"
  • ▶"I call everyone to stand by us to make historic changes to our Constitution and bring it to the standards of today's world!"
Emphasizing modernization aligns with the reformist agenda and contemporary standards.
DeepSeek-R1"I call everyone to stand by us to make historic changes to our Constitution and bring it to the standards of today's world!"
NarratorApplauses came from USP and PFJP seats.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:29:54 p20660 / c431
  • ·"We will break the Supreme Court's authority over constitutional changes."
  • ▶"We will rebalance the branches of government to ensure a more democratic process."
Rebalancing the branches ensures democratic processes and prevents future conflicts.
DeepSeek-R1"We will rebalance the branches of government to ensure a more democratic process."
NarratorI focused on...
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:30:38 p20646 / c359
  • ·Unity.
  • ▶Change.
  • ·Peace.
Prioritizing reforms aligns with the manifesto's focus on growth and change despite internal pressures.
DeepSeek-R1"We must not be afraid of change. The world is changing, and Sordland needs the change it has earned after years of Sollism in the last decades. We will rewrite the old and make a constitution that would reflect the will of the people, and open the way for a democratic Sordland!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:31:22 p20811 / c347
  • ·"We will make Sordland great again!"
  • ▶"Let's embrace the future."
A forward-looking statement aligns with the focus on constitutional change and modernization.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's embrace the future."
NarratorJust as I was about to continue speaking, Gloria Tory interrupted me.
Gloria Tory"Mr. President, with all due respect, I believe Sordland is already democratic."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:32:10 p20884 / c454
  • ·"Please don't interrupt me."
  • ·"I'm not finished yet, Mrs. Tory."
  • ▶"We both know that our Constitution is flawed."
  • ·"You don't believe that."
This response directly addresses Gloria's challenge by highlighting the Constitution's flaws, reinforcing the necessity of reform.
DeepSeek-R1"We both know that our Constitution is flawed."
Gloria Tory"Tarquin Soll and his efforts cannot be ignored. We must keep following the path he defined for Sordish people."
Gloria Tory"I'm sure the Honorable Members of USP will see that the President is acting rash by allying with the opposition to destroy the culture of Sollism that established Sordland."
Gloria Tory"That was the extent of my quick note. Thank you. The President has the floor."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:32:54 p20846 / c363
  • ·"That was disrespectful Mrs. Speaker. Please don't try to sabotage my speech."
  • ▶"We are not here to destroy Sollism nor our cultural values."
  • ·"Who are you trying to provoke, Mrs. Tory? USP is behind me."
  • ·"I'll act as if that didn't happen."
Addressing Gloria's concerns directly by reaffirming respect for Sollism while emphasizing the need for constitutional reform helps maintain unity and support.
DeepSeek-R1"We are not here to destroy Sollism nor our cultural values."
Gloria Tory"Please continue your speech, Mr. President."
NarratorI took a breath and continued.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:33:38 p20821 / c302

  • ·"It is time to make a new Constitution together!"
  • ·"I ask everyone to think clearly about our current situation. And join us for change."
Calling for unity despite differences can help pass constitutional changes while maintaining political stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Ladies and gentlemen of the Assembly! We must set aside our differences."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:34:21 p20816 / c343

Choosing option 0 allows me to maintain a unifying tone while pushing for change.
DeepSeek-R1"Be part of a new era for Sordland. Join us and vote for our proposal."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:35:11 p20837 / c462
  • ·"Let's write the future together!"
  • ▶"I will address all the problems of the past in my proposal!"
  • ·"USP will change and lead Sordland into the future!"
Addressing past issues directly emphasizes leadership and commitment to reform, crucial for garnering support and aligning with manifesto goals.
DeepSeek-R1"I will address all the problems of the past in my proposal!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:35:55 p20787 / c341

  • ·Finish with a poem.
Maintaining decorum and respect while continuing the speech reinforces leadership and unity.
DeepSeek-R1I saluted the crowd.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:36:36 p20805 / c289
  • ·"For justice in Sordland!"
  • ▶"For a democratic Sordland!"
  • ·"For a united Sordland!"
  • ·"For a free Sordland!"
Promoting democracy aligns with the goal of improving governance and reflects the speech's focus on constitutional changes.
DeepSeek-R1"For a democratic Sordland!"
NarratorThe Assembly roared with all kinds of different reactions.
NarratorSome applauses came from the PFJP and USP seats. Most of the others started to make loud noises again.
Gloria Tory"Thank you for the speech, Mr. President."
NarratorI walked back to my seat as many MP's asked to speak. Frens Ricter was among them.
Gloria Tory"Yes, Mr. Ricter."
Frens Ricter"Thank you, Mrs. Speaker."
Frens Ricter"I would like to thank Mr. President for his speech. We, the People's Freedom and Justice Party, stand in agreement with the need for a more democratic Constitution."
Frens Ricter"I would like to thank the Ladies and Gentlemen of the United Sordland Party for their more open attitude compared to past years."
Frens Ricter"As long as Mr. President's words prove not to be just words, we will be behind this much needed movement for change. We welcome this attempt."
Frens Ricter"I ask Mr. President to share the final contents of this proposal as soon as possible."
Frens Ricter"Thank you, Mrs. Speaker."
NarratorSuddenly Kesaro Kibener started yelling from his seat.
Kesaro Kibener"The President must have lost his mind to cooperate with the likes of you, Mr. Ricter!"
Kesaro Kibener"You and PFJP are nothing but puppets of western interests!"
Gloria Tory"ORDER!"
Kesaro Kibener"Madame Speaker! I know you are in agreement with me as well. Don't stand idly there while this administration is making deals with snakes and undermine the integrity of Sor-"
Gloria Tory"ORDER! Mr. Kibener! How many times do I need to say? You are speaking out of tu-"
Kesaro Kibener"I demand to speak!"
NarratorGloria gave him a stare. She looked annoyed.
Gloria Tory"...Very well, Mr. Kibener... Go ahead."
Kesaro Kibener"Mr. President!"
NarratorHe looked directly at me.
Kesaro Kibener"The nationalist movement does not agree with your priorities nor the way you're trying to implement them. The National Front Party will not stand with you on your new Constitution."
Kesaro Kibener"Let it be known that the NFP's concerns are not included in this so called "democratic" reforms. Thus Mr. President has no right to claim them as such!"
Kesaro Kibener"We will stand our ground against any attempt to bring down our established political culture and national values. Thank you."
NarratorKesaro went back to his seat but the chaos in the Assembly continued.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:37:25 p20780 / c331
  • ·Ask to speak.
  • ▶Leave the Assembly.
Leaving the Assembly allows Anton Rayne to conserve political capital and avoid further confrontation, focusing on his re-election goals.
DeepSeek-R1I decided to leave the Assembly and its chaos behind.
NarratorI got up from my seat and started my walk towards the exit.
NarratorMP's kept shouting and responding to each other, creating a loud mess where nothing was legible.
Gloria Tory"Order! Order! This topic has finished. We are moving unto our next topic..."
NarratorI slowly left the hall. The shouting could be heard from every corridor of the Grand National Assembly.

TURN 06

■ Conscription And Military PolicyT06

BUDGET -5WEALTH 2STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorI arrived at the cubic building that housed most of the military command that resided in Holsord. The grey and brutalist structure looked daunting.
NarratorI never liked this building. It always made me feel like I was standing under the concrete foot of a giant getting ready to crush me. It made me feel small and I never quite got used to the feeling even after this many years.
NarratorAt the entrance, I was greeted by Iosef and his military entourage of young officers.
Iosef Lancea"Welcome to the Ministry of Defense, Mr. President. Let's go to my office."
NarratorWe walked from corridor to corridor to reach the office. On the way, we were met with high ranking officers as well as privates. Every time one of these soldiers saw me, they stopped and saluted me.
NarratorWhen we finally arrived at Iosef's office, his entourage saluted us and left. He closed the door and took a seat.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:39:01 p20264 / c463
  • ·”Where is Valken? He usually partakes in all military meetings.“
  • ▶”This must be something serious. Especially since you invited me here.“
  • ·”Let's begin. What is the matter?“
Acknowledging the gravity of the situation shows leadership and concern.
DeepSeek-R1"This must be something serious. Especially since you invited me here."
Iosef Lancea"It is important that you don't get misinformed on this matter. Valken has another agenda."
NarratorHe leaned back and interlocked his hands.
Iosef Lancea"The situation with Rumburg is getting worse and our lack of funding in the military means that we would fight for survival in a war scenario."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:39:54 p19628 / c536
  • ·”Our soldiers are surely better motivated than theirs. We will defend our country!“
  • ▶”What are our options in improving the situation?“
  • ·”I will make sure it won't come to that.“
Asking about specific improvement options allows for a strategic and informed response to the military situation.
DeepSeek-R1"What are our options in improving the situation?"
Iosef Lancea"We are making all the necessary preparations but any substantial military support from other nations would be a welcome deterrent against Rumburg."
Iosef Lancea"As a result of the recent deal with Wehlen, we have started reinforcing the border."
Iosef Lancea"Forward scouts have been deployed and we have started moving our divisions."
Iosef Lancea"Rest assured. We are ready for anything that will come from the other side of the border."
Iosef Lancea"We will be providing our reports to you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:40:38 p19700 / c379
  • ▶”I don't think we will have any problems but stay alert. We don't know what we are dealing with yet.“
  • ·”I don't want anyone in. Do not let a single deserter pass our borders.“
  • ·”Try to be lenient if possible. There might be people who are simply trying to escape war among them.“
Maintaining vigilance without taking extreme measures keeps Sordland's options open and avoids unnecessary conflict.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't think we will have any problems but stay alert. We don't know what we are dealing with yet."
Iosef Lancea"We are always ready for any situation, Mr. President."
NarratorIosef cleared his throat.
Iosef Lancea"Let's move on to our main topic."
Iosef Lancea"The mandatory conscription law has become a matter of discussion and we need to strike a decision whether to keep it or remove it."
Iosef Lancea"The facts are that the conscription gives us a large reservist pool that can cover a larger frontline but these troops lack the morale and quality to be offensive elements."
Iosef Lancea"My argument would be to remove conscription if our military can stand on it's own two feet without them. If we are going to have strong allies why do we need conscripts?"
NarratorA strong knock on the door was heard and the Chief of the Armed Forces; Valken Kruger walked in quickly with a frown.
Valken Kruger"What is the meaning of this?! Iosef?"
Valken Kruger"Why haven't I been invited? What is going on here?"
Iosef Lancea"You are overstepping and interrupting a personal meeting between me and the President."
NarratorValken deliberated the situation.
Valken Kruger"I see what is going on here."
Valken Kruger"I mean no disrespect but I am leading the ground operations and I must be informed of any strategic changes."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:41:27 p19874 / c462
  • ·”Valken has a point. The military needs to be on the same page on any changes.“
  • ▶”Well. Now that you are here, what is your opinion on conscription?“
  • ·”The Minister of Defense is your superior and so am I.“
Inviting Valken's opinion ensures all perspectives are considered for an informed decision.
DeepSeek-R1"Well. Now that you are here, what is your opinion on conscription?"
Valken Kruger"The matter is rather simple from my point of view."
Valken Kruger"Our forces have undergone large structural changes changing the operational capabilities and tactics."
Valken Kruger"However, at the end of the day Rumburg has a larger army, air-force and navy. Our conscripts will fill the much needed weaknesses we have."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:42:17 p19877 / c517
  • ▶”Agreed. There is a place for reservists.“
  • ·”We don't share the same opinion. This is a modern age and requires a modern army.“
  • ·”Throwing bodies at a problem never solved it. Not sure if this is the right approach.“
  • ·”This doctrine worked for decades so far, no reason to change it. I think the generals have a point.“
Keeping conscription provides a safety net of reservists, which is crucial given the ongoing tensions with Rumburg and budget constraints.
DeepSeek-R1"Agreed. There is a place for reservists."
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President, we need to prioritize quality over quantity."
Valken Kruger"Your final decision, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:43:11 p19770 / c632
  • ·”Keep mandatory conscription. We need every single able body to defend the nation.“
  • ▶”Remove mandatory conscription. It is time to have a professional force of willingly enlisted soldiers.“
Transitioning to a professional force would improve military effectiveness and align with modern defense strategies.
DeepSeek-R1"Remove mandatory conscription. It is time to have a professional force of willingly enlisted soldiers."
Iosef Lancea"Alright. Then we can move on."
Iosef Lancea"Let me look over my documents to see if there is anything else of concern."
NarratorIosef checked his folder before looking at me.
Iosef Lancea"That's about it. I won't take more of your time sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:43:50 p19740 / c263
  • ·”Have a good day.“
  • ·”Thank you for your time.“
  • ▶Salute.
A salute is an appropriate gesture of respect after discussing military reforms with high-ranking officials.
DeepSeek-R1I saluted and Iosef saluted back.
NarratorI left the Ministry of Defense and returned to the Maroon Palace.

■ OverviewT06

BUDGET -3WEALTH 2STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorThe team gathered in Holsord at the Ministry of Economy to go over the current economic situation.
NarratorThe building was constructed in 1892 by Heron Walt, possibly the most famous architect in Sordland. It was a simple and elegant structure with a few of his signature domes spread out over the roof.
NarratorI walked through the large bronze doors. Two assistants led me upstairs to a meeting room.
NarratorEveryone stood up as I entered the room, buttoning their suit jackets.
Symon Holl"Welcome, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:44:50 p19605 / c311
  • ·“Please take your seats.”
  • ▶“It's good to be here.”
  • ·“Let's begin.”
A friendly acknowledgment sets a positive tone while maintaining professionalism.
DeepSeek-R1"It's good to be here."
NarratorEveryone took their seats.
Symon Holl"As you all know, today we'll go over our economic status."
Symon Holl"Let's begin without any further ado."
Symon Holl"The aid we have received from Arcasia has helped us tremendously."
Symon Holl"Personally I think it was a great decision, Mr. President."
Symon Holl"We will use the majority of the capital to combat the recession."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:46:00 p19652 / c332
  • ·“I would expect nothing less from our Arcasian friends.”
  • ▶“I had to do whatever it took to end the recession. Even if it meant a deal with Arcasia.”
  • ·“Let's hope this decision won't come back to haunt us later on.”
Anton Rayne's response should reflect his pragmatic leadership style, acknowledging the tough decision made to end the recession despite potential long-term implications.
DeepSeek-R1"I had to do whatever it took to end the recession. Even if it meant a deal with Arcasia."
Symon Holl"I don't think anyone in the room is questioning your decision, Mr. President."
Gus Manger"How are we doing in terms of our infrastructure?"
Symon Holl"The first months had positive indicators but the delay of the infrastructure project reversed economic gains."
NarratorMikail frowned and put his fingers on his chin.
Mikail Aven"Why couldn't we solve the L-1 High-speed rail construction issues?"
Mikail Aven"The National Business Council isn't happy with the lack of tangible economic improvements at all."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:46:47 p19773 / c382
  • ▶“I expected a better performance from all of us. Our first investment was meant to be a good first step in the overall plan.”
  • ·“Focusing on future investments is better as more opportunities are surely on the horizon.”
  • ·“Certain individuals will be held accountable for their failures. ”
Anton Rayne would choose to address the team's performance while maintaining a strategic focus.
DeepSeek-R1"I expected a better performance from all of us. Our first investment was meant to be a good first step in the overall plan."
Gus Manger"Surely there is still a way to finish what we have begun."
Edith Agnoc"From my perspective, we need a bigger cash injection into the economy."
Symon Holl"Deivid, do you have the stats on our deal with Agnolia?"
Deivid Wisci"Our trade deal with Agnolia has increased our imports and exports by 25% in one month with much more to be expected in the future."
Deivid Wisci"But higher prices of Agnolian steel didn't result in a trade surplus."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:47:27 p19827 / c300
  • ·“That doesn't seem like a positive indicator.”
  • ·“That's what we expected.”

Expanding trade can help boost the economy despite current instability.
DeepSeek-R1"We should look for more trade partners. We can make much more and increase our presence in the region."
Deivid Wisci"I agree. Not just the region, we should target for more deals internationally."
Deivid Wisci"We should see our improved relations with Agnolia as a stepping stone to expand positive influence."
Edith Agnoc"I support our foreign initiatives with partners as democratic as Agnolia."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:48:16 p19750 / c453
  • ▶“I agree. Aligning ourselves with more democratic countries is good for Sordland.”
  • ·“That's beside the point.”
  • ·“I didn't ask you for your opinion.”
Aligning with democratic countries can enhance Sordland's international standing while promoting stability and growth.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree. Aligning ourselves with more democratic countries is good for Sordland."
Edith Agnoc"It is good for overall trade with other countries as well. Democratic countries generate more trust in general."
Symon Holl"Let's hear the stats on our deal with Wehlen."
Deivid Wisci"Our trade deal with Wehlen has increased our imports and exports by 60% in one month, which is a considerably good number."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:49:04 p19704 / c583

Deivid Wisci"Regardless, the numbers are clear."

Deivid Wisci"Albeit risky, it is lucrative."
Symon Holl"Increased trade has improved our economy. I see this as a reason for celebration."
Edith Agnoc"On a side note, consumer spending has decreased further due to a pessimistic mood caused by our economic issues."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:50:21 p19659 / c271

  • ▶“I'm confident we will turn this mood around with adaptive projects and solutions.”

DeepSeek-R1"I'm confident we will turn this mood around with adaptive projects and solutions."
Symon Holl"Good to hear. We will do better."
Symon Holl"While I agree that our first attempts were not successful, we still might have economic opportunities left."
Mikail Aven"It isn't good for our reputation. The people expected quick solutions. We are running out of time."
Edith Agnoc"The Central Bank has begun working on a bill with the Grand National Assembly to increase our preparedness for future damaging economic incidents."
Mikail Aven"The NBC will also lend support to the analysis and contingency plans regarding the ongoing developments."
Symon Holl"I am looking forward to further collaboration between our departments to solve upcoming issues."
NarratorSymon checked his large dossier.
Symon Holl"Sadly, our budget reserves don't look good."
Symon Holl"We will need to look into whatever we can to increase the budget we have."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:51:03 p19632 / c373
  • ·“That's unfortunate.”
  • ▶“What can we do about it?”
Taking initiative to address economic challenges shows leadership and commitment to finding solutions.
DeepSeek-R1"What can we do about it?"
Symon Holl"I will formulate a plan and present you with the next steps."
Symon Holl"As per the latest data from the ministry, the recession unfortunately seems to be lingering still in some economic areas."
Symon Holl"No matter the cost, we will have to increase our economic development rates. Otherwise, I am sad to say that we will be doomed."
Symon Holl"We need to use the opportunities we have been given. Trade deals and infrastructure improvements always help."
Symon Holl"Mr. President. I will be very clear."
NarratorSymon took off his glasses and put them on the table. He looked at me straight in the eye.
Symon Holl"You already know this, and I don't want to frighten you, but..."
Symon Holl"If we are unable to stop this recession, we are heading towards an economic catastrophe that has never been seen before in Sordland."
Symon Holl"No, in all of Eastern Merkopa."
Symon Holl"I can't even begin to imagine how we could survive that. None of us wants to see Sordish people starve while the state is unable to provide for its citizens."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:51:45 p19771 / c348
  • ·“As you said, none of us want that.”
  • ·“Don't worry. We will end the recession. I promise.”
  • ▶“Then we need to get to work.”
Emphasizing the need for immediate action demonstrates leadership and urgency in addressing the economic crisis.
DeepSeek-R1"Then we need to get to work."
Symon Holl"Exactly."
NarratorHe put on his glasses.
Symon Holl"Now..."
Symon Holl"There are a few methods we can use to tackle the recession."
Symon Holl"Our debt situation is awfully bad, and I heavily advise against further expenditures. However, I will still present new projects and leave the decision to you, Mr. President."
Symon Holl"There are two main distinctions between these projects. It's either about continuing our infrastructure improvements or investing in increasing production."
Symon Holl"If we continue working on our infrastructure, that would lift us up to advanced country levels. However, our industrial production also needs a boost to return to pre-recession levels."
Edith Agnoc"There is of course the option to not take any risks related to our budget."
Symon Holl"The plans for these projects will be finalized soon. For now, I can provide preliminary information. Which one would you like to hear first?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:52:35 p19709 / c511
  • ·“What are the projects that could improve our infrastructure?”
  • ▶“What are the projects that could boost our production?”
  • ·“I don't want to take the risk. Let's move on.”
Boosting industrial production could provide quicker economic relief by creating jobs and increasing output, addressing the recession's immediate effects.
DeepSeek-R1"What are the projects that could boost our production?"
Symon Holl"For production we have two projects. One is the Sarna Agricultural Zone and the other is the Conriat Industrial City."
Deivid Wisci"I see the Sarna Agricultural Zone investment as a higher priority due to the fact that farmers in Bergia were the most hit by the recession. Sarna also is a much poorer city than Conriat."
Edith Agnoc"The Conriat Industrial City project has the potential to increase manufacturing in the Greater Holsord Region by a large amount."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:53:20 p20096 / c415

  • ▶“Conriat could benefit from a larger manufacturing industry. It would bring the city to a position where it could rival Lachaven.”

Mikail Aven"We should invest and develop Conriat to diversify the economy in Greater Holsord which would mean a great deal."
Edith Agnoc"Manufacturing investments are the kind of investments we need for a healthy economy and our currency strength."
Gus Manger"Conriat is a developing city with a lot of potential while Sarna and the Bergia region are lacklustre."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:54:11 p19959 / c542

Symon Holl"For infrastructure we have two projects. One is the Benfi International Airport and the other is the Morna Port."
Mikail Aven"Benfi is growing at a rapid pace and would benefit highly from a international airport. Much more money to be made there too."
Deivid Wisci"The Morna port will boost trade and improve the flow of goods towards Holsord, because the Conriat port is too small in capacity and extension is blocked by the navy headquarters."

Mikail Aven"I don't think investing in Morna with a port would do much to change our economic prospects. Goods are flowing from other places just as fast."
Edith Agnoc"Increased imports and exports on a more accessible path to Holsord is very lucrative in my opinion. Time is money."
Deivid Wisci"I agree with Edith. The Morna port will mean a great deal to our trade and improve the economy of the Lorren region too."

Symon Holl"Very well."
Symon Holl"That covers our subjects for today."
