■ Bergia MeetingT06

BUDGET -3WEALTH 2STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorNia Morgna and Lileas Graf scheduled a meeting to discuss an ongoing legal case that required my attention.
NarratorEarly in the morning, we convened in my office.
Lileas Graf"Thank you for being here at this hour, Mr. President."
Lileas Graf"We need to inform you about some news from Bergia."
Nia Morgna"There is a court case initiated by a Bludish citizen which involves a possible revision of the constitution. Unfortunately, it has the potential to affect the whole country."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:57:00 p19556 / c511
  • ·"I'm listening."
  • ▶"Explain, please."
  • ·"Great. Bad news from Bergia again. What's new?"
Anton Rayne should seek detailed information about the court case's implications and its potential impact on national stability before responding.
DeepSeek-R1"Explain, please."
Lileas Graf"I'll start from the beginning. As you know, after the discovery of KA-74s in separatist hideouts, there have been more than a few attacks initiated and claimed by the BFF."
Lileas Graf"Due to the lack of security funding, some of these -- otherwise avoidable -- attacks couldn't be stopped. This led to some increase in the tensions in Bergia."
NarratorLileas sighed.
Lileas Graf"The main problem is with the security structure of Bergia. Due to the special status of the region, operations on the ground are run by local security forces that are not bound to the Interior."
Lileas Graf"Which means my hands are tied."
Nia Morgna"Unfortunately, the court backlog is piling up with increasingly frequent rulings that are unjust. This has led to many of our citizens losing their trust in our justice system. This is particularly a problem for the residents of Bergia."
Nia Morgna"It's definitely not helping with the increasing tensions between the Bludish populace and Sords."
Lileas Graf"It increased even further after Operation Bear Trap. We have been receiving more and more intelligence about some kind of a 'preparation' by the terrorists."
Lileas Graf"We can't allow the conflict in Wehlen to spread to Sordland. And we know that the Wehlen and Sordland divisions of the BFF are in close cooperation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:57:47 p19794 / c433
  • ·"This is worrying."
  • ·"I think the threat is overblown."
  • ·"You have my ear."
  • ▶"That's precisely why we're cooperating with Wehzek forces to defend our borders."
Referencing the cooperation with Wehlen forces highlights strategic military alliances against BFF threats.
DeepSeek-R1"That's precisely why we're cooperating with Wehzek forces to defend our borders."
Nia Morgna"The main problem is not security but the very right to existence of the Bludish people. I'd still like to believe your perspective can be changed on this subject."
Lileas Graf"We'll get there, Ms. Morgna. As I was saying..."
Lileas Graf"The increasing threat of the BFF led Governor Bron to -- justifiably -- resort to his emergency powers to curb the danger."
Lileas Graf"His discovery of militant training grounds disguised as schools was commendable, as well as his quick handling of bomb threats coming from major cities like Deyr."
Lileas Graf"However, his latest policies have been questionable. Especially the 'Peaceful Resettlement' order. It has been met with massive anger from the majority of the Bluds in the region."
Nia Morgna"That is a massive understatement. Governor Bron has abused his powers and clearly acted against the constitution."
Nia Morgna"And quite surprisingly, Ishval Ersen has managed to successfully push his case to the higher courts. He is suing Phelix Bron for infringing upon the liberties of Bludish people."
Nia Morgna"He also raises a conflict in the constitution and argues that the way it is written makes it open to interpretation and allows people like Governor Bron to abuse their powers."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:58:35 p20180 / c482
  • ·"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
  • ·"What exactly is this case about?"
  • ·"What are the accusations against the governor?"
  • ▶"What exactly does Ersen claim about the conflict in the constitution?"
Understanding Ersen's constitutional claims is key to balancing legal rights with national security.
DeepSeek-R1"What exactly does Ersen claim about the conflict in the constitution?"
Nia Morgna"As you know, the rights and liberties Article 7 alludes to are described in Article 100 in further detail."
Nia Morgna"All citizens, in theory, can exercise their rights described in these articles and are equally subject to law as described, and these rights cannot be suspended by the government under any circumstances other than a defensive war."
Nia Morgna"Mr. Ersen claims the line: 'Every Sord is equal before the law' in Article 7 does not include other ethnicities and thus gives the state the right to suspend or limit the rights of non-Sords defined in Article 100."
Nia Morgna"Courts have repeatedly protected Governor Bron in the past, and it is true that he bets on Mr. Hawker justifying his actions through Article 7. However, there is also Article 6 -- which the courts could have taken into account already."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R112:59:19 p20377 / c363
  • ·"And what is the problem with Article 6?"
  • ·"Explain."
  • ·"This is getting very complicated."
  • ▶"Article 7 is an embarrassment that needs to be removed. I wish we could also tackle this in our proposal for constitutional change."
Tying the constitutional issue to broader reform goals shows leadership and alignment with the manifesto.
DeepSeek-R1"Article 7 is an embarrassment that needs to be removed. I wish we could also tackle this in our proposal for constitutional change."
Nia Morgna"It wasn't a possibility according to the preliminary committee meetings."
Nia Morgna"But there might be a way around it in the near future."
Nia Morgna"As you know, Article 6 goes: 'Everyone who is bound to the Sordish state by citizenship is a Sord'."
Lileas Graf"Which clarifies clearly that the constitution does not discriminate against other ethnicities or cultures. Citizens of this country are called Sords."
Nia Morgna"But did you ask Bludish people what they think? Yes, it's true that this article has protected them on a number of occasions, as it defines Sord as a non-ethnic term, but it is left up to the interpretation of the courts."
Nia Morgna"They could have argued that Article 6 extends the rights defined in Article 7 to all citizens of Sordland regardless of race. But they didn't. Yet."
Nia Morgna"They can potentially push this case to the Supreme Court."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:00:10 p20395 / c563
  • ·"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
  • ·"What exactly is this case about?"
  • ▶"What are the accusations against the governor?"
Understanding the specific accusations against Governor Bron will help assess the impact on Bludish people and national stability.
DeepSeek-R1"What are the accusations against the governor?"
Lileas Graf"Mr. Bron has sadly evicted thousands of Bluds from their homes in Deyr recently. The fact that this happened in a city with relatively low levels of BFF activity is a sloppy decision at best."
Nia Morgna"A sloppy decision? This is a direct violation of their right to property. The BFF activity levels have nothing to do with this."
Lileas Graf"But it does, Ms. Morgna. The state can take any measures to fight with terror inside its borders. The decision would have been justified if those neighborhoods were in fact labelled as 'threat level red'."
Lileas Graf"...But they were not. Hence, he did not have the authority to evict those citizens. In this case in Deyr, the governor has definitely violated his authority."
Nia Morgna"You are underplaying it. This eviction was only the last of the many he ordered as part of Bergia's *peaceful resettlement policies*. This is not about Deyr."
Nia Morgna"There are hundreds of accusations listed in this case, ranging from violent actions by law enforcement officers to open discrimination in local municipalities."
Lileas Graf"Calm down, Nia dear. I'm not disagreeing with you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:00:53 p20410 / c359
  • ·"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
  • ▶"What exactly is this case about?"
Asking about the specifics of the case will provide clarity on its implications for Sordland.
DeepSeek-R1"What exactly is this case about?"
Nia Morgna"Ishval Ersen is claiming that the current constitution gives the legal grounds to discriminate against Bludish people, directly infringing upon their rights as citizens."
Nia Morgna"He claims Article 7 of the constitution -- citing the line: 'Every Sord is equal before the law' -- is in direct conflict with the freedoms and rights defined in Article 100."
Nia Morgna"Governor Phelix Bron is accused of using Article 7 to justify his oppression of the Bludish community."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:01:41 p20380 / c456
  • ·"I have enough information."
  • ▶"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
Asking who Ishval Ersen is will provide deeper insight into the case's credibility and implications.
DeepSeek-R1"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
Nia Morgna"Ishval Ersen is a lawyer and Bludish rights activist. He lives in Deyr and has experienced the systemic racism in the region firsthand."
Nia Morgna"He was evicted last month from his home in Deyr on the grounds of 'threatening public stability and safety'."
Nia Morgna"He was also shot at by local law enforcement forces several times in the last two years and managed to survive it all so far. The attackers, of course, were never punished."
Lileas Graf"As much as the eviction seems unjust, Mr. Ersen must be further investigated for his potential ties to dangerous organizations. I'd like to believe the forces wouldn't have fired at him without proper reason."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:02:24 p20503 / c368
  • ▶"What the Bludish community is going through in Bergia is unacceptable. We must do what it takes to stop this madness."
  • ·"Ms. Graf is right. We must first be completely sure that Mr. Ersen is innocent."
  • ·"Are you joking, Ms. Graf? You'd like to *believe*? The police are getting out of control under your management."
Expressing strong opposition to the discrimination and committing to action maintains political capital and public trust.
DeepSeek-R1"What the Bludish community is going through in Bergia is unacceptable. We must do what it takes to stop this madness."
Nia Morgna"That we must."
NarratorNia stacked the papers in front of her.
Nia Morgna"In any case... The situation might be troubling if it reaches the Supreme Court and Mr. Hawker manages to sabotage it by altering the current interpretation. It would be disastrous for Bludish rights."
Nia Morgna"I will be doing my best to ensure a positive result comes out of this case. But I'm not sure if the centrists would be willing to agree with Mr. Ersen."
Lileas Graf"Why would Mr. Hawker even sabotage it? The current interpretation is strictly Sollist. I'm sure he will follow the constitution and charge Governor Bron for unconstitutional behavior."
Lileas Graf"According to the foundational values of this republic, everyone who is a citizen of this country is a Sord. The constitution is clear about this and does not allow for discrimination."
Nia Morgna"Maybe Hawker is not the man you think he is, Ms. Graf. From what I've heard, his stance on this subject may not be the standard Sollist interpretation but... something darker."
Nia Morgna"I'm afraid he'll reject the case altogether and find Governor Bron innocent. If this happens, it would make discrimination against Bludish people legal."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:03:16 p20663 / c511
  • ▶"Do you think Justice Hawker and the Old Guards would go that far?"
  • ·"This is insane. Isn't there anything I can do?"
  • ·"Honestly, I couldn't care less. It would be better for this country."
Asking about Justice Hawker and the Old Guards' potential involvement is strategic, seeking to understand if there's broader manipulation or power dynamics at play.
DeepSeek-R1"Do you think Justice Hawker and the Old Guards would go that far?"
Nia Morgna"According to the rumors within the Supreme Court, yes. It is already unusual that the case is making its way up to them."
Lileas Graf"To be honest, I don't think this is a matter for our administration. We must keep out from judicial affairs."
Nia Morgna"As much as I hate to say it, I somewhat agree with Lileas on this. I'd prefer to keep the status quo over risking a complete abolishment of rights. We still have the power to make tangible reforms."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:04:22 p20778 / c853
  • ▶"Your lack of faith is disappointing. I must say I didn't expect that from you. And what about your tangible reforms?"
  • ·"What if we can convince the Justices to agree with Ersen? Wouldn't that give us precedent to change said articles of the constitution?"
  • ·"You must put pressure on the system and block this case from reaching the Supreme Court, Ms. Morgna."
  • ·"You are right. We shouldn't meddle in the judiciary."
Anton Rayne chooses to challenge Nia Morgna's pessimism and emphasize the importance of tangible reforms, asserting leadership and pushing for change despite risks.
DeepSeek-R1"Your lack of faith is disappointing. I must say I didn't expect that from you. And what about your tangible reforms?"
Nia Morgna"I'm simply stating the obvious, Mr. President. Instead of openly attacking the court, we can bring real change with our lawmaking powers."
Lileas Graf"Ms. Morgna is right. There is something that the government can do to address the situation on the ground."
Lileas Graf"It is time we push for change within the security structure of Bergia and the special status that resulted in de facto autonomy. It clearly isn't working."
Nia Morgna"The resettlement policies in Bergia and the Ersen case have changed the mood of the lawmakers. We can use this opportunity to push for the removal of Tarquin Soll's security zone decree. It is a much bigger possibility than before."
Nia Morgna"Of course racism is rampant throughout our country, but Bludish citizens inside Bergia are having a much worse time than the Bludish residents of cities like Lachaven or Holsord. If we focus on Bergia, we can remove its special status."
Nia Morgna"This alone would be a huge step for Bludish rights. Unfortunately, as long as Governor Bron is in charge of Bergia with the powers he has, no judicial reforms would reverse the situation on the ground."
Lileas Graf"To put it simply, we want to work on a detailed proposal to restructure Bergia. I believe it must become a region of Sordland like any other. We must centralize the system and abolish the local security forces."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:05:10 p20927 / c412
  • ·"Are you sure we can even revoke the special status of Bergia?"
  • ·"Are you absolutely certain that your security forces can do a better job than Bergian police?"
  • ▶"I don't see how a local change is more important than removing the basis of discrimination in our very constitution."
  • ·"I don't agree with changing the structure in Bergia now. We shouldn't disrupt Governor Bron's policies."
Emphasizing the need for constitutional change highlights the importance of addressing systemic discrimination over local structural reforms.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't see how a local change is more important than removing the basis of discrimination in our very constitution."
Nia Morgna"It's not. But when the forces on the ground don't even respect the constitution, fixing it alone would not suffice."
Lileas Graf"Let's wake up to the real world. There is no way the Assembly or the Court would pass an amendment to Article 6 or 7."
Nia Morgna"If our current proposal passes, we will no longer need the Court to change the constitution."
Nia Morgna"And the Assembly can always be convinced later."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:05:59 p20929 / c460
  • ·"Are you sure we can even revoke the special status of Bergia?"
  • ·"Are you absolutely certain that your security forces can do a better job than Bergian police?"
  • ·"I don't agree with changing the structure in Bergia now. We shouldn't disrupt Governor Bron's policies."
  • ▶"I still think we can add these amendments to our proposal before the vote. The current proposal can still be modified."
Modifying the proposal to include constitutional changes directly addresses discrimination and aligns with progressive goals.
DeepSeek-R1"I still think we can add these amendments to our proposal before the vote. The current proposal can still be modified."
Lileas Graf"You can do whatever you want with your amendments, Mr. President. But at least hear about our proposal first."
Lileas Graf"Nia and I both agree that we need to abolish the Special Zone of Bergia. This is the first point that the proposal will address."
Lileas Graf"This would also mean taking away the special authority of the governor."
Nia Morgna"I agree with removing the governor as well as his insane authority. But I don't support a complete centralization of the region. I propose that we restructure the Special Zone, democratize it and acknowledge the Bludish movement."
Lileas Graf"How does that resolve anything? How do you solve any problem by dividing the region from the rest of Sordland? We've tried that already."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:06:47 p21003 / c461
  • ▶"Hold on, Lileas. I want to hear Nia's proposal."
  • ·"I agree with Lileas. Replacing a special zone with another one wouldn't help anyone."
  • ·"I don't agree with either of your points."
Choosing to hear Nia's proposal acknowledges the value of alternative solutions and keeps the conversation open.
DeepSeek-R1"Hold on, Lileas. I want to hear Nia's proposal."
Nia Morgna"Thank you, Mr. President."
Nia Morgna"Millions of Bludish people suffered over the last decades. We saw a massive uprising, terror attacks and a governor going rogue. These people only wanted to live in this country as equals, but they were denied that."
Nia Morgna"Why don't we allow Bergians to elect their own local government? Why is Holsord so keen on prying into the lives of the Bludish people?"
Nia Morgna"We should replace the oppressive laws of the special zone with a democratic system that allows for proper local representation. I propose the 'Bergia Semi-Autonomous Region'."
Lileas Graf"I'm sorry to tell you, Ms. Morgna, but Bergia is part of Sordland as much as Agnland or Holsord. You're bringing secessionist Fetih Ejall's lifelong quest here as *your proposal*. What's wrong with you?"
Lileas Graf"What's next? Secession of Bergia and a formation of an independent Bludish state? Autonomy for Agnland? And why don't you hold a referendum for the Rums of Estord, and ask them if they want to join Rumburg while you're at it?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:07:47 p21276 / c688
  • ▶"Lileas has a point. Bergia shouldn't have any restrictions or privileges compared to any other region."
  • ·"I'm supportive of a semi-autonomous Bergia. This is what the mainstream Bludish movement wants."
  • ·"I also want to hear Ms. Graf's proposal before deciding."
Maintaining national unity while addressing concerns cautiously to avoid economic strain and potential unrest.
DeepSeek-R1"Lileas has a point. Bergia shouldn't have any restrictions or privileges compared to any other region."
NarratorLileas smiled.
Lileas Graf"So it's decided, then? I can start working on the proposal as soon as possible?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:08:34 p21306 / c368
  • ·"Yes. It's been decided, Ms. Graf. You can get to work immediately. I'm counting on you."
  • ·"On second thought, semi-autonomy is the way to go. Start the work immediately, Ms. Morgna. I'm counting on you."
  • ▶"On second thought... I think it's best if we keep the status quo. We will not change anything about Bergia."
Maintaining the status quo avoids judicial conflict and focuses on economic recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"On second thought... I think it's best if we keep the status quo. We will not change anything about Bergia."
Lileas Graf"I must say I'm disappointed, Mr. President."
Nia Morgna"With your constitutional proposal on the horizon, I expected a different response."
Nia Morgna"I share the same sentiments as Lileas on this, Mr. President. Your inaction is frankly very disappointing."
Lileas Graf"Don't waste your breath, Nia. The president has already made his decision."
Lileas Graf"Let's take our leave."
Nia Morgna"You're right. Let's go."
Nia Morgna"Have a nice day, Mr. President."
NarratorThey left the office.

■ Health BudgetT06

BUDGET -5WEALTH 2STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorI was having my afternoon tea in the state house office in Deyr and going through newspapers.
NarratorOne of the headlines piqued my interest. It read 'Polio Crisis in Wehlen'.
NarratorBefore I could start reading, I heard a couple of knocks. Paskal entered followed by Lucian.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. It's time for the scheduled healthcare meeting."
Paskal Beniwoll"Good afternoon."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:10:31 p20671 / c208
  • ·“Please take your seats.”
  • ·“Very well. Let's begin.”
  • ▶Nod.
A nod is a quick way to acknowledge their presence and move efficiently into the meeting.
DeepSeek-R1I nodded and they have taken their seats and Paskal produced documents from his suitcase. He put them on the table.
NarratorPaskal looked at the newspaper I was reading.
Paskal Beniwoll"Very troubling news from Wehlen, Mr. President."
Paskal Beniwoll"I have just returned from a meeting with our top scientists on the matter of Polio. It seems to be spreading."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:11:17 p20568 / c412
  • ·“Indeed. This might have dire consequences in the region.”
  • ·“I'm not worried. It will stop before it reaches Sordland.”
  • ▶“What are we doing to prepare?”
  • ·“Why am I just learning this from a newspaper?”
Anton Rayne focuses on critical issues and realpolitik. Acknowledging the potential impact of a polio crisis aligns with his pragmatic approach by addressing public health concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"What are we doing to prepare?"
Paskal Beniwoll"We have notified all of our healthcare staff, especially the ones closer to the Wehlen border. Unfortunately, these are also the more rural areas where we lack equipment and staff."
Paskal Beniwoll"What I was told by our researchers was that with the current infection rate, there will soon be millions of people affected by Polio."
Paskal Beniwoll"We need to be ready for any possibility."
Lucian Galade"I doubt it is that serious yet."
Lucian Galade"Regardless, back to the topic. Mr. Beniwoll."
NarratorPaskal put on his reading glasses and went through the documents. He didn't say anything for a whole minute.
Lucian Galade"Mr. Beniwoll?"
Paskal Beniwoll"Yes. Yes, of course. Let's start."
NarratorHe put the papers down, cleared his throat and looked at me.
Paskal Beniwoll"In general, I am very content with the budget decision that was made."
Paskal Beniwoll"This comes as a very pleasant surprise that we all welcomed at the ministry."
Paskal Beniwoll"The overall mood has been positive as the administration focuses more on the healthcare. I'm excited about what we can achieve with the allocated funds."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:12:11 p20701 / c586
  • ·“Do you have any specific areas of focus in mind?”
  • ·“Providing better healthcare is our goal. This administration will be known to have supported the welfare services.”
  • ▶“We have to minimize service disparity in the country. The situation isn't good at all.”
Option 2 directly addresses the disparity in healthcare services, which ties into both economic stability and social equity, crucial for preventing further issues from spilling over.
DeepSeek-R1"We have to minimize service disparity in the country. The situation isn't good at all."
Paskal Beniwoll"I'm personally invested to solve the horrible infant and maternal death rates along with your requests."
Paskal Beniwoll"As you know, I have been preparing a new privatization program that would create funds through selling of unused ministry property and some extra taxes."
Lucian Galade"I know of this too, Paskal. But this goes against our economic doctrine. Additionally, health is a key sector people expect to have in public hands."
Lucian Galade"Aside from creating additional funds, there is a long-term problem with privatization in a country like Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:12:59 p20739 / c446
  • ·“I don't see a problem with privatization. It would create additional funds and boost enterprise.”
  • ▶“You are right. There might be structural issues... Especially due to private greed and market manipulation.”
  • ·“What are those problems?”
Recognizing potential risks of privatization aligns with cautious economic policies and maintains public trust.
DeepSeek-R1"You are right. There might be structural issues... Especially due to private greed and market manipulation."
Lucian Galade"Also in a country where most people don't have access to quality healthcare..."
Paskal Beniwoll"I do agree that there is some concern there... But the benefits of competitive and quality services is greater."
Lucian Galade"This is not Arcasia nor Lespia. You are talking about Sordland, in which the healthcare is free for now."
Lucian Galade"We have people who have their lives depending on a service that is free to them."
Lucian Galade"Messing with that structure will definitely hurt us politically in the future. That I know for sure."
Lucian Galade"But the choice is yours, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:13:52 p20898 / c548
  • ·“We will promote privatization and allow the private sector to invest and buy government's health properties.” [+1 Government Budget]
  • ▶“We'll keep healthcare in state control. Don't allow any private involvement that could endanger core health services.”
Maintaining state control over healthcare prevents risks associated with privatization, ensuring equitable access and protecting core public services.
DeepSeek-R1"We'll keep healthcare in state control. Don't allow any private involvement that could endanger core health services."
Paskal Beniwoll"As you wish. The health services shall remain in public hands."
Lucian Galade"Good choice."
Paskal Beniwoll"Now let's get to the big question of how we will spend the extra funds dedicated to the Ministry of Health."
Paskal Beniwoll"I urge you to consider these proposals. We could build new hospitals where necessary, divert funds to increase the salaries of our health workers, or invest in enhancing the existing treatment facilities in rural areas."
Paskal Beniwoll"Each of these options presents a unique path towards improving healthcare access and quality in Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:14:40 p20986 / c441
  • ·“Building new hospitals sounds interesting. It would also boost our popularity.”
  • ·“Increasing salaries should have a good effect for doctors, nurses and other staff who work so hard for our citizens.”
  • ▶“Improving treatment in rural areas is a must. It would prevent mistreatment, disease and sickness.”
Improving treatment facilities in rural areas will address systemic health issues and reduce disparities between urban and rural regions.
DeepSeek-R1"Improving treatment in rural areas is a must. It would prevent mistreatment, disease and sickness."
Paskal Beniwoll"The sad reality is that our rural clinics are woefully outdated, ill-equipped, and understaffed."
Paskal Beniwoll"This stark disparity between urban and rural healthcare is a major contributor to the high maternal mortality rates and prevalence of other diseases in our rural communities."
Paskal Beniwoll"Addressing this issue is one of my key objectives in this term, and I believe an overhaul of the rural healthcare infrastructure is a critical step in that direction."
Lucian Galade"We're seeing an uptick in minor outbreaks in specific regions."
Lucian Galade"It's largely due to outdated facilities that lack proper hygiene standards and equipment."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:15:33 p21064 / c494
  • ·“Building new hospitals sounds interesting. It would also boost our popularity.”
  • ·“Increasing salaries should have a good effect for doctors, nurses and other staff who work so hard for our citizens.”
  • ▶“We can proceed with the decision, Minister Beniwoll.”
Proceeding with the decision allows Anton to manage funds carefully while still addressing healthcare needs.
DeepSeek-R1"We can proceed with the decision, Minister Beniwoll."
Paskal Beniwoll"In my book 'The Unfortunate Man', I depicted the hardships of a poor mining family, a vivid illustration of the stark health inequalities in our society."
Paskal Beniwoll"As Minister of Health, I strive to rectify these disparities."
Paskal Beniwoll"Therefore, I believe improving treatment in rural areas should be our course of action, Mr. President."
Lucian Galade"It would be best to consider the political advantages of each one, as well as the practical advantages before making a decision."
NarratorA few minutes went by in contemplation as the moment to make a decision came near.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:16:16 p21073 / c314
  • ·“Build new hospitals where they are needed. Access to health will be available for all.”
  • ·“Increase salaries of doctors, nurses and other health staff with incentives for rural areas.”
  • ▶“Improve rural treatment by hiring health staff and upgrading equipment in rural areas.”
Upgrading equipment and hiring staff directly improves rural healthcare quality and accessibility.
DeepSeek-R1"Improve rural treatment by hiring health staff and upgrading equipment in rural areas."
Paskal Beniwoll"New contracts for improvements will be drawn up immediately."
Paskal Beniwoll"Well, that wraps up our discussion for now."
Lucian Galade"Very well. Fruitful meeting nevertheless. Nice and short."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:17:02 p21022 / c385
  • ·“Just the way I like it.”
  • ▶“Thank you for the meeting.”
  • ·“Dismissed.”
A polite and professional acknowledgment of the meeting is appropriate given the context.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you for the meeting."
NarratorPaskal gathered his documents and left along with Lucian.

■ Bill Central Bank ReinforcementT06

BUDGET -5WEALTH 2STABILITY 2Danger
Bill for decisionCentral Bank Reinforcement Act — The Central Bank Reinforcement Act led by Edith Agnoc and the GNA intends to bolster the strength and stability of Sordland's national currency and bolster the overall economy. The Grand National Assembly advocates for the following measures: Section I mandates the accumulation of foreign exchange reserves. By buying foreign currencies, especially those of Sordland's main trading partners, the Central Bank can exert more control over the value of our currency. Section II outlines the adoption of a prudent fiscal policy. By reducing the national debt and limiting budget deficits, the country can present itself as a more stable economic entity, attracting foreign investment and improving our credit rating, which in turn would enable us to borrow at lower interest rates, if necessary. Section III proposes increased legal and informational support for industries with high export potential, such as our emerging electronics sector in Gelsord, wine production in Borin, and pharmaceutical manufacturing in Lachaven. By increasing exports, the demand for our currency on the international market will rise, thus strengthening its value. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R113:18:09 p20840 / c524
  • ▶Sign: Central Bank Reinforcement Act
  • ·Veto: Central Bank Reinforcement Act
The Central Bank Reinforcement Act offers strategic measures to stabilize Sordland's economy by strengthening currency control and fiscal discipline, which could attract foreign investment and improve credit ratings. While it may strain the budget in the short term, these actions aim to enhance long-term economic stability and growth.

■ Reformist Convince PFJPT06

BUDGET -6WEALTH 2STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorThe preparations for the new Constitution were nearly complete. Soon we were going to propose it in the Grand National Assembly. A huge step for our administration.
NarratorBut we desperately needed more support. That's why I had invited Frens Ricter for a chat about the upcoming vote. He and his party held the key to our success.
NarratorWe met in the presidential office at the end of the day. He arrived in his signature navy blue suit and handed me a box of famous Arcasian chocolates before settling down on the couch.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:19:02 p21152 / c250
  • ·"It's good to have you here, Mr. Ricter."
  • ·"Thank you for the gift."
  • ▶"You are a fan of Arcasia, aren't you?"
Engaging with his background could build rapport and mutual interest.
DeepSeek-R1"You are a fan of Arcasia, aren't you?"
Frens Ricter"A fan of their chocolates, yes."
NarratorHe pointed to the box.
Frens Ricter"You don't like them?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:19:48 p21104 / c371
  • ▶"Oh, that's not what I meant. Thanks for the gift."
  • ·"I don't like chocolate."
  • ·"I don't like Arcasia."
A polite response maintains good relations with Frens Ricter, crucial for political support.
DeepSeek-R1"Oh, that's not what I meant. Thanks for the gift."
Frens Ricter"It's nothing, Mr. Rayne."
NarratorHe took a folder from his briefcase and flipped it open. I could see the scribbles he'd made in the margins of my draft proposal.
Frens Ricter"I have to say..."
Frens Ricter"I am surprised by this proposal you've prepared for the new Constitution. I really do not understand your reasoning behind some of the changes."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:20:30 p21080 / c263
  • ·"I thought we were on the same page."
  • ▶"What parts don't you understand?"
  • ·"What's on your mind?"
Asking Frens Ricter to explain his confusion about the constitutional changes shows openness to dialogue and clarifies potential misunderstandings.
DeepSeek-R1"What parts don't you understand?"
Frens Ricter"I don't know where to begin."
Frens Ricter"You have ignored our demands to lower the undemocratic electoral threshold."
Frens Ricter"How are you calling this a democratic package?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:21:14 p21061 / c350
  • ·"These are all necessary to maintain stability."
  • ▶"Be a realist, Mr. Ricter. This proposal is the best we can do."
  • ·"You prefer Soll's Constitution then?"
Emphasizing realism highlights feasibility while maintaining a conciliatory tone to secure potential support.
DeepSeek-R1"Be a realist, Mr. Ricter. This proposal is the best we can do."
Frens Ricter"A realist?"
Frens Ricter"Is that your excuse, Mr. President? So this proposal is realistic but what we demand is not?"
Frens Ricter"And you're saying this while you're begging me for my party's support."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:21:56 p21045 / c286
  • ·"I can't deny that we are desperate for your support."
  • ·"Be it small or great, a step forward is a step forward."
  • ·"I still believe we can accomplish meaningful change together."
  • ▶"Yes, Frens. And you will give me your support."
It's direct and asserts the necessity of his support, emphasizing mutual understanding.

Frens Ricter"I'm not sure if that was a threat, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:22:45 p20981 / c482
  • ·"Of course not."
  • ▶"It was."
  • ·"I just know that you will do the right thing."
The Central Bank Reinforcement Act will strengthen Sordland's economy by improving currency stability and attracting foreign investment, crucial for long-term recovery despite short-term budget cuts.
DeepSeek-R1"It was."
NarratorHe repositioned himself on the couch.
Frens Ricter"Oh, good."
Frens Ricter"Then I don't have to feel bad about watching you commit political suicide."
Frens Ricter"We have survived Tarquin Soll, surely we will survive you as well. Don't expect me to be scared of your threats, Mr. Rayne."
Frens Ricter"If there's nothing else you'd like to talk about, I'd like to get going."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:23:32 p21127 / c397

  • ·"You're not going anywhere before we have a deal."

Apologizing and seeking agreement might help maintain a positive relationship with Frens Ricter, crucial for securing the necessary votes.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm sorry, Mr. Ricter. I'm sure we can still agree on something."
Frens Ricter"Well..."
Frens Ricter"I value Sordland and democracy more than anything, Mr. President. And I also have to look out for myself and my party."
Frens Ricter"Surely you understand."
Frens Ricter"And if we're talking about democracy here... I first need to trust your agenda."
Frens Ricter"And I currently do not."
Frens Ricter"The first bill you signed effectively removed all other parties from funding. You are hijacking the elections from the very start. How can we trust you now?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:24:19 p21095 / c391

DeepSeek-R1"The past is past, Mr. Ricter. Let's fix the future."
Frens Ricter"Well, it's not so simple, Mr. Rayne. You came here with a proposal that ignored our demands. On top of it, you're not even acknowledging your mistakes."
Frens Ricter"I think it's too late to fix anything now. I can't see any deals being made."
Frens Ricter"It's a shame things won't work out between us."

Frens Ricter"Are you joking?"

Frens Ricter"Great. Then we have a deal."

DeepSeek-R1"Before you leave, I'd like to address a few things I've heard about you."
Frens Ricter"There are so many 'things' said about me that I can't keep track of them anymore. What exactly do you mean?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:28:16 p21206 / c619

DeepSeek-R1"Do you have some sort of secret partnership with President Dwight Walker? There are rumors about his influence on you."
Frens Ricter"Because I've met with Walker before? I am the leader of Sordland's largest opposition party and free to visit anyone I please. But since you asked, I will explain the context of our relationship."
Frens Ricter"As you know, the downfall of President Alphonso was abrupt. He was the only progressive hope we had in the USP. Our brief alliance with him accomplished great things, but we ran into a very concerning revelation."
Frens Ricter"In our last meeting before the USP abandoned him, President Alphonso said that Soll was not gone and still pulled the strings behind his back. He was suspicious about a conspiracy against his reformist agenda and he feared everything would collapse around him..."
Frens Ricter"...And it did. Afterwards the party voted you in. I don't think it was a coincidence, Mr. Rayne."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:29:11 p21600 / c574
  • ▶"Wait. What are you trying to say?"
  • ·"You think Soll and the Old Guard orchestrated all of this?"
  • ·"You're saying I was elected because Soll wanted it?"

DeepSeek-R1"Wait. What are you trying to say?"
Frens Ricter"I am trying to say that everything leading up to the 1953 election was orchestrated by the Old Guard."
Frens Ricter"After realizing the true extent of the Sollist deep state's influence, I..."
NarratorHe looked down for a second and continued.
Frens Ricter"We are in this together, so I will be honest. I lost hope that Sordland could ever achieve the liberties and democracy that I'd been working so hard for as long as Sollism was still alive and well. So I decided to act."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:29:57 p21607 / c336

  • ▶"I hope you didn't do anything to compromise our country."
  • ·"I don't like where this is going."
I want to address his concerns directly by showing concern for national security.
DeepSeek-R1"I hope you didn't do anything to compromise our country."
Frens Ricter"Of course not."
Frens Ricter"I was contacted by an Arcasian senator and one of Arcasia's biggest tycoons. And I decided to agree to their meeting in hopes of receiving support."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:30:47 p21602 / c433

  • ▶"You did what!?"
Confronting Frens Ricter directly about the rumors shows strength and addresses potential threats to national sovereignty.
DeepSeek-R1"You did what!?"
Frens Ricter"Please allow me to continue, Mr. Rayne."
Frens Ricter"I flew to Arcasia and I eventually met President Walker. He offered political and economic support for me and my party, asking us to lead Sordland into the democratic sphere and become a member of the ATO. But the exploitative conditions he was demanding would have had crippling effects on our economy."
Frens Ricter"I told him that I was only there to increase economic cooperation and nothing else. I did not get their support, nor did I do anything illegal. To this day, I feel ashamed to have gone to the feet of Arcasia like that. But at least I managed to stop myself from becoming the very thing I've been fighting against."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:31:35 p21750 / c394

  • ·"So the intel was correct after all..."

DeepSeek-R1"I am grateful that you're telling me all this."
Frens Ricter"Thanks for taking it so well. We must always be honest with one another."
Frens Ricter"You have the authority to investigate this and potentially prosecute me for it."
Frens Ricter"I gave you my trust. I hope I have yours too. I will always acknowledge my wrongs and keep fighting for a better future. I believe we are not that different in this."

  • ▶"I appreciate your trust in me, but I'll need to think more about this."
Anton needs to be cautious and consider Frens' honesty carefully.
DeepSeek-R1"I appreciate your trust in me, but I'll need to think more about this."
Frens Ricter"That's fair."
Frens Ricter"Anyway, if you ever want to discuss this topic further, feel free to call me. I don't have anything to hide."
NarratorHe stood up.
Frens Ricter"I'll get going now, if you'll excuse me. Thanks for the meeting."
NarratorWe shook hands.
Frens Ricter"It's nice to have a president that we can finally have a dialogue with."
NarratorHe laughed.
Frens Ricter"I'll see you in the history books. Have a nice evening for now."
NarratorHe left the room.

■ Education SpendingT06

BUDGET -6WEALTH 2STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorAfter a meeting with the rector and a brief tour of the University of Valgen, we gathered in a room with Ciara.
NarratorThe entire campus was visible from the windows. Students were reading books, studying and having picnics under the trees.
NarratorIt reminded me of my own times at Lachaven Business School -- at least, the more carefree moments.
Ciara Walda"Reminiscing about the past?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:34:08 p21132 / c340
  • ·“How were your university years?”
  • ·“Yes. It had its ups and downs but it was still fun.”
  • ▶“It's nothing.”
Maintaining focus on current priorities keeps the conversation aligned with governance and avoids unnecessary distractions.
DeepSeek-R1"It's nothing."
Ciara Walda"Let's begin then."
Ciara Walda"I want to start off by saying this."
Ciara Walda"Stagnation doesn't get anyone anywhere. Simply maintaining my ministry's funds got us sitting duck with the dozens of issues left unsolvable."
Ciara Walda"And as you have already stated, improving Sordland's sexist and horrendously outdated education system is low on your priorities list."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:35:06 p21216 / c636
  • ·“Do not question my decision, Ciara.”
  • ·“There is nothing wrong with the current education system as it is.”
  • ▶“There are other priorities.”
There are more immediate priorities for the budget, such as economic recovery and military reform.
DeepSeek-R1"There are other priorities."
Ciara Walda"What priorities can there be other than the future of Sordland?"
Ciara Walda"Never mind. There is no point talking about what's already done."
NarratorShe brought out a stack of papers from her bag.
NarratorSpinning her ballpoint pen, she went through the papers, organising some of them together.
Ciara Walda"That's all there is for now."
Ciara Walda"I have talked with Ms. Suno to schedule some time to discuss the curriculum. She's a very astute woman, your secretary. You may want to consider promoting her."
Ciara Walda"We will meet again very soon."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:35:47 p21266 / c236
  • ·“Thank you for your efforts.”
  • ·“Keep up the good work.”
  • ▶“See you at our next meeting.”
Encourages continued dialogue and shows interest in future meetings.
DeepSeek-R1"See you at our next meeting."
NarratorCiara put the papers back in her bag and left the meeting room.

■ CurriculumT06

BUDGET -6WEALTH 2STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorCiara had scheduled a short meeting at the Ministry of Education to discuss the current curriculum taught in schools.
NarratorThe ministry building was located in the government district right next to the Ministry of Health. It was tall, narrow and wedge-shaped, like a slightly opened book.
NarratorNear the front gate, there were many statues of world-renowned scientists and thinkers. The largest, of course, was of Artvin Einstavn, the greatest scientist of all time.
NarratorAbove the main entrance was the familiar inscription, 'Instreten sis er Anginicar av Gereigna, int olla Almergwolltene, int olla Mexis'. 'Education is the premise of progress, in all societies, in all families.'
NarratorInside the building, I was greeted by Ciara's assistants. They led me to a meeting room where she was waiting inside. She stood and gripped my hand.
Ciara Walda"Good afternoon, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:37:49 p21173 / c353
  • ▶“Good afternoon, Ciara.”
  • ·“Let's start.”
I need to acknowledge Ciara's efforts but set clear boundaries about current priorities.
DeepSeek-R1"Good afternoon, Ciara."
Ciara Walda"I will cut straight to the chase."
Ciara Walda"You've elected not to reform the Sordland education system -- I've already voiced my many objections to that, but I respect your decision. There is, however, one sticking point."
Ciara Walda"It involves evolution versus creationism."
Ciara Walda"As it currently stands, schools across Sordland can decide whether to include evolution, creationism or both in their curriculum."
Ciara Walda"Rural schools often decide in favor of creationism only, which puts students at a disadvantage when it comes to entering university. The effect is especially noticeable among young women."
Ciara Walda"My suggestion is to make the teaching of evolution mandatory, and preferably ban creationism altogether."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:38:41 p21206 / c465
  • ▶“I don't agree with teaching our children supernatural nonsense like creationism.”
  • ·“I don't see a problem with teaching our children both theories.”
  • ·“Evolution is the idea that ought to be banned. It's dangerous."
I need to balance progressive reforms with budget constraints and political stability.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't agree with teaching our children supernatural nonsense like creationism."
Ciara Walda"That's great to hear! It sounds like we are already aligned on the matter."
NarratorLucian Galade knocked and entered.
Lucian Galade"Excuse me. Some urgent business kept me away."
Ciara Walda"Welcome, Mr. Galade."
Lucian Galade"Mr. President."
Lucian Galade"Ms. Walda."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:39:33 p21230 / c535
  • ▶“Why were you late?”
  • ·“We were just discussing the curriculum. Ciara has made some excellent points.”
  • ·“Glad you came. I need to hear some opinions that make sense.”
Asking why Lucian Galade was late maintains neutrality and focuses on logistics rather than taking a side in the sensitive discussion.
DeepSeek-R1"Why were you late?"
Lucian Galade"The General Staff had some concerns about the Rumburg border. I already provided you with a report. Nothing else."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:40:16 p21248 / c307
  • ▶“We were just discussing the curriculum. Ciara has made some excellent points.”
  • ·“Glad you came. I need to hear some opinions that make sense.”
I want to acknowledge Ciara's points but need to address Lucian's concerns about national security.
DeepSeek-R1"We were just discussing the curriculum. Ciara has made some excellent points."
Lucian Galade"Very well. Let's hear them."
Ciara Walda"Continuing from my previous point..."
Ciara Walda"...It is my conviction that we should require the mandatory teaching of evolution in Sordland's public schools and cut creationism completely out."
Ciara Walda"If we want children from rural areas to have the same opportunities as their urban counterparts, this is the way."
Ciara Walda"Opening students' minds and reducing the role of religion in schools will also lead to more equality between young men and women."
Lucian Galade"Ms. Walda, don't bring your pet cause into this. Mr. President, there are other considerations you may not have thought about."
Lucian Galade"Although I myself believe in evolution, we must be careful not to alienate our rural conservative voter base."
Lucian Galade"We owe a lot to their support. Forcing their school districts to teach evolution could irreparably damage our standing with them."
Ciara Walda"Every modern nation is reducing or removing creationism in their curriculum. Yet we are supposed to allow schools to promote fundamental -- and, yes, sexist -- ideas from a thousand years back?"
Lucian Galade"So is that what we are doing now? Copying what other nations are doing instead of paving our own way?"
Ciara Walda"If it works, why not? Look at the analysis. It is clear that the superpowers are doing something right."
Ciara Walda"Should we reinvent the wheel, Mr. Galade? You speak as if we don't import *any* influences from other countries."
Lucian Galade"That's not what I said."
NarratorTheir voices started to get louder and louder. I held up my hands for silence.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:41:04 p21272 / c318
  • ▶“We can still allow creationism to be taught alongside evolution. Ciara, you could live with that.”
  • ·“Evolution is the way forward. Creationism has no place in the public school curriculum.”
  • ·“Schools should be able to teach creationism exclusively.”
Balancing education with sensitivity to cultural values maintains support while promoting progress.
DeepSeek-R1"We can still allow creationism to be taught alongside evolution. Ciara, you could live with that."
Ciara Walda"Yes, although I won't like it. I still believe schools are no place for religion."
Lucian Galade"I won't like it, either. You'll have to prepare to lose a lot of voters."
NarratorCiara looked me in the eye.
Ciara Walda"It is your decision. Don't forget, future generations of Sordish youth are counting on you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:41:59 p21361 / c448
  • ·“From now on, creationism shall be banned and only evolution will be taught in schools.”
  • ▶“I will make the teaching of evolution mandatory, but schools may promote creationism alongside it if they wish."
  • ·"We will stick to the current system. Evolution will not be mandatory, and schools that wish to teach creationism exclusively may do so."
A compromise is needed to balance educational progress with political pragmatism.
DeepSeek-R1"I will make the teaching of evolution mandatory, but schools may promote creationism alongside it if they wish."
Lucian Galade"Unfortunate, but it will have a better outcome than banning creationism altogether."
Ciara Walda"I disagree. Rejecting creationism would have been a signal that we were redirecting Sordland toward a scientifically and technologically advanced future."
NarratorThey turned and looked at each other. Lucian lifted his eyebrow and Ciara held his gaze for a couple of seconds.
NarratorThe meeting ended shortly after.

■ Victory DayT06

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorSerge drove us to the city of Jen, the stronghold of Sordish nationalists, for an event organized by the opposition parties to discuss current events. In reality, it was probably a publicity stunt, to show Sordland that the leaders of the Assembly could sit around the same table.
NarratorThe National Front Party, which governed the city, undoubtedly had the biggest hand in the organization of the meeting. It was not a coincidence that it fell on "Victory Day".
NarratorToday was a day of special symbolic importance for Sordland. Every year, on the 14th of March, proud patriots from all ages gathered to celebrate the end of the Sordish Civil War and Tarquin Soll's victory.
NarratorJen had survived one of the worst atrocities of the war, and its Victory Day celebrations were the most jubilant in all of Sordland.
NarratorOriginally, only Petr and I were scheduled to attend. But after learning about new developments with the Ishval Ersen case, Nia Morgna was invited as well. Representatives from both the PFJP and NFP had told us they wanted to talk about the case in more detail.
NarratorNia departed Holsord first. Petr and I left on our own a little later.
NarratorPetr was sitting next to me in the presidential car, skimming through the morning newspaper in silence. Suddenly, Serge rolled down the soundproof window.
Serge Wolkner"Mr. President. Mr. Vice President. We are about to arrive at the Jen Center of Culture. Ms. Morgna had already arrived and she will be waiting for you."
NarratorPetr raised his head to look out the window. He folded the newspaper and laid it beside him.
Petr Vectern"Wow. That was too comfortable of a drive to have been this fast."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:44:14 p22149 / c227
  • ·"See? I wasn't joking. Serge is the best."
  • ▶"Thank you, Serge."
  • ·"How are you today, Serge?"
Maintaining a polite tone with staff is crucial for respect and professionalism.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Serge."
Serge Wolkner"I hope the journey was comfortable, sir."
Petr Vectern"Sure was, Serge."
NarratorSerge took a left turn and drove through the tiny streets of Jen. The city looked surprisingly clean and well-maintained. There was no sign of the destruction the city suffered during one of the heaviest battles of the Civil War.
NarratorWe slowed down in front of a colorful plaza overlooking a massive concrete building with a giant sign that read: "Jen Center of Culture."
Petr Vectern"Looks like the NFP mayors have really transformed the city. Wasn't this an abandoned warehouse for the longest time?"
Serge Wolkner"I believe it used to function as a rebel militia base during -- and a little while after -- the Civil War."
Petr Vectern"Oh, right... The Black Battalion."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:45:06 p22239 / c290
  • ▶"Let's not sour the mood with memories of the Civil War."
  • ·"It's been a while since I've heard that name."
  • ·"The Black Battalion?"
To maintain a positive tone during Victory Day celebrations, it's better to avoid discussing sensitive historical groups like the Black Battalion.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's not sour the mood with memories of the Civil War."
Petr Vectern"Sorry. You're right."
NarratorSerge stopped the car and went outside to hold our doors.
Serge Wolkner"Here we are, sirs. I hope you have a great day."
NarratorWe got out of the car and walked towards the entrance of the building. Every spot around us was decorated with the flags of Sordland.
NarratorThe comically large amount of flags almost fully enveloped the buildings around the plaza in maroon, white and gold.
NarratorAt the entrance, Kesaro Kibener, the head of the National Front Party, was waiting for us in his iconic brown suit, watching the sky.
Petr Vectern"Look at this guy. So full of himself."
Petr Vectern"How did this clown convince the PFJP to have trilateral talks with them anyway?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:46:10 p22285 / c637
  • ·"Probably the same reason why we're here. It's Victory Day, remember?"
  • ▶"Honestly, it says more about Frens and the PFJP."
  • ·"He's a dangerously competent man, Petr. We should be cautious with him."
Frens Ricter's willingness to meet with Kesaro Kibener likely stems from the PFJP's strategic moves to gain political leverage.
DeepSeek-R1"Honestly, it says more about Frens and the PFJP."
Petr Vectern"I don't trust them."
NarratorSeeing us arrive, Kesaro approached us with a smile.
Kesaro Kibener"Thank you for being here on this glorious day, Mr. President and Mr. Vice President."
Kesaro Kibener"As the former mayor of Jen, it is an honor to welcome you to this fine city."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:47:01 p22210 / c308
  • ▶"Thank you, Mr. Kibener."
  • ·"It looks like you were a good mayor. Maybe you should think about running for local office again."
  • ·"It is good to be here."
  • ·Shake his hand in silence.
Maintaining a polite yet neutral interaction with Kesaro Kibener as part of the event's proceedings.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Mr. Kibener."
NarratorWe shook hands.
Petr Vectern"Thank you for organizing this, Mr. Kibener."
Kesaro Kibener"The representatives of the PFJP and Minister Morgna are already inside the private meeting room. Hopefully we'll be finished before the festivities start."
NarratorKesaro gestured at the doors behind him.
Kesaro Kibener"Shall we?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:48:01 p22113 / c483
  • ·"Please."
  • ·Nod.
  • ▶"Before we go in... is there any way we can discuss how you can help us with the constitutional changes?"
Asking for Kesaro's help could secure needed votes and show willingness to collaborate, crucial for passing constitutional changes.
DeepSeek-R1"Before we go in... is there any way we can discuss how you can help us with the constitutional changes?"
Kesaro Kibener"I'm afraid not."
Kesaro Kibener"Besides, this is neither the time nor place to have such a conversation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:49:04 p22061 / c582
  • ·"So be it."
  • ▶"I understand."
  • ·"You're making a mistake."
Choosing 'I understand' maintains decorum and respect, acknowledging Kesaro's stance while keeping dialogue open.
DeepSeek-R1"I understand."
Kesaro Kibener"Of course, I'm not saying nothing can change. The results of today's meeting can still, possibly, influence some of our members..."
Kesaro Kibener"But let's just go inside now. Please follow me."
NarratorWe entered the building and walked through a massive conference hall until we approached a door towards the back of the building.
Kesaro Kibener"We'll have the meeting here."
NarratorKesaro opened the doors for us and we entered the meeting room. PFJP leader Frens Ricter and his deputy Manoly Suheil was already seated at the left side of the table.
NarratorAs soon as we entered, Nia came to greet us. After showing our seats, Kesaro sat down next to his deputy, Remus Holstron.
Kesaro Kibener"This is such a meaningful day. Thank you for coming here on behalf of your parties."
Kesaro Kibener"But despite the festive atmosphere, we and the PFJP want to raise a few important concerns about the worrying state of affairs in Sordland."
NarratorKesaro stopped for a moment to check his notes.
Remus Holstron"Very worrying, indeed."
Remus Holstron"Mr. Ricter and his analysts have been working on an analysis of the Blud problem. As soon as they brought it up, we gave our support without reservations."
Nia Morgna"The *Blud problem*?"
Kesaro Kibener"Mr. Hol-"
Manoly Suheil"I'm sorry to interrupt, but the analysis is about racism and radicalization in Sordland. And our main topic is the case of 'Ersen v. Sordland'. Please don't try to spin it."
Kesaro Kibener"Of course, Ms. Suheil. Mr. Holstron just wanted to point out that this analysis was focused mainly on topics related to the Bludish people."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:50:08 p22135 / c706
  • ▶"Why are we discussing this on Victory Day?"
  • ·"I'm surprised that your parties have conducted a joint analysis."
  • ·"I refuse to discuss this topic without any Bludish representation."
Pointing out the irony of discussing Bludish issues on Victory Day highlights potential hypocrisy and shifts focus to their motives.
DeepSeek-R1"Why are we discussing this on Victory Day?"
Remus Holstron"It seemed fitting."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:51:04 p22133 / c503
  • ·"Hold on. If this meeting is going to turn into a racist summit, I will take my leave immediately."
  • ▶"I will hear you out. But know that I will not act on anything before talking to the representatives of the Bludish community."
  • ·"Alright. I'm listening."
Anton chooses to listen but won't act without consulting Bludish representatives, maintaining balance and fairness.
DeepSeek-R1"I will hear you out. But know that I will not act on anything before talking to the representatives of the Bludish community."
Kesaro Kibener"Let's get back on track, ladies and gentlemen."
NarratorHe gestured at Frens.
Kesaro Kibener"I want to give the word to Mr. Ricter to explain the issue."
Frens Ricter"Thank you."
Frens Ricter"Our analysis of the current tensions between the Bludish and Sordish population in Sordland displays a worrying picture."
Frens Ricter"As much as I hate to say it, the threat of the Bludish Freedom Front is getting real again. The situation with Rumburg and Wehlen is very concerning."
Frens Ricter"The increasingly discriminatory actions of the Governor of Bergia are resulting in the radicalization of many young Bludish people."
Frens Ricter"Distressingly, the number of radicals have almost doubled after the implication of our armed forces in the Operation Bear Trap."
Kesaro Kibener"I must also point out that the recruitment numbers of the BFF have significantly increased within Nurist communities."
NarratorPetr frantically looked through his papers.
Petr Vectern"That is correct. The reports show that 39 Nurist sanctuaries in Bergia are now classified as 'red threats' by the authorities. Many priests in the region are also suspected of having ties to the BFF."
Kesaro Kibener"The Religious Harmony Bill would have directly crippled this network they established within our sanctuaries. As the threat the BFF poses is a direct one, this issue requires direct actions that would bear direct results."
Kesaro Kibener"I'm disappointed by the lack of action taken by the USP administration."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:52:00 p22069 / c492
  • ▶"Your bill would have unnecessarily raised the already high tensions."
  • ·"I'm regretting my decision to veto that bill."
  • ·"Your bill would have made everything worse."
  • ·"And what do you suggest we do?"
Kesaro Kibener is criticizing the lack of action on a bill aimed at reducing religious influence in schools, which could have increased tensions. Anton counters by pointing out that tensions are already high and doesn't want to exacerbate them.
DeepSeek-R1"Your bill would have unnecessarily raised the already high tensions."
Manoly Suheil"You did well by vetoing it, Mr. President."
Kesaro Kibener"Our suggestion is to finally enforce the official language of our country as stated in the constitution."
Kesaro Kibener"We are working on the Unified Language Act. It is directly aimed at the breeding grounds of the Bludish Freedom Front."
Remus Holstron"The police have infiltrated 32 BFF training grounds in remote towns around Bergia that posed as middle schools. Despite all the evidence, similar schools keep popping up."
Remus Holstron"And so-called activists are constantly blocking Governor Bron's attempts to stop these schools, claiming he is attacking their *right to language*."
Nia Morgna"You can't take away the Bludish people's right to be educated in their own language. This is insane."
Kesaro Kibener"Please read the proposal carefully. It does not take away anyone's rights. It only gives the state a way to control the radicalization in these schools that teach Bludish nationalist propaganda."
Kesaro Kibener"And according to the Constitution, the language of Sordland is Sordish. If nothing is being done to enforce that, we are breaching the Constitution. This law simply enforces it."
Remus Holstron"With all due respect, Mr. Kibener, this bill is not strong enough. The Bludish language should be completely banned. You know people like Ms. Suheil or Ms. Morgna will never even vote for your watered-down bill."
Kesaro Kibener"Remus."
NarratorKesaro repositioned himself on his chair and continued.
Kesaro Kibener"True. It does not ban Bludish language or Bludish education. It will instead make Sordish mandatory to learn for everyone living in this country. This will only decrease the tensions in the long term."
Frens Ricter"That's a fair point. However, the wording of the bill is far too vague to work as intended. It gives the government too much power over educational institutes."
Kesaro Kibener"I disagree. The language is quite clear and is in line with the Constitution. But we can still improve it, of course. It is true that it gives more control, but it is a necessary compromise."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:53:04 p22318 / c594
  • ·"I'll sign the bill if it reaches me."
  • ▶"I'll have to read it in detail to decide if I can support it."
  • ·"I don't see how this helps with the situation."
  • ·"Even if the Assembly supports it, I will surely be using my veto on it."
Anton needs more information on how this bill aligns with his manifesto goals before deciding.
DeepSeek-R1"I'll have to read it in detail to decide if I can support it."
Manoly Suheil"The PFJP will not allow this bill to pass the Assembly."
Frens Ricter"Let's not make statements that the party hasn't agreed on yet."
NarratorManoly looked furious. She opened her mouth to respond, then read the room and closed it again.
Remus Holstron"As if the PFJP has the influence to change the outcome..."
Frens Ricter"If I may continue with our analysis..."
Frens Ricter"The situation is also connected to Rumburg, as everybody already knows. In the case of a future incursion, BFF forces in Sordland could become an existential threat."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:54:00 p22368 / c389
  • ·"With all due respect, I already received this information from Internal Affairs. Tell us something we don't know."
  • ▶"You sound like Ms. Graf."
  • ·"Maybe the NFP influences you more than you think, Mr. Ricter."
Frens Ricter's comments about Sollist influence suggest he suspects a conspiracy behind my election, potentially undermining my legitimacy. Comparing him to Lileas Graf deflects the accusation and associates him with cautious opposition without directly confronting his claim.
DeepSeek-R1"You sound like Ms. Graf."
NarratorNia smiled for a brief moment and nodded in approval.
Frens Ricter"Just let me finish, Mr. President."
NarratorHe adjusted his tie and continued.
Frens Ricter"Our analysis matches with what the Ministry of Interior shared with us. But despite this fact, we believe the ministry is hiding information from us, which is actively fuelling the tensions behind the scenes."
Frens Ricter"As much I now support a strict response against the BFF, it must also be coupled with the expansion of rights for the Bludish people. This is the only way to fight against radicalization."
Remus Holstron"That is BFF propaganda. What rights are we talking about here? What can a Sord in Sordland do that a Blud cannot?"
Remus Holstron"You are using soft language as a cover-up for your willingness to negotiate with terrorists. You are a BFF apologist, Mr. Ricter!"
NarratorRemus shouted the last sentence. Petr tapped on the table to get his attention.
Petr Vectern"Please don't raise your voice, Mr. Holstron."
NarratorThe mood in the room had turned sour.
Remus Holstron"Is the USP that desperate for Mr. Ricter's support now?"
NarratorManoly bellowed out a loud laugh.
Manoly Suheil"Mr. Vectern is the last person to sway the PFJP."
Petr Vectern"What is that supposed to mean?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:54:48 p22373 / c280
  • ·"Enough!"
  • ▶"Can we return to the topic?"
  • ·"This is a mess. I thought we were here for a proper meeting."
To maintain control and steer the discussion back to the main topic.
DeepSeek-R1"Can we return to the topic?"
NarratorKesaro and Petr nodded.
Kesaro Kibener"You heard the man."
Kesaro Kibener"Please finish your point, Mr. Ricter."
Frens Ricter"We require your support with a more pressing matter. This might have very significant consequences for our Bludish citizens and may serve as a dangerous trigger if we're not careful."
NarratorFrens gestured at Manoly. She carefully placed several pieces of paper in front of her.
Manoly Suheil"Mr. President. Mr. Vice President. And Ms. Minister. As you already know, there is a major Supreme Court case at the moment."
Manoly Suheil"Ishval Ersen is suing the Governor of Bergia, Phelix Bron, for infringing upon the liberties of Bludish people and demands that the constitution be revised."
Manoly Suheil"Because of the argument and the implications, Phelix Bron and, consequently, the very constitution of Sordland is under trial. The fact is, he needs to be punished and the Constitution must be reviewed."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:55:37 p22386 / c331

  • ·"We are already aware of this case."
  • ▶"Please explain."
Anton needs to gather more information before making a decision.
DeepSeek-R1"Please explain."
NarratorNia raised her hand and spoke.
Nia Morgna"I was actively involved in this case. Because of the implications with the constitution, there is no way the Supreme Court will agree with Ishval Ersen on all of the points."
Frens Ricter"Not if all three parties of the Assembly support Ersen together. We can put pressure on them. And we only need them to agree on a few of those points."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:56:33 p22412 / c490

CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:57:24 p22428 / c416

  • ·"What is the most likely outcome of this case?"

DeepSeek-R1"What are your plans and suggestions?"
Kesaro Kibener"In short, the plan is to release a unified statement to the Justices declaring our support for Ersen and explaining why this is so important."
Kesaro Kibener"The only good result would be if the Supreme Court reiterates its commitment to the pillar of 'unity' in Sollism."
Kesaro Kibener"Mr. President... Despite previously being against it, I now completely understand why Sordland still needs Sollism. We need unity more than ever."
Kesaro Kibener"And Article 6 is that unity."
Remus Holstron"We do not need Sollist unity."
NarratorKesaro stared Remus down and continued.
Kesaro Kibener"If the Court reiterates the definition of 'Sord' in Article 6 as an all-encompassing term, they would be acknowledging that Ersen and others like him are indeed protected by the 'Rights of the Sord Peoples' -- which they are."
Kesaro Kibener"As a result, the resettlement policy in Deyr would be reversed, opening the way for Governor Bron's removal and the de-escalation of tensions in Bergia."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:58:18 p22508 / c408
  • ·"Is that true, Ms. Morgna?"
  • ·"Doesn't that mean the existence of the Bludish people is literally not being acknowledged?"
  • ▶"That actually makes a lot of sense."
  • ·"But what about those who don't want to be called a Sord?"
  • ·"This doesn't fix the core of the problem."
  • ·"I understand. And what does the PFJP think?"
Agreeing with Kesaro shows pragmatic leadership and willingness to compromise for stability.
DeepSeek-R1"That actually makes a lot of sense."
Frens Ricter"It can work for the time being, but I believe we can push for a more comprehensive outcome."
Manoly Suheil"Exactly. Our current Constitution fails to recognize the Bludish people. It is time we abolish the inherent racism in it."
Kesaro Kibener"The citizens of Sordland are Sords. The constitution clearly defines that in Article 6. It encompasses everybody. This shouldn't be up for debate."
Kesaro Kibener"What racism are you talking about? Sollism is based on civic nationalism, not ethnic."
Kesaro Kibener"I do not understand why someone would go against such a broad and inclusive law. I don't see the commies criticizing their Contanan identity, when in fact that country is filled with different ethnicities."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R113:59:13 p22603 / c492
  • ·"I agree with Mr. Kibener. A United Sordland is what we need."
  • ·"I see your point. But I still don't understand why the NFP suddenly had a change of heart."
  • ▶"Because Contana has nothing to do with this and Article 6 is inherently racist."
  • ·"Why are you even comparing Sordland with United Contana? Are you alright, Mr. Kibener?"

DeepSeek-R1"Because Contana has nothing to do with this and Article 6 is inherently racist."
Kesaro Kibener"Does *the Malenyev* call himself Kyovian before Contanan? Why is it that Bluds cannot do the same? Why can't the Sord patriots wish for unity?"
Petr Vectern"You have a point. But I must point out that 'Contana' is the word for a geographic entity, unlike 'Sord'."
Kesaro Kibener"This is Sordland. You can subscribe to whatever idea you want, but if you are a citizen, you are a Sord. This is how nations work."
Remus Holstron"The reality is the Bluds do not respect nor recognize the laws and authority of Sordland. They'd rather separate themselves from Sords and protest the very laws that protect them..."
Remus Holstron"...so that they can cry about *injustice* and ask Sordland's enemies to come and *save* them. I personally do not agree with the 'Bluds are Sords' Sollist crap."
Nia Morgna"I can't believe the words being thrown around at this meeting."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:00:00 p22589 / c271

  • ·"Neither can I. I think it's time we wrap this up."
  • ·"Mr. Kibener has a point."

DeepSeek-R1"And what is the position of the PFJP?"
Frens Ricter"I personally somewhat agree with Mr. Kibener on this. However, our position as the PFJP is that Article 6 does indeed discriminate against non-Sords."
Frens Ricter"And having a positive outcome for this case alone doesn't suffice."
Frens Ricter"We agree to come together in support of Ishval Ersen, but we believe our statement should include the acknowledgement of the inherent racism within the Constitution."
Manoly Suheil"We must use this opportunity to show the flaws in the Constitution. If the Court agrees that there is in fact conflict between the Articles, or recognize the discrimi-"
Kesaro Kibener"And the Hawker Court is going to listen to that?"
Kesaro Kibener"Besides, we do not agree with your 'inherent racism' hogwash. You have to state that it is your party's opinion and does not reflect the NFP. We won't take part in that."
Petr Vectern"That would make the statement look weak and disorganized. If there's no consistent strategy, I don't think it would work."

  • ·"What is the most likely outcome of this case?"
  • ·"I've heard enough."

DeepSeek-R1"What's the significance of this case?"
Kesaro Kibener"I'll be straight with you, Mr. President. If the Court rules against Ersen, this will most likely turn into a BFF campaign for a Bludish revolution. We ask everybody to recognize this."
Kesaro Kibener"I and the NFP are ready to push for de-escalation and aid our nation during this dangerous time."
Nia Morgna"I'll be completely honest. I would never have expected that from you."
NarratorRemus was visibly annoyed but kept himself silent.
Kesaro Kibener"Because the case is now in the hands of the Supreme Court, this ruling will influence all future implementation of the law and the decision-making of the lower courts."
Kesaro Kibener"We have the Minister of Justice here with us too. I'm sure she would not disagree with what I just said."
NarratorNia did not comment but nodded.
Frens Ricter"I'm afraid Mr. Kibener is right about this. If the Court ignores this case now, this may turn into something... massive. Therefore we have to pressure the Court not to escalate the already high tensions."

■ Marcel CallsT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorI was in my office, looking through the agenda for the upcoming meeting at the Ministry of Economy.
NarratorThe meeting was going to be about the ownership of the Big Four, the four corporations at the heart of Sordland's ownership debate: Heart of Sordland, Bergia Steel, the Sordish State Corporation and Nedam Mining Group.
NarratorSome of these companies were controlled in part or entirely by the state. The others were mostly or completely privatized. I had the power to change that fact.
NarratorThe phone rang and I put the agenda down.
Pick up the phone.
DeepSeek-R1I picked up the phone.
Marcel Koronti"Greetings, Mr. President. It has been a while, hasn't it?"
Marcel Koronti"Let me try to remember. When was the last time we had a chat?"
Marcel Koronti"Oh, I remember. It was in Conriat when you refused my gracious offer."

  • ·“I've been starting to regret that decision.”
  • ·“Why did you call me?”

DeepSeek-R1"For a good reason."
Marcel Koronti"Well, we are both adults here. I am sure we can let past be past and focus on the future."
Marcel Koronti"Although this time, I am talking about the short-term future..."
Marcel Koronti"Your upcoming ownership meeting, for example."
Marcel Koronti"But before we move on to that..."
Marcel Koronti"I can't help but mention the tax breaks, Mr. President."
Marcel Koronti"They have been extraordinarily beneficial to our quarterly performance. HOS is now operating on a much larger revenue pool."
Marcel Koronti"You have successfully strengthened the backbone of the Sordish economy."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:12:36 p22913 / c480

  • ▶“Needless to say, I still expect you to reinvest any surplus back into the economy to create more jobs.”

Encourages corporate responsibility by linking their success to reinvestments in the economy.
DeepSeek-R1"Needless to say, I still expect you to reinvest any surplus back into the economy to create more jobs."
Marcel Koronti"Increasing our investments inside Sordland is a key topic in our board discussions."
NarratorHe paused for a few seconds.
Marcel Koronti"Out of goodwill, I will share a key piece of information."
Marcel Koronti"The Lotherberg Group has contingency plans if you decide to do anything rash that would disrupt their empires."
Marcel Koronti"You can't blame them, can you? Imagine someone trying to take your authority and personal wealth away without your consent."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:13:34 p22938 / c535

Marcel Koronti"That is good to hear."
Marcel Koronti"If everyone plays their cards right, it will be all fine."
Marcel Koronti"Now."
Marcel Koronti"Let's talk about those cards."
Marcel Koronti"Since the start of your campaign, you've adamantly promoted a planned economy."
Marcel Koronti"Going by that, I am worried that you will attempt to nationalize Sordland's largest private corporations. Nationalization as a concept goes against everything my father built and stood for."
Marcel Koronti"If you do want to go down this road, I must insist on you excluding HOS from any such plan. That is my most important request."
Marcel Koronti"Think of your corporate allies as a sea of ravenous sharks surrounding you. We could co-exist peacefully, or..."
Marcel Koronti"Ah, let's not even imagine that scenario."

Marcel Koronti"That is excellent to hear!"
Marcel Koronti"I knew we would be on the same page!"
Marcel Koronti"I am raising my glass to you right now, Mr. President."
Marcel Koronti"There is actually one more thing I would like to discuss."
Marcel Koronti"I will be blunt."
Marcel Koronti"Mr. Tusk is unfit to be the spokesperson for the Lotherberg Group. His archaic thinking has only preserved a status quo that benefits himself and precious few others."
Marcel Koronti"It is high time something changed."

■ Economic DirectionT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorLucian scheduled some time with me to discuss the potential political repercussions of the upcoming economy meeting.
NarratorHe arrived on time as usual and took his seat right across from me.
Lucian Galade"Good morning, Mr. President."
NarratorLucian had dark circles below his eyes. The plethora of political developments must've been weighing even on a workaholic like him.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:17:52 p22475 / c265
  • ·“You look a bit tired. Are you getting enough sleep?”
  • ▶“Let's discuss this upcoming meeting.”
