DeepSeek-R1"Let's discuss this upcoming meeting."
Lucian Galade"I booked some of your time to pre-plan for today's meeting at the Ministry of Economy."
Lucian Galade"But before going into the economic direction, I want to give you a short status update."
Lucian Galade"After your supportive statements of Ishval Ersen together with Mr. Ricter, the Supreme Court decided not to act at all, and delayed the case until further notice."
Lucian Galade"I'm afraid Chief Justice Hawker seems to be protecting Governor Bron from persecution. This will only increase tensions."
Lucian Galade"Antagonizing him before the vote for the constitution was perhaps not the best idea."
Lucian Galade"Moreover, the Bludish community in Bergia is getting increasingly restless over this topic. Nobody knows how long they'll keep delaying the case."
Lucian Galade"This, of course, allows Governor Bron to keep his seat and continue his resettlement policies in Bergia, among others."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:19:12 p22793 / c283
  • ·"This is very troubling. Do you have any idea how we can proceed?"
  • ▶"Ms. Morgna was right about how extreme Mr. Hawker's opinions were. I should have known this would be the outcome."

Anton Rayne agrees with Morgna's assessment of Hawker's extreme views, showing awareness of the problematic delay in the Ersen case.
DeepSeek-R1"Ms. Morgna was right about how extreme Mr. Hawker's opinions were. I should have known this would be the outcome."
Lucian Galade"There was nothing you could do, sir."
Lucian Galade"Our strategy should be to get public opinion behind us."
Lucian Galade"We should use every future opportunity we get to increase our standing with the Bludish community. They may have a considerable influence on the elections."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:20:11 p22840 / c345

  • ▶"Thank you for the input."

DeepSeek-R1"Thank you for the input."
Lucian Galade"Of course, sir."
Lucian Galade"There is one more thing."
Lucian Galade"Despite showing support after your statements about Ishval Ersen, the representatives of the Bludish community -- namely Mansoun Leke and Fetih Ejall -- have just released statements condemning our administration."
Lucian Galade"They criticized your policies against the Bludish community and called your pro-Ersen statement 'a poor attempt of tricking the Bludish people'."
Lucian Galade"They have enormous influence over our Bludish population. Just something to be aware of."
NarratorHe took a look at his watch.
Lucian Galade"For now, let's return back to the real reason we are here. Our economic direction."
Lucian Galade"We need to be cautious. Any grand plans about the economy also heavily influence our political standing."
Lucian Galade"We would be changing our relationship with the Old Guard, the Oligarchs and the opposition."
Lucian Galade"As you know, this meeting will give you the opportunity to alter the ownership of the Big Four, the corporations at the center of Sordland's ownership question."
Lucian Galade"We can start the process of nationalization or privatization, or we can just keep the status quo."
Lucian Galade"Again, I remind you that any change will tip an already-fragile balance."
Lucian Galade"I personally think we should stay out of this and keep things as they are. There is no need to make new political enemies at this stage in our term."
Lucian Galade"But ultimately, the decision is yours."
Lucian Galade"What are you leaning towards? How should we move forward?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:21:09 p23198 / c354

  • ▶“Our administration will avoid upsetting the economic structure and maintain the status quo.”
  • ·“We will privatize these companies.” [Privatization Path]
Maintaining the status quo avoids creating new political enemies and preserves economic stability during a fragile period.
DeepSeek-R1"Our administration will avoid upsetting the economic structure and maintain the status quo."
Lucian Galade"Excellent. This is the safest way to victory."
NarratorLucian took a note in his notebook.
Lucian Galade"Although I think the Oligarchs would've liked us to privatize everything, maintaining the status quo will not disturb our relationship with them."
Lucian Galade"This means more stability. Both economically and politically."
Lucian Galade"Speaking of stability..."
Lucian Galade"Some of my connections indicate that there seems to be a contest for leadership in the Lotherberg Group."
Lucian Galade"It looks like Marcel Koronti is making moves to become the key figure."
Lucian Galade"Koronti probably wants his father's seat as the head of the Group and Tusk doesn't want to let go of it."
Lucian Galade"It's a significant power struggle considering these are the heads of Sordland's two largest private corporations."
Lucian Galade"Another reason why I am supporting your decision to leave the situation as it is."
Lucian Galade"There is no reason for us to interfere, directly or indirectly, in their internal struggles."
Lucian Galade"Did Marcel or Tusk contact you?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:22:10 p23091 / c388

  • ▶“No, they haven't.”

DeepSeek-R1"No, they haven't."
NarratorLucian gave me a long, measured look.
Lucian Galade"Strange."
Lucian Galade"I was sure one of them would contact you."
Lucian Galade"At any rate, this internal conflict among the Oligarchs puts us in an interesting position."
Lucian Galade"Our relationship with Walter Tusk hasn't been good so far. Any move we make now has to be calculated and thought through."
NarratorLucian looked at his watch.
Lucian Galade"Very well then. I'll cancel the meeting since we are not taking any actions."
Lucian Galade"I must get back to work."
Lucian Galade"I am glad you have made this decision."
Lucian Galade"Thank you for your time, sir."
NarratorLucian got up from his chair and left the office.

■ Oligarchs Final MeetingT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorLivia intercepted me in front of my office and informed me that I had a call from Walter Tusk.
NarratorI entered and picked up the phone.

Walter Tusk"I will give all the information I have to the officials."
Walter Tusk"I want you to start an investigation into Heart of Sordland. That's all I ask."
Walter Tusk"That's not too much to ask, is it?"
Walter Tusk"You will bring a known criminal to justice."

■ Bill Language BillT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
Bill for decisionUnified Education Language Act — The Grand National Assembly advances the Unified Education Language Act (U.E.L.A.) to strengthen national unity and facilitate consistent, comprehensive learning across Sordland. Section I of the U.E.L.A. stipulates that all educational institutions in Sordland are required to conduct their teaching in the country's official language, except in the case of foreign language instruction. To offer courses in any language other than Sordish, institutions must obtain explicit approval from the Ministry of Education. Any universities, technical colleges, or other educational institutions found to be non-compliant will face a temporary suspension of operations until they apply for and receive the necessary approval. Section II of the U.E.L.A. establishes the enforcement procedures of the Act. Schools found to be in violation of these guidelines may face penalties, which may include reduction or withdrawal of funding. In severe cases or cases of persistent non-compliance, total shutdowns may be enforced. This enforcement mechanism reinforces a unified language policy, ensuring a cohesive educational experience across the nation.
BILL DeepSeek-R114:26:47 p22081 / c615
  • ·Sign: Unified Education Language Act
  • ▶Veto: Unified Education Language Act
Signing the Unified Education Language Act risks increasing ethnic tensions by marginalizing Bludish communities, potentially undermining national unity and stability.

■ Assembly VoteT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorSerge was driving me towards the Grand National Assembly for today's historic vote. It was a big day.
NarratorI wondered whether my attempt at changing the Constitution would end any differently than Alphonso's.
NarratorI looked out the window as the noise of the city diminished, and saw that we were already inside the Palace complex.
NarratorThe complex housed the buildings of all government branches in the centre of Holsord. It was one of the biggest developments in Sordland.
NarratorThe Maroon Palace stood on a small hilltop surrounded by trees. We passed by the palace and entered the forest that separated it from the Grand National Assembly.
NarratorWe drove on the small road that wound through the forest. It was a warm day, so I rolled down the window. I could hear birds singing from the trees.
Serge Wolkner"Are you okay back there, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:28:15 p22223 / c474
  • ▶"I'm good."
  • ·"Hey, Serge. I'm okay. How are you doing?"
  • ·"I'm stressed about today."
Anton Rayne is focused on maintaining stability and economic growth, prioritizing his re-election goals over symbolic gestures.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm good."
Serge Wolkner"That's good to hear."
NarratorSerge made a left turn out the forest and entered the vast garden area of the Assembly.
Serge Wolkner"Did you know that Mr. Tarquin Soll came to Holsord this morning?"
Serge Wolkner"I heard some politicians talking about it today. Apparently this is the first time he's come to the city in the last five years."
Serge Wolkner"I thought he left the mainland and lived on Duru Island, never to return to politics. But they were saying he might be here to exercise his Member of Honor rights for the first time..."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:29:07 p22362 / c306
  • ·"Wait, what? Who was talking about this?"
  • ▶"Slow down, Serge. Are you sure?"
  • ·"That's great. Maybe I'll meet him."
  • ·"That's troubling..."
Anton Rayne is known for his cautious and pragmatic approach, so he would likely want to confirm the accuracy of the information first.
DeepSeek-R1"Slow down, Serge. Are you sure?"
Serge Wolkner"Yes sir. I overheard several men talking about it around the gardens of the Assembly."
Serge Wolkner"It could just be rumors, of course."
Serge Wolkner"Do you think he's here because of today's vote?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:29:58 p22273 / c301
  • ·"Yes, Serge. Most likely."
  • ▶"I bet it was just some rumor. I don't think he'll appear out of nowhere."
  • ·"I hope not."
  • ·"He's probably here to make a scene."
Anton Rayne prefers to maintain a pragmatic approach by dismissing the rumor to avoid unnecessary distractions.
DeepSeek-R1"I bet it was just some rumor. I don't think he'll appear out of nowhere."
Serge Wolkner"You know better than I do, sir."
NarratorSerge drove inside the gates of the parkway and parked the car.
Serge Wolkner"We have arrived, sir."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:30:48 p22185 / c308
  • ·"Thank you Serge."
  • ·"We'll talk later. See you, Serge."

Anton needs to focus on arriving at the Grand National Assembly for the crucial vote and doesn't want distractions from rumors.
DeepSeek-R1"Good. I must go now."
Serge Wolkner"Of course, sir. Good luck with the vote!"
NarratorI walked up the white stone stairs of the Grand National Assembly. The entrance looked like a temple gate from the classical era.
NarratorThe door opened to reveal vast corridors of wood and white stone. I joined the crowd of people who were walking slowly towards the parliamentary hall.
NarratorSuddenly, I noticed Lucian emerge from the crowd of packed politicians in front of me. He looked relieved when he saw me.
Lucian Galade"Ah, sir. There you are. Have you seen Vice President Vectern? He's nowhere to be found."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:32:06 p22038 / c831
  • ▶"I've just arrived here, Lucian."
  • ·"I'm sure he's among the crowd somewhere."
  • ·"I don't think he'll miss a vote this important..."
Anton Rayne chooses to respond by acknowledging his arrival, as it is the most neutral and factual response in the given context.
DeepSeek-R1"I've just arrived here, Lucian."
Lucian Galade"I hope Mr. Vectern arrives soon too..."
Lucian Galade"At any rate, how about yourself? Are you ready to finally face the Assembly, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:33:11 p22057 / c626
  • ·"As ready as I've ever been, Lucian."
  • ·"Yes. I hope it works out."
  • ·"I'm still a bit stressed."

Anton Rayne would choose to acknowledge hearing a worrying rumor to maintain awareness of potential political developments.
DeepSeek-R1"Lucian. I've just heard something worrying from Serge..."
Lucian Galade"We'll address that later, sir. Come, we must go inside."
NarratorWe followed the crowd into the parliamentary hall. After we were inside, Lucian and I separated to take our assigned seats. I went up to the mezzanine overlooking the hall and sat down.
NarratorI waited as the MPs took their places inside the hall one by one.
NarratorAfter a while, I saw Gloria walk to her elevated seat at the center of the hall.
Gloria Tory"Ladies and gentlemen..."
Gloria Tory"Please take your seats. We will shortly begin with today's agenda of the USP's proposed changes to the Constitution of Sordland."
NarratorAfter a short while, everybody was in their seats.
Gloria Tory"According to the current Constitution, constitutional amendments require a two-thirds majority in order to reach Assembly approval."
Gloria Tory"If the vote succeeds, the proposal will be sent to the Supreme Court."
Gloria Tory"The proposal in question includes these points..."
NarratorShe started reading the proposal to the Assembly.
Gloria Tory"First section of the changes... Article 57 is modified with the following..."
NarratorShe continued reading the proposal, highlighting each section.
Gloria Tory"Section 2, paragraph 36..."
NarratorShe went on...
Gloria Tory"...may not exercise his right to..."
NarratorAnd on...
Gloria Tory"...the Justices of the Supreme Court..."
NarratorMost of the MPs seemed like they were already falling asleep.
Gloria Tory"...a simple majority is considered..."
NarratorThe seat was really hurting my back.
Gloria Tory"...Section 4, paragraph 44a...
NarratorIt felt like an eternity had passed. Finally, she finished reading the changes.
Gloria Tory"...I hereby invite all of you to vote."
NarratorShe struck her gavel down. The loud bang made some MPs jump up in shock as they woke from their deep sleep.
Gloria Tory"As I previously stated, it will require a two-thirds majority in order to pass..."
Gloria Tory"You may now cast your votes."
NarratorI felt the need to stand up and stretch. I looked down at the hall from the platform I was seated on.
NarratorSome Assembly members immediately walked to the ballot box to cast their votes. Most of them, however, began to congregate in groups around the hall, discussing the changes.

Mansoun Leke"I wish you good luck with the vote."
NarratorHe gave me a cold stare.
Mansoun Leke"Excuse me."
NarratorHe straightened his tie and walked away.

Gloria Tory"Only a few."

NarratorThey went ahead and cast their votes.
Albin Clavin"Let's win this, Mr. President!"

■ Assembly Vote Results MeetingT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorLucian, Petr and I met in my office and decided to move out onto the balcony to get some fresh air in the increasing summer heat.
NarratorThey both looked uncomfortable in their suits. Petr was trying not to make eye contact and kept letting out depressing sighs.
NarratorHe wiped the sweat off his forehead with a handkerchief.
Petr Vectern"I don't know what to say... I still can't believe we didn't even achieve a simple majority."
Petr Vectern"The party has finally spoken."

Lucian Galade"Not to mention Tarquin Soll himself."
Petr Vectern"Yeah... What the hell was that all about? Soll appearing out of nowhere..."

Lucian Galade"We'll be organizing a cabinet meeting to discuss our present and future state of affairs."

■ Decision Rumburg WhistleblowerT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
EventRumburg Whistleblower Arrives: A whistle-blower from the Rumburg Security Bureau has escaped to Sordland by crossing the border and turned himself to the authorities in Estord. Agent Chelston Hailstone has promised to reveal extremely sensitive information about the development of a weapons program. We can give refuge to him and risk antagonising Rumburg or send him back.
EVENT DeepSeek-R114:55:33 p20433 / c754
  • ▶Grant asylum
  • ·Send agent back

■ Personal Theatre VisitT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorI had been invited to an exclusive preview of Alfred Kcirbuk's new film, "The Morning Shall Come".
NarratorAlthough I wasn't in much of a moviegoing mood, it would have been unpresidential not to attend.
NarratorWith its massive budget and Kcirbuk's directorial cachet, this drama was set to be a milestone for Sordland's burgeoning film scene. Personally, though, I was just looking forward to spending some time with my family.
NarratorIt was a very hot and sunny day as Serge drove us to the old capital. The film was to be screened in the historic Cinema Anglei dei Erlory -- the very first cinema in Sordland.
NarratorSerge had maxed out the air conditioning to keep the Kadilla comfortable. But that wasn't the only reason I felt a chill in the air.
NarratorI noticed Franc fidgeting with his tie anxiously.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:56:23 p20790 / c297

  • ▶"What's wrong, Franc?"
  • ·Ignore him and talk to Deana.

DeepSeek-R1"What's wrong, Franc?"
Franc"Since when do you care what's happening in my life?"

Franc"Busy with failure?"

NarratorI turned to Deana.

Deana"What about you, Papa? I know you're sad, but failure helps us grow, right?"

DeepSeek-R1"I'm just fine, Deana. Everything's going to be wonderful from now on, right, Monica?"
Monica"Of course. Let's just focus on having a nice time at the movies."
NarratorMonica folded her arms. We drove on in silence.
NarratorSerge rolled down the soundproof glass between us.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, we are about to enter Erlory. We'll be at the cinema in a few minutes."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R114:59:16 p20861 / c241
  • ▶"Thank you, Serge."
  • ·"Finally."
  • ·"Good. Thanks for telling me."

DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Serge."
NarratorI took a brief look outside as Serge rolled the soundproof glass up again.
NarratorI could see the many towers of Erlory rising over what was left of the old city walls.
NarratorIn the distance, there were several planes flying towards the city.
NarratorAs they got closer to us, I saw that they were military planes that looked like the sharp-edged Arcasian designs. They were probably headed for our new joint military base.
NarratorSerge knocked on the glass and gestured forwards.
NarratorWe were at the main square of Erlory, and the historic 'Cinema Anglei'—the Cinema of Angels—was right in front of us.
Deana"Is this the place?"
Franc"Yeah."
NarratorIt was an old baroque-style corner building with walls that looked like marble from a distance. There were exquisite ornaments on each side of every window.
NarratorSerge pulled the car to the entrance of the building, which was lit by vivid neon stripes in many colors.
Monica"Come on. Let's get out."
NarratorSerge opened the doors, and we exited onto the red carpet.
NarratorWe were just about to enter the building when a journalist managed to block the path.
Reporter"Mr. President! Mr. President!"
Reporter"How are you handling the defeat of your constitutional reforms in the Assembly?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:00:08 p20849 / c447

Reporter"Thank you, Mr. President. And were you aware that Tarquin Soll would be present at the proceedings?"

NarratorBefore she could ask anything else, the guards ushered us inside the theater.
NarratorIn contrast with the cinema's baroque-style exterior, the inside was modern and sleek, with bare walls painted in black, red and white.
NarratorThe film's cast and crew were already in the foyer, with glasses of champagne in their hands.
NarratorWe approached the crowd and were welcomed by the producers and the event organizers.
NarratorA man walked up to us with quick steps.
Alfred Kcirbuk"Madame! Mr. President! It's so good to see you here."

Monica"Why don't we take our seats? It's about to start."
NarratorWe entered the screening room, the lights dimmed, and the film began.
NarratorIt was a period drama set in the 1870s, about a Sordish soldier who pursues a doomed romance with a widowed Bludish farmer during the conquest of Bergia.
NarratorAs the credits rolled, I made an early exit to use the men's room. I was washing my hands when I heard an unmistakeable voice behind me.
Tarquin Soll"So, what did you think?"
NarratorTarquin Soll leaned on his cane. He had the same frail appearance I'd noticed at the Assembly meeting. Yet up close, I could still sense the raw magnetism that had kept him in power for 20 years.

■ Dayof DescensionT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorMorning haze surrounded the city of Deyr as the sun dawned on the Day of Descension. The holiest day of the Nurity religion, it celebrated the first message received from God by Saint Dast and his thirty disciples.
NarratorAs per tradition, the celebration was held inside the Archsanctuary of Dastnurity, closed to the public eye.
NarratorThis year, however, for the first time ever, it was going to be televised.
NarratorThat was Lucian's idea. As he saw it, granting the people a rare glimpse of the Grand Ceremony, and showing them how seriously their President took this holy event, would ease any tensions that lingered in the region.
NarratorAnd so we arrived at the largest cathedral in Sordland. A huge crowd gathered around our convoy as our car pulled up to the entrance.
Lucian Galade"This will work out well. Trust me, sir."
Lucian Galade"Of course, that's only if the prayer is done completely correctly. Please don't forget the order."
Lucian Galade"First, you will need to grasp the holy sceptre with your left hand, then touch the altar with your right. Kneel before the Archpriest and wait for him to put the sword on your shoulder."
Lucian Galade"Then touch the sword with the hand you touched the altar with. Hand the sceptre over to the Archpriest and receive the sword before passing it on to a disciple."
Lucian Galade"Pretty straightforward, isn't it?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:10:19 p20512 / c472
  • ·“It's just a ceremony. Whatever.”
  • ·“Yeah, sure.”
  • ▶“I didn't understand a word you just said.”
Anton wants to maintain control and focus on political matters rather than engage deeply in symbolic religious discussions.
DeepSeek-R1"I didn't understand a word you just said."
Lucian Galade"Just do your best to remember it. We can't afford to lose our religious base over this."
Lucian Galade"You will have to go first. I will come inside later and we will meet up after your part is done. Good luck, sir."
Exit the car.
DeepSeek-R1I exited the car and I was greeted by a chorus of cheers and camera flashes. I waved to the people as I made my way to the grand gates.
NarratorThe Archsanctuary was a sight to see. With its towering visage, it cast a shadow over a large portion of the city. I had to crane my neck to see the narrow spires at the top. Just standing next to it made me feel awe and dread in equal measure.
NarratorAs I reached the gates, I spotted my welcoming committee. The Archpriest and his disciples were waiting for my arrival. At my approach, they all bowed their heads in respect.
NarratorThe Archpriest gave me an infectious smile as he straightened back up. Tall and handsome, in his mid-50s, he was one of the youngest and most popular Archpriests in history. Some of his more avid followers even likened him to Saint Dast.
Archpriest"Mr. President. Praise God! It is a blessing to see you on our most holy day again. Welcome, welcome."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:12:07 p20648 / c417
  • ·Bow your head before the Archpriest.
  • ·“Archpriest.”
  • ▶“Thank you. It is nice to be here in Deyr again.”
Expressing gratitude for being in Deyr reinforces respect for tradition and public relations.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you. It is nice to be here in Deyr again."
NarratorThe crowd was still cheering loudly. The Archpriest turned and raised both of his hands, and everyone immediately fell silent.
Archpriest"Oh, great people of Sordland! Once again we are gathered here to honor the Day of Descension!"
Archpriest"I am indescribably happy to be sharing this moment with you as well as every Blud and Sord, old and young, tuning in to their television to watch this holy commemoration!"
Archpriest"Along with the leader of our great nation, I say: Let the holy day begin!"
Archpriest"Stay far from evil, oh sons and daughters, for evil hides itself under many disguises. Disguises such as blasphemy, apostasy and even alcohol!"
Archpriest"Be good, my children! Praise God! Praise Sordland!"
NarratorHe smiled beatifically as the crowd burst into rapturous applause. After it subsided, he turned to me.
Archpriest"Shall we move inside for your confession?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:13:05 p19997 / c462
  • ·Pull the Archpriest aside for a photo.
  • ▶“Sure. Let's get this over with.”
  • ·“My confession?”
Agreeing to the Archpriest's request shows respect and unity during a significant religious event, potentially enhancing public perception.
DeepSeek-R1"Sure. Let's get this over with."
Archpriest"Most excellent."
NarratorHe led me through the main hall, which had a stratospheric ceiling and Nurist icons lining the walls. We walked up a creaking spiral staircase to the top floor and finally arrived in front of the confession chamber.
Archpriest"Right this way."
NarratorHe gestured towards a chair in the dark room. The room smelled of old mahogany and scented candles. The Archpriest proceeded to light each and every one of the candles. One for each virtue and vice.
Archpriest"I will be right with you."
NarratorHe left the room. A minute later, I heard the sound of a sliding window right next to me. He spoke with a very soft voice.
Archpriest"Anton Rayne. Speak. Speak and confess now before the one God and may He repent your sins on the most holy Day of Descension."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:13:51 p20098 / c347
  • ·“I confess that I have been a bad husband.”
  • ·“I confess that I am not sure what I am doing for Sordland is enough.”
  • ▶“I confess nothing. I haven't sinned.”
Maintaining control and avoiding unnecessary personal exposure preserves the image of authority needed for leadership.
DeepSeek-R1"I confess nothing. I haven't sinned."
Archpriest"Any man sins. We must all come to terms with it. This is your chance to repent."
Archpriest"Please repent, Mr. President and you shall be freed of the burden of your sins. If you beg for forgiveness from God."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:14:28 p20027 / c150
  • ▶“I do not beg, Archpriest.”
  • ·“Fine. I will confess.”
  • ·“Do not test my limits. I kindly ask of you.”
Maintaining dignity and control by declining to confess.
DeepSeek-R1"I do not beg, Archpriest."
Archpriest"Pride is the most dangerous vice of all. Perhaps you will come to this realization at one point in your life."
NarratorThe sliding window closed, and the Archpriest entered the confession chamber.
Archpriest"Let's move on to my quarters before the ceremony."
NarratorHe led me through the thousand-year-old corridors to his office. The smell of dust and old tomes was in the air, along with the scent of burning incense being prepared for the ceremony.
Archpriest"Well then, please have a seat."
NarratorThe wooden chair next to him looked like it might turn to dust at a single touch. I sat down gingerly. It was surprisingly sturdy.
Archpriest"Are you ready for the ceremony?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:15:30 p19971 / c576
  • ·“Absolutely.”
  • ▶“I am a bit nervous.”
  • ·“Yes. Let's get on with it.”
Expressing nervousness acknowledges the gravity of the situation and aligns with the spiritual context.
DeepSeek-R1"I am a bit nervous."
Archpriest"Happens to the best of us. It will be televised, after all."
NarratorHe leaned back in his chair comfortably.
Archpriest"I wanted to give you something. A gift to celebrate this special day."
NarratorHe pulled out a large rectangular box from his desk drawer. The box was lined with gold and expensive gemstones.
Archpriest"A beautiful sight, isn't it?"
NarratorHe opened the box and showed me what was inside.
NarratorIt was a dagger. The handle looked like it was made of pure gold and had a very large gem at the end. On the blade, the words were inscribed: "Vectern sis da!". Victory is close. On the other side, another set of words: "King Egmund".
Archpriest"This dagger belonged to King Egmund. It is a very important artifact of the Kingdom of Sordland era. It is now yours, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:16:22 p20108 / c415
  • ▶“Thank you. It is an honor.”
  • ·“I can not accept this.”
Accepting the dagger strengthens political ties and shows respect for tradition.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you. It is an honor."
Archpriest"Think of the past and think of who we are. Traditions are what brought us to this point."
Archpriest"Now then, it should be right about time."
NarratorA couple of knocks were heard on the door and a young boy came in. He bowed before the Archpriest, then me, before letting us know that the ceremony was about to begin.
NarratorWe made our way downstairs where the ceremony was already in progress. The choir sang angelically as the Archpriest took his position beside the altar. When they finished their hymn, an expectant silence fell over the room. It was time for my part.
NarratorI started my walk, bowed and approached the altar.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:17:08 p20066 / c351
  • ▶Grab the sceptre with your left hand, then touch the altar with your right.
  • ·Grab the sceptre with your right hand, then touch the altar with your left.
Following the correct order as advised by Lucian Galade will maintain religious harmony and avoid political backlash.
DeepSeek-R1I grabbed the sceptre with my left hand, then touched the altar with my right.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:17:51 p20053 / c279
  • ·Bow to the Archpriest.
  • ▶Kneel before the Archpriest.
  • ·Kiss the ring of the Archpriest.
Kneeling is a respectful gesture that appropriately acknowledges the Archpriest's position without overstepping presidential dignity.
DeepSeek-R1Sceptre in hand, I knelt before the Archpriest.
NarratorThe Archpriest lay the sword on my shoulder.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:18:31 p20014 / c301
  • ▶Touch the sword with your left hand.
  • ·Touch the sword with your right hand.
Anton Rayne should touch the sword with his left hand as instructed by Lucian Galade to avoid offending religious traditions and maintain public trust.
DeepSeek-R1I touched the sword with my left hand.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:19:21 p19962 / c465
  • ▶Hand the sceptre to the Archpriest.
  • ·Hand the sceptre to the disciples.
Handing the sceptre back to the Archpriest reinforces tradition and maintains respect for religious authority, crucial for balancing reform efforts with public sentiment.
DeepSeek-R1I handed the sceptre to the Archpriest and waited for him to give me the sword.
NarratorThe Archpriest handed me the sword.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:20:18 p19932 / c553
  • ·Pass the sword to a disciple.
  • ·Hand the sword back to the Archpriest.
  • ▶Place the sword on the altar.

DeepSeek-R1I placed the sword on the altar. The Archpriest picked it back up and handed it to one of the disciples.
NarratorI got up and proceeded to my designated spot next to Lucian. He leaned over and whispered in my ear.
Lucian Galade"Unfortunately that was not quite correct, sir."
NarratorMy eyes met the Archpriest's and he very slightly shook his head.
NarratorThe ceremony went on for another hour and a half. Finally, the noon bells chimed, marking the end of the cathedral service.
NarratorWe left the cathedral to greet the waiting crowd.
NarratorAs I walked outside, I heard scattered boos. A man in a Worker's Party of Bludia shirt shouted that I was only here for show, that I couldn't even bother to get the ceremony right. Others yelled in agreement.
Lucian Galade"We should go before things get heated."
NarratorWe entered our cars and drove back to our suites in Deyr.

■ Diplomacy Overview 2And Trade DealT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorThe doors to the White Room, like everything else in the Maroon Palace's main nerve center, were painted white. I took a deep breath before pushing them open.
NarratorTime for yet another meeting. At least this time, it was with people who liked me.
NarratorThe attendees rose up from their seats.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President."
NarratorIosef nodded. He and Lucian sat down, while Deivid remained standing.
Deivid Wisci"Now that you are here, let's talk about our diplomatic strategy."
NarratorDeivid cleared his throat.
Deivid Wisci"Mr. President, if I may, I would like to provide you with a short overview of where we are at right now."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:21:27 p19607 / c349
  • ·“Go ahead.”
  • ▶Nod and gesture for Deivid to begin.
Anton Rayne nods and gestures for Deivid Wisci to begin his presentation on diplomatic strategy.
DeepSeek-R1I nodded and gestured for Deivid to begin.
Deivid Wisci"We have successfully passed trade deals with both Wehlen and Agnolia, which means we have increased our presence in those regions."
Deivid Wisci"These deals were important pieces of leverage and significant first steps into the global arena."
Deivid Wisci"We must now look towards the future."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:22:23 p19705 / c504
  • ·“Go on.”
  • ·“What do you mean by looking towards the future?”
  • ▶“It's time to take the next steps.”
Encouraging action aligns with Rayne's need for strong leadership post-reform failure.
DeepSeek-R1"It's time to take the next steps."
Deivid Wisci"Precisely."
NarratorDeivid sat down, pulled out a few papers and started going through them as he kept talking.
Deivid Wisci"Wehlen and Agnolia were practice runs. We must begin improving our relationship with greater nations."
Deivid Wisci"We need to elevate our international standing, especially with the threat of Rumburg looming."
NarratorIosef cleared his throat.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"You know me. I don't trust the 'helping hands' other countries offer us. For a good reason."
Iosef Lancea"But I also know when those helping hands are necessary. And right now, we need all the help we can get."
Iosef Lancea"I've said this over and over, but I will repeat it until you get it through your skull: Rumburg will come. Sooner or later. "
Iosef Lancea"And with the current state of our military, if a Rumburg invasion happens, Sordland will cease to exist."
Iosef Lancea"We need allies."
Lucian Galade"We ought to head General Lancea's advice, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:23:11 p19825 / c382
  • ·“I trust your assessment, Iosef.”
  • ▶“Thank you for your input. I will consider it.”
  • ·“Nonsense. Our country can defend itself just fine without allies.”
Expressing gratitude and showing consideration for Iosef's advice maintains respect and acknowledges his concerns without committing immediately.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you for your input. I will consider it."
Deivid Wisci"A military perspective is important, but these deals are about more than just gaining allies."
Deivid Wisci"We also need to consider the benefits to our economy such deals will provide."
Lucian Galade"Especially when we are *still* struggling to end the recession."
NarratorDeivid took a seat and brought out two stacks of documents.
Deivid Wisci"At the start of our term, I had a comprehensive trade deal plan laid out."
Deivid Wisci"Following the visits to Wehlen and Agnolia, we were to pursue potential trade deals with two greater nations."
Deivid Wisci"Valgsland is one of them. Or at least *was*."
Deivid Wisci"Unfortunately, a trade deal with them no longer seems possible."
Deivid Wisci"In Valgsland's case, their intentions on Heljiland have been clear for many years. Our deal with Agnolia regarding the island did not sit well with them,"
Lucian Galade"That's an understatement. The Alliance of Nations has spent years debating the status of Heljiland."
Lucian Galade"Becoming one of the first world leaders to recognise the island as Agnolian was quite the statement."
Deivid Wisci"As a result, we won't be able to conduct a trade deal let alone proceed with any diplomatic missions at the moment."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:24:02 p19997 / c501
  • ·“We won't let Agnolia get bullied by Valgsland.”
  • ·“Unfortunate. So much needless dispute over such a small island...”
  • ▶“What is done is done. Let's look forward.”
Anton Rayne chooses to look ahead, maintaining a pragmatic stance.
DeepSeek-R1"What is done is done. Let's look forward."
Deivid Wisci"As you wish."
Deivid Wisci"As we stand right now, any kind of deal with Valgsland is simply out of the question."
Deivid Wisci"Moving on to Lespia."
Deivid Wisci"Our southern neighbour is one of the wealthiest countries in Eastern Merkopa."
Deivid Wisci"A trade deal with them would be very beneficial, not just for our economy, but also for our regional and international presence."
Deivid Wisci"Not to mention that they are in the Arcasian sphere."
Lucian Galade"Because we would be following through with our promise to lean westwards, this would also strengthen our voter base."
Deivid Wisci"Indeed. This deal could also be the first step in allying ourselves with Lespia or maybe even entering ATO."

Lucian Galade"Looking at my stats here, we now have a small tungsten surplus."
Deivid Wisci"Which makes an excellent resource for trade."

Deivid Wisci"I need to know your decision before we can proceed."
Deivid Wisci"What should we do?"

NarratorI arrived home from work, hung my jacket up, and put my briefcase in the hall.
NarratorI awaited my usual welcome from Deana before realizing she was out at one of her art classes. Franc was still at school, and Monica... it had been a long time since she'd come to the door to greet me.
NarratorFrom the living room, I heard a muffled sob.
NarratorI entered to find Monica sitting on the sofa, a letter in her hands and a look of despair on her face.

Monica"It's from the Department of Education. Franc's university exam results are in."

Monica"See for yourself."
NarratorI looked at the page. Below Franc's name and address, his score was written: 435 points, out of a total of 1000.
NarratorAt the bottom of the letter, the word "Failed" was stamped in red.
Monica"All those years of effort, all those private lessons... all down the drain."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:28:32 p19151 / c327

DeepSeek-R1"Does he know the results yet?"
Monica"No, but he should be back from school soon."
Monica"I just don't understand. Franc's a smart boy -- why would he sabotage himself like this?"
NarratorI heard a key turning in the front door. Franc stomped in and was about to go straight upstairs when he noticed us both in the living room.
Franc"Mom? Dad? What's going on?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:29:15 p19168 / c350

Waiting for Monica to start the conversation would allow her to handle the sensitive situation more sensitively.

Franc"I... I need a moment."

Franc"I know."

Petr Vectern"Please."
NarratorI sat down.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R115:42:21 p18277 / c410

  • ▶"What's on our agenda?"
Anton wants to take charge of the meeting by focusing on the agenda.
DeepSeek-R1"What's on our agenda?"
Petr Vectern"We'll be discussing our current status and planning the remainder of our term."
NarratorPetr walked to his seat but didn't sit down. He took a look at the ministers.
Petr Vectern"Now that Mr. President is here, we can begin the meeting. I know that some of you have had your concerns about our style of governance so far. There will be a time to speak up about these in the meeting today."
Petr Vectern"But I must also thank you all for your hard work so far. We are very lucky to have a team like this. All of you have been invaluable to this administration."
NarratorNia and Ciara grimaced at his remarks.
Petr Vectern"Having said that... Now we have reached a critical point of our term. We are nearing the end."
Petr Vectern"We have worked long and hard but we couldn't reform the Sordish Constitution..."
Petr Vectern"Our organization was flawed. Our party was not united. The very parliament we control has denied us the reforms."
Petr Vectern"Lucian and I have begun investigating who let us down at the vote when we needed them the most."
Petr Vectern"Gloria and the conservative wing of the party didn't back us up."
Petr Vectern"Kesaro Kibener and the National Front MP's didn't like the proposal and voted against it."
NarratorHe paused and looked around the room.
Petr Vectern"But the past is past. We must now look towards our future."
Petr Vectern"We are investigating and analyzing our faults. We will be taking measures accordingly."
NarratorHe glanced at both Lucian and me.
Petr Vectern"Our party still remains divided. In order to look at the future, we may need to make some changes."
Petr Vectern"On the other hand, the opposition is exploiting our failure to reform. The latest polls show a steep increase in the opposition's support."
Petr Vectern"So... Let's start the meeting with a brief look at some reports."
NarratorHe fumbled with the stack of papers in front of him.
Petr Vectern"With the latest developments, we have seen an enormous decrease in our popularity. The public opinion about our party is at an all-time low."
NarratorHe took out a piece of paper from the stack and inspected it.
Petr Vectern"Despite everything, we have managed to greatly slow down the recession."
Petr Vectern"If everything goes well, it is a matter of time that people will feel its results. Our economy is not looking too bad."
Petr Vectern"This is the time to give the best we can. For this administration and for Sordland."
NarratorPetr turned to me.
Petr Vectern"I'll give the word to the President now."
NarratorHe sat down.

Symon Holl"Mr. President. Dear colleagues."
Symon Holl"Mr. Vice President was pretty on point with his summary of the current economic status."
Symon Holl"The recession is slowly getting under control. We still couldn't reach a positive GDP growth. This year the projected value was recorded as -4 percent growth."
Symon Holl"However, this has not yet reflected well with our debt situation. Our total national debt has increased about 55 billion SR this year. If we maintain such a deficit over the next years we will face a massive debt crisis which could crash the economy."
Symon Holl"The assembly is working on introducing new tax bills which will generate more revenue for the government. These efforts will depend on the government deficit situation."
Symon Holl"On the other hand, unemployment reports indicate that we achieved a minimal increase in employment. It looks like we managed to cut it from 16 percent to 15 percent."
Symon Holl"Thousands were put back to work."

■ Address To The NationT07

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 5Stable
NarratorLucian and I walked into my office in the Maroon Palace to make my Address to the Nation speech.

TURN 08

■ Personal Franc LeavesT08

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 5Stable
NarratorIt was a cloudy, rainy day in Holsord.
NarratorThe gloomy weather echoed my own feelings. My constitutional changes had been defeated. I was back to square one.
NarratorI couldn't spend the day moping, however. Franc was finally about to leave home.
NarratorMy son piled the last of his bags next to the door. Serge and the assistant driver were waiting outside next to the car.
Monica"Did you pack everything?"
Monica"Don't forget to give me a call *immediately* after you arrive."
Franc"I will. I think I'm all packed."
NarratorMonica helped him put his backpack on while Serge started carrying the luggage to the car.
Monica"I made you a sack lunch! I'll get it from the kitchen."
NarratorShe dashed down the hall. Franc and I turned to face each other in the doorway.
Franc"So, I guess this is it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:04:30 p18528 / c236
  • ·"I guess it is."
  • ▶"I know studying abroad wasn't your first choice. But I hope you can live with it."
  • ·"Don't forget where you came from, son."
Express empathy and understanding to provide emotional support during Franc's challenging time.
DeepSeek-R1"I know studying abroad wasn't your first choice. But I hope you can live with it."
Franc"I will, don't worry."
NarratorFranc shifted his weight nervously.
Franc"You still never told me about your university years, but I know I've had it easy compared to you. I hope moving abroad gives me a chance to prove myself the same way you did."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:05:16 p18634 / c277
  • ·"For your sake, I hope that chance never comes."
  • ·"Just don't do anything stupid."
  • ·"Don't model your life on mine. You're your own man now, Franc."
  • ▶"You don't have to prove yourself to anyone. Especially not me."
Encouraging Franc to be independent and not feel pressured to prove himself.
DeepSeek-R1"You don't have to prove yourself to anyone. Especially not me."
NarratorFranc smiled.
Franc"All right, Dad."
NarratorAt that moment, Deana rushed to the door.
Deana"Franc! Franc!"
Deana"I'm gonna miss you."
Franc"I'll miss you too. Don't forget, you've got to give Mom and Dad an extra hard time for both of us now."
NarratorDeana nodded.
Deana"Okay."
NarratorFranc picked up his sister and gave her a big hug. Monica appeared in the doorway, holding a paper bag.
Monica"I cut the crusts off your sandwich, just the way you used to like."
Franc"I'm not a kid anymore, Mom."
Monica"Can't a mother pretend? Oh, Franc!"
NarratorShe embraced him with tears in her eyes. Franc looked as if he was about to cry as well.
NarratorI caught her glaring at me over his shoulder. I hoped someday she would understand why I'd decided to send him away.
NarratorSerge approached.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, Franc needs to catch the next ship to United Contana which leaves from Conriat Port in four hours."
Serge Wolkner"We will have to leave now to make it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:06:06 p18793 / c318
  • ·"Thank you, Serge."
  • ▶"Time to go, Franc."
It's time for Franc to leave for his studies abroad.
DeepSeek-R1"Time to go, Franc."
NarratorFranc turned around and took one last look at me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:06:48 p18803 / c284
  • ·Say nothing.
  • ▶"Good luck."
  • ·"See you soon."
  • ·"Make me proud."
Anton Rayne wants to encourage his son in a concise and supportive manner.
DeepSeek-R1"Good luck."
NarratorFranc nodded, before heading to the car.
NarratorSerge looked at his watch.
Serge Wolkner"Time to go."
NarratorFranc waved his hand as he entered the car.
NarratorThe engine started and they were on their way.
NarratorMonica stood next to me as they drove away. Her eyes were teary.
Monica"There he goes."
Monica"It already feels different."
NarratorShe put her head on my shoulder.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:07:34 p18689 / c304
  • ·"I know."
  • ·"He will be fine."
  • ·"We still have each other."
  • ▶Stroke her hair.
A physical gesture of comfort would best convey empathy in this emotional moment.
DeepSeek-R1I stroked her hair supportively.
NarratorShe turned to me, kissed me on my cheek and put her head on my shoulder again.
Monica"With the defeat of your reforms, I hope we will be alright."
NarratorWe watched for a few more minutes, even after the car was no longer in sight.
NarratorI sighed and closed the door.

■ Late Railway OpeningT08

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 5Stable
NarratorIt was finally happening. Tomorrow would be the grand opening ceremony of the L-1 High-speed rail.
NarratorThough the construction delay had taken its toll on our morale, this was still a proud moment for all of us.
NarratorSymon and Gus, who had heavily assisted in the project, had come to discuss the ceremony plans with Lucian and myself.
NarratorLucian stood up.
Lucian Galade"First of all..."
Lucian Galade"This day might have arrived later than expected, but it doesn't change the fact that we are here now."
Lucian Galade"This is still a big success. Congratulations, everyone."
Symon Holl"To many more projects like this."
Gus Manger"Well done, everyone."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:08:49 p18267 / c289
  • ▶“Good work.”
  • ·“We couldn't have done this without our excellent team.”
  • ·Nod.
Recognizing the team's effort with positive reinforcement aligns with successful project completion.
DeepSeek-R1"Good work."
Lucian Galade"Indeed."
Lucian Galade"Let's move on to our plans for the opening tomorrow."
Lucian Galade"It's been a long time since the last successful infrastructure project. From a public relations point of view, we can certainly capitalize on the occasion."
Lucian Galade"I have a plan on how to do that."
Lucian Galade"If the President accepts, driving the first high-speed train to Lachaven would be great for our image nationwide."
Lucian Galade"What are your thoughts?"
Symon Holl"I don't support anything beyond the opening ceremony. The railway's completion speaks for itself."
Gus Manger"I see your point. But involving the President further could be beneficial for the administration."
Lucian Galade"I think so too. Mr. President?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:10:17 p18379 / c562
  • ▶“I agree. Get me on that train."
  • ·“I'd prefer we just do the opening ceremony. No need for senseless spectacle.”
Agreeing to drive the first train will boost public morale and demonstrate progress despite political setbacks.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree. Get me on that train."
Lucian Galade"Very well."
Gus Manger"Sounds fantastic. The President arriving to Lachaven in the driver's seat would make the headlines for sure."
Lucian Galade"Additionally, a large crowd has been invited to the ceremony and will be watching the opening speech."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:11:14 p18383 / c449
  • ▶“Nothing like a cheerful crowd. Looking forward to it.”
  • ·“It will be a good opportunity for exposure.”
  • ·“Like the good old campaign days.”
  • ·“I guess we can't avoid that.”
Anton Rayne would choose to highlight the opportunity for a positive public image.
DeepSeek-R1"Nothing like a cheerful crowd. Looking forward to it."
Lucian Galade"I think all of us are."
Symon Holl"On another note. It goes without mentioning that this was a team effort."
Symon Holl"Beginning with the President who put his trust in me, I want to thank all who were involved in L-1."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:12:01 p18531 / c373
  • ·“Symon, you deserve the credit. After all, it was you who came up with the project.”
  • ▶“Thank you for your kind words. It truly means a good deal.”
  • ·“Thank you.”
Expressing gratitude in a meaningful way that acknowledges the effort and teamwork involved.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you for your kind words. It truly means a good deal."
Gus Manger"We had our disagreements, but the important thing was the outcome. The Sordish people have been waiting a long time for this."
Gus Manger"We will show our best face tomorrow. Meanwhile, I'll make sure that our message about improving the countryside gets across."
Symon Holl"Alright then. We have a lot of work to do. See you tomorrow morning."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R116:12:50 p18541 / c394
  • ▶“See you at the ceremony.”
  • ·“Goodbye for now.”
  • ·“Once again, good work everyone.”
The opening of the L-1 High-speed rail is a significant event, and showing commitment to attending reinforces unity and progress.
DeepSeek-R1"See you at the ceremony."
NarratorWe all dispersed in order to prepare for the big day.
NarratorThe next morning, a stage had been erected inside the central station.
NarratorIt was a sunny day in our usually cloudy capital city.
NarratorThe crowd looked quite large. I spotted many party members.
NarratorWe took our places onstage.
Signal for Lucian to begin the ceremony.
DeepSeek-R1I gave the green light to Lucian, and the opening address began.
Lucian Galade"Brothers and sisters of Sordland!"
Lucian Galade"I welcome you Holsordians today as a proud servant of this country."
NarratorLucian's voice echoed in the central station. Thousands of Sordland and USP flags began to wave as the loud cheers intensified.
Lucian Galade"Without further ado, I invite the President of Sordland to the stage."
Walk to the podium.
DeepSeek-R1I walked up to the podium to address the crowd. The press was taking dozens of pictures.

Symon Holl"Thanks, Mr. President."

Symon Holl"Once we cut that ribbon and the citizens of Holsord get on the train, I believe you'll agree we have succeeded."

Lucian Galade"Citizens of Holsord! There you have it!"
Lucian Galade"As promised, Anton Rayne, the 4th President of Sordland, has delivered!"

Cut the ribbon.

Symon Holl"Congratulations to all. Hopefully this success will lead to many more."

Lucian Galade"The journey to Lachaven should take about four hours. The rest of us will be on the train as well."

NarratorThe locomotive assistant flipped a few switches, and the train began pulling out of the central station. Lucian waved at the press photographers as I "drove".
NarratorThe new train was in pristine condition. Its electric engine provided the smoothest ride I'd ever had.
NarratorAfter an uneventful four hours on board, we were welcomed by a throng of citizens at Lachaven station.
NarratorThe railway officially opened to the public right after we left, and thousands began using it.
NarratorIt was a great victory for my administration.

■ Presidential PowersT08

BUDGET -4WEALTH 1STABILITY 5Stable
NarratorLucian and I sat in my office, waiting for Petr. I was at my desk and he was on the sofa, legs crossed.
Lucian Galade"Sir. I have some news for you."
Lucian Galade"Mr. Holl and I have been working on several possible plans to boost the economy, as is our focus."
Lucian Galade"He is currently compiling a very detailed report with suggested projects."
Lucian Galade"I'll make sure you have a chance to see it soon."
NarratorHe looked at his watch again.
Lucian Galade"Looks like Mr. Vice President is late yet another time."
NarratorHe lowered his voice.
Lucian Galade"I am slightly concerned about Petr."
Lucian Galade"He seems to have his mind occupied. He's been late to almost every meeting we've had recently."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:14:50 p17065 / c333
  • ▶"I noticed it too. I need to talk to him in private sometime."
  • ·"I know Petr well. There is nothing to be concerned about."
  • ·"I don't know what's wrong, but I also do not appreciate him being late all the time."
Anton Rayne prefers to handle concerns discreetly to maintain control and harmony within his administration.
DeepSeek-R1"I noticed it too. I need to talk to him in private sometime."
NarratorSuddenly there was a knock at the door.
NarratorIt was Petr. He walked inside with files in both hands.
Petr Vectern"Gentlemen!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:15:32 p18457 / c387
  • ▶"Welcome Petr. Have a seat."
  • ·"Finally. Let's begin the meeting."
  • ·"Why are you so late?"
A friendly welcome maintains professionalism and avoids immediate confrontation.
DeepSeek-R1"Welcome. Petr. Have a seat."
Petr Vectern"Thank you."
NarratorHe placed the files on my table, took out several documents and organized them in front of me.
Petr Vectern"Here is everything, just as you asked. Our plans for our next reforms."
Petr Vectern"You can use your presidential decrees to enact reforms now. We have enough political capital. I also brought a list of suggested reforms from our party."
Petr Vectern"What are your thoughts, Anton?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:16:18 p18463 / c509
  • ·"I have a few ideas myself."
  • ▶"Let's go over the documents."
Reviewing the documents first ensures a thorough understanding of the proposed reforms before contributing personal ideas.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's go over the documents."
NarratorI took a look at the documents.
One-time decree panel10 decree(s) available, 3 slot(s) remaining
DECREE DeepSeek-R100:17:04 p18912 / c595
  • ·Amnesty for All Soll-Era Political Prisoners — The political persecutions of the post civil war era left thousands of prisoners waiting for their life sentences in prisons like Antel Rock. A presidential pardon decree for all political prisoners would see arbitrarily persecuted activists and thought-leaders regain their freedom.
  • ·De-Sollinisation — The influence of President Soll is in every facet of society with his portraits, statues and name dotting every possible spot. The administration can begin a de-Sollinisation process with a decree to remove the cult personality that has been established around the second President.
  • ▶Enact Value Added Tax — A new decree is set to enact a 20 percent Value Added Tax. This tax addition targeting all sectors is a strategy to generate fresh tax revenues amidst increasing financial pressures. Businesses will collect and remit the tax to the Ministry of Economy, marking a significant readjustment.
  • ·Gender Equality In Education — Sordland's education system has different classes and curriculum for genders. Through a new decree we can provide gender equality in the education system by reverting the heterogeneous studies that were imposed by a traditional education doctrine in the mid 30's.
  • ·Allow Private Prisons — All prisons in Sordland are run and funded by the Ministry of Interior. A decree could be passed to allow private companies to construct and operate prisons for profit while still adhering to the rules and guidelines of the Interior.
  • ·Rural Education Institutes — Sordland lacks technical and theoretical institutes for rural regions causing people to migrate to cities for opportunities. A new directive would see the construction of hundreds of education institutes that would teach the rural population in technical and theoretical skills.
  • ·Fair Trade and Competition Commission — Trade and commerce in Sordland has few regulatory measures resulting in unfair competition at times. A new Fair Trade Commission can be established via decree to push for fair market competition by protecting local industries through new regulations.
  • ·Free Prescription Medicine — Prescription medicine in Sordland is partially paid by the Ministry of Health according to the importance of the medication. A new decree would allow all citizens to get their medication completely for free.
  • ·Lower Retirement Age — The retirement age of Sordland had been increased to 65 in the mid 1940's due to economic stagnation. The older voting blocs are requesting a decrease. Our administration can pass a decree that would lower the retirement age to 60.
  • ·Supreme Council of Sordish Radio and Television — Freedom of speech is protected in Sordland but with the rise of new media private organisations gained a massive influence on society. With a new decree we could create a government agency; the SRSC that could protect the independence of the media or control it.
  • ·Done — enact no more (used 0/3 slots)
Enacting a Value Added Tax will provide much-needed revenue for economic recovery. Implementing rural education institutes will address regional disparities and support development in underserved areas. Lowering the retirement age can placate older voters while potentially boosting workforce participation among younger demographics.
One-time decree panel9 decree(s) available, 2 slot(s) remaining

  • ·De-Sollinisation — The influence of President Soll is in every facet of society with his portraits, statues and name dotting every possible spot.   • ·Gender Equality In Education — Sordland's education system has different classes and curriculum for genders. Through a new decree we can provide gender equality in the education system by reverting the heterogeneous studies that were imposed by a traditional education doctrine in the mid 30's.
  • ·Allow Private Prisons — All prisons in Sordland are run and funded by the Ministry of Interior. A decree could be passed to allow private companies to construct and operate prisons for profit while still adhering to the rules and guidelines of the Interior.

One-time decree panel8 decree(s) available, 1 slot(s) remaining
DECREE DeepSeek-R100:18:57 p18745 / c821
  • ·Amnesty for All Soll-Era Political Prisoners — The political persecutions of the post civil war era left thousands of prisoners waiting for their life sentences in prisons like Antel Rock. A presidential pardon decree for all political prisoners would see arbitrarily persecuted activists and thought-leaders regain their freedom.
  • ·De-Sollinisation — The influence of President Soll is in every facet of society with his portraits, statues and name dotting every possible spot. The administration can begin a de-Sollinisation process with a decree to remove the cult personality that has been established around the second President.
  • ·Gender Equality In Education — Sordland's education system has different classes and curriculum for genders. Through a new decree we can provide gender equality in the education system by reverting the heterogeneous studies that were imposed by a traditional education doctrine in the mid 30's.
  • ·Allow Private Prisons — All prisons in Sordland are run and funded by the Ministry of Interior. A decree could be passed to allow private companies to construct and operate prisons for profit while still adhering to the rules and guidelines of the Interior.
  • ▶Fair Trade and Competition Commission — Trade and commerce in Sordland has few regulatory measures resulting in unfair competition at times. A new Fair Trade Commission can be established via decree to push for fair market competition by protecting local industries through new regulations.
  • ·Free Prescription Medicine — Prescription medicine in Sordland is partially paid by the Ministry of Health according to the importance of the medication. A new decree would allow all citizens to get their medication completely for free.
  • ·Lower Retirement Age — The retirement age of Sordland had been increased to 65 in the mid 1940's due to economic stagnation. The older voting blocs are requesting a decrease. Our administration can pass a decree that would lower the retirement age to 60.
  • ·Supreme Council of Sordish Radio and Television — Freedom of speech is protected in Sordland but with the rise of new media private organisations gained a massive influence on society. With a new decree we could create a government agency; the SRSC that could protect the independence of the media or control it.
  • ·Done — enact no more (used 2/3 slots)
Implementing a Fair Trade and Competition Commission aligns with economic stability goals by protecting local industries and promoting fair competition, which supports the manifesto's focus on targeted growth and SME incentives. This choice avoids alienating key constituencies while addressing economic concerns through pragmatic reforms.
NarratorFollowing a brief discussion, I took notes about my decisions.
Lucian Galade"I agree that raising new tax revenues is important but we have to understand the public reaction to a new tax levy."
Lucian Galade"A loss in public opinion is guaranteed as the consumers and the economy adjusts to this change."
Petr Vectern"There is a high chance this might influence the average citizen more negatively."
Petr Vectern"We better put the newly generated money to good use."
Petr Vectern"And your next decision is opening rural education institutes? The last person I've heard mention that was Artor Wisci. This is a dead topic you're reviving."
Petr Vectern"Sollists always protested the idea of educating villagers and farmers the same as urban workers. They claim it will lead Malenyevist ideas to surge in the countryside."
Lucian Galade"They're not entirely wrong. It is difficult to determine how it will affect our votes though. Rural voters are a very important base for us, and maybe this will help us win them over."
Petr Vectern"Yeah..."
NarratorPetr placed his finger on my notes about the Fair Trade Commission.
Petr Vectern"That's a nice one. The commission will be essential in dealing with the monopolies."
Petr Vectern"It was time we established a body that regulates economic competition."
Petr Vectern"Well, we both respect your decisions, Anton."
Petr Vectern"But now that everything's settled, I should get to work. We'll catch up soon."
NarratorLucian looked at his watch.
Lucian Galade"We actually have six more minutes."
Lucian Galade"But very well. Let's call it here."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:19:40 p18504 / c308
  • ·"Thank you both."
  • ·"Thank you, Lucian."
  • ▶"Thank you, Petr."
Petr Vectern has just presented the reform documents, making it appropriate to thank him specifically.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Petr."

■ Lespia VisitT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 7Stable
NarratorThe Maroon Train slowly started to move.
NarratorThis train had been in service for many years. It had nine locomotives with special quarters and an office. As the name implied, it was painted bright maroon.
NarratorAmong camera flashes and cheers, we departed from the Holsord central train station. Our destination was Perla, the capital of Lespia. The historic route we were using was also called The Southern Express.
NarratorWe passed through the Lespian mountain range to the lowlands, with a short stop at the beautiful Sarea Nueva where Lespian security forces joined us.
NarratorLooking outside the window, the difference in wealth between Valgen and Sarea Nueva was extraordinary.
NarratorI heard a knock on the door to my office on the train and Symon entered.
Symon Holl"Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:22:02 p18689 / c290
  • ·“Symon. Have a seat.”
  • ▶“Yes? What is it?”
A direct response is efficient given the setting.
DeepSeek-R1"Yes? What is it?"
Symon Holl"I wanted to have a quick chat about Prime Minister Alvarez and give you a heads up."
Symon Holl"There have been some rumours about his...demeanour in public appearances."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:22:55 p18798 / c411
  • ·“Go on.”
  • ·“What do you mean? What did he do?”
  • ▶“I can handle him. Don't worry.”
Reassuring Symon that I can handle Alvarez shows confidence and keeps focus on our agenda.
DeepSeek-R1"I can handle him. Don't worry."
Symon Holl"He is known to have met with other state leaders while intoxicated on various occasions."
Symon Holl"Some even call him an alcoholic."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:23:45 p18778 / c348
  • ▶“Is this something that could affect this trade deal?”
  • ·“So the man can't handle his drink. Is that it?”
  • ·“It should be fine. I have dealt with worse than that before.”
Anton Rayne prioritizes the potential impact of Alvarez's behavior on the trade deal, focusing on outcomes and practical concerns.
DeepSeek-R1"Is this something that could affect this trade deal?"
Symon Holl"Honestly, I don't know."
NarratorHe handed me a newspaper. The headline was 'Circling Over Staalport'.
Symon Holl"This happened just yesterday. Apparently he was so drunk that he couldn't get off the plane on a diplomatic visit."
Symon Holl"After this incident, some say he is unfit to serve. But I don't believe that at all."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:24:34 p18789 / c455
  • ▶“Why is that?”
  • ·“I hope he doesn't show up drunk to our meeting too.”
  • ·“These are the Lespians people fear? Ridiculous.”
I need more context on why this could affect our negotiations before reacting.
DeepSeek-R1"Why is that?"
Symon Holl"Although his premiership is riddled with these kind of incidents, I also heard that he is a shrewd man."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:25:11 p18748 / c319
  • ·“Thanks for giving me a heads up.”
  • ▶“Trust me. I can handle him.”
  • ·“Now I am worried.”
I can handle Prime Minister Alvarez's issues while focusing on our trade negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"Trust me. I can handle him."
Symon Holl"I do. I am sure we will be fine, Mr. President."
Symon Holl"We are almost at Perla. I will return to my cabin now."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:25:49 p18692 / c297
  • ·“Very well.”
  • ▶“I'll see you when we arrive.”
Anton Rayne chooses 'I'll see you when we arrive.' to maintain professionalism and focus on the upcoming trade deal despite concerns about Alvarez.
DeepSeek-R1"I'll see you when we arrive."
NarratorA few minutes later, with a long whistle, our train came to a stop. I waved to the crowd from the window.
NarratorThe crowds of reporters that flocked over were pushed away by the Lespian security guards. We made our way to our cars and the convoy began its short trip to the Yellow House.
NarratorThe Yellow House was situated at the very centre of Perla in the middle of a circular park that was off limits to regular citizens. The park was full of roses on small patches.
NarratorThe convoy came to a stop and I made my way to the entrance. The assistants directed me to a room to wait for Prime Minister Alvarez.
Wait.
DeepSeek-R1I sat in the exquisite room and looked up at the golden chandeliers.
NarratorAfter ten minutes. there was still no sign of Prime Minister Alvarez. It was almost time for our meeting.
NarratorMy chair started to feel uncomfortable. I stood up and walked around to look at the various items in the room. A beautiful golden teapot piqued my interest.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:26:36 p18789 / c553

  • ·Leave it alone.
Inspecting the teapot could show interest in Lespia's culture, potentially enhancing rapport with Prime Minister Alvarez.
DeepSeek-R1I started inspecting the teapot.
NarratorWhile I was touching it, the handle broke off. I looked around. Thankfully no one was in the room.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:27:17 p18762 / c360
  • ▶Leave the broken handle next to the teapot.
  • ·Pocket the broken handle.
  • ·Hide it behind a nearby plant.
Leaving the broken handle next to the teapot is the most straightforward and least confrontational option, minimizing potential issues.
DeepSeek-R1I left the broken handle next to the teapot.
NarratorThe door opened and Patricio Alvarez entered.
NarratorAlthough I knew that he was one of the youngest state leaders in the world, I was still surprised by how boyish he looked. At his age, he was leading one of the strongest countries in Eastern Merkopa.
NarratorHe was holding his head like he had a headache and he was stumbling. Upon seeing me, he immediately composed himself. We shook hands.
NarratorHe gestured towards the two couches and we sat down face-to-face.
Patricio Alvarez"President Rayne. Welcome to Perla."
Patricio Alvarez"Good to have you here. I hear a lot about your successes."

A polite and professional response that sets a respectful tone for negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you. It's good to be here, Mr. Alvarez."
Patricio Alvarez"I hope you had a nice trip."
NarratorI smelled the strong odor of alcohol.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:28:35 p18675 / c282

Patricio Alvarez"What was it about again? Can you remind me?"

DeepSeek-R1"We want to provide Sordish tungsten in return for Lespian capital."
Patricio Alvarez"Ah. Yes. Right."
Patricio Alvarez"Sorry. I have just been very busy lately."
Patricio Alvarez"You know...being the Prime Minister makes you pretty busy."
Patricio Alvarez"So, I don't have any issues with this deal."
Patricio Alvarez"Except there is one problem."
NarratorHe smirked.
Patricio Alvarez"Wehlen."
Patricio Alvarez"Your decision to defend the border while Smolak committed war crimes diverted all those refugees here."
Patricio Alvarez"Now we have a full-blown refugee crisis in Lespia."
Patricio Alvarez"Which unfortunately means that the price has gone up, Mr. Rayne."

Patricio Alvarez"I want their oil."

Patricio Alvarez"Do you see where I am going with that?"

■ Personal Petr TalkT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 7Stable
NarratorIt was a dark, chilly evening when Petr called and asked if we could meet. He gave no details -- all he said was that it was important.
NarratorI drove alone through Holsord's suburbs until I reached the meeting point he named: 147 Wruhec Avenue, number 26. Our old campaign headquarters, untouched since the night of the election. This was where everything began.
NarratorI made my way up the dusty staircase of the office building. The light in the second-floor hallway was out, so I used a cigarette lighter to find my way to the wooden door marked 26. I pulled on the handle, but it wouldn't budge. The heavy door had always been a pain to open.
Petr Vectern"Coming."
NarratorPetr pushed on the door with all his weight. It made a screeching sound as it opened.
Petr Vectern"Without further ado, I bid you welcome to this grand nostalgia trip, hosted by yours truly, Petr Vectern!"
NarratorHe made an exaggerated bow. The dust from the opened door was still settling down.
Petr Vectern"Feeling sentimental yet?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:33:56 p18260 / c735
  • ▶“Indeed. I haven't been to this place in ages!”
  • ·“Not really. It's just an office.”
  • ·“I remember it as being much cleaner.”
Acknowledging the nostalgic value of the place reinforces unity and shows appreciation for the shared history with Petr.
DeepSeek-R1"Indeed. I haven't been to this place in ages!"
Petr Vectern"I miss it sometimes. Even that damn door."
Petr Vectern"Come over here. Remember this?"
NarratorHe walked over to his old desk and pointed to the gramophone that had annoyed our neighbors and kept us company for so many late nights. Petr filpped the switch, and it filled the room with swing music from the '20s.
Petr Vectern"I'll be damned. It still works."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:34:34 p18227 / c319
  • ▶“Are you going to tell me why we are here?”
  • ·“So, what are we celebrating?”
  • ·“I thought this office was sold off.”
Anton wants clarity on why they are at the old campaign headquarters.
DeepSeek-R1"Are you going to tell me why we are here?"
Petr Vectern"In a bit."
NarratorHe put his hand under the desk and brought a bottle out. He took a swig out of it and passed the bottle to me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:35:22 p18212 / c678
  • ·“Aren't there any glasses?”
  • ▶Drink.
  • ·“No, thank you.”
Anton should drink to strengthen his alliance with Petr and facilitate open dialogue about future strategies.
DeepSeek-R1I took a swig as well before passing the bottle back to Petr.
Petr Vectern"That's my boy."
NarratorPetr snapped his fingers to the music while going through the items in the room. It looked virtually unchanged since election night.
Petr Vectern"Come on, have a look around. See if it doesn't bring back memories."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:35:52 p18198 / c125

  • ▶Look through the storage boxes.

Looking through the storage boxes might reveal hidden information or items related to our past campaigns.
DeepSeek-R1I walked over to the tower of storage boxes and opened one of them.
NarratorIn one of the storage boxes, I found a heavy object, wrapped safely. I unwrapped it.
NarratorIt was an emerald-colored statue of a goat, the symbol of Sordland. I remembered that this was a gift from Brenda: one of the '53 election campaign assistants. It would look good on my desk in the palace.
Petr Vectern"Brenda, Brenda, Brenda. Always sucking up to you, and where did it get her? Seriously, where? I have no idea where she ended up."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:36:27 p18243 / c296

  • ▶Leave the statue.
Leaving the statue maintains focus on current priorities and avoids sentimental distractions.

CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:37:05 p18245 / c361

Petr Vectern"Yes, yes. All in good time."
NarratorHe gestured to the bottle he had in his hand.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:37:47 p18181 / c416

Accepting the drink strengthens camaraderie with Petr Vectern, crucial for navigating political challenges.

NarratorHe took a very big gulp out of the bottle and staggered to the side afterwards.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:38:28 p18151 / c458

DeepSeek-R1I stayed silent.
Petr Vectern"Let's go up to the roof. The view was always amazing from there."
NarratorWe went up the stairs to the roof. The view was mesmerizing. The buildings of Holsord twinkled below us, while the stars shone bright in the sky.
NarratorWe sat and leaned back against a wall, taking it all in.
