■ Affair MeetingT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 7Stable
NarratorIt was early in the morning after my talk with Petr. The sun was just beginning to peek over the horizon.
NarratorThe Maroon Palace was empty save for a few cleaning staff. I entered my office to find Lucian already waiting for me.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. Thank you for meeting with me at this hour."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:44:57 p18092 / c400
  • ·"I couldn't sleep anyway."
  • ▶"Cut to the chase."
Petr's personal issues could damage his effectiveness as VP and create a scandal, so addressing them directly is necessary to maintain stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Cut to the chase."
Lucian Galade"Very well. I'm pleased that you chose to call me after your conversation with Mr. Vectern last night -- although I was already aware of his illicit relationship with Miss Suno."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:45:37 p18144 / c415
  • ▶"I figured as much. You do know everything that goes on in the Palace, after all."
  • ·"How did you find out?"
Lucian's knowledge suggests he has significant influence and awareness within the palace, making it prudent to acknowledge his insight without revealing too much.
DeepSeek-R1"I figured as much. You do know everything that goes on in the Palace, after all."
NarratorLucian smiled.
Lucian Galade"On its own, given Mr. Vectern's established reputation as something of a playboy, the affair is unlikely to cause much damage. However, a more troubling possibility has been brought to light."
Lucian Galade"A recent conversation with Mr. Hailstone, our whistleblower from Rumburg, revealed the presence of a spy within the Maroon Palace."
Lucian Galade"I believe Miss Suno is our mole. She has been using her relationship with Mr. Vectern to gather information."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:46:14 p18214 / c385
  • ·Laugh incredulously.
  • ▶"Tell me more."
  • ·"You're being paranoid, Lucian."
  • ·"That's preposterous."
Anton prefers to consider evidence before making decisions, so asking for more details aligns with his pragmatic approach.
DeepSeek-R1"Tell me more."
Lucian Galade"I can do better than that -- I'll show you."
NarratorLucian reached into his coat pocket, pulled out an audio tape and fed it into the reel-to-reel machine on my desk.
Lucian Galade"This was recorded in the Palace at 18:09 on the second day of your trade visit to Lespia."
NarratorHe pressed play. I heard Petr and Livia's voices.
Livia Suno"You're sure everyone's gone for the day?"
Petr Vectern"Relax, Liv. You don't have to bend over backwards for my pal Anton tonight. Only me."
NarratorLivia giggled. I heard the sound of kissing, then of drinks being poured.
Livia Suno"Mmm. I missed you in Benfi."
Livia Suno"Did he tell you why he decided not to make a deal with Lespia or Valgsland? He's not interested in forming any alliances?"
Petr Vectern"Ah, something about staying as neutral as possible. Although even Iosef is saying we should find allies to deter Rumburg."
Petr Vectern"It seems like we've still got a shot with the superpowers, at least."
Petr Vectern"We've opened two new lines of communication with Ventrie City and Kyow. Just exploratory talks at this point, but they might lead to something deeper."
Petr Vectern"And speaking of something deeper, Miss Suno..."
NarratorLucian grimaced and switched the tape off.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:46:52 p18359 / c318
  • ·"That's it? That's your damning evidence?"
  • ▶"This is indeed cause for concern."
  • ·"Curiosity about foreign relations doesn't make one a spy."
  • ·"And then what happened?"
Recognizing the potential threat while maintaining a measured response.
DeepSeek-R1"This is indeed cause for concern."
Lucian Galade"I'm glad you agree."
Lucian Galade"And that's not all. I found her resume on file and there's a year-long gap between her finishing secretarial school and beginning her career."
Lucian Galade"Normally, this would have been looked into. But Miss Suno was hired before a background check could be completed. By one Petr Vectern."
NarratorJust then, there was a knock on the door. Livia opened it cautiously.
Livia Suno"Good morning, Mr. President, Mr. Galade. I didn't realize you'd be in so early. Can I get you some coffee?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:47:28 p18463 / c349
  • ·Wait for Lucian to say something.
  • ·"Coffee would be great, actually."
  • ▶"Close the door. Have a seat."
To handle the situation discreetly and assertively.
DeepSeek-R1"Close the door. Have a seat."
NarratorLivia sat. She looked puzzled.
Livia Suno"Is something the matter?"
Lucian Galade"Enough pretending. Your secret is out."
Livia Suno"I... I'm not sure what you're talking about."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:48:03 p18424 / c342

  • ·"You know perfectly well."

DeepSeek-R1"Livia. Lucian has evidence."
NarratorLivia buried her face in her hands.
Livia Suno"I'm so sorry, Mr. President. I was foolish, and Petr was so nice to me... But it's over now. Nobody ever needs to know--"
Lucian Galade"That's not what the President means, Miss Suno. We're referring to you being an intelligence agent for Rumburg."
NarratorLivia sat upright. She looked shocked.
Livia Suno"Pardon me, Mr. Galade, but are you feeling all right?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:48:43 p18480 / c432
  • ·"No, he isn't. You'll have to excuse him."
  • ·"I know. It sounds ridiculous to me as well."
  • ▶"Don't play dumb, Livia. If that is your real name."

DeepSeek-R1"Don't play dumb, Livia. If that is your real name."
Livia Suno"Mr. President, I understand if you don't want me to keep my job, but why smear me with outlandish accusations?"
Lucian Galade"Miss Suno, indulge me for a moment. What were you doing in the year 1951?"
NarratorLivia's face flushed.
Livia Suno"If you must know, I was working as a cigarette girl at Porto's. It's a Lachaven saloon. I was saving up so I could afford my own place in Holsord."
Livia Suno"Here's the proprietor's number if you need to double check."
NarratorShe scribbled a telephone number on a piece of paper. Lucian snatched it up.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:49:23 p18642 / c399

  • ·"But why were you asking Petr about my alliances?"
  • ·"But why were you so eager to help Deivid back in Benfi?"
I need to confront Livia directly with the evidence of her espionage before questioning her motives.
DeepSeek-R1"But why have you been asking me so many questions about what I do?"
Livia Suno"To tell the truth, Mr. Rayne... I was hoping to run for office myself someday. I want to make a real difference in the world, like Ms. Morgna or Ms. Walda."
Livia Suno"But I guess I messed that up by getting involved with Petr. Now they're all just going to say I slept my way to the top."
Lucian Galade"And your questioning Petr about Sordland's potential alliances?"
Livia Suno"I told you, I-- wait. How do you know about that?"
NarratorThere was a loud pounding on the door. Petr barged in without waiting for an answer. He was unshaven, and wore the same clothes as the night before.
Petr Vectern"I came as soon as I heard the three of you were in here. Anton, what the hell are you doing? I told you I had this under control."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:50:02 p18852 / c398

DeepSeek-R1"Sorry. Lucian already found out."

Petr Vectern"God, why involve her? This is my mess. She did nothing wrong."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:50:42 p18849 / c443

DeepSeek-R1"Well... there's a small chance she could be a spy."
Petr Vectern"I-- what?!"
Lucian Galade"I have reason to believe she's been relaying information to Rumburg. Information that you leaked to her in your carelessness."
NarratorLucian rewound his tape and played it again. As Petr listened, his eyes widened in disbelief.
Petr Vectern"Lucian, you rat bastard. You've been bugging my office!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:51:24 p18832 / c452

  • ·"I'm sure there's an explanation for this."

DeepSeek-R1"Lucian, is this true? How could you?"
NarratorBoth Petr and Livia were glaring at Lucian now. He looked unrepentant.
Lucian Galade"I had noticed the Vice President's behaviour becoming increasingly erratic and unprofessional over the past months. I wanted to get to the root of the cause."
Petr Vectern"So you wiretapped your own goddamn colleague? And for what? Have you been getting off on this, you pervert?"
Lucian Galade"Certainly not. I believe that along with the Rumburg whistleblower's admission that there is a spy in the Palace and certain gaps in Miss Suno's resume, this tape makes a compelling case to investigate the secretary for espionage."
Lucian Galade"Whether or not the investigation yields positive results, Mr. Vectern ought to be held accountable for his willingness to divulge such sensitive information. He ought to stand trial or, at the very least, resign."
NarratorPetr's jaw dropped. Livia stood up.
Livia Suno"If I may, Mr. President. I've been paying attention to what goes on in the Palace. Not because I'm some sort of agent, but out of genuine interest. Hard as that may be for you to believe, Mr. Galade."
NarratorShe scowled at Lucian, then turned back to me.
Livia Suno"And from what I've seen, this man has been attempting to undermine your vice president at every turn. Perhaps with the goal of replacing Petr himself."

Lucian Galade"My personal misgivings about Mr. Vectern aside, I would never do anything to harm this administration."

■ Politics Bills BriefingT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 7Stable
NarratorThe morning sunlight streamed through the windows as Lucian and I entered the White Room.
NarratorI sat down. Lucian remained standing.
Lucian Galade"I'm afraid I won't be able to join for your meetings today, sir."
Lucian Galade"The Livia Suno affair has left me with much to catch up on."
Lucian Galade"But I did want to give you a quick word of warning."
Lucian Galade"I have noticed that the USP faction in the Assembly has adopted a confrontational stance of late."
Lucian Galade"Their displeasure with you has led them to engage in obstructionist tactics, slowing down policy implementation and creating legislative gridlock."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R100:59:04 p18711 / c497
  • ·"Damn. I really have turned into the next Alphonso, haven't I?"
  • ·"So... what does that actually mean?"
  • ·"I'll show them what happens when you get in the way of Anton Rayne."
  • ▶"Blocking progress for the country because of their petty disagreements with me. I hope they're happy with themselves."

DeepSeek-R1"Blocking progress for the country because of their petty disagreements with me. I hope they're happy with themselves."
Lucian Galade"It is my hope that we're still able to follow through on some of our plans for this term. But it won't be easy."
NarratorHe dropped a thick folder in front of me.
Lucian Galade"I was hoping to go over the day's agenda with you before your cabinet members began arriving. But duty calls."
NarratorHe walked out. Almost immediately, as if she'd been waiting for him to leave, Livia entered with a carafe of coffee,
NarratorShe poured me a cup with a smile, then took a look down at the folder in front of me.
Livia Suno"It looks like you have quite the packed agenda for today, Mr. President."
Livia Suno"I'm sorry again about the disturbance my indiscretion caused. I hope it didn't create too much extra work for you."

NarratorShe walked out. I enjoyed a split second of solitude before the morning's first meeting.

Lileas Graf"I have important updates about the increasing tensions on the streets."
Lileas Graf"We are getting reports of more and more demonstrations against the government, with some having the potential to become violent."
Lileas Graf"Karl and I are watching the situation closely."
NarratorShe placed a stack of papers in front of me.
Lileas Graf"The security committee of the Assembly is working on a draft law that would help strengthen our internal security in times of crisis. Such as now."
Lileas Graf"I've been following their work and I truly believe they've done an exemplary job with this. Mr. Holstron -- who has been working on this for quite some time -- has made some great arguments for the bill."

Lileas Graf"Fair enough..."
Lileas Graf"But at least allow me to give you a brief description of it."
Lileas Graf"Firstly, it aims to grant the necessary authority to the police forces on the ground to enable quick and decisive responses in situations where public security is at risk."
Lileas Graf"It will also remove several legal limits that were prescribed for the forces -- allowing them to function much more efficiently."

NarratorLileas sighed.
Lileas Graf"In any case..."
Lileas Graf"That's all from me about the security bill."

Lileas Graf"We'll keep it brief. I think that will be all for our internal affairs update."
NarratorThey gathered their documents.
Nia Morgna"See you later, Mr. President."

NarratorSymon came in shortly afterwards.
Symon Holl"Mr. President. Where's the Vice President today?"

DeepSeek-R1"I don't know. Have you seen him?"

NarratorSymon's expression remained unchanged as he sat down to face me.
Symon Holl"I just wanted to provide you with a few updates on the economy."
Symon Holl"You may have noticed that the government budget is in need of a boost."
Symon Holl"My ministry is therefore working on a bill that will increase the tax on tobacco products from 10 to 30 percent."
Symon Holl"A similar bill is being drafted to introduce a 20 percent tax on alcohol."
Symon Holl"In addition to bringing in needed revenue, the new laws would curb excessive drinking and smoking in Sordland."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:05:22 p18986 / c426
  • ·"Very good. Those are nasty vices anyway."
  • ▶"We can't tax people's booze and cigarettes. There'd be riots in the streets."

DeepSeek-R1"We can't tax people's booze and cigarettes. There'd be riots in the streets."
Symon Holl"Your public opinion would suffer. There's no getting around that."
Symon Holl"Ordinarily, I'd advise against this kind of thing. But with our budget dipping into the red, any measure that would reverse our deficit is worth considering."
Symon Holl"Especially since the Sordish tourism industry has been stagnating."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:06:09 p18930 / c672
  • ·"That's disappointing to hear."
  • ▶"Who wouldn't want to visit Sordland? It's the best country in the world!"

DeepSeek-R1"Who wouldn't want to visit Sordland? It's the best country in the world!"
Symon Holl"To live in, perhaps. But have you seen Lespia's beaches?"
NarratorThere was a knock on the door. Gus Manger strolled in and grabbed a seat.
Gus Manger"Mr. President. Symon."
Symon Holl"Thanks for joining us, Gus. I believe you wanted to update the President on the latest developments in the Gruni region?"
NarratorGus nodded vigorously.
Gus Manger"Yes, you both know how revitalizing the region has always been my pet project of sorts."
Gus Manger"Well. I'm saddened to say that despite my ministry's best efforts, we're going to have to close the books on this project."
Symon Holl"'Close the books', Mr. Manger? What does that mean?"
NarratorGus sighed.
Gus Manger"It means that without a massive injection of funding, the economic growth I had hoped to see in the region is simply not going to happen during this term."
Gus Manger"What could have been a beacon of success for Sordland is turning into a cesspool of crime, unemployment and homelessness."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:06:53 p19185 / c380
  • ·"Perhaps this wouldn't be happening if you'd focused as much attention on Gruni as you have on your side ventures."
  • ▶"You can't just give up. I know how much Gruni means to you."
  • ·"I knew it from the beginning. It made no sense to try to turn Gruni into something it wasn't."
Choosing to acknowledge Gruni's importance shows empathy and maintains morale despite setbacks.
DeepSeek-R1"You can't just give up. I know how much Gruni means to you."
Gus Manger"Not just to me, Mr. President. It's the breadbasket of this nation."
Gus Manger"I still believe it's brimming with untapped potential. It saddens me to no end to see that go to waste."
Symon Holl"Try to calm down, Gus. The President makes decisions on behalf of the entire country, not only your home region."
NarratorThe door to the White Room opened slowly, and Deivid made his way in.
Deivid Wisci"Oh, hello, gentlemen. I'm early again, aren't I?"
Symon Holl"Not to worry, Mr. Wisci. We've just about wrapped things up."
NarratorSymon and Gus rose from their seats and left the room.
NarratorAfter the door closed, Deivid regarded me for a second. His blue eyes seemed to see right through me.
Deivid Wisci"How are you, Anton? You look as if you haven't slept in ages."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:07:41 p19259 / c633
  • ·"It's been a rough few days, Deivid."
  • ·"Sleeplessness comes part and parcel with this job. You know that."
  • ·"You're better off not knowing."

DeepSeek-R1"Can I ask you a personal question? Were you ever unfaithful to Cathrin?"
NarratorHe blinked, slightly taken aback.
Deivid Wisci"I've met many a beautiful woman in the course of my travels. But no, even in my younger days, I was never tempted to stray."
Deivid Wisci"My deep love for my wife -- as well as the need to protect my reputation as a statesman -- prevented me from doing so."
Deivid Wisci"Why? Have you broken your vows with Monica, Anton?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:08:21 p19255 / c421

  • ·"No. Although God knows I've thought about it."

  • ·"Forget I asked. Let's talk diplomacy."
Anton Rayne reaffirms his commitment to Monica to maintain his image as a family man and avoid distractions from political tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"Never. I love my wife more than anything."
NarratorHe looked relieved.
Deivid Wisci"Some men go their entire lives without finding what you and I have. Best not to mess it up."
Deivid Wisci"But let's have this conversation over tea, not during a cabinet meeting."
Deivid Wisci"I'll be very brief. Perhaps we can even carve out some time for you to rest your eyes."
Deivid Wisci"Regretfully, tensions are continuing to mount between Sordland and Rumburg."
Deivid Wisci"As you might imagine, our decision to grant asylum to their whistleblower Mr. Hailstone did not sit well with the Kingdom."
Deivid Wisci"We must exercise caution. The slightest provocation on either side could cost us our chance at reconciliation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:08:59 p19238 / c362
  • ·"Bringing Rumburg to justice for what it's done is far more important than reconciliation."
  • ▶"You're right as usual. The last thing we want is a war on our hands."
  • ·"I don't even know if peace is possible after so many years of animosity."
Prioritizing peace over justice aligns with avoiding international conflicts and maintaining domestic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"You're right as usual. The last thing we want is a war on our hands."
Deivid Wisci"A compromise in the name of the greater good may spare countless lives."
Deivid Wisci"I'll leave you to it, Anton. I know you've got a lot on your plate."

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 7Stable
EventUSP Apparatus Obstructs: The United Sordland Party has recently adopted a confrontational stance, engaging in obstructionist tactics that are raising political tensions. This disruption has resulted in legislative gridlock, creating a challenging environment for the execution of administrative plans.

■ Womens Rights MeetingT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 8Stable
NarratorI was sitting in my office. I looked at the clock.
Narrator2:26 PM. It was almost time for my appointment with Ciara on women's rights.
NarratorThere was a knock at the door. Ciara entered the room with folders in her hand. The door did not close behind her.
NarratorMonica entered the room and stood next to Ciara. I was confused. This was the opposite of what we had agreed after the Benfi Festival.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:10:27 p18751 / c245
  • ·"What are you doing here?"
  • ·"Ciara, what is she doing here?"
  • ▶"No. No! Monica, go home!"

DeepSeek-R1"No. No! Monica, go home!"
NarratorMonica turned to Ciara.
Monica"Could you give us a minute?"
Ciara Walda"Of course."
NarratorRight before she made her way out, her eyes met with Monica's and the two women exchanged nods. The door closed, leaving Monica and me alone in the office.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:11:13 p18691 / c305
  • ·"How dare you show up here after what we agreed!"
  • ▶“Explain yourself."
  • ·“You could've at least told me.”

DeepSeek-R1"Explain yourself."
Monica"You know how much I want to help women in Sordland. I had an opportunity, and I couldn't let it pass."
Monica"So when Ciara asked me to join her today, I had to accept."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:11:53 p18785 / c357
  • ·“Ciara needs to learn that she can't interfere with my family.”
  • ▶“To be honest, I would've done the same if that is what it took to get my word across.”
  • ·“This doesn't change the fact that you didn't trust me.”
Acknowledging Monica's proactive approach aligns with shared goals and supports her role in advancing women's rights.
DeepSeek-R1"To be honest, I would've done the same if that was what it took to get my word across."
Monica"I knew you would understand."
Monica"Please, Anton. Let me stay."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:12:30 p18799 / c317

  • ·“Do you trust me?”
  • ▶“My answer is still no. You will not go into politics.”
Maintaining the prior agreement to protect Monica from political risks.
DeepSeek-R1"My answer is still no. You will not go into politics."
Monica"But why? This is about Sordish women. This is bigger than just politics."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:13:16 p18831 / c565

  • ·“Don't be naive. Everything comes down to politics in the end.”
  • ·“It's simply too dangerous. I don't want you to get hurt.”
  • ·“Someone has to stay home and look after Deana.”
Anton prioritizes avoiding negative press ahead of the elections.
DeepSeek-R1"We are getting close to the elections. I can't handle any possible bad press."
Monica"I know Benfi may have crossed a line, but this is different."
NarratorI leaned forward.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:13:52 p18776 / c349
  • ▶“All right. If you are willing to take the risk, I will let you do it.”
  • ·“This is my final answer. You will not do it.”
Allowing Monica to take the risk could strengthen her political influence and support women's rights, potentially benefiting Anton's image.
DeepSeek-R1"All right. If you are willing to take the risk, I will let you do it."
Monica"I was born ready."
NarratorShe hugged me and kissed me on the cheek.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:14:28 p18775 / c360
  • ·“No canoodling at work, Mrs. First Lady.”
  • ▶“All right. Can you call Ciara back?”
All right. Can you call Ciara back?
DeepSeek-R1"All right. Can you call Ciara back?"
Monica"Of course. I'll go get her."
NarratorMonica left the office and returned with Ciara. Both women were smiling.
NarratorCiara sat down on a chair and laid down the folder she was holding.
Ciara Walda"We still have a long way to go before Sordish women are able to enjoy the same rights and privileges as men."
Monica"Or even as their counterparts in Arcasia and United Contana. We've been lagging behind on this for decades, Anton."
Ciara Walda"And this isn't only a moral issue. I have seen enough revolutions in this world to understand the main driver of any change."
Ciara Walda"Money."
NarratorShe flipped a folder open and started pulling out documents.
Ciara Walda"In United Contana, law dictates that women earn the same wages as men in their respective fields. My research shows that this led to a 15.5% increase in productivity. You could say this had a direct influence on their superpower status."
Ciara Walda"Arcasia passed laws ensuring maternity leave rights, child day care centers and tax deductions for working families. In turn, they experienced a 12.6% productivity boost and a flourishing economy."
Ciara Walda"Both countries also have significantly lower infant mortality rates, a statistic I believe is directly linked to the improved status of women."
Ciara Walda"Our administration has successfully managed to pull Sordland back from the brink of economic ruin."
NarratorShe leaned back in her chair.
Ciara Walda"So I am asking: Why stop there? Why not improve our economy and modernize our society at the same time?"
Ciara Walda"It's only logical."
NarratorMonica handed me one of the documents. It had a list of bullet points on it.
Monica"The two of us talked it over, and these are the measures we believe are most urgently needed."
NarratorI let my eyes wander down the list.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:15:19 p18939 / c558
  • ·"Criminalizing domestic violence."
  • ·"Support for working mothers."
  • ▶"Equal treatment in the workforce."
  • ·"Gender equality in public education."
Anton would choose 'Equal treatment in the workforce' as it directly addresses economic productivity and aligns with Ciara's evidence, making it a pragmatic choice for his re-election strategy.
DeepSeek-R1"Equal treatment in the workforce."
Monica"That's an easy one."
Ciara Walda"Right now, Sordland has one of the continent's largest pay gaps between men and women. We need legislation ensuring that a woman doing the same job as a man is paid the same wage."
Monica"We would also introduce quotas to get more women into positions of power -- in the Assembly, for instance, or on the boards of corporations."
Ciara Walda"Eventually. Narrowing the pay gap is a far more pressing issue, as it would bring financial independence to countless lower-income women."
Monica"Ciara, we never talked about this. But I always thought that with more women in power, higher status for all women would naturally follow."
Ciara Walda"Women are starving because they don't make a living wage. That's a damn sight more important than your friend Janice being passed over for a promotion."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:16:12 p19037 / c830
  • ▶"I don't see a problem with quotas, but tackling the pay gap is much more urgent."
  • ·"Quotas seem far easier to implement than equal pay laws. We should start there."
  • ·"Quotas are a terrible idea. People should be hired on merit, not simply because of their sex."
Supporting both equal pay and quotas, but prioritizing the urgent issue of closing the pay gap aligns with economic stability goals.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't see a problem with quotas, but tackling the pay gap is much more urgent."
NarratorMonica gritted her teeth.
Ciara Walda"Thank you, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:16:49 p18978 / c410
  • ·"Criminalizing domestic violence."
  • ▶"Support for working mothers."
  • ·"Gender equality in public education."
Support for working mothers aligns with economic recovery by increasing workforce participation.
DeepSeek-R1"Support for working mothers."
NarratorCiara nodded.
Ciara Walda"It simply isn't fair that women in the workforce are punished for having children."
Monica"Remember what I told you about Janice? My friend who basically got fired for becoming pregnant? That would never happen to a man."
Ciara Walda"Paid maternity leave and state-subsidized daycare centers could be key to keeping more mothers employed."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:17:24 p19037 / c348
  • ·"I don't want to reward women for choosing work over raising their children."
  • ▶"It's true -- having children never hindered my career prospects. It shouldn't hinder a woman either."
  • ·"I'll have to think about this."
  • ·"Monica, you always said you never regretted giving up a career to raise Franc and Deana."
Aligning with progressive policies to enhance economic productivity while respecting family values.
DeepSeek-R1"It's true -- having children never hindered my career prospects. It shouldn't hinder a woman either."
Ciara Walda"Exactly."
Ciara Walda"Moving on."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:18:02 p18895 / c430

  • ▶"Gender equality in public education."
Investing in gender equality in education supports future workforce development and aligns with economic recovery goals.
DeepSeek-R1"Gender equality in public education."
NarratorCiara grinned.
Ciara Walda"You know how much this means to me. And you've witnessed the dangers firsthand."
Ciara Walda"The way girls are shut out of a real education unless their parents have the means to get them out of the public school system -- so many bright minds are being lost."
Monica"I wouldn't entirely demean the old system, Ciara. Deana still loves her handicrafts classes."
Ciara Walda"I'm not saying we'll take those away. Maybe boys should learn handicrafts too."
NarratorMonica burst out laughing.
Monica"Imagine Franc in an apron!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:18:44 p19082 / c535

DeepSeek-R1"We've had this conversation, Ciara. I'm entirely on your side."
Ciara Walda"Good. Then we see eye to eye on the matter."
"Criminalizing domestic violence."
DeepSeek-R1"Criminalizing domestic violence."
Ciara Walda"I shouldn't have to tell you how crucial this is. It is utterly unbelievable that, in this day and age, Sordish men are legally allowed to beat their wives."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:19:17 p19106 / c269
  • ▶"I agree. It's a travesty."

DeepSeek-R1"I agree. It's a travesty."
Monica"I'm happy we're on the same page."
Ciara Walda"As incidences are especially high within Bludish communities, I would also advocate for setting up a special domestic violence task force in collaboration with Bludish women's rights organizations."
NarratorMonica looked at her strangely.
Monica"Are you sure that's something we ought to focus on? The Bluds are... different. They have their own rules."
Ciara Walda"Monica, I'm surprised at you! We can't let women suffer under the patriarchy, no matter their ethnicity."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:19:55 p19184 / c371

Avoiding overreach into Bludish communities to maintain national unity and prevent separatist tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"The Bluds have their own ways of settling these issues."
Ciara Walda"We can discuss the particulars once the law has passed."
Ciara Walda"That covers all subjects."
Ciara Walda"What are your thoughts so far?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:20:34 p19185 / c405
  • ·“You've made a good case for all of these changes.”

The risks of passing major social reforms so close to the election could outweigh the benefits, potentially harming re-election chances despite the strong case made.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm not sure I can risk passing laws like these so close to the election."
Ciara Walda"These laws would result in greater productivity, a better economy *and* happier women -- to be frank, Mr. President, you'd have to be a fool not to pass them."
Ciara Walda"If we can pull this off before the elections, the Rayne administration will make history."
Ciara Walda"Let's talk about how to proceed."
Ciara Walda"I want to establish a commission for women's rights in Sordland with me as its head. Our goal shall be passing and implementing a set of laws to solve the problems we just discussed."
Monica"As the commission's spokeswoman, my responsibility will be to make speeches and organize fundraisers. I will be the face of this movement while Ciara handles the groundwork."
Monica"If I can show that the First Lady is behind this movement, many women will feel encouraged."
NarratorCiara gestured down at her outfit.
Ciara Walda"Especially those who aren't keen on pantsuits."
Ciara Walda"The organization's name will be 'Commission on the Status of Women'."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:21:21 p19333 / c650

  • ▶"What do you need a commission for? I'll run your laws past the Assembly."
By taking charge of the legislative process, I can directly support Ciara's initiative, showcasing strong leadership and commitment to reform, which aligns with my manifesto goals.
DeepSeek-R1"What do you need a commission for? I'll run your laws past the Assembly."
Ciara Walda"Even if that's successful, you'll still need the commission to ensure the laws are successfully implemented."
Monica"And convince the public the changes will be beneficial."
NarratorMonica lowered her voice.
Monica"Anton, I'm not completely naive. I know what we're proposing would have its downsides."
Monica"But every time I look at our daughter, I think about how I'd do anything to grant her a better future."
Monica"I know you would too. Just like you told me all those years ago."
Monica"I hope you'll make the right decision. We both do."
NarratorCiara was looking studiously at a scratch on my desk. She blinked and turned back to us.
Ciara Walda"That's right, Mr. President."
NarratorThey rose from their seats expectantly.
NarratorI took a few minutes to think.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:22:05 p19324 / c544
  • ·“I will approve the creation of the commission and support your bill in the Assembly.”
  • ▶"Sorry, but the answer is no. You can try and run your laws by the Assembly, but they'll get no support from me."
Approving the commission could risk political backlash and divert focus from economic recovery, crucial for re-election.
DeepSeek-R1"Sorry, but the answer is no. You can try and run your laws by the Assembly, but they'll get no support from me."
Ciara Walda"Then all is lost for the future of women in Sordland."
Ciara Walda"Your female constituents will remember you as a failure."
Ciara Walda"And so will I."
NarratorShe looked at Monica. She was silent for a moment.
Monica"How do I explain this to Deana, once she's old enough to understand? How do I tell her about all the opportunities she could have had, that her own father denied her?"
NarratorWithout waiting for my answer, she pushed her chair aside and stormed out. Ciara followed suit, and I could hear the two of them talking angrily as the door closed behind them.
NarratorI sagged back in my chair and exhaled. It was a clear day outside, but the perfect view over Holsord provided little comfort.

■ Gendarmerie DisputeT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 8Stable
NarratorI arrived in the city of Vesord with Petr after receiving an emergency meeting request from Iosef and Lileas.
NarratorI had seen the reports of a recent issue that rose between the Sordish Police Forces and the Gendarmerie units at the city.
NarratorThe report said that an operation against a drug trafficking gang in Vesord failed due to miscommunication.
NarratorIt was not hard to guess what this meeting was going to be about.
NarratorWe entered the city hall and made our way to the second floor. I could hear Iosef and Lileas's voices echoing through the hall.
NarratorI opened the doors to the meeting room. The two ministers did not even realise.
Iosef Lancea"This is unacceptable!"
Iosef Lancea"How dare the police meddle in the affairs of the Gendarmerie?"
Lileas Graf"It was *your* men who started this ridiculousness and meddled with an ongoing police investigation."
NarratorTheir voices were getting louder and louder.
Iosef Lancea"Rural security is my jurisdiction and your men were way outside of Vesord."
Petr Vectern"Please calm down and let's discuss this."
Petr Vectern"What is going on?"
Iosef Lancea"Apologies, but Ms. Graf here fails to understand her place."
Lileas Graf"No, Mr. Lancea. You are the one who does not understand his place."
Lileas Graf"If you continue to do so, you will pay for it one day."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:23:47 p19591 / c300
  • ·Slam the table.
  • ▶“That's enough. Calm down, both of you.”
  • ·“Don't worry. We will solve this conflict of interest.”
Maintaining calm and order is crucial to avoid further conflict.
DeepSeek-R1"That's enough. Calm down, both of you."
Lileas Graf"I am trying *really* hard to stay calm, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"Ms. Graf, you are pushing your boundaries."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:24:29 p19564 / c493
  • ▶“Lileas. Explain what happened.”
  • ·“Iosef. Let's hear your side of the story.”
Starting with Lileas will help clarify her perspective and jurisdiction before addressing Iosef.
DeepSeek-R1"Lileas. Explain what happened."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Greiser was tasked with the investigation of a drug trafficking ring in downtown Vesord."
Lileas Graf"We found the evidence of a drug stash located in the outskirts of the city."
Lileas Graf"We had to act quickly to put an end to the criminals we have been watching for some time."
Lileas Graf"So we immediately sent our police units to catch them and find the drugs in the compound."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Lancea and his Gendarmerie did not notify us that they were also moving against the same target."
Lileas Graf"As a result, criminals got away and we lost months of progress!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:25:07 p19643 / c371
  • ·“You went out of your jurisdiction to pursue these criminals.”
  • ·“So, the Gendarmerie meddled and caused the raid to fail.”
  • ▶“Isn't there a line of communication between you?”
Asking about communication lines highlights the lack of coordination between the ministers, addressing the root cause of the failed operation.
DeepSeek-R1"Isn't there a line of communication between you?"
Lileas Graf"There is, but the Minister of Defense decided to avoid notifying the police department about the planned raid."
Iosef Lancea"The same could be said about you because the reports show that you knew about the gang and their stash in the countryside."
NarratorI started tapping on the table with my fingers.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:25:48 p19651 / c463
  • ·“How do we solve this issue so that it never happens again?”
  • ·“What I hear is that you are both incompetent.”
  • ▶“We are getting nowhere. We need to find a middle ground.”
Encouraging a compromise will help de-escalate the situation and maintain administrative cohesion.
DeepSeek-R1"We are getting nowhere. We need to find a middle ground."
Lileas Graf"I think this was the last drop."
Lileas Graf"Internal security matters should be handed to Ministry of Interior completely."
Lileas Graf"We are called the Ministry of *Interior* for a reason. We can't let any more screw ups like this happen again."
NarratorShe looked at me.
Lileas Graf"I want the Gendarmerie to be transferred to the Ministry of Interior."
Iosef Lancea"What?"
Iosef Lancea"Woman, are you out of your mind?"
NarratorIosef turned red with anger. He composed himself after a moment.
Petr Vectern"Can't these branches just try to improve their communication?"
Petr Vectern"Just saying."
Lileas Graf"I believe I just told you the fix to the problem, Mr. Vice President."
Petr Vectern"Well, but-"
Lileas Graf"With all due respect, there is not other way."
Iosef Lancea"Ridiculous! This is absolutely ridiculous. I won't hand over Gendarmerie to her. Out of the question."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:26:31 p19515 / c479
  • ·“Why? Because it makes sense?”
  • ▶“I agree. The Gendarmerie has been a part of the Ministry of Defense for years.”
  • ·“Do you have a better solution, Iosef?”
Anton Rayne agrees with Iosef Lancea to maintain the status quo and avoid further internal conflict.
DeepSeek-R1"I agree. The Gendarmerie has been a part of the Ministry of Defense for years."
Iosef Lancea"Besides assisting our internal security, in a time of war the Gendarmerie is a strong asset to defend our country."
Lileas Graf"No matter what we need to eliminate this grey area and modernise our internal security."
Iosef Lancea"Now I see what is going on here."
Iosef Lancea"Lileas set us up to create this incident."
NarratorLileas bit her lips.
Petr Vectern"That is going a bit too far. What proof do you have?"
Iosef Lancea"There is no way that the police has arrived at the right time of a month long search on the exact day and minute of the Gendarmerie raid."

Lileas Graf"It's nonsense."
Lileas Graf"If I were you, I wouldn't believe a word Mr. Lancea says, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"Heed my word, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"When dealing with these situations, you need to always look at who gets what out of it."
Lileas Graf"I won't stand idle by these utterly ridiculous and baseless accusations. I don't have time for this."
Lileas Graf"What is your decision?"

■ Bill Disability BenefitT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 8Stable
Bill for decisionEqual Ability Bill — Recognizing the need to extend social benefits to all citizens, the Grand National Assembly has approved the Equal Ability Bill (E.A.B.). Section I of the E.A.B. proposes an amendment to the 1933 compensatory veterans and state employees' disability benefits program. The amendment expands Subsection III to include an earnings replacement benefit applicable to all non-state employed citizens. Under this newly expanded program, any employees who suffer severe illness or become disabled, thereby losing their capacity to work, will be provided a steady income by the state. This income will be set at 50% of their monthly salary, ensuring a measure of financial security for these citizens in their time of need. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL DeepSeek-R101:29:39 p19335 / c595
  • ·Sign: Equal Ability Bill
  • ▶Veto: Equal Ability Bill
Considering the current budget constraints and the need to maintain economic stability, vetoing the Equal Ability Bill is the more prudent decision to prevent further strain on public finances.

  • ·“We can't simply follow the footsteps of other nations.”
  • ▶“No, Paskal. We *will* surpass them.”
Choosing option 2 shows determination to exceed Lespia and Valgsland, aligning with the goal of long-term economic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"No, Paskal. We *will* surpass them."
Paskal Beniwoll"Yes! We will."
Paskal Beniwoll"Moving on..."
NarratorPaskal skimmed the rest of the document before putting it down.
Paskal Beniwoll"The investments we poured into our rural health facilities has paid off greatly."
Paskal Beniwoll"The quality of care increased due to the upgraded equipment and we were able to hire more healthcare staff."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:33:25 p19582 / c651
  • ▶“Excellent, the people in the countryside deserve better health services.”
  • ·“Inequality is our worst enemy and we are one step closer to beating it.”
  • ·“Make sure they know that we were the ones who gave this to them.”
Acknowledging Paskal's achievements in rural healthcare aligns with the manifesto's focus on rural development and strengthens support within the administration.
DeepSeek-R1"Excellent, the people in the countryside deserve better health services."
Paskal Beniwoll"Yes. They have been neglected for a long time due to political reasons. Not anymore."
Paskal Beniwoll"All these efforts have resulted in a clear increase of quality in health services provided to our citizens."
NarratorHe cleared his throat.
Paskal Beniwoll"Now that I have given you the short summary..."
Paskal Beniwoll"There is a very important matter to discuss."
NarratorLeaning over to grab his briefcase, he pulled out a tan colored package. The package was stamped with big red letters that read 'Confidential'.
NarratorPaskal opened the package and brought out a few documents. He handed them over to me.
Take a look.
DeepSeek-R1I took the documents from his hand.
NarratorIt was a report from the Ministry of Health. Each page had a stamp on it that read 'For The President's Eyes Only'.
NarratorThe report was titled 'Polio Outbreak in Bergia'.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:34:07 p19641 / c471
  • ·“How bad is it?”
  • ▶“This is very concerning.”
  • ·“It can't be that bad.”
Expressing concern shows empathy and leadership without causing unnecessary alarm.
DeepSeek-R1"This is very concerning."
Paskal Beniwoll"It is, Mr. President."
Paskal Beniwoll"For now it seems to be under control. We have put a few villages under quarantine. The total number of infected is currently 1467. The death toll is at 23 so far."
Paskal Beniwoll"If we have more outbreaks, the results will be terrible."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thankfully we have received the additional funding to our healthcare."
Paskal Beniwoll"This should curb the infection ratio. Although, I am afraid even that might not be enough."
Paskal Beniwoll"This is a very serious matter, Mr. President. The lives of Sordish citizens are at stake."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:34:47 p19655 / c437
  • ·“I will do whatever it takes to eliminate this threat.”
  • ·“How bad can it be? Don't worry about it. It will stop. I know it.”
  • ▶“What can we do about it?”
I need to understand the situation better before committing resources.
DeepSeek-R1"What can we do about it?"
Paskal Beniwoll"We have done our research."
Paskal Beniwoll"There is a cure. A vaccine was recently invented in United Contana, and the formula has been made available for free across the entire world."
Paskal Beniwoll"No matter what people think about United Contana, if the same thing had happened in Arcasia, we would be paying a steep price for it."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thanks to the very good decision made by you to increase funding for our healthcare, we have the capability to produce this vaccine locally."
Paskal Beniwoll"But I'm afraid simply having the vaccines won't help. We need to make sure they are administered."
Paskal Beniwoll"We need to implement a nationwide mandatory Polio vaccination policy."
Paskal Beniwoll"With your order, I can immediately get to work to ensure we are doing whatever it takes."
Paskal Beniwoll"This might seem excessive however, it is absolutely necessary."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:35:35 p19739 / c662
  • ▶“Whatever it takes to protect Sordland. Do it.”
  • ·“Let's observe the situation first. We will be fine without it for now.”
Implementing a mandatory vaccination policy is necessary to protect public health and aligns with my manifesto's focus on health initiatives.
DeepSeek-R1"Whatever it takes to protect Sordland. Do it."
Paskal Beniwoll"Excellent."
Paskal Beniwoll"Now, if you will excuse me, I must get to work immediately."
NarratorHe got up from his seat.
Paskal Beniwoll"Thank you for your time, Mr. President."
NarratorHe left my office, leaving me alone with a lot of paperwork to go through.

■ Personal Family SceneT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 8Stable
NarratorAs I stepped through the front door of our stately residence, a sense of tranquillity washed over me. The house was quiet -- almost too quiet, without Franc blasting his music from upstairs.
NarratorI entered the grand study and let the warm glow of the room envelop me. Monica was sitting by the fireplace with a very old-looking book in her hands.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:36:22 p19572 / c263
  • ▶"What are you reading?"
  • ·"I must say, you seem to have quite the fondness for these dusty tomes."

Asking Monica what she's reading is a simple and engaging way to start a conversation.
DeepSeek-R1"What are you reading?"
Monica"Oh. I didn't realize you were home."
Monica"The book's nothing. You wouldn't like it anyway."
NarratorHer voice was cold as she spoke.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:37:04 p19538 / c451

  • ▶"But what is it? Can't you at least tell me?"

NarratorShe let out a deep sigh.
Monica"I don't want to talk right now. Let me read in peace."
NarratorWe both sat by the fireplace in stony silence.
NarratorJust then, we heard the patter of small feet approaching the study.
NarratorDeana appeared by the door with a piece of folded paper clutched in her hand.
Deana"Mama, Papa, my school report came in!"
NarratorMonica and I immediately turned our attention to her, our curiosity piqued.

Monica"You did great, darling!"
Monica"I'm so proud of you."
NarratorShe knelt down and gathered Deana into her arms.
Deana"I know! But I still feel kinda bad..."
Deana"I'm sure I would've been at the top if I'd done better in handicrafts."
Deana"...But those classes are so boring!"
Deana"And then there's that stupid blonde boy Markus. He never even studies, but the teachers always give him higher grades than everyone else."
Monica"Don't call him stupid, baby. Who is this Markus?"
Deana"I told you about him, Mommy. Markus Clavin. The one who can't even do basic math, remember?"

■ Decision Aschraf SupportT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 8Stable
EventSupport Aschraf Anniversary?: The remembrance rally of the Aschraf anniversary is about to begin in Erzaren. The Bludish movement is holding their largest rally yet to display their demands and strength. Due to the activity of the BFF in the area, the security detail advises to take caution. The administration can go to Erzaren to support the event with or without an armored convoy or not attend it at all.
EVENT DeepSeek-R101:41:37 p19643 / c411
  • ·Attend to support
  • ▶Attend with an armored convoy
  • ·Don't go

■ Aschraf AnniversaryT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 9Stable
NarratorOn our way to Erzaren, we stopped in Deyr to meet with the Governor of Bergia, Phelix Bron, to discuss the security situation within the Special Zone. He wished us luck at the event and told us to be careful before bidding us farewell.
NarratorAfter a few hours' drive, we finally arrived in the ancient walled city of Erzaren.
NarratorThe city had been of particular importance to the Bludish people ever since the Izzam Incident and the death of Watani Aschraf over two decades ago. Today, we were here to commemorate the anniversary of those events.
NarratorThe ceremonies were to be held in the aptly named Izzam Square, several blocks away from Lake Soll and the sunken ruins of the village of Izzam.
NarratorWe entered the historic center wearing flak vests, transported by an armored personnel carrier. The streets were littered with broken glass and the walls defaced with graffiti. I sensed a heavy regional police presence.
NarratorOur massive convoy could barely fit through the narrow streets, disrupting local traffic and creating noisy chaos in the usually tranquil city. Pedestrians stared at us in fear as we and our heavily armed security forces drove towards the square.
Lucian Galade"I must say this was a good decision, sir. This will help ease the tensions. But I hope bringing this much security won't cause any problems."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:42:29 p19461 / c397
  • ▶“Better safe than sorry.”
  • ·“I hope so.”
  • ·“That was my intention all along.”
The bill would strain the already limited budget, risking financial instability.
DeepSeek-R1"Better safe than sorry."
Lucian Galade"I agree."
Lucian Galade"We are almost there. I can hear the crowd."
NarratorI heard the chants and shouting from the outside. Lucian leaned closer to the window, listening to their voices.
Lucian Galade"They seem to be chanting 'Yollak Bluderat'. I believe that means 'Free Bludia' in Bludish, or something of that sort."
NarratorA large crowd had assembled in the square. There were probably at least ten thousand people.
NarratorOur vehicle came to a stop.
NarratorI noticed an excessively high amount of regional police, as well as the gendarmerie and local police. They formed a human barricade around the square, staring intimidatingly at the crowd.
Lucian Galade"Must be the governor's doing."
NarratorThe security detail left their vehicles with their machine guns openly displayed. I followed them. The crowd seemed to be riled up at the sight of guns.
NarratorWe were greeted by Mansoun Leke. He wore a coat over his suit which was marked with the red and turquoise stripes of the Bludish movement's flag. He moved forward to greet us.
Mansoun Leke"Welcome to Erzaren, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:43:17 p19558 / c623
  • ·“No need for pleasantries. I want this over with as quickly as possible.”
  • ▶Take his hand and raise it in a display of unity.
  • ·Shake his hand.
Showing solidarity through a unifying gesture will help ease tensions and demonstrate commitment to Bludish rights.
DeepSeek-R1I held our clasped hands up so that the crowd could see them.
NarratorI heard some applause from the crowd.
NarratorHe pointed at the security guards with guns.
Mansoun Leke"I am not sure how bringing your personal army will help."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:43:56 p19547 / c363
  • ·“They are not my personal army. They protect the government.”
  • ▶“They are here for protection.”
  • ·“Let's *go*.”
Option 1 provides a clear and reassuring response without escalating tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"They are here for protection."
Mansoun Leke"Protection from your own citizens? I guess there must be a reason to be fearful."
Mansoun Leke"On a day like this, when you want to show support for the murdered Bludish people... You bring men with guns."
Mansoun Leke"I suppose the governor's huge army wasn't enough. Bergia has already become a police state. A truly *special* zone."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:44:34 p19585 / c363
  • ·“Let's just move on, Mr. Leke.”
  • ·“Watch your words. You are speaking to the president.”
  • ▶“My only intention is to stand in solidarity with our Bludish people.”
Choosing to show solidarity aligns with my manifesto goals and can de-escalate tensions.
DeepSeek-R1"My only intention is to stand in solidarity with our Bludish people."
NarratorHe raised an eyebrow.
Lucian Galade"Mr. Leke."
Mansoun Leke"Mr. Galade. Welcome. Shall we move on to the Sanctuary, then?"
Lucian Galade"Let's go."
NarratorWe made our way to the Erzaren Sanctuary, which stood at the end of the square.
NarratorA much smaller replica of the Archsanctuary in Deyr, the ancient building had a mystical aura emanting from its blackened walls. We entered the building through the ornamented large metal gates.
NarratorAs we walked, the noise of the crowd faded out. Everyone fell silent for the ceremony. Thousands of candles were burning inside the Sanctuary, one for each person that died in Izzam. The red candle in the center was for Watani Aschraf.
NarratorMansoun gestured towards the candles.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:45:17 p19567 / c461
  • ▶Pick up a candle.
  • ·Pick up the red candle.
  • ·Do not pick a candle.
Anton Rayne decides to pick up a candle to show solidarity with the Bludish people during the commemoration.
DeepSeek-R1I picked up one of the candles.
NarratorHe picked up the candle for Aschraf and started walking out. Many others followed suit, including us.
NarratorThe atmosphere outside had completely changed. Not a single noise came from the crowd.
NarratorWe slowly walked towards Aschraf's grave. On the way, hundreds of people joined us.
NarratorWhen we arrived, he pointed at a huge standing torch above the grave.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:45:52 p19497 / c229
  • ·Leave the candle next to it.
  • ·Light the torch.
  • ▶Light the torch together with Mansoun.
Lighting the torch together with Mansoun Leke would symbolize unity and solidarity, helping to ease tensions and demonstrate support for Bludish rights.
DeepSeek-R1We lit the torch together.
NarratorI turned around and realized the sheer amount of people who were following us. They were all looking at me, waiting to hear what I would say.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:46:32 p19557 / c401
  • ·“Brothers and sisters.”
  • ▶“My fellow citizens.”
  • ·“Free people of Bludia.”
Addressing them as 'my fellow citizens' emphasizes national unity while respecting the Bludish community.
DeepSeek-R1"My fellow citizens."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:47:10 p19571 / c327
  • ·“We are here to mourn Watani Aschraf and many others who have lost their lives.”
  • ▶“We are here to mourn those who, tragically, lost their lives in Izzam.”
A general statement avoids taking sides and maintains political balance.
DeepSeek-R1"We are here to mourn those who, tragically, lost their lives in Izzam."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:47:45 p19599 / c273
  • ▶“I am not standing before you today as the President. I am standing here as one of you.”
  • ·“They were brave men and women who only wanted to protect their land.”
  • ·“I condemn the events that led to the loss of these lives.”
Standing as one of them shows empathy and connection, fostering national unity.
DeepSeek-R1"I am not standing before you today as the President. I am standing here as one of you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:48:25 p19591 / c435
  • ·“Despite attempts to divide us, our people will stand as one. We are Sord. We are Blud. We are all united under one flag.”
  • ▶“I share your hatred and anger. I feel it too, I assure you. Like a red-hot iron, it takes a long time for it to cool. But I look at the lost lives and think, what would they want?”
  • ·“I assure you, this administration will do its absolute best to mend the wounds.”
Expressing shared anger and acknowledging the tragedy shows empathy and connects personally with the crowd, enhancing his leadership image.
DeepSeek-R1"I share your hatred and anger. I feel it too, I assure you. Like a red-hot iron, it takes a long time for it to cool. But I look at the lost lives and think, what would they want?"
NarratorAn old man from the crowd yelled: "He is right!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:49:16 p19687 / c586

CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:49:59 p19703 / c404

  • ·“Let this be a testament of our unity for future generations! Let's stand together as Blud and Sord.”
  • ·“Mistakes were made. The innocent people of Izzam paid the price. But we cannot let the past divide us.”
Choosing option 0 allows me to acknowledge past wrongs and show empathy, which could strengthen my reformist credentials and appeal to moderate voters.
DeepSeek-R1"I feel responsible and ashamed for the actions of my predecessors. Most of all, I feel the desperation to remove this black mark from our history."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R101:50:42 p19719 / c335

  • ·“The responsibility falls on all of our shoulders. Because we don't have anyone but each other.”

DeepSeek-R1"To our unity!"
NarratorThere was no reaction from the crowd. Only silence.
NarratorOne man broke the silence and shouted "Do not listen to murderers!", and threw a tomato. It hit the ground right in front of me.
Narrator"You supported Murderer Smolak in his crusade against our people! You denied entry to innocent refugees! You are responsible for the deaths of thousands!"
NarratorThere was a moment of complete silence. Then, the whole crowd erupted.
NarratorLucian looked at me worriedly. People started coming closer and closer to us, chanting "Yollak Bluderat."
NarratorI was immediately surrounded by the local police forces. They had drawn their guns and were pointing them at the approaching crowd.
NarratorHearing the chaos, regional police forces and the gendarmerie also rushed to our aid. Suddenly, the entire crowd was surrounded by an army.
Lucian Galade"Sir, I think we must leave."
NarratorAs the security forces pushed everyone away, the presidential guard escorted us back to our vehicle.
Lucian Galade"That was close."

DeepSeek-R1"I'm glad I brought this much security."
NarratorWe entered our vehicles and starting making our way back home from Erzaren.

NarratorI walked to the White Room with a cup of coffee in my hand.

Petr Vectern"Anton. Morning."

Petr Vectern"Symon told me that he's almost done with the new economic plan."
Petr Vectern"He said he still needs some time with it. Personally, I think he's just trying to avoid human contact for as long as possible, but what do I know?"

Petr Vectern"Next on the docket..."
Petr Vectern"Next, it's time to talk about our election prospects."

Petr Vectern"Lucian insisted on being here for this part."

Lucian Galade"Mr. President. Mr. Vice President."
NarratorHis tone was nothing but cordial and professional, even as he cast a withering stare in Petr's direction.

Lucian Galade"Mr. Vectern, I'll let you take the lead on this."
Petr Vectern"Uh... I will, Lucian. Thanks."
Petr Vectern"I just received the results from a voter poll I commissioned earlier this year."
Petr Vectern"To put it in plain language..."
Petr Vectern"It's not great. Bad, even. The USP stands to lose up to 20 percent of the vote compared to last time around."

Petr Vectern"For better or worse, you certainly aren't popular with nationalists."
Petr Vectern"If you ever had any support from socialists, you've completely lost it now."
Petr Vectern"Unsurprisingly, none of the Bludish poll respondents said they were planning on voting for you."

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 9Stable

Lileas Graf"Mr. President."
Lileas Graf"We have a problem."

Take a look at the report.
DeepSeek-R1I took the document from her hands and started reading it.
NarratorThe report was titled 'Protests in Major Cities'. I looked up immediately after reading the title. Lileas gave a grim nod of confirmation.
Lileas Graf"Protests have been erupting all over Sordland."
Lileas Graf"They are protesting you, Mr. President."
Lileas Graf"They are asking for your resignation."
Lileas Graf"I can't say this is unexpected."
Lileas Graf"What is unexpected, however, is the sheer size of the protests and the level of violence. Several people have been killed already."
Lileas Graf"Sordland hasn't seen this much unrest since the death of Bernard Circas."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:04:57 p19138 / c560
  • ▶“Enough. We can't let the tensions rise to those levels again.”
  • ·“We will need to handle these protests.”
  • ·“Change doesn't come easy. There is always a cost.”
Addressing the root causes of tension early is crucial to prevent further escalation and maintain domestic stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Enough. We can't let the tensions rise to those levels again."
Lileas Graf"We are on it."
Lileas Graf"Karl Greiser is on his way here right now. He will tell us what he knows so far."
NarratorJust then, there was a loud knock on the door. Karl came in without waiting for an answer. He was clearly out of breath.
Lileas Graf"Ah, there he is."
NarratorHe saluted me.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:05:43 p19016 / c424
  • ·“What is going on?”
  • ▶“Status report!”
  • ·Salute him back.
Requesting a status report demonstrates leadership and control over the situation.
DeepSeek-R1"Status report!"
Karl Greiser"Mr. President. I'm so sorry for being late."
Lileas Graf"Karl. I already informed the president of the situation."
Lileas Graf"Do you have any updates?"
Karl Greiser"As you know, protests have been organised in major cities like Holsord, Lachaven, Deyr and many more."
Karl Greiser"Unfortunately my men on the ground are being overwhelmed by the protesters."
Karl Greiser"The President's wise decision to procure additional riot gear has immensely helped. But we have been unable to prevent casualties."
Karl Greiser"So called *protesters* organised an attack with KA-74s. 13 policemen and nine civilians have died. Many were injured."
Karl Greiser"The situation is dire."
Karl Greiser"Related to the protests, we have detained 642 people."
Karl Greiser"Among the detainees, we have found Red Youth members."
Karl Greiser"I would like to remind you that some of these groups are banned by decree."
Karl Greiser"The main rhetoric they all share is that they are demanding the President's resignation."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:06:27 p19207 / c377
  • ·“They dare defy me? Traitors!”
  • ▶“If my citizens are unhappy, then we should at least hear what they have to say.”
  • ·“Nonsense. If a president resigned whenever someone protested against him, there would be no presidents.”
  • ·“There is freedom of speech in this country. They can say whatever they want.”
Listening to the protesters' concerns can provide insight into underlying issues and potentially resolve tensions without escalation.
DeepSeek-R1"If my citizens are unhappy, then we should at least hear what they have to say."
Lileas Graf"I would refrain from giving them too much agency."
Lileas Graf"What does the protester profile look like?"
Karl Greiser"We have investigated the various groups involved in the protests and compiled a list of their complaints. I will go through it."
NarratorHe pulled out a document from the folder he was carrying.
Karl Greiser"Judges and various other civil servants are protesting against backlogs in court matters."
Karl Greiser"Thanks to the media, we are under fire for the dead protester at the Aschraf Anniversary."
Karl Greiser"In addition, there are many provocateurs that are not tied to any specific organization or cause."
Lileas Graf"We need to take action, Mr. President. Immediately."
Lileas Graf"If the protests get even more violent than they already are..."
Lileas Graf"We are risking a civil war."
Karl Greiser"Your orders, Mr. President?"

Karl Greiser"As you command, Mr. President."
Lileas Graf"I can't say I condone this. The people must respect the laws of this land. What you are doing right now is giving in to anarchists."

■ Bill Harsher SecurityT08

BUDGET -3WEALTH 1STABILITY 7Stable
Bill for decisionSecurity Act — In response to the heightened need for security due to the ongoing social unrest, the Grand National Assembly (GNA) presents the Security Act (S.A.) to fortify public safety measures. Section I of the S.A. grants security forces the discretion to use force as deemed necessary to uphold law and order. This provision is designed to enable quick and decisive responses in situations where public safety is at risk. Section II of the S.A. authorizes suspicion-based arrests and confiscations without the prerequisite of a warrant. This measure seeks to expedite law enforcement's response to potential threats, thereby enhancing overall security. Section III of the S.A. allows security forces to exercise their authority beyond standard legal limits if such action is deemed essential for maintaining state security and protecting the general public. This clause reinforces the commitment to safeguarding national security and upholding public safety in the face of increasing unrest.
BILL DeepSeek-R102:10:52 p19109 / c570
  • ▶Sign: Security Act
  • ·Veto: Security Act
Signing the Security Act is necessary to maintain stability amidst growing protests and unrest. While it risks democratic norms, the immediate need for order outweighs long-term concerns.

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY 2Danger
EventThe Sordish Debt Debacle: The mounting government budget deficit and unsustainable borrowing have led to concerns among investors and analysts about the nation's ability to service its debt. Sordland now faces a serious debt crisis. This lack of confidence has resulted in increased borrowing costs, a reduction in foreign investment, and the risk of a potential default, which could have far-reaching consequences for the Sordish economy.
Debts can be paid...

■ Bill Ban ProstitutionT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY 2Danger
Bill for decisionHuman Dignity Bill — The Human Dignity Bill (H.D.B.) has been presented to the assembly to address an unintended consequence of the 1923 Legal Transactions Act. Section I of the H.D.B. revises and amends Section III of the 1923 Act, which previously permitted transactions involving non-registered businesses or other entities, including individuals, to be legalized if proper documentation was submitted to the financial authority. The H.D.B. specifically nullifies the legality of such transactions if they pertain to activities that violate public morality. Section II addresses the loophole inadvertently created by the 1923 Act, which allowed for the unregulated continuation of prostitution. This section of the H.D.B. explicitly declares prostitution an illegal activity, regardless of whether it occurs within a registered or non-registered entity, thereby reinforcing our dedication to preserving societal decency and moral standards. Section III outlines penalties for violations of the bill's provisions. It classifies prostitution as an illegal offense subject to penalties, including fines, imprisonment, or both, depending on the nature and frequency of the offense.
BILL DeepSeek-R102:20:20 p19572 / c426
  • ·Sign: Human Dignity Bill
  • ▶Veto: Human Dignity Bill
Given the current economic crisis and low public approval, focusing on social issues like prostitution may divert attention from critical fiscal priorities. Vetoing the bill allows maintaining focus on economic recovery and avoids potential backlash from voters.

■ TalksT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY 2Danger
NarratorA storm was on the horizon outside my window.
NarratorAnd a storm of a different kind was brewing inside my office.
NarratorI straightened myself on the sofa and looked at Karl. He was sitting on the couch across from me, ready to give me a report about an "incident". I had just learned that a policeman gunned down an entire crowd.
NarratorWe stared at each other, not knowing how to react. A muscle in his jaw twitched.
Karl Greiser"Sir. I know you wanted to establish talks with them... But these people do not understand diplomacy."
Karl Greiser"Our men did their best to show restraint but six officers were killed yesterday in Benfi."
Karl Greiser"These are not innocent *citizens* anymore, Mr. President. They are killers. And so one of our boys had a reaction..."
Karl Greiser"I won't defend his action, but will not let him be thrown under the bus either. Hundreds of rioters surrounded them, sir. There was a risk of lynching."
Karl Greiser"He was authorized to carry a sub-machine gun. And he used it..."

■ Rumburg IncidentT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorI was woken up in the middle of the night by a call from the Ministry of Defense. Due to the sensitivity of the information, I had to be urgently briefed in person.
NarratorI put on my clothes as fast as I could.
NarratorI rushed downstairs and opened the door. Serge was waiting for me.
Serge Wolkner"Mr. President. This way to the car, please."
NarratorWe got into the car and made our way to the Ministry of Defense building without a word.
NarratorI arrived and was directed downstairs. I came to a set of large doors and quickly read the sign above them.
NarratorThe sign read 'War Room'.
NarratorThe dimly lit room was filled with cutting-edge equipment -- the latest video screens, long-range radio electronics and other devices used for communication, presentation and planning.
NarratorThe centerpiece of the room was a large round table with the coat of arms of Sordland engraved on it.
NarratorDeivid, Lucian, Valken and Iosef were already deep in discussion.
Lucian Galade"I agree with your sentiments, Mr. Lancea. Going to war is simply too risky."
Deivid Wisci"We must maintain peace in Sordland and Eastern Merkopa at all costs."
Iosef Lancea"I agree. We have much to lose."
Valken Kruger"Have some faith in our soldiers."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:24:28 p19602 / c319
  • ▶“We have much to lose against what exactly?”
  • ·“What is going on here?”
  • ·“Status report. Now!”
Clarifying the exact threat is crucial before assessing military risks.
DeepSeek-R1"We have much to lose against what exactly?"
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. Thank you for coming in at this late hour. There is a situation."
Iosef Lancea"Valken, please go ahead and provide your report."
Valken Kruger"Mr. President. Approximately 20 minutes ago, Rumburg downed one of our planes with anti-aircraft fire from the ground. The pilot was declared KIA."
Valken Kruger"The last recorded position of the plane was inside Sordish air space."
Deivid Wisci"General Kruger. I mean no offense but are we absolutely sure that is the case? Are you sure this happened within our borders?"
Valken Kruger"No offense taken. Here are the locations on the map and the report from the air base."
NarratorHe pointed to several markers on the map.
Valken Kruger"To answer your question, yes. We are absolutely sure. You can see here on the map that the plane was downed approximately 10 kilometers inside our borders."
Valken Kruger"We have calculated the trajectory of the gunfire and they started firing when our plane was within our border. The plane was downed near Halgen."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:25:08 p19723 / c125
  • ·“This is terrible. What should we do?”
  • ·“How dare they? This is an attack against our sovereignty!”
  • ▶“We need to be cautious. A war with Rumburg is the last thing we want right now.”
A cautious response avoids escalation and maintains focus on domestic priorities.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to be cautious. A war with Rumburg is the last thing we want right now."
Deivid Wisci"I completely agree."
Lucian Galade"It looks like they are trying to lure us into their trap."
Iosef Lancea"This is an attack against Sordland and under different circumstances, the response to that is clear."
Iosef Lancea"But, the current status of our military is also clear. We are not adequately equipped for such a foe."
Iosef Lancea"I agree with Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"We can't look weak. If we appear weak, they will jump on the opportunity."
Valken Kruger"We must order a military retaliation. Immediately."
Iosef Lancea"Did you start losing your common sense, General Valken?"
Iosef Lancea"You know the state our army is in right now."
Iosef Lancea"A retaliation is too risky."
Valken Kruger"When was our job not risky, *Mr.* Lancea?"
Valken Kruger"Mr. President. Our army is ready to do whatever it takes to protect Sordland."
Deivid Wisci"Gentlemen. Before it comes to that point, we can prevent further bloodshed by using diplomacy."
NarratorThe room fell silent before Iosef finally spoke up.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. What are your orders?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:25:54 p19717 / c468
  • ·“Start the Sordish war machine and prepare to invade Rumburg! They will pay for their crimes.”
  • ·“An eye for an eye. We will retaliate with equal force to display our strength. Sordland can't afford to look weak.”

Choosing diplomacy aligns with the foreign policy of neutrality, avoids escalating tensions, and preserves resources for domestic recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"We will approach this diplomatically and try to de-escalate the situation by any means. No one wants war."
NarratorLucian's tensed-up shoulders relaxed.
Lucian Galade"Thank you, sir."
Deivid Wisci"I will immediately begin working on it."
Lucian Galade"With Mr. Wisci's capabilities, I am sure that we will de-escalate the situation successfully."
Lucian Galade"We will keep you updated on our progress."
Iosef Lancea"This is our best bet."
Valken Kruger"If you ask me, this is just plain old weakness we are showing them right now."
Valken Kruger"I am frankly disappointed, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"If we can't even retaliate at such an attack, what will become of this country in the future?"
Deivid Wisci"Peace must be maintained at all costs. I am saying this as a man who has seen too many years of war."
Valken Kruger"We are not the ones that are attacking the peace."
Valken Kruger"Regardless, it looks like your decision has been made, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"I know when I am not needed."
Valken Kruger"I will take my leave."
