Iosef Lancea"I think we should hold a military parade to display our strength."
Deivid Wisci"I am with Mr. Lancea on this one. This should work well as a deterrent and it will come in handy as a tool to de-escalate the situation."
Lucian Galade"That is a good idea."
Lucian Galade"Simple and effective without causing any ruckus."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:26:36 p19697 / c244
  • ·“That makes sense. Organise the military parade.”
  • ▶“No. We need to create a peaceful image for Sordland.”
Focusing on a peaceful approach aligns with domestic priorities and avoids unnecessary escalation.
DeepSeek-R1"No. We need to create a peaceful image for Sordland."
Iosef Lancea"Understood."
Iosef Lancea"That is all, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"I will keep you updated on the situation."
Iosef Lancea"For now, have a good night's sleep."
Iosef Lancea"You will need it."
NarratorI couldn't sleep at all that night.

■ PreparationsT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorI was sitting right across from Deivid in the White Room after he scheduled an urgent meeting.
NarratorI knew that the matter must've been serious.
NarratorHe insisted on waiting for Iosef and Lucian so we did.
NarratorLucian entered the room with Iosef by his side.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. We came as fast as we could."
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. Mr. Wisci."
Lucian Galade"What is going on?"
NarratorDeivid cleared his throat.
Deivid Wisci"Welcome, everyone."
Deivid Wisci"Due to the potential ramifications, I wanted everyone to be present for this meeting."
Deivid Wisci"We have received an envoy from Arcasia. They are requesting a state visit."
Deivid Wisci"Judging from the current climate, it is not hard to see what this is about."
Deivid Wisci"ATO is always trying to expand its influence after all. They will most likely try to convince us to join."
Deivid Wisci"In addition, as part of this state visit, Dwight Walker will, of course, meet with you in person, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:27:41 p19626 / c508
  • ·“It's about time. I wanted to meet with him as well.”
  • ▶“There is something we could gain from this state visit.”
  • ·“As much as I share their ideals, we need to be wary.”
Emphasizing potential economic benefits while maintaining caution about foreign alliances.
DeepSeek-R1"There is something we could gain from this state visit."
Iosef Lancea"Normally, I would be very much against the influence of any superpower."
Iosef Lancea"But as much as I hate to admit..."
Iosef Lancea"We need allies to bolster our defense."
Iosef Lancea"That's why I am not against the idea of him coming here to extend his *helping hand*."
Iosef Lancea"On one condition..."
Iosef Lancea"Sordish military command must belong to Sordland at all costs."
Iosef Lancea"Otherwise our sovereignty will be in grave danger."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:28:34 p19692 / c498
  • ·“Have no doubt, Iosef. I have sworn an oath to this country.”
  • ▶“Let's see what he wants first. I will decide when I talk to him.”
  • ·“Everything is on the table if it ensures the well-being of Sordland.”
  • ·“We don't need any outside help.”
Evaluate the envoy's intentions before making a decision to ensure it aligns with domestic priorities.
DeepSeek-R1"Let's see what he wants first. I will decide when I talk to him."
Iosef Lancea"I hope that will be enough."
Lucian Galade"Mr. Walker never gives anything for free. There will be some concessions for sure."
Lucian Galade"Mr. Wisci you are our most experienced foreign policy expert. What's your take on this? What do you think is the right action?"
Deivid Wisci"Rumburg is bound to increase diplomatic pressure in the upcoming AN meeting. Backchannels are reporting rumours about Queen Beatrice Livingston meeting with military leaders in Thornbourg."
Deivid Wisci"I am afraid it might not be possible to stand against Rumburg without strong allies. Soon, we will have to take a side in this global rivalry to avert a potential conflict with Rumburg."
Deivid Wisci"And I recently found out of a planned meeting between the Queen and Chairman Malenyev of United Contana. The ATO and President Walker might be the necessary evil to stop worse things from happening."
Deivid Wisci"I say we should consider this offer very seriously."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:29:27 p20027 / c615
  • ·“Lucian. What do you think?”
  • ▶“Very well. Accept their offer.”
  • ·“I don't want to involve ourselves with superpowers. Decline their offer.”
Accepting the offer from Dwight Walker could provide much-needed military and economic support, helping stabilize Sordland amidst internal unrest and external threats.
DeepSeek-R1"Very well. Accept their offer."
Deivid Wisci"I will send our response immediately."
Lucian Galade"Then there is a tiny detail we need to get over with it."
Lucian Galade"How should we welcome their delegation, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:30:08 p19981 / c281
  • ·“Make it as extravagant as you can. We need to impress them.”
  • ·“A military welcome is in order. They need to see our might.”
  • ▶“A plain welcome will suffice. They are the ones who are requesting this.”
A plain welcome is appropriate given the current tensions and economic struggles, allowing focus on addressing internal issues without overextending resources.
DeepSeek-R1"A plain welcome will suffice. They are the ones who are requesting this."
Lucian Galade"As you wish. I have taken a note."
Deivid Wisci"I think that is all for today."
Deivid Wisci"Thank you all for attending."
NarratorAs we dispersed, the world map hanging on the wall in the room caught my attention. I walked over to it.
NarratorHow often were the lines on the world map redrawn over the recent decades?
NarratorWhat about the future?

■ Arcasia VisitT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorThe door of the car opened and flashes went off.
NarratorDwight Walker, President of Arcasia, Head of the ATO came out of the car, smiling and waving at the enormous crowd that gathered. It was not every day an Arcasian president came to visit Sordland after all.
NarratorPresident Walker stepped on the carpet and started to make his way towards us.
NarratorAlong with Monica, I started walking over to meet him mid way.
NarratorFinally, I was in reaching distance to one of the most powerful men in the world.
NarratorHe looked at Monica first, bowed and kissed her hand for a second longer than I liked.
Dwight Walker"Mr. Rayne. If you told me I would get to meet with your lovely wife, I would've come sooner."
Dwight Walker"I'm charmed, Ms. Rayne."
Monica"Oh my. Thank you, Mr. Walker. It's a pleasure to meet with you."
Dwight Walker"No."
Dwight Walker"The pleasure is all mine, Ms. Rayne."
Monica"I hope your wife and the family is well."
Dwight Walker"They are very well, Ms. Rayne. Thank you. Unfortunately they couldn't make it here today."
Dwight Walker"And who do we have here?"
Dwight Walker"Mr. President. It is so good to finally meet with you."
NarratorWe shook hands.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:31:04 p19463 / c377
  • ▶“It is really good to see you, Mr. President.”
  • ·“I wanted to meet with you for the longest time.”
  • ·“Welcome to Sordland.”
A friendly and professional greeting maintains decorum while acknowledging the significance of the visit.
DeepSeek-R1"It is really good to see you, Mr. President."
Dwight Walker"I assure you, the feeling is mutual. That's why I am here in fact."
NarratorHe turned and looked around.
Dwight Walker"I was almost going to think that you weren't expecting me."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:31:46 p19540 / c292
  • ·“We are very sorry to hear that.”
  • ▶“I'm a simple man. Not really a fan of extravagance.”
  • ·“Of course not! You are our guest of honor!”
Choosing option 1 shows humility and aligns with Rayne's image as a practical leader focused on domestic issues.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm a simple man. Not really a fan of extravagance."
Dwight Walker"Me, Mr. President... I am the exact opposite."
NarratorHe laughed.
NarratorWe started walking towards the reception hall in the palace. Our path was cleared of people at once.
NarratorHe was simply looking around at the extraordinary interior design of the Maroon Palace. Statues, paintings and various ornaments filled both sides of the corridor.
NarratorWe passed the corridor and entered the reception hall. The crowd inside was composed of various politicians and state figures.
NarratorUpon our entry, they started clapping. Dry martinis were handed to both of us.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:32:33 p19592 / c402
  • ▶“Ladies and gentlemen. I present to you, President Walker.”
  • ·“Everyone. Let's give a warm welcome to our guest of honor.”
  • ·“Don't mind us. We were just passing by.”
A formal introduction maintains dignity and sets a respectful tone for the meeting.
DeepSeek-R1"Ladies and gentlemen. I present to you, President Walker."
NarratorPeople started clapping.
Dwight Walker"Thank you. President Rayne. It is really good to be here."
Dwight Walker"And thank you everyone for the warm welcome."
NarratorWith another thunderous round of applause, the jazz band started playing.
NarratorPresident Walker and I separated and started mingling with the crowd. The reception was in full swing.
NarratorAfter some time chatting with the attendees, President Walker approached us. He bowed before my wife.
Dwight Walker"Ms. Rayne. Can I call you Monica?"
NarratorShe looked over at me before smiling at him.
Monica"Yes, of course. If I can call you Dwight."
NarratorHe took her hand and they started dancing. I turned around to talk to the waiter.
NarratorI was into my third martini when I heard a whistle. I turned around and saw Monica, dancing very closely with President Walker. All eyes in the room were on them.
NarratorPresident Walker grabbed Monica's waist.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:33:16 p19560 / c389
  • ▶“President Walker, why don't we have a chat somewhere quiet?”
  • ·Interject and dance with Monica yourself.
  • ·Finish your martini.
Taking President Walker aside would prevent a public scene and allow for discreet handling of any sensitive matters.
DeepSeek-R1"President Walker, why don't we have a chat somewhere quiet?"
Dwight Walker"Right when we were getting to the good part."
Dwight Walker"Fine. Let's have a chat."
NarratorThe President and I swapped our drinks for Erlory Maroon 30 year olds. I knew a special spot. With our drinks at hand, we went down the stairs to the basement.
NarratorGradually, the music and the sound of the party started to fade away.
NarratorWe arrived at a cozy little corner office with not much inside other than a table and comfortable chairs.
NarratorHe took a seat and I followed suit. He pulled out two cigars.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:33:58 p19611 / c395
  • ·“I don't smoke, Mr. Walker. Thanks.”
  • ▶Take one of the cigars.
Accepting the cigar builds rapport with Dwight Walker, which could be advantageous for future negotiations.
DeepSeek-R1I took one of the cigars.
NarratorHe pulled out a Rippo lighter and lit my cigar before lighting his own.
NarratorWe clinked our glasses and took a sip.
Dwight Walker"Ah. This is a really good one. Very smoky. I like it."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:34:40 p19554 / c419
  • ·“I'm glad you liked it.”
  • ·“Never had an Erlory Maroon before?”
  • ▶Wait for him to start the conversation.
Maintaining control by allowing Dwight Walker to set the tone ensures a strategic response.
DeepSeek-R1I waited for him to start the conversation.
NarratorHe took a puff from his cigar, filling the small room with smoke.
Dwight Walker"Definitely miles better than Valgslandian whiskey."
Dwight Walker"But, I like Lespian whiskey more. It pairs better with their cigars."
Dwight Walker"You see, Mr. Rayne. Whiskeys are a little bit like countries."
Dwight Walker"Some are stronger, some are weaker. Some are older, some are younger. Many are complex but sometimes simple is the best."
Dwight Walker"I personally like stronger whiskeys in general. That's why I liked this one. Yes. It is definitely promising."
Dwight Walker"But whatever you drink, in the end, it all boils down to your own taste. And I assure you, Mr. Rayne. My sense of taste is *very* refined."
Dwight Walker"What type of whiskey do you like?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:35:23 p19674 / c403
  • ▶“I like complex flavours.”
  • ·“I like stronger ones.”
  • ·“I prefer wine.”
  • ·“I am good with whatever is made here in Sordland.”
Choosing 'I like complex flavours' reflects a nuanced and diplomatic response, aligning with the goal of maintaining stability while considering external alliances.
DeepSeek-R1"I like complex flavours."
Dwight Walker"As good as they are, they can get tiring."
NarratorHe tugged at his tie to loosen it a little.
Dwight Walker"You know why I'm here."
Dwight Walker"I want to make you an offer."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:36:04 p19669 / c359
  • ·“The type I can't refuse?”
  • ▶“I'm listening.”
  • ·“I was waiting for this moment.”
  • ·“Let's get it over with.”
Accepting the offer opens strategic opportunities but requires careful consideration of potential consequences.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm listening."
Dwight Walker"Good."
Dwight Walker"Listen closely."
Dwight Walker"Times are changing and so is the balance of the world."
Dwight Walker"Soon, instead of a fractured world, we will see a more united world. There is us, ATO against them."
Dwight Walker"I want you by our side. The winning side."
Dwight Walker"I think there is much we can achieve together and you have shown me the proof."
Dwight Walker"If you accept, I would like to extend you a warm welcome to ATO as a full member state. This of course comes along with lots of privileges and responsibilities."
Dwight Walker"In ATO, we protect each other. We have a mutual defense clause in the case of war."
Dwight Walker"Join us, Mr. Rayne and you will have the entire backing of ATO. Soldiers, jet fighters, warships... whatever you like."
Dwight Walker"During peacetime, you will see the effects even more! Our combined production output will benefit both Sordland and ATO. We will have free-trade zone agreements, free visa access, investments, you name it."
Dwight Walker"All I ask in return is mutual loyalty to one another in the pact."
Dwight Walker"The Sordish Armed Forces will be a member of the ATO military structure. All branches be it air, navy, army will collaborate with the alliance network. We will provide assistance and training for free."
Dwight Walker"Our armies will learn how to fight together and be stronger than ever in the face of the Malenyevist threat!"
Dwight Walker"Let's make history. Here. Tonight. What do you say?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:36:51 p19808 / c485
  • ·“I say aye! I accept, Mr. Walker. [Join ATO]”
  • ·“Is that all you are asking for? I was expecting more demands.”
  • ·“I assure you we can hold out our own just fine.”
  • ▶“Thank you for the offer however, we currently have no plans to enter ATO.”
Anton prefers neutrality and domestic focus over international alliances.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you for the offer however, we currently have no plans to enter ATO."
Dwight Walker"At least I know that I tried."
Dwight Walker"Don't close yourself to the opportunity. Maybe you are not ready now but you might be ready in the future."
Dwight Walker"I usually get what I want, Mr. Rayne and trust me, you won't get rid of me that easily."
NarratorHe smiled and we shook hands.
Dwight Walker"Regardless, it was good to meet with you. I hope this is the start of something good."
NarratorHe grabbed his glass and downed the remaining whiskey.
Dwight Walker"Now it starts to taste better!"
Dwight Walker"Let's go upstairs, Mr. Rayne and join the party, Mr. Rayne."
Dwight Walker"I have a few more dance moves to show off. Unlike Sordland, I don't dance alone."

■ Personal Monica Final TalkT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorIt was late at night when I exited Serge's car, walked through the gates of the presidential complex and unlocked my front door.
NarratorMonica and I weren't speaking much at all these days. I'd been spending more and more time in the office, just to avoid the chilly atmosphere that awaited me at home.
NarratorThe lights in the kitchen were still on. I walked in to find Monica sitting alone at the table, pouring the dregs of a bottle of Kebij into her glass.
Monica"Well, if it isn't the savior of Sordish women! Our knight in shining armor!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:37:47 p19529 / c332
  • ▶"At your service, ma'am."

Monica"This would never have happened if you'd supported us."

Monica"Oh, have I ever."

Monica"I don't think I can do this anymore. I'm calling Evelyn's divorce attorney."

Monica"I couldn't care less about your so-called progress. You failed on the issue that matters most to me."

CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:42:23 p19624 / c673

NarratorMonica gave me the faintest of nods.
Monica"Can-- can you find somewhere else to sleep tonight? I need to be alone for a little while."
NarratorI nodded numbly. A short time later, bag in hand, I called Serge and asked him to take me to the closest hotel.
NarratorAs we were driving away, I took a look back at the house where my wife and daughter were sleeping. I hoped I hadn't lost them forever.

NarratorThe sun was starting to peek through the blinds of my suite in The Grand Chariot Hotel in Anrica.

DeepSeek-R1"It is time, my fellow Sords. A new dawn is upon us and it begins right here and right now!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:55:19 p20002 / c292
  • ▶"So remember: Vote Rayne '57!"
  • ·"A Morgna wes core!"
  • ·"It's time to get back on board the Rayne Train!"
Encouraging a vote is the most direct way to rally support for re-election.

NarratorMy re-election campaign had officially begun.
NarratorI stepped down from the podium.

■ Economic Overview And ActionT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorI arrived at the Ministry of Economy and proceeded upstairs. An assistant waiting by the door to the meeting room took my jacket.
NarratorI opened the door and entered the largest boardroom in the building. Symon was sitting alone with a glass of water and his documents laid out before him.
NarratorHe stood up when I entered and approached me. After a quick handshake I took a seat.
Symon Holl"Good afternoon, Mr. President."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:56:12 p19633 / c126
  • ·“Good afternoon.”
  • ▶“How is it going?”
  • ·“Good to see you.”
Asking how things are going shows interest in the current state of affairs and invites an update.
DeepSeek-R1"How is it going?"
Symon Holl"I'm pretty swamped with work. Barely getting any time with the family."
NarratorThe glee in his voice was barely disguised.
NarratorA knock was heard on the door and Petr entered the room.
Petr Vectern"I hope I'm not late."
Petr Vectern"Did you start yet?"
Symon Holl"Mr. Vice President. No, not yet."
Symon Holl"Now that everyone is here... let's begin."
Symon Holl"As you all know, we're here to review the economic progress we've made over the past term and decide how to proceed."
Symon Holl"I will brief you quickly on what we have done so far. Just as a refresher."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:56:58 p19616 / c399
  • ▶“Very well. Let's hear it."
  • ·Gesture for him to begin.
Anton Rayne should acknowledge the need for a detailed briefing to address the severe economic downturn.
DeepSeek-R1"Very well. Let's hear it."
NarratorHe gathered the documents in the pile in front of him and adjusted his glasses.
NarratorFrom the pile, he produced two booklets and handed them to us.
Symon Holl"Our extra focus on the economy has resulted in some small-scale positive growth. The extra resources have been a boon to my ministry."
Symon Holl"It would be great if we followed up on this focus during our next term. Let's take a deep-dive into the details now."
Symon Holl"Please turn to page 5."
Turn to page 5.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 5.
Symon Holl"Let's start off with our economy plan."
Symon Holl"I would like to say we are on track, but unfortunately, I cannot. Our decisions so far have not been in line with our initial intention for a planned economy."
Symon Holl"It looks like we are going for a mixed approach instead, something between a planned and a market economy."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:57:39 p19664 / c310
  • ▶“I don't see a problem with that. It is sometimes necessary and justified to deviate from a plan.”
  • ·“It is unfortunate that we could not follow through. I blame the circumstances."
  • ·“I know. There is no need to rub it in, Symon.”
A pragmatic approach is needed given the circumstances.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't see a problem with that. It is sometimes necessary and justified to deviate from a plan."
Petr Vectern"I agree. Plans change all the time."
Symon Holl"Just pointing out the facts here."
Symon Holl"Let's move on with the report."
Symon Holl"Please turn to page 16."
Turn to page 16.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 16, titled "Status of Trade and the Effects of Foreign Policy".
Symon Holl"I am happy to say that our trade has increased since the beginning of our term."
Symon Holl"We are on the right path. More trade means a more vibrant economy, which means unbridled economic development."
Symon Holl"Please turn to page 17."
Turn to page 17.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 17.
Symon Holl"In our attempt to repair Sordland's economic system, we went for a mixed approach. We tried to cater to the needs of both businesses and Sordish citizens."
Symon Holl"It's been a delicate balance."
Symon Holl"Thankfully, living standards are steadily increasing. We've been receiving fewer and fewer complaints about our welfare systems."
Petr Vectern"That's great. For our election prospects, I mean."
Symon Holl"Exactly."
Symon Holl"Meanwhile, businesses are enjoying a lower tax rate compared to the start of our term."
Symon Holl"Now, to page 34."
Turn to page 34.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 34.
Symon Holl"Our production rate has gone down since the beginning of the term. We're at a low point right now."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:58:19 p19573 / c237
  • ·“Whose fault is that? Someone must be to blame.”
  • ▶“Then we need to fix it as soon as possible. I need everyone on this next term.”
  • ·“We had to make sacrifices and cuts.”
We need to address the economic downturn urgently and demonstrate strong leadership by mobilizing efforts for recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"Then we need to fix it as soon as possible. I need everyone on this next term."
Petr Vectern"Absolutely."
Symon Holl"I'll note that down."
Symon Holl"Moving on to logistics...."
Symon Holl"We have an infrastructure problem, and the economy won't truly flourish unless we solve it."
Symon Holl"We need to make new roads and improve the existing ones. People and businesses alike have been complaining."
Symon Holl"Smoother commutes, easier access and faster delivery times would help everyone out."
Symon Holl"We are very close to the end. I want to talk about a few numbers. Please turn to page 57. I have prepared a chart."
Turn to page 57.
DeepSeek-R1I turned to page 57.
Symon Holl"Our inflation rate is currently at a staggering 49%, way up from 10%. It might be the highest in Sordland's history."
Symon Holl"The Sordish ren has been wildly fluctuating as a result, and consumer prices have gone up."
Petr Vectern"Which means that Sordish citizens are angry at us."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:58:58 p19639 / c338
  • ·“Rightfully so. We have failed them. We need to take responsibility.”
  • ·“If we are not re-elected, we can't help them.”
  • ▶“We need to fix this. There is nothing more important than the wellbeing of our citizens.”
Fixing the economy is the most crucial step to regain public trust and ensure long-term stability.
DeepSeek-R1"We need to fix this. There is nothing more important than the well-being of our citizens."
Symon Holl"Agreed."
Symon Holl"Mr. President. Do you have any questions so far?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R102:59:34 p19633 / c312
  • ▶“What is our debt situation?”
  • ·“How are we doing with unemployment?”
  • ·“What are the latest numbers on our GDP?”
  • ·“No. Let's move on.”
Understanding the debt situation is crucial given the high inflation and potential economic instability.
DeepSeek-R1"What is our debt situation?"
Symon Holl"Our debt is at 513 billion SR, way more than the 427 billion SR at the beginning of our term."
Symon Holl"Basically, it has budged significantly down from where we started."
Symon Holl"I would still consider this as a economic loss as we inherited a recession. We essentially increased the possibility of an uncontrollable debt spiral."
Symon Holl"Due to our reckless spending in the first term, I am sorry to say that we have entered a debt crisis. According to my calculation, we won't be able to pay up our government debt without the assistance of a third party."
Symon Holl"It has even disrupted fundings to our welfare plans. Some construction projects and programs needed to be put on hold because of that."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:00:15 p19658 / c424
  • ·“How are we doing with unemployment?”
  • ·“What are the latest numbers on our GDP?”
  • ▶“Those are all the questions I had.”
Given the critical economic situation discussed, it's efficient to move towards actionable steps rather than additional questions.
DeepSeek-R1"Those are all the questions I had."
Symon Holl"Very well."
Symon Holl"I want to quickly highlight our budget expenditure."
Symon Holl"My numbers indicate that overall, we have been generously spending our government budget."
Symon Holl"Money that is not spent is just pieces of paper, so that's good for us."
Symon Holl"Now, going into the elections and hopefully a second term, we need to decide on the way forward."
Symon Holl"One thing is clear. We need a bigger budget."
Symon Holl"Unfortunately we are facing strong resistance from the party apparatus and state mechanisms."
Symon Holl"This means we won't be able to execute new opportunities to reduce our deficit."
Symon Holl"Now that that's out of way..."
Symon Holl"During our first term, we have faced many challenges in the midst of a recession."
Symon Holl"We have worked hard to fight against the odds but it was not enough."
Symon Holl"Violent unrests are still happening across the country. The tensions chased away potential investments that would have created more jobs and boosted our economy."
NarratorSymon paused to wipe his glasses. He swallowed before continuing.
Symon Holl"Mr. President, you may have surmised from the numbers I have given you that the economy has worsened considerably over the course of our administration."
Symon Holl"I wanted to prepare you for the truth. This is no longer a mere recession. We've entered a period that historians might reasonably refer to as the Sordish Great Depression."
Symon Holl"We have broken our promises and failed our people. My only hope is that future generations will be able to forgive us when they look back."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:00:55 p19572 / c294
  • ·“All of the blame falls on your shoulders as the Minister of Economy, Symon. You will resign before the next term.”
  • ▶“We have done all we could. Now is the time to look to the future and try to fix our mistakes.”
  • ·“This is all my fault. I made the decisions that led us here.”
Acknowledging past mistakes while focusing on future solutions maintains leadership unity and shows commitment to recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"We have done all we could. Now is the time to look to the future and try to fix our mistakes."
Petr Vectern"Yes! Some things can't be undone. But for the economy, at least, there's still hope."
Symon Holl"We will get through this together."
Symon Holl"That is all for our meeting today."
Symon Holl"Thank you for coming."
NarratorThe meeting dispersed. I hurried to the cafeteria to get a cup of coffee.

■ Decision Penalty Sordish DepressionT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY -9Danger
EventSordish Great Depression: The current economic climate in Sordland has taken a severe downturn, leading to what is now being referred to as the 'Sordish Great Depression'. The sharp rise in unemployment coupled with a steep decline in Gross Domestic Product displays the gravity of the situation. Prevailing concerns about government overspending have further aggravated the crisis, giving rise to financial instability.
Our nation will recover...

■ Television InterviewT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorThe makeup crew had just finished their preparations for the interview.
NarratorLucian stopped me on my way out to the balcony of the Blue Mansion, where the filming crew was standing ready.
Lucian Galade"Are you ready for the interview, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:01:56 p19489 / c548

  • ▶"Honestly, I'm not sure if this was a good idea."

Lucian Galade"Trust me, sir. I have done my best to make this happen. This is very important for our administration."
Lucian Galade"The crew is prepared to start filming. Mr. Sacker is excited to interview you."
Lucian Galade"As I explained before, it would be bad press to decline this offer. They are the most prestigious interview program in the world."
Lucian Galade"And they are from Kyrute. The country is known for its neutrality. I don't think there will be too much pressure."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:02:39 p19736 / c419
  • ▶"I hope so, Lucian."
  • ·"It's fine. I can handle it."
  • ·"You and your ideas... Let's get this over with."

DeepSeek-R1"I hope so, Lucian."
NarratorI walked to the balcony and sat on the seat prepared for me, directly across from Mr. Sacker.
Tuomas Sacker"Mr. President. Welcome to One-On-One."
Tuomas Sacker"Thank you so much for taking the time to speak with us."
Tuomas Sacker"Let us begin with the latest incident between Sordland and Rumburg."
Tuomas Sacker"An aircraft of the Sordish Air Forces was shot down by Rumburgian anti-air guns a few weeks ago. Officials from the Rumburgian government proclaimed that the aircraft was flying inside the airspace of Rumburg."
Tuomas Sacker"Your government has been silent apart from a few statements which claimed that your plane was indeed in Sordish airspace when it was shot down. Do you believe the Rumburgian forces initiated an intentional attack?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:03:22 p19900 / c456
  • ·"We have reason to believe that, yes. Rumburg has become increasingly aggressive over the last years."
  • ▶"I don't want to think that they attacked us intentionally. It must have been a mistake."
  • ·"Is that even a question? Rumburg is a threat to the whole region. This was an act of war."
To avoid escalating tensions and maintain a focus on domestic stability and economic recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't want to think that they attacked us intentionally. It must have been a mistake."
Tuomas Sacker"Does that mean your side is willing to have diplomatic talks with Rumburg?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:04:00 p19895 / c341
  • ▶"Of course. Rumburg is our neighbor, and we want to have good relations with all of our neighbors."
  • ·"If they're willing to, yes."
Emphasizing diplomacy aligns with avoiding conflict and maintaining stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Of course. Rumburg is our neighbor, and we want to have good relations with all of our neighbors."
Tuomas Sacker"Do you believe Queen Livingston shares the same sentiment?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:04:39 p20012 / c368
  • ·"I hope she does. We're willing to work together to achieve peace in Eastern Merkopa."
  • ·"Unfortunately, I doubt that. She has been increasingly hostile against our country since I took office."
  • ▶"I can't say that for sure. We'll have to ask the Queen herself."
Choosing to remain diplomatic and open avoids escalating tensions and maintains the possibility of peaceful dialogue.
DeepSeek-R1"I can't say that for sure. We'll have to ask the Queen herself."
Tuomas Sacker"This week, both the Arcasian and United Contanan governments have condemned the airplane incident."
Tuomas Sacker"Do you think they would be willing to serve as mediators in the case of a broader conflict between your countries?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:05:16 p20073 / c307

I think Arcasia and Contana would get involved but not necessarily as mediators.

NarratorHe stopped for a moment and took a brief look at his papers.
Tuomas Sacker"Let's continue on that note."
Tuomas Sacker"I want to expand a little more on your foreign policy."
Tuomas Sacker"Let's talk about the ongoing conflict around Heljiland Island."
Tuomas Sacker"The Valgslandian navy has been increasingly active in the Markian Sea. As you know, they've been harassing Agnolian ships for months now."
Tuomas Sacker"I believe Sordland stands with Agnolia on this matter. In fact, despite condemnation from Valgslandian leader Emmerich Hegel, you recognized Heljiland as Agnolian land."
Tuomas Sacker"What do you think about Valgsland's actions and their claims on the island?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:06:00 p20224 / c426

Expressing concern over Valgsland's actions while maintaining neutrality helps avoid entanglement in international disputes, preserving resources for domestic priorities.

Tuomas Sacker"Do you think the hostilities between Agnolia and Valgsland might erupt into a hot war?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:06:43 p20286 / c413

  • ·"It is definitely a possibility. Therefore, all nations of Eastern Merkopa should do their best to calm the situation."

Avoiding conflict and focusing on domestic issues is crucial for re-election.
DeepSeek-R1"I don't think it would go that far. Both sides are able to see reason."
Tuomas Sacker"Would Sordland also get involved if the situation gets out of control?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:07:26 p20248 / c423

  • ·"I hope the situation won't come to that."

Avoiding international entanglements focuses domestic efforts on economic recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"No. Sordland has no intention of intervening in other nations' conflicts."
Tuomas Sacker"Okay, let's move on to another topic."
Tuomas Sacker"Last year, a huge conflict took place close to your borders with Wehlen."
Tuomas Sacker"When the Wehzek forces partook in a massacre of Bludish civilians under the guise of eliminating the Bludish Freedom Front..."
Tuomas Sacker"...Sordland decided to watch the atrocities take place."
Tuomas Sacker"In fact, you not only closed the borders to the refugees fleeing from a massacre, your border guards worked in cooperation with the Wehzek forces."
Tuomas Sacker"Will you accept responsibility of thousands of lives that were lost in Wehlen?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:08:12 p20188 / c276

  • ▶"It breaks my heart to see so many people die in Wehlen. Could we have done something? Definitely. But the conflict had nothing to do with us."
Expressing regret without admitting direct involvement helps maintain a diplomatic stance while acknowledging the tragedy.
DeepSeek-R1"It breaks my heart to see so many people die in Wehlen. Could we have done something? Definitely. But the conflict had nothing to do with us."
Tuomas Sacker"Many have criticized Sordland for its role in this conflict. Do you not accept any of the criticisms?"

Tuomas Sacker"But how about-"
NarratorOn the gesture of the director, he stopped before finishing his sentence.
Tuomas Sacker"Well... It seems that's all the time we have for today."

■ Lileas DiscussionT09

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorThe historic Graf Manor lay on the outskirts of Arvory, at the end of a long, winding drive. Nearly two centuries old, it was as formidable as Lileas herself.
NarratorLileas had invited me here for a private dinner, just the two of us.
NarratorI wasn't sure of her motivations, but was happy to have an excuse to escape the chaos back in Holsord.
NarratorSerge dropped me off at the door.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:11:14 p19782 / c546
  • ▶Knock.
  • ·Go inside without knocking.
Knocking shows respect for Lileas's privacy and maintains professionalism.
DeepSeek-R1I used the heavy brass door knocker to signal my arrival. The butler answered and showed me into the parlor.
NarratorGas lanterns lent the wood-paneled room a cosy orange glow. Lileas was sitting on the sofa. Above her hung a nearly life-sized portrait of a sallow, unhappy-looking young woman in a lace ball gown.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:11:56 p19813 / c298
  • ·"Is that you?"
  • ·"Is that your mother?"
  • ▶"Is that your grandmother?"
The painting likely depicts an ancestor, making option 2 the most fitting.
DeepSeek-R1"Is that your grandmother?"
Lileas Graf"My mother, actually."
Lileas Graf"I was her only child, you know. Giving birth to me left her medically unable to conceive."
Lileas Graf"My father never could forgive her for not bearing him a son. And she never forgave me."
NarratorShe took a measured sip of one of the two glasses of sparkling wine she was holding, and handed me the other.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:12:48 p19865 / c701
  • ·"Sounds like a painful childhood."
  • ▶"Get to the point, Lileas. Why did you invite me here?"
To address Lileas Graf's cryptic remarks and focus on the matter at hand.
DeepSeek-R1"Get to the point, Lileas. Why did you invite me here?"
Lileas Graf"I couldn't let you go a full term without having one of my home-cooked suppers."
NarratorThe butler silently stepped into the parlor and rang a silver bell.
Lileas Graf"Ah. Speaking of which."
NarratorShe led me into the dining room, where a glittering chandelier hung from the high ceiling. Beneath it was a long table set for two. A woman in an apron and chef's hat, Wehzek by her appearance, ceremoniously presented a roast rack of venison.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:13:26 p19905 / c312
  • ▶Start eating.
  • ·"Smells delicious."
  • ·"A home-cooked supper, huh?"
I need to focus on recovery, so starting to eat is the best option.
DeepSeek-R1I grabbed my fork.
Lileas Graf"Aren't you forgetting something?"
NarratorLileas bowed her head and said a quick prayer, then motioned for me to dig in.
NarratorIt was some of the best meat I'd ever had. We ate in silence for a while, then Lileas pensively laid down her cutlery.
Lileas Graf"Anton, I want you to know that I have nothing but respect for you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:14:08 p19906 / c412

  • ·"I respect you too."
  • ▶"But?"
I want to prompt Lileas to clarify her intentions and motivations for inviting me.
DeepSeek-R1"But?"
Lileas Graf"But, given certain developments that have occurred over the course of your term, I believe you are no longer fit to lead the United Sordland Party."
NarratorLileas took a deep breath.
Lileas Graf"I wanted you to be the first to know that I myself will be running against you at the Party Congress."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:14:53 p19933 / c511

  • ·"I forbid you from doing that."

  • ▶"I suspected that might happen."
A calm and composed response acknowledging her decision would be more effective than an emotional outburst or confrontation.
DeepSeek-R1"I suspected that might happen."
Lileas Graf"Of course you did. You aren't stupid."
Lileas Graf"Did you think I'd sit by as you made a mockery out of this country's constitution?"
Lileas Graf"The very constitution that is keeping this nation safe from internal and external threats."
Lileas Graf"You're a disgrace to the good name of Tarquin Soll."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:15:40 p20104 / c574

  • ·"Did you poison my food?"
  • ▶"How much money will it take for you to stay on my side?"

DeepSeek-R1"How much money will it take for you to stay on my side?"
NarratorLileas gave a short laugh and gestured at our grand surroundings.
Lileas Graf"Do I look like I need money?"
Lileas Graf"I'm doing you a courtesy tonight. I didn't want to surprise you at the Congress."
Lileas Graf"And I wanted to give you time to consider backing me."

Lileas Graf"I'll have the butler show you out."
NarratorShe stayed seated as her butler gently led me outside.
NarratorAs Serge and I drove away, I looked in the rearview mirror and saw her silhouette in the doorway.
NarratorSo I was being challenged from within my own party. At least Lileas wouldn't pose much of a threat.
NarratorOr so I hoped.

TURN 10

■ FCAnrica MatchT10

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorToday, the management of FC Anrica had invited me to attend a pivotal football match. At Lucian's urging, I accepted.
NarratorThe team was representing Sordland in the international arena. According to Lucian, it would have been un-presidential not to put in an appearance.
NarratorFC Anrica was finally in the Merkopan Champions Cup -- the most prestigious football tournament in the world. For the first time ever, a Sordish team had managed to qualify for the Knockout phase.
NarratorMillions of Sordish people were waiting to watch FC Anrica compete against the legendary Rizian team Real Montaklar. Despite all odds being stacked against the team, the mood was full of hope.
NarratorA win today would bring Anrica to the quarter finals, a feat that no Sordish team had ever managed to achieve.
NarratorAnd of course, all presidents of Sordland -- current and former -- were invited to watch the historic competition.
NarratorI was told that both Tarquin Soll and Ewald Alphonso would be joining me in the lounge reserved for us.
Lucian Galade"How are you feeling, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:18:05 p20327 / c402
  • ·"I'm fine, Lucian. Thank you."

NarratorWe made our way towards the Maroon Stadium. The arena was situated in one of the most crowded quarters of Anrica, surrounded by dense urban development.
NarratorAs we wound our way through the streets, I noticed the name of Tarquin Soll everywhere. Since I'd last been here, markets, restaurants and even pubs named after the Colonel had all popped up.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:18:46 p20242 / c294
  • ▶"Why is Anrica so crazy about Soll all of a sudden?"
  • ·"Am I imagining things, or did we just pass a pub called 'Alco-Soll'?"

DeepSeek-R1"Why is Anrica so crazy about Soll all of a sudden?"
Lucian Galade"I noticed it as well."
Lucian Galade"I've heard it is part of an ad campaign for Anrica. An attempt to draw tourists, or at least Sollist ones, to the city. Soll's legacy still lives on for millions, after all."
NarratorLucian's pocket radio crackled. After responding, he turned to me.
Lucian Galade"Sir. The security gave us the clear and informed us that Tarquin Soll has already arrived at the lounge."
Lucian Galade"Good luck. I know he's quite the imposing presence."
Lucian Galade"Please follow the guards to the top floor. You will be seated in the VIP lounge."

DeepSeek-R1"That's a pity. I'll save you some snacks."
NarratorI followed the security. We entered beneath a giant sign that read: "Welcome to the Hall of Martyrs."
NarratorThe corridors inside were painted almost completely in maroon. Adorning them were various depictions of the Sordish goat and numerous jerseys, awards and team photographs.
NarratorOn our way up, I was greeted by the president of the club. Beaming with pride, he immediately shook my hand.
NarratorBehind him, Mayor Curtan Leste and Gus Manger were waiting to greet me as well. All of them had FC Anrica pins attached to their collars.
Curtan Leste"Welcome to Anrica, Mr. President."
Curtan Leste"You are always welcome in Anrica, and it is great to see you show support in person."
Curtan Leste"Thanks for being here. This match means a lot for all of us Anricans."
Gus Manger"And all of Sordland! Never before has a Sordish team reached this stage in the Merkopan Cup."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R103:20:25 p20673 / c520
  • ·"It is great to be here in Anrica."
  • ▶"I have high hopes for today. This is a big achievement for Sordish football."
  • ·"Honestly, I don't know much about football. But I'm here for the players and for my country."
Expressing high hopes for the match highlights national pride and unity, which is crucial given the economic struggles.
DeepSeek-R1"I have high hopes for today. This is a big achievement for Sordish football."
Gus Manger"Football is of rapidly expanding importance in Sordland and beyond. It's good that we see the opportunity in it."
NarratorThe president of the club walked in, handed us gift bags full of FC Anrica merchandise and gave a speech stressing the importance of today's match.
NarratorAfter delivering his best wishes, he kindly pointed us towards the lounge, which was surrounded by heavily armed men.
NarratorOnce I finally found myself inside, I was greeted by an amazing view of the entire pitch through floor-to-ceiling windows.
NarratorTarquin Soll was already seated on the leather sofa, casting his hawk-like gaze out onto the field. He did not react when I entered.

A neutral yet slightly passive-aggressive comment acknowledging their past conflicts without escalating tension.
DeepSeek-R1"We seem to be crossing each other's paths on the oddest of occasions."
NarratorTarquin Soll leaned on his cane and turned his head to look at me.
Tarquin Soll"Movies and football. Well, a retired man like me has earned the right to a little leisure. You, though..."
NarratorHe smiled at me and gestured at the vacant spot beside him.
Tarquin Soll"Please. Join me."
NarratorI approached the sofa and sat next to him.
Tarquin Soll"I am surprised that you have the audacity to face me after the way our last meeting ended."
Tarquin Soll"I suppose that demands some respect."

Tarquin Soll"You have strayed from the values of the party."
Tarquin Soll"I am warning you, Anton. Don't you dare go too far."

NarratorAs the players of Real Montaklar lined up in the middle of the field, FC Anrica fans started chanting: "We'll eat you whole for Colonel Soll!"
NarratorRight after the referee signalled the start of the match, the door behind us closed with a thud.
Ewald Alphonso"Well, Colonel? How do you feel about being Anrica's new poster boy?"
Ewald Alphonso"It looks like it's working well for the city. And if you moved back home, I'm sure you'd get free meals and drinks everywhere you went."
NarratorEwald Alphonso quickly walked up to the sofa, shook our hands and sat next to us, smiling as he did so.
Tarquin Soll"Ha. You know, I never had a strong connection to this place. Mr. Leste should have asked my permission before using my name to market his city."
Ewald Alphonso"Oh, it was actually Gus Manger's idea."
Tarquin Soll"Why am I not surprised? He's full of ideas, your Mr. Manger, except when it comes to the job he's actually supposed to be doing."
Ewald Alphonso"No need to be so dismissive. Gus has proven himself time and again. His work rejuvenated Sordish industries. And without him, Anrica wouldn't be the city it is today."

NarratorAlphonso sighed.
Ewald Alphonso"I don't intend to take on the role of his personal spokesman."
Ewald Alphonso"You know..."
Ewald Alphonso"I was in Rizia a few days ago to meet with our energy partners."
Ewald Alphonso"I was happy to find out that the royals over there appear to be in favor of pursuing friendlier relations with Sordland."
Ewald Alphonso"I still don't think they will do anything to harm their connection with Rumburg, but cooperating with them was the right call."
Tarquin Soll"You're fond of the monarchists now, Ewald? They must be filling your pockets with Rizian gold."
Ewald Alphonso"For Dast's sake, Mr. Soll, try to see the positive in what I do. For once."
Tarquin Soll"How about you claim responsibility for once? It is your fault that a giant like Gasom was brought to the brink of collapse."
Ewald Alphonso"I've done ever-
Tarquin Soll"Admit it. It was too much of a responsibility for you. A man with your profit-focused mentality should have never been given the keys to such a vital sector."

NarratorFrom that moment on, we turned our complete attention to the match in front of us.
NarratorThe Rizian players kept dominating the field.
NarratorThe referee blew the whistle. It was halftime.
Ewald Alphonso"That was rough."
NarratorWe heard a noise from above us. A zeppelin was flying over the stadium with a giant poster hanging beneath it.
NarratorI noticed that it was an advertisement for the Alphonso Foundation. At the center of the poster stood Ewald Alphonso, portrayed with a warm smile and a twinkle in his eye.
NarratorIn his outstretched hand, he held a whimsical compass, glowing with a soft golden light. Surrounding him were people from all walks of life and cultures.
NarratorThe tagline, written in elegant and uplifting typography, was prominently displayed at the bottom: "Alphonso Foundation: Bringing Hope To Those In Need, One Act of Kindness at a Time."

NarratorJust then, the speakers in the stadium started playing a sound recording in full volume.
Narrator"Ladies and gentlemen, we bring you a message from the Alphonso Foundation, dedicated to spreading kindness and hope to the less fortunate."
Narrator"Picture a Sordland where compassion shines brighter than the stars in the night sky, where the power of generosity uplifts those who need it most. That Sordland can exist, thanks to the Alphonso Foundation."
Narrator"Led by the charismatic and esteemed President Ewald Alphonso, this foundation is on a mission to make a difference in the lives of our fellow citizens, one act of kindness at a time."
Tarquin Soll"You call this kindness?"
Narrator"Every Sordish Ren you donate to the Alphonso Foundation helps us provide shelter, food, and a chance for a brighter future to those without a home, without hope."
Narrator"Thanks to the donations of President Ewald Alphonso and thousands of Sordish citizens, the Alphonso Foundation has already lifted hundreds out of homelessness."
Tarquin Soll"A couple of hundred people? Congratulations to you both. This is how Sordland will eradicate poverty."
Narrator"Won't you join us in this noble endeavor? Just call the number, 1-600-ALPHONSO, to make your contribution today. Your support will help us extend a helping hand to those in need."
NarratorThe recording ended with a few seconds of upbeat jazzy background music that slowly faded out.

Tarquin Soll"You know I never called myself a hero, Ewald. It was my supporters who portrayed me as one. Don't spin the truth."

Ewald Alphonso"YES!"

Ewald Alphonso"Damn. That was close."
NarratorHe suddenly pointed to a group of people who were holding signs in the front row.

NarratorWith only a few minutes left on the clock, it looked as though the match would end with a 1-0 loss for FC Anrica.

Ewald Alphonso"I knew it."

■ Alliance Of NationsT10

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorI, Deivid Wisci and the rest of the Sordish delegation to the Alliance of Nations arrived in Kyrute. The local police escorted us to the Alliance's headquarters in downtown Konstantium, immediately recognizable by its shining dome.
NarratorOn entering the building, we were met by a parliamentary guide who briefed us about the procedures. After a strict security screening, the staff ushered us to the third floor.
NarratorWe entered the massive circular assembly hall and took our designated seats. Deivid sat next to me.
Deivid Wisci"All of the world's most powerful people under one gleaming dome."

  • ·"I'm glad to be among them."

Deivid Wisci"It is truly astounding. Humanity has come far."
Deivid Wisci"How are you feeling, Anton? Being the subject of so much media attention must take a toll on you and the family."

Ephraim Nines"It is my great pleasure to invite the Prime Minister of Lespia to address the assembly."
NarratorPatricio Alvarez stood and walked up to the podium as the assembly applauded.
Patricio Alvarez"Mr. President, it is my pleasure to return to this podium."

Deivid Wisci"Let's see how crazy this year's session will get."

Patricio Alvarez"When I last spoke here, the message I gave was of encouragement and support to this organization and the vital role it plays."
Patricio Alvarez"Of all the challenges Eastern Merkopa and the whole world has faced since then, one has grown larger and more imminent than any other."
Patricio Alvarez"The threat of the so-called 'socialist' states and the actions taken by their authoritarian rulers with no regard to international law."

Patricio Alvarez"...Today, the need to come together as the leaders of the free world is more important than ever."
Patricio Alvarez"The CSP, under the leadership of United Contana, has been fuelling Valgsland's military-industrial complex and its expansionist aims in Eastern Merkopa."
Patricio Alvarez"The world cannot turn a blind eye to the increasing naval presence of Valgsland in the Markian Sea."
Patricio Alvarez"We hereby call for the immediate withdrawal of all naval vessels with any offensive capability from international waters."
NarratorA lot of noise came from the assembly hall in response. Most of the Western Merkopan delegates were clapping, while others either shouted in protest or sat in stony silence.
Patricio Alvarez"Let us also not forget about the recent events that transpired in an another state with socialist teachings. A state that wages war against its own innocent citizens. Wehlen."

Patricio Alvarez"Wehlen violated our past treaties and mobilized their armed forces, deploying them close to our borders and authorizing them to partake in massacres."
Patricio Alvarez"Lespia was affected not only by the large wave of refugees coming from Wehlen, but by the subsequent government seizure of all of our companies that were active in the country."
Patricio Alvarez"We hereby call the member states to action against Wehlen and its rogue government."
Patricio Alvarez"Thank you."

NarratorWiktor turned to President Nines and bowed slightly.
Wiktor Smolak"Mr. President."
NarratorHe then turned back at us and stared out into the hall for a few seconds.
Wiktor Smolak"Fellow delegates."
Wiktor Smolak"It is true that the world today faces a multitude of challenges."
Wiktor Smolak"In the midst of these challenges, the world's most powerful leaders gather here in Kyrute."
Wiktor Smolak"And what do they do? They make threats. Promote hate and spread lies."
Wiktor Smolak"Is this the Alliance of Nations we all want? Where is the cooperation and friendship? Where is the unity?"
Wiktor Smolak"I want to remind you of the wars that the West fought on our soil... Of the foreign troops who occupied our country and seized our valuable resources."
Wiktor Smolak"I want to remind you how they stole the oil from our sacred soil. And they did not know when to stop."
Wiktor Smolak"The ATO soldiers left but their interference never ceased. They financed armed rebel groups whose only purpose is to create terror inside our country."
Wiktor Smolak"So, fellow delegates! Don't you dare speak about Wehlen before you acknowledge your involvement in our civil war... Before you acknowledge your own dark past."

Deivid Wisci"What did you expect, Mr. President?"
Deivid Wisci"It's President Smolak..."
Wiktor Smolak"As is well known, after the tremendous tragedy of the Wehlen Civil War, Arcasia made a deal with United Contana, where they agreed upon the withdrawal of their troops along with the removal of half of our army."
Wiktor Smolak"Not only did they weaken us, we were left alone in facing the genocidal Bludish Freedom Front, which the ATO funds together with Rumburg."
Wiktor Smolak"I will show you some of the evidence of foreign interference."
NarratorHe picked up a large stack of papers and waved them back and forth.
Wiktor Smolak"I have clear evidence of direct aid from Rumburg to the Bludish Freedom Front. They smuggled tons of KA-74s in Wehlen. They smuggled in their spies. Smuggled in mercenary commanders."

DeepSeek-R1I kept listening.
Wiktor Smolak"These documents will be published everywhere. Copies have already been sent to the Investigative Affairs of the Alliance of Nations."
Wiktor Smolak"I expect this organization to do its task and investigate this properly, instead of leaving Wehlen behind as they have always done."
Wiktor Smolak"Coming back to the claims of the Lespian representative... First, there is no evidence of civilian massacres by the Wehzek forces, nor so-called purges of minorities."
Wiktor Smolak"Second, the operation was limited only to the militant BFF, not the Bluds in general."
Wiktor Smolak"Third, we know your government is also implicated in the funding of the BFF. And that your oil companies aided their militants during the operation."
Wiktor Smolak"Fourth, as the state, we only took what was ours. The companies the delegate spoke of were not only aiding militants, they were brought to Wehlen by force."
Wiktor Smolak"Know that Wehlen will no longer recognize the right of any other country to determine what type of weapons we are permitted to possess or what type of policies we follow within our borders."
Wiktor Smolak"Wehlen stands stronger and more united than ever. Our people have spoken. The enemies inside our borders are crushed."
Wiktor Smolak"Now, I ask every delegate here to read our report on the connections of the West with the armed militants in Eastern Merkopa. And I ask the Alliance of Nations to do what's right."
Wiktor Smolak"Wehlen only wants peace. Our people want peace. They are counting on the support and understanding of all the nations of the world."
Wiktor Smolak"Their proclamation is, 'Free Wehlen or Death'."
NarratorThe assembly roared, with particularly loud applause coming from the seats of Rikan nations.

Maartin Van Hoorten"Mr. President. Fellow delegates of the Assembly, and friends."
Maartin Van Hoorten"It is an honor to be with you today."
Maartin Van Hoorten"I wish we could meet under different circumstances."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Once again, the CSP nations -- and in particular Valgsland -- have shown us what they're willing to do to achieve their political aims."
Maartin Van Hoorten"I must first thank the delegates from Sordland for being brave enough to recognize our Heljiland, when the rest of the world left us alone."
Maartin Van Hoorten"We were one of the first countries to propose proper cooperation among Eastern Merkopan nations."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Yet when we were threatened by Valgslandian expansionism, nobody came to our side."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Although Mr. Alvarez voiced his indirect support for us earlier in today's session, I can't keep myself from wondering where he was when our fleets were under attack by the very *socialists* he spoke about."
Maartin Van Hoorten"So, here I am, asking for the support of the fellow member states of the Alliance of Nations. Agnolia is under attack by Valgsland."

NarratorNines repeatedly banged his gavel.
Ephraim Nines"Order!"

Ephraim Nines"Silence, Chancellor. Mr. Prime Minister, please continue."
Maartin Van Hoorten"As you all know, our drilling ships, oil tankers and even fishing vessels have been disappearing in the Markian Sea. Captured or sunk by Valgsland."
Maartin Van Hoorten"And the Valgslandian fleet is patrolling the seas around Heljiland with complete disregard to international laws."
Maartin Van Hoorten"I call on the Alliance of Nations to find a resolution to this never-ending conflict that Valgsland created. We demand the immediate withdrawal of all naval forces from our territorial waters."
Maartin Van Hoorten"Malenyevist terror surrounds us on all sides."
Maartin Van Hoorten"I call the democratic world to action. Against the tyranny of Malenyevism, authoritarianism, militarism and extremism."

DeepSeek-R1I watched Hegel and waited for him to continue.
Deivid Wisci"Here we go..."
Emmerich Hegel"First of all, I want to extend a due welcome to the new states from Rika that have joined this organization."
Emmerich Hegel"After fighting for years against colonialism and imperialism, they more than earned our membership vote. I am overjoyed to see them finally represented in this assembly."
Emmerich Hegel"As our numbers grow and more states are represented in this stage, we come ever closer to finding resolutions to the conflicts that plague us."
Emmerich Hegel"For hundreds of years, the dominant economies of Merkopa razed, pillaged and exploited the continents of Rika and Xina."
Emmerich Hegel"Sadly, Valgsland was also a part of this plunder in the years before the revolution, when it still clung to the idea of imperialism."
Emmerich Hegel"But when our masses found their voice, the old colonial powers and their new financiers turned against them."
Emmerich Hegel"These powers did whatever they could to hamper our revolutions. They did not want the people to be heard."
Emmerich Hegel"They are scared of us for showing the possibility of a better path. Of course they don't want us to succeed. We threaten the *productivity* that runs on the blood and sweat of their working class."
Emmerich Hegel"And now they have started labelling us the aggressors. Yet we all know it was the Arcasians who built up military bases all around Merkopa. It was the ATO that was first formed to fight against Malenyevism."

Emmerich Hegel"Valgsland holds the right to protect its territorial waters and trade from threats."
Emmerich Hegel"The delegates from Lespia and Agnolia brought up the Falenco Treaty."
Emmerich Hegel"But what were they doing when ATO forces patrolled the Antacean Sea? They were silent."
Emmerich Hegel"And what about Heljiland? How about the Valgs being killed by the Agnolian government because they protested against an appointed governor?"
Emmerich Hegel"Agnolia has no right to claim they are upholding liberty and democracy. And the ATO has no right to intervene in the matters between Agnolia and Valgsland."
Emmerich Hegel"Just as no country in the world has the right to determine what type of naval vessels we use, no country can force us not to defend our seas."
Emmerich Hegel"If there's a conflict the Alliance can resolve, let's start with the Arcasian base on Ravnos Island. Lespia has been housing Arcasian troops and missiles by our border for years and none of the member states even talked about it."
Emmerich Hegel"I also want the member states to look into the Arcasian bases in both Lespia and Sordland."
Emmerich Hegel"And if the Alliance of Nations truly claims to have the people's best interests at heart, they cannot stay silent about what the Agnolian government did in Heljiland. How they took away the rights of our people..."
Emmerich Hegel"Perhaps then, there can be proper dialogue between our nations, and the Alliance of Nations can finally demonstrate that it can serve its intended purpose."
Emmerich Hegel"But unfortunately, the Alliance of Nations remains all too willing to overlook such human rights violations."
Emmerich Hegel"However, the Valgslandian government is ready as always to fight the forces of oppression and colonial servitude on behalf of free people everywhere."

NarratorPresident Nines announced the next speaker: Queen Beatrice Livingston.
Beatrice Livingston"Dear President of the Assembly, dear delegates, dear leaders of the world."
Beatrice Livingston"Just as my fellow Eastern Merkopan delegates spoke about the troubles plaguing our continent..."
Beatrice Livingston"...I also have to bring attention to the plight of the people of the north."
Beatrice Livingston"The Alliance of Nations, the ATO, the CSP and even the OMEC have never supported our people when they've been through tragic atrocities."
Beatrice Livingston"Unlike many of the western countries here, we never received financial aid when natural disasters struck our lands."
Beatrice Livingston"The pain of our people was never recognized. Not even when they faced the threat of genocide by the hands of the Sordish kings..."
Beatrice Livingston"In light of all that, I implore this Alliance to take a closer look at the actions of our southern neighbors."
Beatrice Livingston"They took in traitors and gave them protection."
Beatrice Livingston"Their president himself came to power amid murder and unrest. Yet people from his administration had the guts to blame our government for inciting violence."
Beatrice Livingston"And they expect the world to believe their lies."

Beatrice Livingston"Sordland closed their borders to innocent Bludish people fleeing from being massacred by the Wehzek forces."
Beatrice Livingston"They even supported and cooperated with Wehlen in their ethnic war against the Bludish people."
Beatrice Livingston"I hereby invite all of you to determine the initiation of a proper intergovernmental investigation into Sordland."
Beatrice Livingston"If the Alliance continues to shirk its given duties, you can trust in Rumburg to take matters into her own hands."
Beatrice Livingston"It is also important to point out that Rumburg will not stand idly by if more Arcasian military are transferred to Eastern Merkopa."
Beatrice Livingston"I hereby condemn both the ATO and Sordland for allowing Arcasia to settle in our region."
Beatrice Livingston"Before I finish, I also want to say..."
Beatrice Livingston"Mr. Smolak. I'm looking forward to reading your report. I wonder what this so-called 'evidence' is all about."
Beatrice Livingston"Know that your slander does not work on us. Know that we are ready to die for our freedom. Know that our kingdom stands firm and strong."
Beatrice Livingston"Expect a grand awakening..."
Beatrice Livingston"Thank you."

CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:02:17 p21279 / c750
  • ·"The AN can no longer fail to recognize the machinations of the enemies of peace."
  • ·"We stand and always will stand, for the rights of the people of Sordland and Eastern Merkopa, just as those in other continents."
  • ▶"If the right actions are not taken, it will be quite evident that international relations cannot continue on such basis."
Emphasizing the need for international cooperation can secure support and aid crucial for Sordland's economy.
DeepSeek-R1"If the right actions are not taken, it will be quite evident that international relations cannot continue on such a basis."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:03:00 p21343 / c334
  • ▶"Long live the Alliance of Nations. Long live unity and peace."
  • ·"There cannot be any more procrastination or delay. The AN must adopt decisions according to the wishes of the people of the world."
  • ·"The people of Sordland and I are honored to have expressed our concerns before this noble assembly."
A respectful and unifying statement is the best approach to maintain peace.
DeepSeek-R1"Long live the Alliance of Nations. Long live unity and peace."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:03:48 p21299 / c549
  • ▶"Peace in Merkopa, peace in the world."
  • ·"A Morgna wes core!"
  • ·"Long live the democracies of the world!"
Choosing peace emphasizes diplomacy and cooperation, fitting the context of tense relations and aligning with the goal of maintaining neutrality while seeking international support for economic recovery.
DeepSeek-R1"Peace in Merkopa, peace in the world."
NarratorThere was some applause from the assembly.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:04:31 p21219 / c385
  • ▶"Thank you."
  • ·Raise your fist.
A simple thank you maintains decorum and avoids unnecessary confrontation.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you."
NarratorI left the podium.
NarratorI walked back to my seat. Deivid did not stop applauding until I sat down.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:05:11 p21232 / c271
  • ·"Thank you Deivid."
  • ▶"How was it?"
  • ·"Finally, it's over."
Engaging with Deivid's opinion can provide insight into his stance and support.
DeepSeek-R1"How was it?"
Deivid Wisci"It was not a bad speech, Mr. President. You did well."
NarratorPresident Nines announced the next speaker: Dwight Walker.
Dwight Walker"Mr. President, I first want to congratulate you on your election to this high office."
Dwight Walker"Mr. Secretary, delegates to the Alliance of Nations, ladies and gentlemen, fellow friends..."
Dwight Walker"We meet again on our quest to bring peace and light into this world. But even though some of the clouds have lifted, the world has not yet escaped the darkness."
Dwight Walker"The Contanan Security Pact is rapidly expanding its influence."
Dwight Walker"It is very troubling to see the democratic nations of Merkopa be threatened by those Malenyevist powers."
Dwight Walker"I refer, of course, to the presence of Valgslandian and Contanan fleets in the Markian Sea. They are harassing other nations and hampering international trade."
Dwight Walker"Is this not an attack against world peace? An attack against the free-trading nations of the world?"
Dwight Walker"Today, our obligations to world peace are being tested by United Contana and the Malenyevist bloc. If we fail to counter this threat..."
Dwight Walker"If we stay idle and allow them to surround us with their war machines and blockades..."
Dwight Walker"If the attackers do not face any consequences..."
Dwight Walker"Then this unstable peace we believed we had built will be the world's downfall, and posterity will point its finger at us for not stopping it."
NarratorEmmerich Hegel and the other Valgslandian delegates started making noise again.
NarratorThis time, Hegel had taken off his shoe and was banging the table with it.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:06:05 p21253 / c659
  • ·"What the heck?"
  • ·"I have no words. The chancellor has gone mad."
  • ▶Keep watching.
Keeping silent allows Anton to maintain composure and assess the situation strategically before responding.
DeepSeek-R1I kept watching.
NarratorDespite the noise Hegel made, President Walker continued his speech without pause.
Dwight Walker"Despite its claims, the CSP's actions will only bring about war."
Dwight Walker"Yet we still believe United Contana and Arcasia, together with their allies, can come to an understanding. For our mutual benefit and our desire to avoid the destruction of the world."
Dwight Walker"We believe that all the people of the world must be free to choose their own future. No country, be it Arcasia or United Contana, has the right to impose its values on others."
Dwight Walker"But I will say this to the leaders of United Contana... If either of us is to be secure, we need a peaceful cooperation. Not defense systems, forced blockades or shows of naval superiority."
Dwight Walker"If the Alliance of Nations cannot find a resolution to this conflict, the ATO remains ready and able to react."
NarratorHegel stood up with the shoe in his hand.
Emmerich Hegel"What is that supposed to mean? You're promoting war in the Alliance of Nations?"
Emmerich Hegel"Mr. President! This canno-"
NarratorNines slammed the gavel down.
Ephraim Nines"SILENCE!"
Dwight Walker"Mr. Chancellor, please let me finish. We did not interrupt you when you addressed the assembly."
Dwight Walker"Finally, I propose that the Alliance of Nations undertake the noble cause of helping the people of the world through rewriting and properly enforcing its Human Rights Declaration."
Dwight Walker"The Alliance of Nations cannot survive as a static organization. Its size and duties are increasing enormously. It should adapt to the changing world."
Dwight Walker"But peace and rights do not rest in the hands of this organization alone. They rely on the hearts of our people and the spirit of cooperation between our nations."
Dwight Walker"So, let it be known, my fellow inhabitants of this world... Let us take our stand and see if we can finally achieve a lasting peace."
Dwight Walker"Thank you."
NarratorMost of the assembly applauded his speech, the ATO delegates in particular.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:06:56 p21368 / c509
  • ·"That speech had very dangerous connotations to it."
  • ▶Applaud his speech.
  • ·Don't react.
Aligning with Dwight Walker's speech supports Sordland's foreign policy goals and strengthens ties with ATO.
DeepSeek-R1I applauded.
NarratorThe applause continued for some time. Walker shook hands with various delegates on his way back to his seat.
NarratorPresident Nines spoke into the microphone once more.
Ephraim Nines"We have one final speaker before the break. Leon Malenyev of United Contana."
NarratorMalenyev slowly walked to the rostrum in his iconic suit. All of the CSP delegates stood up and applauded him.
Leon Malenyev"Honorable chairman and fellow delegates to the Alliance of Nations."
Leon Malenyev"Here we are again at the most esteemed meeting of state representatives in the world."
Leon Malenyev"However, increasingly, we are being threatened, disregarded, attacked or ignored in this assembly."
Leon Malenyev"The representative of Arcasia has just shown this unfriendly attitude towards us yet again."
Leon Malenyev"Arcasia would have you believe we are standing in the way of lasting peace. Yet, the peace they talk about was only possible thanks to our missiles and our growing base in the developing world."
Leon Malenyev"The ATO kept waging wars against people's revolutions and forcing new independent states to be their puppets. Binding them to themselves through the Arcasian Lira."
Leon Malenyev"And now yet another Arcasian president is telling us that we are the aggressors and they are the keepers of peace."
Leon Malenyev"We will not be silent anymore against these lies! When was this peace you talk about? Where was this cooperation between our countries? Have you ever accepted our invitation to talk about de-nuclearization?"
Leon Malenyev"If you don't like us, don't accept our deals, don't listen to our concerns, and keep pointing fingers at us, why did you invite us to the Alliance of Nations?"
Leon Malenyev"Every year, United Contana comes to this assembly to address the real problems of *real* people. People who suffer because of your systems, your wars and your ignorance."
Leon Malenyev"Whether you like it or not, we'll keep representing the laborers, the slaves, the oppressed of this world. And believe it or disregard it, history is on our side."
Leon Malenyev"We say to the representatives of capitalist countries... Come and visit us. Come and see the revolution working in Contana."
Leon Malenyev"Show our continent to your people without distorting the reality. If you cannot do that, then you need not come."
Leon Malenyev"We believe socialism will triumph over capitalism in the end. Such is the logical conclusion of the development of humankind."
NarratorThis time, the Lespian delegates started laughing very loudly. Some other delegates booed.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:07:47 p21615 / c467
  • ·"Ridiculous..."
  • ·"Malenyev's living in his dreams."
  • ▶Keep watching.
Maintaining silence allows for observation and prevents escalation.
DeepSeek-R1I kept watching.
Leon Malenyev"You can keep laughing, gentlemen. Communism will bury you."
NarratorSuddenly the laughing turned into shouting.
Leon Malenyev"If you really believe in cooperation, we will be waiting! We're not going anywhere."
Leon Malenyev"However, if the ATO keeps intervening in our matters... It is natural that the CSP would react."
Leon Malenyev"Despite threats from the ATO, Rumburg or any other nation or organization, we must not succumb to further division and violence."
Leon Malenyev"We must ask ourselves, what do the people of our countries expect of us? They surely don't want war and bloodshed."
Leon Malenyev"So let us leave to our children and grandchildren, good memories of our time and a better future for all. Let them look at history and say: once there were great divisions and complex problems, but the leaders of the world came together in the Alliance of Nations and succeeded in settling them in order to ensure our peaceful lives."
Leon Malenyev"Let us act in such a way to make this session not a session of threats, but a session of hopes. And the realization of them."
Leon Malenyev"The government of United Contana and the united peoples of Contana are ready to serve their duties for world peace."
Leon Malenyev"Long live the people's revolution."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:08:36 p21685 / c495
  • ·"That was a great speech."
  • ·Applaud his speech.
  • ▶Don't react.
Staying neutral avoids taking sides and maintains leverage.
DeepSeek-R1I did not react to his speech.
NarratorThe assembly broke into massive applause.
NarratorThe applause continued for some time. Malenyev slowly walked back to his seat.
Ephraim Nines"Thank you, ladies and gentlemen. We will reconvene later in the day for the final agenda items and closing remarks."
NarratorDeivid stood and stretched his arms.
Deivid Wisci"That was.... A lot."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:09:29 p21641 / c526

  • ▶"It was troubling. Tensions are too high."

  • ▶Move wealth out
  • ·Keep it
