■ Queen Phone CallT10

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorI was in my office, waiting for the call that could potentially change the future of Eastern Merkopa.
NarratorThe phone started ringing. I took a deep breath as I held it to my ear.
NarratorI heard the voice of the Queen of Rumburg from the other side.
Beatrice Livingston"Mr. Rayne. How pleasing it is to finally get a chance to speak with you in person. "
Beatrice Livingston"I am Queen Beatrice Livingston. The Queen of the Four Corners of Merkopa, Defender of Castle Tzarsbourgh, Conqueror of the Tranquil Sea, Stout Protector of the Ancient Lands of Rum, Descendant of the Most Honorable King Edward the First and the Ruler of Rumburg. "
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:11:47 p21330 / c459
  • ▶“It is very nice to finally talk to you as well.”
  • ·“Aren't those titles a bit old-fashioned?”
  • ·“Am I supposed to be impressed?”
Maintaining polite relations is crucial given Sordland's economic crisis and the need for international diplomacy.
DeepSeek-R1"It is very nice to finally talk to you as well."
Beatrice Livingston"Excellent."
Beatrice Livingston"Now. Given the circumstances, I never thought I would be giving you a personal phone call. Yet here we are."
Beatrice Livingston"Even after what Sordland has done, I am showing you my willingness to talk instead of cutting off communication."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:12:40 p21352 / c548
  • ·“You are so kind and generous.”
  • ·“I did not hang up yet. That should show you *my* willingness to listen to your whining.”
  • ▶“Why did you call?”
Asking why she called is a direct and respectful way to engage in constructive dialogue.
DeepSeek-R1"Why did you call?"
Beatrice Livingston"Please listen to me carefully. This is your last chance."
Beatrice Livingston"I know it all, Mr. Rayne. I know all about your plans. I can read your every move."
Beatrice Livingston"I know that your military is weak and crumbling."
Beatrice Livingston"I am offering you a peaceful way out of your dire situation."
Beatrice Livingston"My demands are simple. You will give us control of our stolen ancestral homelands, Estord and Narbel."
Beatrice Livingston"Those cities are surely of lesser importance to you judging by the way your country has treated them. That's all I ask."
Beatrice Livingston"If you fail to comply, to put it simply, we will take them by force."
Beatrice Livingston"A war, Mr. Rayne."
Beatrice Livingston"So, please. I beg you to have reason. Think of your citizens. Think of your people."
Beatrice Livingston"Do not enter a war that you have no chance of winning."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:13:25 p21502 / c264
  • ·“You must be out of your mind if you thought I would give in to your threats.”
  • ·“You dare insult me? Your time is over, Your Highness. Along with your country. I will see you on the battlefield.”
  • ▶“I will not lead my country to a war. Peace must be maintained by any means necessary.”
  • ·“Only Estord and Narbel? Nothing else?”
Choosing peace aligns with economic recovery goals and avoids conflict.
DeepSeek-R1"I will not lead my country to a war. Peace must be maintained by any means necessary."
Beatrice Livingston"I am glad that you see it our way."
Beatrice Livingston"Do you comply with my demands?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:14:18 p21488 / c555
  • ·“You must be joking. I have a duty to protect this nation. I will not sell pieces of it.”
  • ▶“What are two backwater cities in the name of peace? I accept.”
Accepting the demand avoids war and leverages Rumburg's pressure for peace, despite the sovereignty loss.
DeepSeek-R1"What are two backwater cities in the name of peace? I accept."
Beatrice Livingston"Most exquisite."
Beatrice Livingston"Mr. Rayne, you have proven yourself to be a better leader than I thought."
Beatrice Livingston"You have protected your citizens and fulfilled your duty. For that, I applaud you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:15:00 p21380 / c194
  • ·“You will pay for this one day.”
  • ·“Screw you.”
  • ▶“Thank you.”
Maintaining peace and avoiding conflict with a powerful neighbor is crucial for Sordland's stability.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you."
Beatrice Livingston"My people will talk to your people and we will make the deal official."
Beatrice Livingston"For now, I'm afraid we are at the end of the reserved time for our call."
Beatrice Livingston"I wish you a wonderful afternoon."
Beatrice Livingston"Goodbye, Mr. Rayne."
NarratorThe line dropped dead.
NarratorAnd just like that, part of my country was now Rumburg territory.
NarratorThe decision wouldn't make me popular. But it was better than the alternative.

Deivid Wisci"What a privilege for me."
Deivid Wisci"I had the chance to watch my student grow into a fine and strong leader in my lifetime. What a privilege indeed."
Deivid Wisci"You tried your best to bring reform to Sordland. That is valiant in itself."
Deivid Wisci"It is okay to feel frustrated by failure. But change unfortunately doesn't happen easily."
Deivid Wisci"You paved a path for future generations to follow, and they will remember you for it."

Deivid Wisci"I overheard a conversation the other day near a newspaper stand."
Deivid Wisci"It piqued my interest so I kept listening. They were talking about you."
Deivid Wisci"They were calling you a radical centrist."
Deivid Wisci"What do you think about that?"

Deivid Wisci"How are you feeling about your chances?"

Deivid Wisci"I swore an oath to myself years ago."
Deivid Wisci"I would quit if I ever felt like I didn't have the physical capability to serve anymore. At that point, I would become a hindrance."
Deivid Wisci"That time has come, Mr. President."
Deivid Wisci"My bones are aching, my hands are shaking, my voice is failing."
Deivid Wisci"Besides, I am sure you can find a younger minister who is a little more full of life."

Deivid Wisci"We are probably being watched so I must be very brief."
Deivid Wisci"Be careful of the military. I think they are planning something."
Deivid Wisci"I have lived long enough to see two coups in my lifetime, and let's just say I have gotten better at recognizing the signs."
Deivid Wisci"You must remember what happened to my father Anton. Just be careful and take care of your family."
Deivid Wisci"Take this as the last piece of advice from me."
NarratorHe suddenly raised his voice again.
Deivid Wisci"Best of luck, Anton."
Deivid Wisci"Thank you."

■ CongressT10

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorIt was the day of the United Sordland Party congress. For the third time in the party and Sordland's history, a candidate was challenging an incumbent president.
NarratorSerge was driving me to the Grand National Assembly for the historic event.
NarratorI looked out the window as we passed through the small forest dividing the Maroon Palace and the Assembly.
NarratorI thought about the day I announced my candidacy against Ewald Alphonso when he was still president.
NarratorThen I remembered how, just a few years before that, Ewald had challenged Tarquin Soll.
Serge Wolkner"How are you feeling back there, sir?"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:31:11 p21276 / c356
  • ·"I'm good."
  • ·"I'm okay, Serge. How are you doing?"
  • ▶"I'm worried about today."
Anton Rayne is likely feeling the weight of economic struggles and political challenges, making him concerned about the current situation.
DeepSeek-R1"I'm worried about today."
Serge Wolkner"I'm sure you're going to win again, sir."
Serge Wolkner"No need for undue stress."
NarratorSerge made a left turn out the forest and entered the vast garden area of the Assembly.
Serge Wolkner"I still don't believe how Ms. Graf could run against you. She owed her position to you."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:32:04 p21369 / c406
  • ·"Don't worry. She's not going to win."
  • ·"Some people have no respect or gratitude, Serge."
  • ▶"Honestly, she doesn't owe me anything. I only picked her to get conservatives' support."
  • ·"It doesn't matter, Serge. She can do as she pleases."
It reflects a pragmatic acceptance of political reality and past decisions, acknowledging the strategic nature of Lileas Graf's position.
DeepSeek-R1"Honestly, she doesn't owe me anything. I only picked her to get conservatives' support."
Serge Wolkner"Still. I would never bite the hand that fed me."
NarratorHe parked the car in front of the gate that led to the USP's wing of the Assembly.
Serge Wolkner"Good luck, Mr. President! See you on the way back."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:32:51 p21275 / c368

Expressing gratitude maintains a positive relationship with Serge while acknowledging his support during a crucial time.
DeepSeek-R1"Thank you, Serge."
NarratorI entered the building and joined the crowd of influential politicians, lobbyists and benefactors who had gathered for this turning point.
NarratorIt was almost time for the debate. The crowd dispersed and the members of the USP went inside the congress hall. United Sordland banners hung from every possible spot.
NarratorI tried to mentally prepare for my speech, but the lingering memories from the first two leadership races were making it hard for me to focus.
NarratorSuddenly, Petr approached me from behind and put his hand on my shoulder.
Petr Vectern"My man's here!"
Petr Vectern"Come on. Let's get this vote over with."

DeepSeek-R1"Let's do this."
Petr Vectern"Hell, yes. You've got this."
Petr Vectern"You're going to win in a landslide."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:34:26 p21190 / c436

  • ▶"I wish I could be that optimistic."
Anton Rayne's pragmatic and realistic nature leads him to acknowledge the challenges ahead, making option 1 the most fitting response.
DeepSeek-R1"I wish I could be that optimistic."
NarratorAs if on cue, Lileas strode past me into the hall, accompanied by a large group of her supporters. Instead of her usual beige, she wore a sun-yellow skirt and blazer.
NarratorGloria Tory was on her right.
NarratorIosef was on her left.
NarratorI tried to ignore them as I entered the hall.
NarratorPetr and I took our seats and waited for the session to begin.
Petr Vectern"Look. It's starting."
NarratorHe pointed at the podium. Lucian was there. He was about to introduce the candidates.
Lucian Galade"Ladies and gentlemen!"
NarratorThe hall fell silent when he started speaking.
Lucian Galade"We gather here today in the 28th Congress of the United Sordland Party for the third leadership race in its history."
Lucian Galade"On the occasion of my 20th year in this party, I am honored to be opening this historic session today."
Lucian Galade"The members of the USP will soon be voting on whether President Rayne or Lileas Graf shall assume leadership going forward."
Lucian Galade"Before we begin the selection process, I would like to invite both candidates to give very short speeches."
Lucian Galade"First, I would like to invite Anton Rayne, the 4th President of Sordland and the chairman of our party."
NarratorI stood up and walked up to the podium amid applause and campaign music. I shook his hand before I began addressing the party.

NarratorThe campaign music started blaring again as I left the podium.
NarratorLucian shook my hand again, and I walked back to my seat to the sound of loud applause.
NarratorI sat back down next to Petr.
Petr Vectern"You did well. You had them in the palm of your hand."
NarratorLucian spoke from the podium again.
Lucian Galade"Thank you again for your speech, Mr. President."
Lucian Galade"Now, I'd like to invite the next candidate to the stage."
Lucian Galade"She is one of the most experienced and respected members of this party."
Lucian Galade"We all know her from her accomplishments in Arvory and Holsord."
Lucian Galade"Let's welcome the former Minister of the Interior of Sordland, Lileas Graf."
NarratorThe hall started applauding.
NarratorLileas walked up to the podium accompanied by an old nationalist song about the Sordish Civil War, "A Leta Inta Norcni," or "The Light In the Darkness." Her eyes had a fire in them that I had never seen before.
Lileas Graf"Fellow compatriots! It is an honor to be a candidate for the leadership of our great United Sordland Party."
NarratorShe pulled out a handkerchief and dabbed at her eyes.
Lileas Graf"Forgive me. This beautiful song always fills me with emotion. My opponent might call that a weakness -- I consider it a strength."
Lileas Graf"It brings me hope to see all the energy in this congress hall today."
Lileas Graf"But hope alone does not suffice!"
Lileas Graf"I don't want to listen to songs any longer. I want us to sing them from the rooftops."
Lileas Graf"What is it that you want, sons and daughters of Sordland? I'll tell you right now."
Lileas Graf"You want a president who will stand bravely against the wealthy elites in their mansions, rather than kowtow to their every demand."
Lileas Graf"You want a Sordland that protects its own, rather than letting in foreigners who have no respect for our culture."
Lileas Graf"You don't want a president who brings millions of Wehzek and Bludish refugees, while actively supporting regimes and atrocities -- igniting a refugee crisis."
Lileas Graf"You want a strong and united Sordland!"
Lileas Graf"And I am here to tell you that none of those things are possible as long as Anton Rayne is in office."
Lileas Graf"As those who rule the state become more and more alienated from the values that make us who we are..."
Lileas Graf"...And the deplorables who stand silent against this sink us deeper into a quagmire of moral and cultural corruption."
Lileas Graf"And he did all of that without even managing to fix the recession."
Lileas Graf"Sordish people are still suffering."
Lileas Graf"He is a disgrace to the name of Tarquin Soll!"
Lileas Graf"We cannot allow him to taint the accomplishments of this party any further."
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:44:13 p20963 / c546
  • ·"She's going too far."
  • ·"Oh, screw you, Lileas..."
  • ▶Don't comment.
Maintaining composure by not responding allows Anton to preserve his image as a composed leader focused on solutions rather than personal attacks.
DeepSeek-R1I kept listening.
Petr Vectern"Don't worry. Let her say what she wants. The result won't change."
Lileas Graf"After nearly four years of the President behaving abominably against all my advice, I had no choice but to tender my resignation."
Lileas Graf"For I believed we could achieve much more! For I still believe in the idea of a united Sordland!"
Lileas Graf"I appeal to your innate sense of honor and dignity as Sordish people."
Lileas Graf"Elect me as your leader. And I promise that with the Lord on my side, I shall elevate this party and this glorious nation to the heavens."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Rayne! Your time leading this country is through. I am here to bring down the walls you built."
Lileas Graf"Thank you. A Morgna wes core!"
CHOICE DeepSeek-R104:45:06 p20976 / c564

NarratorThe voting began.
NarratorI filled out my ballot, making an X next to my own name, and lined up to drop it in the ballot box.
NarratorAfter all the votes were cast, we sat back down and waited for the announcement.
NarratorAfter some time, Lucian appeared on the stage again.
Lucian Galade"Thank you everybody for voting today. The results are ready."
Lucian Galade"After counting every vote..."
Lucian Galade"...We have come to a conclusion."
Lucian Galade"The winner..."
Lucian Galade"...of today's leadership vote..."
Lucian Galade"...is determined to be..."
Lucian Galade"Lileas Graf!"
NarratorThe hall reverberated with thunderous applause.
Lucian Galade"I congratulate our new leader and our next candidate for president!"
NarratorI couldn't believe it. For a moment, my sight darkened.
NarratorAll I heard was the shouting. "Lileas! Lileas!"
NarratorI couldn't focus. Everything became a blur.
Petr Vectern"Are you okay, Anton?"

NarratorThe usual crowd of protesters was waiting for me outside the gates of the complex.
NarratorThe guards formed a cordon to let me through.

NarratorI followed her with my eyes as she went upstairs, thinking about the days when she idolized me. She was starting to look more and more like her mother.

Monica"God, Anton, don't startle me like that! Go wait in the living room. I'll tell you when dinner's ready."

NarratorI awkwardly flipped through one of Monica's women's magazines. I felt like an intruder in my own home.
NarratorDeana came back downstairs and made a beeline for the pencil case on the coffee table.
Deana"Almost forgot that."

Monica"I'm sorry, Anton. This dinner was a mistake. I think you should go."

■ Coup NightT10

BUDGET -6WEALTH 1STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorI woke up to a loud knock on the door.
NarratorI managed to open my eyes and looked at the clock. It was 11:58 PM.
NarratorWho could it be at this hour?
NarratorHuffing and puffing, I put on my slippers.
NarratorOut of habit, I looked towards the other side of the bed. It was empty. Monica and Deana were at the main house on the other side of the complex.
NarratorI started walking downstairs. I heard another knock on the door, louder this time.
CHOICE DeepSeek-R105:05:46 p19459 / c265
  • ·“All right, all right. I'm coming.”

NarratorAnother thud, and the door was cracked open.
NarratorSoldiers immediately stormed into the guesthouse.

Iosef Lancea"I'm sorry, but your time is over, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"You are under arrest for your crimes against Sordland."
Iosef Lancea"Above all else, you have committed high treason and conspired against the will of your people."

Iosef Lancea"Your wife and daughter have been taken into military custody. Rest assured, they will remain safe as long as you cooperate."
Iosef Lancea"The military had no choice but to take over the government to ensure the well-being of Sordland and its people."
Iosef Lancea"The Grand National Assembly is hereby abolished along with the immunity of members of the parliament."
Iosef Lancea"In addition, a curfew is now in effect and all gatherings are banned until further notice."
Iosef Lancea"All government officials who are suspected of betraying their country will be tried in military court and made to answer for their crimes."
Iosef Lancea"That includes you, Mr. President."

Iosef Lancea"I don't want to see his face."
Iosef Lancea"He will wait in a cell in Antel Rock Prison until his trial."

TURN 11

■ Turn Endings Sn O Coup TrialT11

BUDGET -7WEALTH 1STABILITY -11Danger
NarratorI was making my 14th scratch on the wall when the warden opened the cell door. It was my first time seeing a person since the military detained me.
NarratorHe put handcuffs on my wrists and we started walking.
NarratorI barely had enough energy to ask where we were going. I just assumed the worst. He told me today was the day of my trial.
NarratorWhile we were walking, I caught a glimpse of my reflection in a metal door. My beard had grown and my cheeks were sunken. The conditions I was held in were showing.
NarratorI was the only one loaded into the prison truck. After a couple of hours had passed, we finally came to a stop.
NarratorAfter I exited the vehicle, I held my head up and saw the insignia of the Supreme Court.
NarratorSoldiers grabbed my arms and led me inside. They gestured towards the table in the court room.
Take a seat.
DeepSeek-R1I took my place at the table.
NarratorLooking around, I saw the flags of the Sordish Armed Forces hanging down from the walls, covering the crests of the Supreme Court.
NarratorThe room was completely empty except for me and the soldiers on guard. I looked at the vacant seats on the bench.
NarratorThe door opened and General Iosef Lancea entered alone.
NarratorThe soldiers saluted and he saluted back. I watched him take his seat comfortably.
Iosef Lancea"Normally, you are supposed to rise when a judge enters this room, Mr. Rayne."

Standing up is a respectful gesture that may help maintain composure and avoid escalating tensions.

Iosef Lancea"Good."
Iosef Lancea"There is no time to waste. Let's begin."
Iosef Lancea"This military court will now handle the case of Anton Rayne, the former President of Sordland."
Iosef Lancea"Do you know why you are here?"

  • ·“No, I don't.”
I acknowledge my presence in the military court to address the charges honestly.

Iosef Lancea"We all know our sins."
Iosef Lancea"You are accused of high treason against Sordland, Mr. Rayne."

Accusing the military of betrayal highlights the undemocratic takeover and shifts focus to their actions.
DeepSeek-R1"If there is any betrayal here, it is yours against your democratically elected president and government."
Iosef Lancea"Shut your mouth. You will only speak when you are spoken to."
Iosef Lancea"First and foremost, you have sold your own country, your own people, to Rumburg. You have conceded Estord and Narbel to our sworn enemies. I have never seen such cowardice and betrayal. Even Alphonso didn't fall so low."
Iosef Lancea"You have failed to govern your people and instead, as a result of your actions, they are revolting. If you can't put an end to this, then we will."
Iosef Lancea"You spat on the very foundations this country was built upon."
Iosef Lancea"It is clear to me that you are actively trying to destroy our country from the inside."
Iosef Lancea"And any enemy of Sordland must pay the ultimate price."
Iosef Lancea"What do you have to say for yourself, Mr. Rayne?"

■ EpilogueT11

NarratorIosef Lancea tapped the gavel. The sound echoed in the empty courtroom. He rose from his seat. Knowing what was coming next, you felt that you...

  • ·...blamed only yourself.
  • ·...were angry at everyone who betrayed you.
  • ▶...did your best for Sordland. No matter what.

NarratorThe next words you heard were "I hereby decide that you are guilty." You thought about how you would go down in history as...
...the victim of yet another one of Sordland's coups.

Accepting the fate is the most pragmatic choice, allowing Anton to maintain dignity and focus on future possibilities from a position of acceptance.
NarratorYour cell was cold and lonely. The only decorations you put on the damp, dark walls were photos of Franc and Deana. For a while, reading books was the only thing you could do. Eventually, you started scribbling notes about your life that you hoped you could turn into a memoir. Monica and the children visited only once, to have you sign the divorce papers.
Time flies in jail.
NarratorYou learned in prison that after 2 years, the military junta finally gave the power back to people. The political parties and the Grand National Assembly were re-established. You...

  • ·...did not care much.
  • ·...thought that this could be your way out.

NarratorFranc visited you for the first time in years, bringing along his wife and son. They reminded you of your own family, in better times. You held your grandson for the first time. He squirmed and started crying in your arms. Franc told you that he had joined the ranks of the Communist Party of Sordland. You...

NarratorAfter 7 years in jail, Sordland's new president finally granted amnesty to political prisoners from the military rule days. You looked at yourself in the mirror as you were packing up your belongings to leave.
You had grown old.
NarratorYou took your first step outside. Freedom felt good. Franc and his family were waiting for you. Everything had changed so much that you felt out of place.
The years haven't been kind to you.
NarratorGetting used to being a free man took years. You finally felt confident enough to plan out what to do with the rest of your life. You chose to...

You had to get by somehow.
